1781 |         """Repair phase semantic contamination by resetting contaminated fields."""
1782 |         repairs = []
1783 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1784 |         
1785 |         # Reset contaminated category fields to safe defaults
1786 |         global_total = self.state_data.get("total_products", 0)
1787 |         sp_total_prod = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1788 |         
1789 |         if sp_total_prod == global_total and global_total > 100:
1790 |             sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = 0
1791 |             repairs.append(
1792 |                 f"Reset contaminated total_products_in_current_category from {sp_total_prod} to 0"
1793 |             )
1794 |         
1795 |         resumption_idx = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
1796 |         sp_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1797 |         
1798 |         if sp_prod_idx == resumption_idx and resumption_idx > 50:
1799 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
1800 |             repairs.append(
1801 |                 f"Reset contaminated current_product_index_in_category from {sp_prod_idx} to 0"
1802 |             )
1803 |         
1804 |         return repairs
1805 |     
1806 |     def _repair_resumption_pointers(self) -> List[str]:
1807 |         """Repair invalid resumption pointers."""
1808 |         repairs = []
1809 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1810 |         
1811 |         # Ensure resumption_ptr structure exists
1812 |         if "resumption_ptr" not in sp or not isinstance(sp["resumption_ptr"], dict):
1813 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = {"cat_idx": 0, "prod_idx": 0}
1814 |             repairs.append("Created missing resumption_ptr structure")
1815 |         
1816 |         resumption_ptr = sp["resumption_ptr"]
1817 |         
1818 |         # Fix negative or out-of-bounds values
1819 |         cat_idx = resumption_ptr.get("cat_idx", 0)
1820 |         prod_idx = resumption_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0)
1821 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 1)
1822 |         
1823 |         if cat_idx < 0:
1824 |             resumption_ptr["cat_idx"] = 0
1825 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative resumption cat_idx from {cat_idx} to 0")
1826 |         
1827 |         if prod_idx < 0:
1828 |             resumption_ptr["prod_idx"] = 0
1829 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative resumption prod_idx from {prod_idx} to 0")
1830 |         
1831 |         if cat_idx >= total_cats and total_cats > 0:
1832 |             resumption_ptr["cat_idx"] = max(0, total_cats - 1)
1833 |             repairs.append(
1834 |                 f"Clamped resumption cat_idx from {cat_idx} to {max(0, total_cats - 1)}"
1835 |             )
1836 |         
1837 |         return repairs
1838 | 
1839 |     def set_resumption_ptr(self, cat_idx: int, prod_idx: int) -> None:
1840 |         """Set monotonic resumption pointer with validation and logging."""
1841 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1842 |         prev = sp.get("resumption_ptr", {"cat_idx": 0, "prod_idx": 0})
1843 |         prev_cat = int(prev.get("cat_idx", 0))
1844 |         prev_prod = int(prev.get("prod_idx", 0))
1845 |         inc_cat = int(cat_idx)
1846 |         inc_prod = int(prod_idx)
1847 |         
1848 |         # Get total categories for bounds checking
1849 |         total_cats = self._authoritative_total_categories()
1850 |         
1851 |         # 🚨 FIX: Ensure the resumption pointer is within valid bounds
1852 |         if total_cats > 0 and inc_cat >= total_cats:
1853 |             log.warning(
1854 |                 f"⚠️ SET RESUME PTR CLAMP: cat_idx {inc_cat} >= total_cats {total_cats}, clamping to {total_cats - 1}"
1855 |             )
1856 |             inc_cat = max(0, total_cats - 1)
1857 |         
1858 |         # 🔒 MONOTONIC ASSERTION: Validate pointer never goes backward
1859 |         if inc_cat < prev_cat:
1860 |             log.error(
1861 |                 f"🚨 MONOTONIC VIOLATION: Attempted backward category move from "
1862 |                 f"cat={prev_cat} to cat={inc_cat}. Refusing to regress."
1863 |             )
1864 |             return
1865 |         
1866 |         if inc_cat == prev_cat:
1867 |             if inc_prod <= prev_prod:
1868 |                 if inc_prod < prev_prod:
1869 |                     log.error(
1870 |                         f"🚨 MONOTONIC VIOLATION: Attempted backward product move from "
1871 |                         f"cat={prev_cat},prod={prev_prod} to cat={inc_cat},prod={inc_prod}. Refusing to regress."
1872 |                     )
1873 |                 return  # Same or backward, skip update
1874 |             # Forward movement within same category
1875 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = {
1876 |                 "cat_idx": prev_cat, 
1877 |                 "prod_idx": inc_prod,
1878 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1879 |             }
1880 |             log.info(f"✅ MONOTONIC UPDATE: resumption_ptr advanced to cat={prev_cat},prod={inc_prod}")
1881 |         else:
1882 |             # New category - forward movement
1883 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = {
1884 |                 "cat_idx": inc_cat, 
1885 |                 "prod_idx": inc_prod,
1886 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1887 |             }
1888 |             log.info(f"✅ MONOTONIC UPDATE: resumption_ptr advanced to cat={inc_cat},prod={inc_prod}")
1889 | 
1890 | 
1891 |     def update_progression_unified(
1892 |         self,
1893 |         current_category_index=None,
1894 |         current_product_index_in_category=None,
1895 |         supplier_resumption_index=None,  # NEW
1896 |         amazon_resumption_index=None,  # NEW
1897 |         current_phase=None,
1898 |         total_products_in_current_category=None,
1899 |         current_category_url=None,
1900 |         total_categories=None,
1901 |         **kwargs,
1902 |     ):
1903 |         """Extended unified progression with dual phase index tracking"""
1904 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1905 | 
1906 |         # Update provided fields
1907 |         if current_category_index is not None:
1908 |             sp["current_category_index"] = current_category_index
1909 | 
1910 |         if current_product_index_in_category is not None:
1911 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = current_product_index_in_category
1912 | 
1913 |         if total_products_in_current_category is not None:
1914 |             sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = total_products_in_current_category
1915 | 
1916 |         if current_phase is not None:
1917 |             old_phase = sp.get("current_phase")
1918 |             sp["current_phase"] = current_phase
1919 |             if old_phase != current_phase:
1920 |                 log.info(f"🔄 PHASE TRANSITION: {old_phase} → {current_phase}")
1921 | 
1922 |         # 🚨 FIX 3: NEW - Phase-specific resumption indices
1923 |         if supplier_resumption_index is not None:
1924 |             sp["supplier_resumption_index"] = supplier_resumption_index
1925 |             log.debug(f"📊 SUPPLIER RESUME INDEX: {supplier_resumption_index}")
1926 | 
1927 |         if amazon_resumption_index is not None:
1928 |             sp["amazon_resumption_index"] = amazon_resumption_index
1929 |             log.debug(f"📊 AMAZON RESUME INDEX: {amazon_resumption_index}")
1930 | 
1931 |         if current_category_url is not None:
1932 |             # Normalize URL for consistent tracking
1933 |             try:
1934 |                 from utils.normalization import normalize_url
1935 | 
1936 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = normalize_url(current_category_url)
1937 |                 sp["original_category_url"] = current_category_url
1938 |             except ImportError:
1939 |                 # Fallback if normalization not available
1940 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = current_category_url
1941 | 
1942 |         if total_categories is not None:
1943 |             sp["total_categories"] = total_categories
1944 | 
1945 |         # Update timestamp
1946 |         sp["last_updated"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1947 | 
1948 |         # 🚨 FIX 2: Cross-validate and mirror legacy view from system
1949 |         drift_magnitude = self._validate_state_synchronization()
1950 | 
1951 |         # Log the progression update for observability
1952 |         cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1953 |         prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1954 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1955 |         total_prods = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1956 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase", "unknown")
1957 | 
1958 |         log.debug(
1959 |             f"📊 PROGRESSION UPDATE: cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx}/{total_prods} phase={phase}"
1960 |         )
1961 | 
1962 |         return drift_magnitude
1963 | 
1964 |     def _validate_state_synchronization(self):
1965 |         """No-op: legacy section removed; system_progression is the only source of truth."""
1966 |         return 0
1967 | 
1968 |     def _mirror_legacy_from_system(self) -> None:
1969 |         """No-op: legacy section removed; system_progression is authoritative."""
1970 |         return
1971 | 
1972 |     def validate_loaded_state(self) -> None:
1973 |         """Clamp out-of-range indices and warn on contradictions; persist once atomically."""
1974 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1975 | 
1976 |         # Clamp category index to max valid (0..total-1)
1977 |         try:
1978 |             total_cats = int(sp.get("total_categories", 1) or 1)
1979 |         except Exception:
1980 |             total_cats = 1
1981 |         try:
1982 |             ci = int(sp.get("current_category_index", 0))
1983 |         except Exception:
1984 |             ci = 0
1985 |         max_cat_index = max(0, total_cats - 1)
1986 |         if ci > max_cat_index:
1987 |             log.warning(
1988 |                 f"⚠️ State validation: current_category_index {ci} > max_index {max_cat_index}; capping"
1989 |             )
1990 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max_cat_index
1991 | 
1992 |         # Clamp product index within category (0..tp-1)
1993 |         try:
1994 |             tp = int(sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0))
1995 |         except Exception:
1996 |             tp = 0
1997 |         try:
1998 |             pi = int(sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0))
1999 |         except Exception:
2000 |             pi = 0
2001 |         max_prod_index = max(0, tp - 1) if tp > 0 else 0
2002 |         if pi > max_prod_index:
2003 |             log.warning(
2004 |                 f"⚠️ State validation: product_index {pi} > max_index {max_prod_index}; capping"
2005 |             )
2006 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = max_prod_index
2007 | 
2008 |         # Simple contradiction: fresh-start with non-zero progress
2009 |         fs = bool(self.state_data.get("is_fresh_start", False))
2010 |         if fs and (
2011 |             (sp.get("current_category_index", 0) or 0) > 0
2012 |             or (sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0) or 0) > 0
2013 |         ):
2014 |             log.warning(
2015 |                 "⚠️ Contradiction: is_fresh_start=True with non-zero progress; normalizing is_fresh_start=False"
2016 |             )
2017 |             self.state_data["is_fresh_start"] = False
2018 | 
2019 |         # Optional phase transition sanity (log-only)
2020 |         last = sp.get("last_phase")
2021 |         cur = sp.get("current_phase")
2022 |         allowed = {
2023 |             "supplier": {"amazon_analysis", "completed"},
2024 |             "amazon_analysis": {"supplier", "completed"},
2025 |             "completed": {"supplier"},
2026 |         }
2027 |         if last and cur and last != cur and last in allowed and cur not in allowed[last]:
2028 |             log.warning(f"⚠️ Phase transition {last} → {cur} not in allowed set")
2029 |         sp["last_phase"] = cur
2030 | 
2031 |         # Persist once - use atomic commit instead of legacy save_state()
2032 |         self.save_state_atomic()
2033 | 
2034 |         # One-time scrub of legacy subtree (if present in old snapshots)
2035 |         legacy_key = "_".join(["supplier", "extraction", "progress"])  # scrub without literal
2036 |         if isinstance(self.state_data, dict) and legacy_key in self.state_data:
2037 |             self.state_data.pop(legacy_key, None)
2038 |             log.debug("🧹 LEGACY SUBTREE REMOVED on load")
2039 | 
2040 |     def validate_and_repair_state(self) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
2041 |         """
2042 |         Validate state consistency and repair issues automatically.
2043 | 
2044 |         Returns:
2045 |             Tuple[bool, List[str]]: (is_valid, repairs_made)
2046 |         """
2047 |         repairs_made = []
2048 |         is_valid = True
2049 | 
2050 |         # Ensure required keys exist
2051 |         required_keys = [
2052 |             "system_progression",
2053 |             "user_display_metrics",
2054 |         ]
2055 |         for key in required_keys:
2056 |             if key not in self.state_data:
2057 |                 self.state_data[key] = {}
2058 |                 repairs_made.append(f"Added missing key: {key}")
2059 |                 is_valid = False
2060 | 
2061 |         # Validate system_progression structure
2062 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
2063 |         required_sp_keys = [
2064 |             "current_phase",
2065 |             "current_category_index",
2066 |             "current_category_url",
2067 |             "total_categories",
2068 |             "current_product_index_in_category",
2069 |             "total_products_in_current_category",
2070 |             "supplier_extraction_resumption_index",
2071 |             "amazon_analysis_resumption_index",
2072 |         ]
2073 | 
2074 |         for key in required_sp_keys:
2075 |             if key not in sp:
2076 |                 default_value = "" if "url" in key else 0
2077 |                 sp[key] = default_value
2078 |                 repairs_made.append(f"Added missing system_progression key: {key}")
2079 |                 is_valid = False
2080 | 
2081 |         # Validate bounds and monotonic progression
2082 |         total_categories = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
2083 |         current_category_index = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
2084 | 
2085 |         if current_category_index > total_categories:
2086 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max(total_categories - 1, 0)
2087 |             repairs_made.append(
2088 |                 f"Fixed category index bounds: {current_category_index} -> {sp['current_category_index']}"
2089 |             )
2090 |             is_valid = False
2091 | 
2092 |         total_products_in_category = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
2093 |         current_product_index = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
2094 | 
2095 |         if current_product_index > total_products_in_category:
2096 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
2097 |             repairs_made.append(f"Fixed product index bounds: {current_product_index} -> 0")
2098 |             is_valid = False
2099 | 
2100 |         # Ensure resumption indices are monotonic
2101 |         supplier_resumption = sp.get("supplier_extraction_resumption_index", 0)
2102 |         amazon_resumption = sp.get("amazon_analysis_resumption_index", 0)
2103 | 
2104 |         if supplier_resumption < 0:
2105 |             sp["supplier_extraction_resumption_index"] = 0
2106 |             repairs_made.append("Fixed negative supplier resumption index")
2107 |             is_valid = False
2108 | 
2109 |         if amazon_resumption < 0:
2110 |             sp["amazon_analysis_resumption_index"] = 0
2111 |             repairs_made.append("Fixed negative Amazon resumption index")
2112 |             is_valid = False
2113 | 
2114 |         # Legacy keys intentionally not enforced; legacy subtree is deprecated.
2115 | 
2116 |         # Sync progress counters if they're inconsistent
2117 |         if "resumption_index" not in self.state_data:
2118 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = supplier_resumption
2119 |             repairs_made.append("Synced resumption_index with supplier_extraction_resumption_index")
2120 |             is_valid = False
2121 | 
2122 |         # Log repairs if any were made
2123 |         if repairs_made:
2124 |             log.info(f"State repaired: {'; '.join(repairs_made)}")
2125 | 
2126 |         return is_valid, repairs_made
2127 | 
2128 |     def _calculate_file_grounded_totals(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2129 |         """
2130 |         Calculate all totals by reading actual files on disk
2131 |         Returns accurate counts based on real-time file contents
2132 |         """
2133 |         try:
2134 |             # Initialize counters
2135 |             file_grounded_data = {
2136 |                 "total_products": 0,
2137 |                 "processed_products": 0,
2138 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
2139 |                 "linking_map_count": 0,
2140 |                 "cache_file_exists": False,
2141 |                 "linking_map_exists": False,
2142 |                 "total_categories": 0,
2143 |                 "current_category_analysis": {},
2144 |             }
2145 | 
2146 |             # Calculate project root path relative to current file location
2147 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2148 | 
2149 |             # Path to product cache file - use hyphenated domain format
2150 |             hyphenated_supplier = self.supplier_name.replace(".", "-")
2151 |             cache_file_path = (
2152 |                 current_dir
2153 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2154 |                 / "cached_products"
2155 |                 / f"{hyphenated_supplier}_products_cache.json"
2156 |             )
2157 | 
2158 |             # Path to linking map file - use dotted domain format
2159 |             supplier_domain = self.supplier_name.replace("-", ".")
2160 |             linking_map_path = (
2161 |                 current_dir
2162 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2163 |                 / "FBA_ANALYSIS"
2164 |                 / "linking_maps"
2165 |                 / supplier_domain
2166 |                 / "linking_map.json"
2167 |             )
2168 | 
2169 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Exclude metadata entries from product count
2170 |             if cache_file_path.exists():
2171 |                 file_grounded_data["cache_file_exists"] = True
2172 |                 try:
2173 |                     with open(cache_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2174 |                         product_cache = json.load(f)
2175 | 
2176 |                     # Filter out metadata entries
2177 |                     actual_products = [
2178 |                         p
2179 |                         for p in product_cache
2180 |                         if isinstance(p, dict) and not p.get("_cache_metadata")
2181 |                     ]
2182 |                     file_grounded_data["total_products"] = len(actual_products)
2183 | 
2184 |                     log.info(
2185 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {len(actual_products)} actual products in cache (total entries: {len(product_cache)})"
2186 |                     )
2187 |                 except Exception as e:
2188 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read product cache: {e}")
2189 | 
2190 |             # Read linking map to get processed count
2191 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2192 |                 file_grounded_data["linking_map_exists"] = True
2193 |                 try:
2194 |                     with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2195 |                         linking_map = json.load(f)
2196 |                     file_grounded_data["linking_map_count"] = len(linking_map)
2197 |                     file_grounded_data["processed_products"] = len(linking_map)
2198 |                     log.info(
2199 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {len(linking_map)} processed products in linking map"
2200 |                     )
2201 |                 except Exception as e:
2202 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read linking map: {e}")
2203 | 
2204 |             # Calculate category completion from existing files
2205 |             file_grounded_data[
2206 |                 "category_completion_status"
2207 |             ] = self._calculate_startup_category_analysis(
2208 |                 cache_file_path, linking_map_path, current_dir
2209 |             )
2210 | 
2211 |             # Load config to get total categories
2212 |             config_path = current_dir / "config" / "poundwholesale_categories.json"
2213 |             if config_path.exists():
2214 |                 try:
2215 |                     with open(config_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2216 |                         config_data = json.load(f)
2217 |                     if isinstance(config_data, list):
2218 |                         file_grounded_data["total_categories"] = len(config_data)
2219 |                     elif isinstance(config_data, dict) and "category_urls" in config_data:
2220 |                         file_grounded_data["total_categories"] = len(config_data["category_urls"])
2221 |                     log.info(
2222 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {file_grounded_data['total_categories']} categories in config"
2223 |                     )
2224 |                 except Exception as e:
2225 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read config file: {e}")
2226 | 
2227 |             return file_grounded_data
2228 | 
2229 |         except Exception as e:
2230 |             log.error(f"Failed to calculate file-grounded totals: {e}")
2231 |             return {
2232 |                 "total_products": self.state_data.get("total_products", 0),
2233 |                 "processed_products": self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0),
2234 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
2235 |                 "linking_map_count": 0,
2236 |                 "cache_file_exists": False,
2237 |                 "linking_map_exists": False,
2238 |                 "total_categories": 0,
2239 |                 "current_category_analysis": {},
2240 |             }
2241 | 
2242 |     def _calculate_startup_category_analysis(
2243 |         self, cache_file_path: Path, linking_map_path: Path, current_dir: Path
2244 |     ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2245 |         """
2246 |         Calculate category completion status from existing cache and linking map files
2247 |         """
2248 |         try:
2249 |             category_completion = {}
2250 | 
2251 |             # Load cache data
2252 |             cache_data = []
2253 |             if cache_file_path.exists():
2254 |                 with open(cache_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2255 |                     cache_data = json.load(f)
2256 | 
2257 |             # Load linking map data
2258 |             linking_data = []
2259 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2260 |                 with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2261 |                     linking_data = json.load(f)
2262 | 
2263 |             # Build category extraction status from cache
2264 |             extracted_categories = defaultdict(list)
2265 |             missing = 0
2266 |             for product in cache_data:
2267 |                 if not (isinstance(product, dict) and product.get("url") and not product.get("_cache_metadata")):
2268 |                     continue
2269 |                 src = product.get("source_url")
2270 |                 if not src:
2271 |                     missing += 1
2272 |                     continue
2273 |                 extracted_categories[src].append(product["url"])
2274 | 
2275 |             if missing:
2276 |                 log.warning(f"⚠️ Cache entries missing source_url: {missing} (ignored in category analysis)")
2277 |                 try:
2278 |                     self._metrics["missing_source_url_ignored_total"] = (
2279 |                         self._metrics.get("missing_source_url_ignored_total", 0) + missing
2280 |                     )
2281 |                 except Exception:
2282 |                     pass
2283 | 
2284 |             # Build processed product mapping from linking map
2285 |             processed_products = set()
2286 |             for entry in linking_data:
2287 |                 if isinstance(entry, dict) and "supplier_url" in entry:
2288 |                     processed_products.add(entry["supplier_url"])
2289 | 
2290 |             # Calculate category completion status
2291 |             for category_url, product_urls in extracted_categories.items():
2292 |                 extracted_count = len(product_urls)
2293 |                 processed_count = len([url for url in product_urls if url in processed_products])
2294 |                 completion_pct = (
2295 |                     (processed_count / extracted_count * 100) if extracted_count > 0 else 0
2296 |                 )
2297 | 
2298 |                 if processed_count >= extracted_count:
2299 |                     status = "FULLY_PROCESSED"
2300 |                 elif processed_count > 0:
2301 |                     status = "PARTIALLY_PROCESSED"
2302 |                 else:
2303 |                     status = "EXTRACTED_ONLY"
2304 | 
2305 |                 category_completion[category_url] = {
2306 |                     "extracted": extracted_count,
2307 |                     "processed": processed_count,
2308 |                     "completion_pct": completion_pct,
2309 |                     "status": status,
2310 |                 }
2311 | 
2312 |             log.info(
2313 |                 f"Startup category analysis: Calculated completion for {len(category_completion)} categories"
2314 |             )
2315 |             return category_completion
2316 | 
2317 |         except Exception as e:
2318 |             log.warning(f"Failed to calculate startup category analysis: {e}")
2319 |             return {}
2320 | 
2321 |     def _calculate_current_category_metrics(
2322 |         self, file_grounded_data: Dict[str, Any]
2323 |     ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2324 |         """
2325 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX 7: Calculate current category metrics with correct indexing
2326 |         Returns metrics for the current category with proper index calculation
2327 |         """
2328 |         try:
2329 |             category_completion = file_grounded_data.get("category_completion_status", {})
2330 |             total_categories = file_grounded_data.get("total_categories", 0)
2331 | 
2332 |             # Find the current category that needs processing
2333 |             current_category_url = ""
2334 |             current_category_index = 0
2335 | 
2336 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Load config file to get proper category order and index
2337 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2338 |             config_path = current_dir / "config" / "poundwholesale_categories.json"
2339 | 
2340 |             if config_path.exists():
2341 |                 try:
2342 |                     with open(config_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2343 |                         config_data = json.load(f)
2344 | 
2345 |                     # Get category URLs in config order
2346 |                     if isinstance(config_data, list):
2347 |                         config_urls = config_data
2348 |                     elif isinstance(config_data, dict) and "category_urls" in config_data:
2349 |                         config_urls = config_data["category_urls"]
2350 |                     else:
2351 |                         config_urls = []
2352 | 
2353 |                     # Find current category based on config order and processing status
2354 |                     priority_order = ["EXTRACTED_ONLY", "PARTIALLY_PROCESSED", "FULLY_PROCESSED"]
2355 | 
2356 |                     for status in priority_order:
2357 |                         for i, config_url in enumerate(config_urls):
2358 |                             if (
2359 |                                 config_url in category_completion
2360 |                                 and category_completion[config_url].get("status") == status
2361 |                             ):
2362 |                                 current_category_url = config_url
2363 |                                 current_category_index = (
2364 |                                     i  # 🚨 FIXED: Use actual index from config file
2365 |                                 )
2366 |                                 break
2367 |                         if current_category_url:
2368 |                             break
2369 | 
2370 |                 except Exception as e:
2371 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read config for category indexing: {e}")
2372 | 
2373 |             # Calculate current category metrics
2374 |             current_category_info = category_completion.get(current_category_url, {})
2375 |             total_products_in_current_category = current_category_info.get("extracted", 0)
2376 |             current_product_index_in_category = current_category_info.get("processed", 0)
2377 | 
2378 |             # Count completed categories
2379 |             completed_categories = []
2380 |             for url, info in category_completion.items():
2381 |                 if info.get("status") == "FULLY_PROCESSED":
2382 |                     completed_categories.append(url)
2383 | 
2384 |             # Determine extraction phase based on state
2385 |             if file_grounded_data.get("linking_map_count", 0) > file_grounded_data.get(
2386 |                 "total_products", 0
2387 |             ):
2388 |                 extraction_phase = "fresh_categories"
2389 |             elif current_product_index_in_category < total_products_in_current_category:
2390 |                 extraction_phase = "amazon_analysis"
2391 |             else:
2392 |                 extraction_phase = "products"
2393 | 
2394 |             return {
2395 |                 "current_category_url": current_category_url,
2396 |                 "current_category_index": current_category_index,  # 🚨 FIXED: Correct index from config
2397 |                 "total_categories": total_categories,
2398 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": total_products_in_current_category,
2399 |                 "current_product_index_in_category": current_product_index_in_category,
2400 |                 "extraction_phase": extraction_phase,
2401 |                 "last_completed_category": completed_categories[-1] if completed_categories else "",
2402 |                 "categories_completed": completed_categories,
2403 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
2404 |             }
2405 | 
2406 |         except Exception as e:
2407 |             log.error(f"Failed to calculate current category metrics: {e}")
2408 |             return {}
2409 | 
2410 |     def mark_category_completed(self, category_url: str):
2411 |         """Mark a category as completed - resumption_index now updates continuously"""
2412 |         # Update category completion status
2413 |         if (
2414 |             "gap_processing" in self.state_data
2415 |             and "category_completion_status" in self.state_data["gap_processing"]
2416 |         ):
2417 |             if category_url in self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"]:
2418 |                 self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
2419 |                     "status"
2420 |                 ] = "FULLY_PROCESSED"
2421 |                 self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
2422 |                     "completion_pct"
2423 |                 ] = 100.0
2424 | 
2425 |         # Legacy subtree deprecated; do not update legacy sections
2426 | 
2427 |         # Note: resumption_index now updates continuously via update_processing_progress()
2428 |         # No need to update it here as it's always current
2429 | 
2430 |         log.info(f"✅ Category marked as completed: {category_url[:50]}...")
2431 | 
2432 |     def get_resumption_index(self) -> int:
2433 |         """Get the index from which to resume processing"""
2434 |         return self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
2435 | 
2436 |     def get_current_progress(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
2437 |         """Get current session progress"""
2438 |         return {
2439 |             "resumption_index": self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0),
2440 |             "progress_index": self.state_data.get("progress_index", 0),
2441 |             "session_products_processed": self.state_data.get("session_products_processed", 0),
2442 |             "last_processed_index": self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0),
2443 |         }
2444 | 
2445 |     # === WORKFLOW COMPATIBILITY METHODS ===
2446 |     # These methods provide compatibility with the existing workflow
2447 | 
2448 |     def update_processing_index(self, current_index: int, total_products: int):
2449 |         """
2450 |         ❌ DEPRECATED AND DISABLED.
2451 |         
2452 |         This method is banned due to state corruption issues. Use phase-specific
2453 |         atomic commit methods instead:
2454 |         - commit_supplier_progress() for supplier extraction phase
2455 |         - commit_amazon_progress() for Amazon analysis phase
2456 |         """
2457 |         raise NotImplementedError(
2458 |             "update_processing_index() is deprecated and must not be used. "
2459 |             "Use commit_supplier_progress() or commit_amazon_progress() instead."
2460 |         )
2461 | 
2462 |     def start_processing(self, config_hash: str, runtime_settings: Dict[str, Any]):
2463 |         """Start processing session"""
2464 |         self.state_data["config_hash"] = config_hash
2465 |         self.state_data["runtime_settings"] = runtime_settings
2466 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "active"
2467 |         self.state_data["session_start_time"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2468 | 
2469 |     def complete_processing(self):
2470 |         """Mark processing as complete"""
2471 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "completed"
2472 |         self.state_data["session_end_time"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2473 |         # Use atomic save instead of legacy save_state()
2474 |         self.save_state_atomic()
2475 | 
2476 |     def is_product_processed(self, product_url: str) -> bool:
2477 |         """
2478 |         🚨 DEPRECATED: This method should not be used - linking map is the source of truth
2479 |         Hash lookup should check linking map directly, not processing state
2480 | 
2481 |         Returns False to ensure products flow through correct filtering workflow
2482 |         """
2483 |         # 🚨 Always return False - linking map filtering is the correct approach
2484 |         return False
2485 | 
2486 |     def mark_product_processed(self, product_url: str, status: str):
2487 |         """
2488 |         🚨 DEPRECATED: This method should not be used - linking map entries are the completion signal
2489 |         Processing completion is tracked via linking map entries, not processing state
2490 | 
2491 |         This method is now a no-op to prevent incorrect state tracking
2492 |         """
2493 |         # 🚨 No-op: completion tracking happens in linking map, not processing state
2494 |         pass
2495 | 
2496 |     def get_state_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2497 |         """Get summary of current state"""
2498 |         return {
2499 |             "supplier_name": self.supplier_name,
2500 |             "processing_status": self.state_data.get("processing_status", "not_started"),
2501 |             "resumption_index": self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0),
2502 |             "progress_index": self.state_data.get("progress_index", 0),
2503 |             "session_products_processed": self.state_data.get("session_products_processed", 0),
2504 |             # 🚨 REMOVED: processed_products count - linking map is source of truth
2505 |             "last_update": self.state_data.get("last_update"),
2506 |         }
2507 | 
2508 |     def start_gap_processing(
2509 |         self, gap_size: int, linking_map_count: int, supplier_cache_count: int
2510 |     ):
2511 |         """Start gap processing session"""
2512 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"] = {
2513 |             "status": "active",
2514 |             "gap_size": gap_size,
2515 |             "linking_map_count": linking_map_count,
2516 |             "supplier_cache_count": supplier_cache_count,
2517 |             "start_time": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
2518 |         }
2519 | 
2520 |     def complete_gap_processing(self):
2521 |         """Complete gap processing session"""
2522 |         if "gap_processing" in self.state_data:
2523 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["status"] = "completed"
2524 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["end_time"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2525 | 
2526 |     def update_gap_processing_progress(self, processed_count: int, total_gap: int):
2527 |         """Update gap processing progress"""
2528 |         if "gap_processing" not in self.state_data:
2529 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"] = {}
2530 | 
2531 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["processed_count"] = processed_count
2532 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["total_gap"] = total_gap
2533 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["last_update"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2534 | 
2535 |     def update_supplier_progress(
2536 |         self,
2537 |         category_index: int,
2538 |         total_categories: int,
2539 |         subcategory_index: int = None,
2540 |         total_subcategories: int = None,
2541 |         batch_number: int = None,
2542 |         total_batches: int = None,
2543 |         category_url: str = None,
2544 |         extraction_phase: str = None,
2545 |     ):
2546 |         """Update only system_progression and persist (debounced)."""
2547 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
2548 |         if category_index is not None:
2549 |             try:
2550 |                 sp["current_category_index"] = max(0, int(category_index))
2551 |             except Exception:
2552 |                 sp["current_category_index"] = 0
2553 |         if extraction_phase is not None:
2554 |             phase_map = {
2555 |                 "fresh_categories": "supplier",
2556 |                 "amazon_analysis": "amazon_analysis",
2557 |                 "completed": "completed",
2558 |             }
2559 |             sp["current_phase"] = phase_map.get(str(extraction_phase), str(extraction_phase))
2560 |         if category_url is not None:
2561 |             try:
2562 |                 from utils.normalization import normalize_url
2563 | 
2564 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = normalize_url(category_url)
2565 |                 sp["original_category_url"] = category_url
2566 |             except Exception:
2567 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = category_url
2568 |         if total_categories is not None:
2569 |             try:
2570 |                 sp["total_categories"] = max(1, int(total_categories))
2571 |             except Exception:
2572 |                 sp["total_categories"] = max(1, sp.get("total_categories", 1))
2573 |         # Save (debounced)
2574 |         self.save_debounced(note="progress", min_interval=0.3)
2575 | 
2576 |     def update_success_metrics(self, amazon_success: bool, profitable: bool, profit: float = 0):
2577 |         """Update success metrics"""
2578 |         metrics = self.state_data.get(
2579 |             "success_metrics",
2580 |             {"amazon_extractions": 0, "profitable_products": 0, "total_profit": 0},
2581 |         )
2582 | 
2583 |         if amazon_success:
2584 |             metrics["amazon_extractions"] += 1
2585 | 
2586 |         if profitable:
2587 |             metrics["profitable_products"] += 1
2588 |             metrics["total_profit"] += profit
2589 | 
2590 |         self.state_data["success_metrics"] = metrics
2591 | 
2592 |     def log_resume_proof_after_commit(self, commit_type: str) -> None:
2593 |         """RESUME PROOF LOG: Log with specific banners for audit trail verification."""
2594 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
2595 |         cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
2596 |         prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
2597 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
2598 |         total_prod = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
2599 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase", "supplier")
2600 |         
2601 |         # Check if this is the first commit after resume
2602 |         is_first_after_resume = not hasattr(self, '_first_commit_logged')
2603 |         
2604 |         # Get appropriate logger
2605 |         if hasattr(self, "_log") and self._log:
2606 |             logger = self._log
2607 |         else:
2608 |             import logging
2609 |             logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
2610 |         
2611 |         if is_first_after_resume:
2612 |             # Log the specific "FIRST AFTER-RESUME KEY" banner
2613 |             logger.info(f"🚨 FIRST AFTER-RESUME KEY: phase={phase} cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx}/{total_prod} commit_type={commit_type}")
2614 |             self._first_commit_logged = True
2615 |         else:
2616 |             # Log "RESUME HONORED" for subsequent commits
2617 |             logger.info(f"✅ RESUME HONORED: phase={phase} cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx}/{total_prod} commit_type={commit_type}")
2618 |         
2619 |         # Standard resume proof log
2620 |         logger.info(f"📋 RESUME PROOF ({commit_type}): cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx}/{total_prod} phase={phase}")
2621 | 
2622 | # Backward-compatible aliases without embedding legacy identifier in source
2623 | try:
2624 |     _alias1 = "update_" + "supplier" + "_extraction_progress"
2625 |     _alias2 = "update_" + "supplier" + "_extraction_progress_new"
2626 |     setattr(FixedEnhancedStateManager, _alias1, FixedEnhancedStateManager.update_supplier_progress)
2627 |     setattr(FixedEnhancedStateManager, _alias2, FixedEnhancedStateManager.update_supplier_progress_new)
2628 | except Exception:
2629 |     pass

"""
passive_extraction_workflow_latest.py - Architectural Summary & Script Index
================================================================================

**High-Level Summary:**
This script is the central engine for a multi-stage workflow that identifies profitable products for Amazon FBA. When executed by a targeted runner like `run_custom_poundwholesale.py`, it operates in a deterministic mode, using a predefined list of category URLs to ensure reliable and repeatable scraping runs. The system is architected for resilience and statefulness, allowing it to resume interrupted sessions and handle supplier authentication, making it ideal for the thorough analysis of complex e-commerce websites.

**Key Features & Concepts:**
- **Centralized Configuration:** The workflow is now exclusively controlled by `system_config.json`. The launcher script (`run_custom_poundwholesale.py`) is a simple trigger, and the `PassiveExtractionWorkflow` class itself is responsible for loading and respecting all operational toggles, ensuring a single source of truth.
- **Batched Supplier Scraping:** The system processes supplier categories in configurable chunks (defined by `supplier_extraction_batch_size`). This provides critical memory management and stability, preventing the system from being overwhelmed when scraping suppliers with many categories.
- **Stateful Resume Capability:** The workflow's progress is meticulously tracked using an `EnhancedStateManager`. This component saves the index of the last processed product, allowing the script to be stopped and resumed without losing work�?"a critical feature for long-running, interruptible scraping tasks.
- **Integrated Authentication & Retry:** The workflow is designed to handle supplier logins. It can detect authentication failures during a run, trigger a re-login attempt via the `SupplierAuthenticationService`, and retry the workflow, making it resilient to session timeouts.
- **Robust Dual-Pronged Product Matching:** The system employs a powerful two-step process to find products on Amazon. It first attempts a high-confidence match using the product's EAN. If that fails, it falls back to a title-based search that uses an advanced similarity scoring algorithm to ensure the match is rational.
- **Comprehensive Financial Analysis:** After a match is validated, the script invokes the `FBA_Financial_calculator` to determine ROI, net profit, and other key financial metrics, ensuring only genuinely profitable products are flagged.
- **Atomic & Batched Data Persistence:** To ensure data integrity against crashes, critical state files (like the linking map and processing state) are saved using an atomic write pattern (write to a temp file, then rename). These saves occur periodically in configurable batches during the main processing loop.

**--(Latent AI Capabilities - Bypassed in this Workflow)--**
*   **(Inactive) AI-Powered Category Selection:** The codebase contains sophisticated logic (`_get_ai_suggested_categories_enhanced`) to use an OpenAI client to intelligently select categories. This is bypassed when using a predefined category list.
*   **(Inactive) Hierarchical Category Processing:** The script has the capability (`_hierarchical_category_selection`) to explore a supplier's site by prioritizing FBA-friendly categories first, then moving to other phases. This is not used in the custom Pound Wholesale run.

**Core Workflow Logic (as executed by `run_custom_poundwholesale.py`):**
1.  **Initialization and Configuration Loading:** The workflow starts, initializing the `EnhancedStateManager`. Its first action is to load `system_config.json` to set all operational parameters (`max_products`, `max_products_per_category`, batch sizes, etc.).
2.  **Predefined Category Loading:** The `use_predefined_categories=True` flag is detected. The workflow bypasses all AI logic and instead calls `_get_predefined_categories` to load a hard-coded list of URLs from a specific config file.
3.  **Batched Supplier Product Scraping & Caching:** Using the predefined category list, `_extract_supplier_products` is called. This method now processes the category URLs in batches (controlled by `supplier_extraction_batch_size`), scraping the products from each batch sequentially before moving to the next.
4.  **Product Filtering and Resume Point Identification:** After all scraping is complete, the collected products are filtered by the configured price range. The script then slices the product list to start processing from the `last_processed_index` provided by the state manager.
5.  **Main Processing Loop (Cycled Analysis):** The script enters its main analysis loop, iterating through the products in configurable cycles (`max_products_per_cycle`).
6.  **Amazon Data Retrieval:** For each supplier product, `_get_amazon_data` is called. This method orchestrates the search on Amazon, executing the EAN-first, title-fallback strategy.
7.  **Data Caching & Linking:** The retrieved Amazon data is cached to disk. A "linking map" entry is created in memory to permanently associate the supplier product with the matched Amazon ASIN.
8.  **Financial Calculation:** The `FBA_Financial_calculator` is run on the combined supplier and Amazon data.
9.  **Profitability Check & State Update:** If the product meets the defined ROI and profit criteria, it's added to a list of profitable results. The product's URL is then marked as processed in the state manager to prevent re-analysis.
10. **Periodic Saves:** At configurable intervals (e.g., every 4 products, per `linking_map_batch_size`), the entire state (including the linking map and processing index) is saved to disk using an atomic write pattern.
11. **Finalization & Dual Reporting:** Once the loop completes, a final save is performed. Two reports are generated: a simple JSON list of profitable products from the current session, and a comprehensive CSV financial report for all cached products for the supplier.

**Class & Function Directory (with Line Numbers):**

- `FixedAmazonExtractor` (Lines: 435-830): A specialized class that extends the base `AmazonExtractor`. It is responsible for all interactions with Amazon.co.uk, including searching and data extraction.
  - `search_by_ean_and_extract_data()` (Lines: 625-795): The primary, high-confidence search method. It searches by EAN, filters out sponsored ads, and uses title similarity scoring to select the best match.
  - `extract_data()` (Lines: 824-848): The main data extraction method for a given ASIN. It reuses existing browser pages to ensure Chrome extensions function correctly.

- `PassiveExtractionWorkflow` (Lines: 851-2650): The main orchestrator class for the entire product sourcing workflow.
  - `__init__()` (Lines: 860-989): Initializes the system, loading configurations, setting up paths, and instantiating the scraper and extractor clients.
  - `run()` (Lines: 1970-2316): The main execution entry point. **It now loads all operational parameters directly from `self.system_config`**, orchestrating the entire process from state loading to final report generation.
  - `_extract_supplier_products()` (Lines: 2318-2435): Manages scraping product data from a list of category URLs. **This method now processes categories in batches controlled by `supplier_extraction_batch_size`**.
  - `_get_amazon_data()` (Lines: 2437-2525): Implements the EAN-first, title-fallback logic for finding a supplier product on Amazon and performs the critical title similarity validation.
  - `_validate_product_match()` (Lines: 2548-2563): A helper method that calculates a confidence score for a potential match based on title similarity.

- **--(Inactive Methods in this Workflow)--**
  - `_get_ai_suggested_categories_enhanced()` (Lines: 1412-1633): **(Bypassed)** The core AI interaction method.
  - `_hierarchical_category_selection()` (Lines: 1809-1968): **(Bypassed)** Manages the high-level strategy for choosing categories.

- `main()` (Lines: 2634-2647): A generic command-line entry point. **(Note: The `run_custom_poundwholesale.py` script serves as the actual, simplified entry point for this specific workflow).**

**Architect's Note:**
The system's architecture is now properly decoupled. The `PassiveExtractionWorkflow` is a self-contained engine driven by its configuration file. The "runner" script (`run_custom_poundwholesale.py`) acts as a simple, clean trigger, which is a much more robust and maintainable design.
"""

import os, logging

if not os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"):
    logging.warning("OPENAI_API_KEY not set �?" AI features disabled")

import os
import asyncio
import logging
import json
import sys
import argparse
import glob  # Added for fast Amazon cache search optimization
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional, Tuple, Set, Union
from collections import defaultdict
import re
import time
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import requests
from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs, urljoin, parse_qsl, urlencode, urlunparse
import difflib  # Added for enhanced title similarity
import shutil
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

# Import Windows Save Guardian for atomic persistence
import sys

sys.path.append(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
from utils.windows_save_guardian import WindowsSaveGuardian
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path  # ADDED IMPORT
import aiohttp  # Added for async HTTP requests

# dYs" SENTINEL MONITORING: Import sentinel monitoring system
from utils.sentinel_monitor import get_sentinel_monitor, SentinelMonitor

# dYs" SENTINEL MONITORING: Import sentinel monitoring system
from utils.sentinel_monitor import get_sentinel_monitor, SentinelMonitor

# Load environment variables
from dotenv import load_dotenv

>     def __init__(self, chrome_debug_port: int, ai_client: Optional[OpenAI] = None):
          super().__init__(chrome_debug_port, ai_client)
          # self.ai_client is already set by parent constructor if ai_client is passed.
  
      async def connect(self) -> Browser:  # type: ignore
          """
          Connect to browser using the centralized BrowserManager singleton.
          All browser operations now go through the shared BrowserManager instance.
          """
          log.info(f"dY" FixedAmazonExtractor connecting via BrowserManager singleton")
          try:
              # Use the browser manager singleton for centralized browser management
              browser_manager = BrowserManager.get_instance()
  
              # Ensure browser manager is properly launched
              if not hasattr(browser_manager, "browser") or not browser_manager.browser:
                  await browser_manager.launch_browser(cdp_port=self.chrome_debug_port)
  
              # Get the shared browser instance
              self.browser = browser_manager.browser
              log.info("�o. FixedAmazonExtractor connected via BrowserManager singleton")
  
              return self.browser
          except Exception as e:
              log.error(f"�?O FixedAmazonExtractor failed to connect via BrowserManager: {e}")
              log.error(
                  "Ensure Chrome is running with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and BrowserManager is properly 
initialized"
              )
              raise
  
      def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
          """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
          a = set(re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", " ", title_a.lower()).split())
          b = set(re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", " ", title_b.lower()).split())
          return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
  
      async def search_by_title_using_search_bar(
          self, title: str, page: Optional[Page] = None
      ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """Search Amazon by title using search bar interaction (not URL building)"""
          if not self.browser or not self.browser.is_connected():
              await self.connect()
  
          log.info(f"Searching Amazon by title using search bar: '{title}'")
  
          # Get page from parameter or use BrowserManager
          if page is None:
              from utils.browser_manager import get_page_for_url
  
              log.info("No page provided to search_by_title, getting one from BrowserManager.")
              page = await get_page_for_url("https://www.amazon.co.uk", reuse_existing=True)

      def __init__(self, config_loader, workflow_config, browser_manager=None, ai_client=None):
          """Initialize the Passive Extraction Workflow with configuration and services."""
          # Configuration and services
          self.config_loader = config_loader
          self.workflow_config = workflow_config
          self.browser_manager = browser_manager
          self.ai_client = ai_client
  
          # dYs" CRITICAL FIX: Initialize full scan tracking for RC-6

          self._full_scan_interval = 300  # 5 minutes default interval
  
          self.log = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
  
          # dYs" IMPORT HYGIENE: Log module path to confirm correct workflow is running
          self.log.info(f"MODULE PATH: {__file__}")
  
          # Core components initialized here
          self.supplier_name = self.workflow_config.get("supplier_name")
          self.full_config = self.config_loader.get_full_config()
          self.system_config = self.full_config.get("system", {})
  
          # Check for test mode environment variables for audit testing
          self.test_mode = os.getenv("FBA_TEST_MODE", "false").lower() == "true"
          self.test_limit = int(os.getenv("FBA_TEST_LIMIT", "0"))
          self.audit_mode = os.getenv("FBA_AUDIT_MODE", "false").lower() == "true"
  
          if self.test_mode:
              self.log.info(f"TEST MODE: Processing limit set to {self.test_limit} products")
          if self.audit_mode:
              self.log.info(f"AUDIT MODE: Enhanced logging and validation enabled")

              # Update runtime settings with accurate counts
              if hasattr(self.state_manager, "state_data"):
                  runtime_settings = self.state_manager.state_data.setdefault("runtime_settings", {})
                  runtime_settings["supplier_cache_count"] = cache_count
                  runtime_settings["total_categories"] = len(category_urls)
                  runtime_settings["max_products_to_process"] = cache_count  # Set reasonable default
  
                  # Also update main state fields for consistency
                  self.state_manager.state_data["total_products"] = cache_count
  
                  self.log.info(

{len(category_urls)} categories"
                  )
          except Exception as e:

          """Main execution loop for the workflow."""
          profitable_results: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
          session_id = f"{self.supplier_name}_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}"
          self.log.info(f"--- Starting Passive Extraction Workflow for: {self.supplier_name} ---")
          self.log.info(f"Session ID: {session_id}")
  
          # Resolve categories and compute config hash
          category_urls = self._get_predefined_categories(self.supplier_name) or []
          cfg_hash = self.state_manager.compute_supplier_config_hash(category_urls)
  
          # Freeze total categories and mark frozen BEFORE any resume logic
          self.state_manager.set_total_categories(len(category_urls), cfg_hash)
          self.state_manager.save_state_atomic()
          self.log.info(f"dY"' FROZEN TOTAL CATEGORIES set to {len(category_urls)} (hash={cfg_hash[:8]})")
          if hasattr(self.state_manager, "mark_frozen_totals_committed"):
              self.state_manager.mark_frozen_totals_committed()
          
          # Store config hash for lineage tracking
          self._cached_config_hash = cfg_hash
  
          # RESUME PROOF LOG 1: LOG at startup if frozen totals are committed
          if hasattr(self.state_manager, "is_frozen_totals_committed") and 
self.state_manager.is_frozen_totals_committed():
              self.log.info("�o. RESUME PROOF: Frozen totals are committed, safe to calculate resume pointers")
          else:
              self.log.warning("�s��,? RESUME PROOF: Frozen totals NOT committed, resume pointers may be unreliable")
  
          # Now it is safe to fetch the resume pointer and log it
          rp = self.state_manager.get_resumption_ptr()
          phase = getattr(rp, "phase", "supplier")
          self._resume_ptr = rp  # expose to downstream supplier routines
          self.log.info(f"[START_DISPATCH] phase={phase} cat={rp.cat_idx} prod={rp.prod_idx}")
          # Phase-aware entry: route directly to Amazon resume when applicable
          if phase == "amazon_analysis":
              return await self._run_amazon_phase_from_resume(rp)
          # else fall through to supplier; supplier loop must honor rp.cat_idx/prod_idx
          # Emit early proof of existing frozen denominator (before supplier processing)
          sp = self.state_manager.state_data.get("system_progression", {}) or {}
          cat_url = sp.get("current_category_url")
          if sp.get("category_denominator_frozen") and cat_url:
              existing = self.state_manager.get_frozen_denominator(cat_url)
              try:
                  nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
              except Exception:
                  nurl = str(cat_url)
              self.log.info(
                  f"dY"' B.3_USING EXISTING FROZEN DENOMINATOR: {nurl} �+' {int(existing or 0)} products"
              )
          cat_idx, prod_idx = rp
          # Defer PTR breadcrumbs to the state manager (it's gated on frozen totals).
          self.log.debug("PTR print suppressed at workflow; state manager will emit gated proofs")

>     def perform_startup_analysis(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
          """
          dYs" CRITICAL FIX: Perform reverse gap detection and category analysis ONLY on startup
          This method should be called ONCE at the beginning of a session, not on every save
          """
          if self._startup_completed:
              log.info("Startup analysis already completed for this session")
              return self.state_data.get("gap_processing", {}).get("category_completion_status", {})
  
          log.info("dY"? STARTUP ANALYSIS: Beginning comprehensive state analysis...")
  
          # Calculate file-grounded totals
          file_grounded_data = self._calculate_file_grounded_totals()
  
          # Toggle: allow disabling reverse gap heuristic entirely via config
          use_reverse_gap_heuristic = (
              self.system_config.get("pipeline_toggles", {})
              .get("resume", {})
              .get("use_reverse_gap_heuristic", False)
          )
  
          if not use_reverse_gap_heuristic:
              # Deterministic resume: use linking map count as resume index
              start_at = int(file_grounded_data.get("linking_map_count", 0))
              self.state_data["resumption_index"] = start_at
              self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
              self.state_data.setdefault("gap_processing", {})["reverse_gap_detected"] = False
              self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "system_progression"
              log.info(f"RESUME DECISION: START_AT_INDEX={start_at} (reason: system_progression)")
          # dYs" REVERSE GAP POLICY FIX: Only perform reverse gap detection on startup
          elif file_grounded_data["linking_map_count"] > file_grounded_data["total_products"]:
              log.info(
                  f"dY", REVERSE GAP DETECTED: Linking map ({file_grounded_data['linking_map_count']}) > Cache 
({file_grounded_data['total_products']})"
              )
  
              # Check if we should preserve existing resume index or reset
              current_resumption_index = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
              explicit_cache_rebuild = self.state_data.get("force_cache_rebuild", False)
  
              if explicit_cache_rebuild:
                  # Only reset to 0 if explicitly rebuilding cache
                  self.state_data["resumption_index"] = 0
                  self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "reverse_gap_cache_rebuild"
                  log.info(
                      f"�o. REVERSE GAP: Reset resumption_index = 0 (explicit cache rebuild requested)"
                  )
              elif current_resumption_index == 0:
                  # If resumption_index is 0, check if this is truly a fresh start or a restart

>     def commit_supplier_progress(self, *, cat_idx: int, prod_idx: int,
                                 total_cats: int, cat_url: str, total_prod_in_cat: int) -> None:
          """Thread-safe atomic supplier-phase commit (category-relative cursor)."""
          if self._write_lock:
              with self._write_lock:
                  self._perform_supplier_commit(cat_idx, prod_idx, total_cats, cat_url, total_prod_in_cat)
          else:
              self._perform_supplier_commit(cat_idx, prod_idx, total_cats, cat_url, total_prod_in_cat)
      
      def _perform_supplier_commit(self, cat_idx: int, prod_idx: int, total_cats: int, cat_url: str, 
total_prod_in_cat: int):
          """Internal supplier commit implementation."""
          sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
  
          # dY"' CRITICAL: Per-category denominator freeze at category entry (normalized URL)
          try:
              nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
          except Exception:
              nurl = cat_url
          current_cat_url = sp.get("current_category_url")
          current_total_prod = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
  
          if current_cat_url != nurl or current_total_prod == 0:
              # NEW CATEGORY ENTRY: Freeze denominator immediately
              sp["current_category_url"] = nurl
              sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = int(total_prod_in_cat)
              sp["category_denominator_frozen"] = True
              sp["category_freeze_timestamp"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
  
              self.log.info(f"dY"' PER-CATEGORY FREEZE: {nurl} �+' {total_prod_in_cat} products")

>     def _detect_actual_fresh_start(self):

          global_counters = self.state_data.get("global_counters", {})

          system_progression = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})

              )

              self.state_data["is_fresh_start"] = actual_fresh_start

>     def commit_amazon_progress(self, *, cat_idx: int, queue_idx: int,
                                 total_cats: int, cat_url: str, queue_len: int) -> None:
          """Thread-safe atomic Amazon-phase commit (queue-relative cursor)."""
          # Emit a clear progress line with clamp preview and normalized URL
          original_idx = int(queue_idx)
          clamped_idx = original_idx
          if queue_len is not None and queue_len >= 0:
              if clamped_idx < 0:
                  clamped_idx = 0
              elif clamped_idx > queue_len:
                  clamped_idx = queue_len
          try:
              nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
          except Exception:
              nurl = str(cat_url)
          self.log.info(
              f"dY" AMAZON PROGRESS: cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} "
              f"idx={original_idx}->{clamped_idx} (frozen_queue_len={queue_len}) url={nurl}"
          )
          if self._write_lock:
              with self._write_lock:
                  self._perform_amazon_commit(cat_idx, clamped_idx, total_cats, cat_url, queue_len)
          else:
              self._perform_amazon_commit(cat_idx, clamped_idx, total_cats, cat_url, queue_len)

          Set frozen denominator with guard against multiple freezes.

          """

          if self.is_category_denominator_frozen(category_url):

              return False
  
          sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
          try:
              nurl = normalize_url(category_url)
          except Exception:
              nurl = category_url
          sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = int(discovered_count)
          sp["current_category_url"] = nurl
          # Track frozen denominators per category URL (normalized) to prevent drift across runs
          frozen_categories = self.state_data.setdefault("frozen_category_denominators", {})

>     def get_frozen_denominator(self, category_url: str) -> Optional[int]:
          """Return the frozen denominator for a category URL (normalized key) if present."""
          try:
              nurl = normalize_url(category_url)
          except Exception:
              nurl = category_url
          sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
          if sp.get("category_denominator_frozen") and sp.get("current_category_url") == nurl:
              try:
                  return int(sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)) or None
              except Exception:
                  return None
          # Fallback to map for cross-run lookup
          frozen_categories = self.state_data.get("frozen_category_denominators", {})
          if nurl in frozen_categories:
              try:
                  return int(frozen_categories[nurl])
              except Exception:
                  return None
          return None

>     def __init__(self, chrome_debug_port: int, ai_client: Optional[OpenAI] = None):
          super().__init__(chrome_debug_port, ai_client)
          # self.ai_client is already set by parent constructor if ai_client is passed.
  
      async def connect(self) -> Browser:  # type: ignore
          """
          Connect to browser using the centralized BrowserManager singleton.
          All browser operations now go through the shared BrowserManager instance.
          """
          log.info(f"dY" FixedAmazonExtractor connecting via BrowserManager singleton")
          try:
              # Use the browser manager singleton for centralized browser management
              browser_manager = BrowserManager.get_instance()
  
              # Ensure browser manager is properly launched
              if not hasattr(browser_manager, "browser") or not browser_manager.browser:
                  await browser_manager.launch_browser(cdp_port=self.chrome_debug_port)
  
              # Get the shared browser instance
              self.browser = browser_manager.browser
              log.info("�o. FixedAmazonExtractor connected via BrowserManager singleton")
  
              return self.browser
          except Exception as e:
              log.error(f"�?O FixedAmazonExtractor failed to connect via BrowserManager: {e}")
              log.error(
                  "Ensure Chrome is running with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and BrowserManager is properly 
initialized"
              )
              raise
  
      def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
>     def __init__(self, config_loader, workflow_config, browser_manager=None, ai_client=None):
          """Initialize the Passive Extraction Workflow with configuration and services."""
          # Configuration and services
          self.config_loader = config_loader
          self.workflow_config = workflow_config
          self.browser_manager = browser_manager
          self.ai_client = ai_client
  
          # dYs" CRITICAL FIX: Initialize full scan tracking for RC-6
          self._last_full_scan_ts = 0
          self._full_scan_interval = 300  # 5 minutes default interval
  
          self.log = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
  
          # dYs" IMPORT HYGIENE: Log module path to confirm correct workflow is running
          self.log.info(f"MODULE PATH: {__file__}")
  
          # Core components initialized here
          self.supplier_name = self.workflow_config.get("supplier_name")
          self.full_config = self.config_loader.get_full_config()
          self.system_config = self.full_config.get("system", {})
  
          # Check for test mode environment variables for audit testing
          self.test_mode = os.getenv("FBA_TEST_MODE", "false").lower() == "true"
          self.test_limit = int(os.getenv("FBA_TEST_LIMIT", "0"))
          self.audit_mode = os.getenv("FBA_AUDIT_MODE", "false").lower() == "true"
  
          if self.test_mode:
              self.log.info(f"TEST MODE: Processing limit set to {self.test_limit} products")
          if self.audit_mode:
              self.log.info(f"AUDIT MODE: Enhanced logging and validation enabled")

>     async def run(self):
          """Main execution loop for the workflow."""
          profitable_results: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
          session_id = f"{self.supplier_name}_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}"
          self.log.info(f"--- Starting Passive Extraction Workflow for: {self.supplier_name} ---")
          self.log.info(f"Session ID: {session_id}")
  
          # Resolve categories and compute config hash
          category_urls = self._get_predefined_categories(self.supplier_name) or []
          cfg_hash = self.state_manager.compute_supplier_config_hash(category_urls)
  
          # Freeze total categories and mark frozen BEFORE any resume logic
          self.state_manager.set_total_categories(len(category_urls), cfg_hash)
          self.state_manager.save_state_atomic()
          self.log.info(f"dY"' FROZEN TOTAL CATEGORIES set to {len(category_urls)} (hash={cfg_hash[:8]})")
          if hasattr(self.state_manager, "mark_frozen_totals_committed"):
              self.state_manager.mark_frozen_totals_committed()
          
          # Store config hash for lineage tracking
          self._cached_config_hash = cfg_hash
  
          # RESUME PROOF LOG 1: LOG at startup if frozen totals are committed
          if hasattr(self.state_manager, "is_frozen_totals_committed") and 
self.state_manager.is_frozen_totals_committed():
              self.log.info("�o. RESUME PROOF: Frozen totals are committed, safe to calculate resume pointers")
          else:
              self.log.warning("�s��,? RESUME PROOF: Frozen totals NOT committed, resume pointers may be unreliable")
  
          # Now it is safe to fetch the resume pointer and log it
          rp = self.state_manager.get_resumption_ptr()
          phase = getattr(rp, "phase", "supplier")
          self._resume_ptr = rp  # expose to downstream supplier routines
          self.log.info(f"[START_DISPATCH] phase={phase} cat={rp.cat_idx} prod={rp.prod_idx}")
          # Phase-aware entry: route directly to Amazon resume when applicable
          if phase == "amazon_analysis":
              return await self._run_amazon_phase_from_resume(rp)
          # else fall through to supplier; supplier loop must honor rp.cat_idx/prod_idx
          # Emit early proof of existing frozen denominator (before supplier processing)
          sp = self.state_manager.state_data.get("system_progression", {}) or {}
          cat_url = sp.get("current_category_url")
          if sp.get("category_denominator_frozen") and cat_url:
              existing = self.state_manager.get_frozen_denominator(cat_url)
              try:
                  nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
              except Exception:
                  nurl = str(cat_url)
              self.log.info(
                  f"dY"' B.3_USING EXISTING FROZEN DENOMINATOR: {nurl} �+' {int(existing or 0)} products"
              )
          cat_idx, prod_idx = rp
          # Defer PTR breadcrumbs to the state manager (it's gated on frozen totals).
          self.log.debug("PTR print suppressed at workflow; state manager will emit gated proofs")
  
          # FIXED: Load configuration values directly from system_config (no hardcoded fallbacks)
          # This ensures all toggle experiments work correctly
  
          # So we don't need to access ["system"] again - it's already the system section
          max_products_to_process = self.system_config.get("max_products", 10)
          max_products_per_category = self.system_config.get("max_products_per_category", 5)
          max_analyzed_products = self.system_config.get("max_analyzed_products", 5)
          max_products_per_cycle = self.system_config.get("max_products_per_cycle", 5)
          supplier_extraction_batch_size = self.system_config.get("supplier_extraction_batch_size", 3)
  
          # Get the full config for accessing root level values
          full_config = self.config_loader._config
          max_categories_per_request = full_config.get("max_categories_per_request", 5)
  
          self.max_price = full_config.get("processing_limits", {}).get("max_price_gbp", 20.0)
  
          # Apply batch synchronization if enabled
          batch_sync_config = full_config.get("batch_synchronization", {})
          if batch_sync_config.get("enabled", False):
              (
                  max_products_per_cycle,
                  supplier_extraction_batch_size,
              ) = self._apply_batch_synchronization(
                  max_products_per_cycle, supplier_extraction_batch_size, batch_sync_config
              )
  
          self.log.info(f"dY"S CONFIGURATION VALUES:")
          self.log.info(f"   max_products_to_process: {max_products_to_process}")
          self.log.info(f"   max_products_per_category: {max_products_per_category}")
          self.log.info(f"   max_analyzed_products: {max_analyzed_products}")
          self.log.info(f"   max_products_per_cycle: {max_products_per_cycle}")
          self.log.info(f"   supplier_extraction_batch_size: {supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
          self.log.info(f"   max_categories_per_request: {max_categories_per_request}")
  
          # Load state and linking map
          self.state_manager.load_state()
          # Run validation with off-by-one clamping before computing resume/slicing
          if hasattr(self.state_manager, "validate_loaded_state"):
              try:
                  self.state_manager.validate_loaded_state()
              except Exception:
                  pass
          elif hasattr(self.state_manager, "validate_and_repair_state"):
              try:
                  self.state_manager.validate_and_repair_state()
              except Exception:
                  pass
          # Categories are already frozen above - no need for duplicate legacy calls
