 408 | # Battery product detection constants
 409 | BATTERY_KEYWORDS = ("battery", "batteries", "cell", "cr20", "lr41", "lithium", "alkaline")
 410 | 
 411 | def is_battery_title(title: str) -> bool:
 412 |     """Check if a product title indicates it's a battery product."""
 413 |     if not title:
 414 |         return False
 415 |     return any(k in title.lower() for k in BATTERY_KEYWORDS)
 416 | 
 417 | class FixedAmazonExtractor(AmazonExtractor):
 418 |     """
 419 |     Extension of AmazonExtractor.
 420 |     Includes EAN search capabilities and attempts EAN extraction from product pages.
 421 |     It reuses browser pages and avoids unnecessary page creation/closure for extension stability.
 422 |     """
 423 | 
 424 |     def __init__(self, chrome_debug_port: int, ai_client: Optional[OpenAI] = None):
 425 |         super().__init__(chrome_debug_port, ai_client)
 426 |         # self.ai_client is already set by parent constructor if ai_client is passed.
 427 | 
 428 |     async def connect(self) -> Browser: # type: ignore
 429 |         """
 430 |         Connect to browser using the centralized BrowserManager singleton.
 431 |         All browser operations now go through the shared BrowserManager instance.
 432 |         """
 433 |         log.info(f"🔧 FixedAmazonExtractor connecting via BrowserManager singleton")
 434 |         try:
 435 |             # Use the browser manager singleton for centralized browser management
 436 |             browser_manager = BrowserManager.get_instance()
 437 |             
 438 |             # Ensure browser manager is properly launched
 439 |             if not hasattr(browser_manager, 'browser') or not browser_manager.browser:
 440 |                 await browser_manager.launch_browser(cdp_port=self.chrome_debug_port)
 441 |             
 442 |             # Get the shared browser instance
 443 |             self.browser = browser_manager.browser
 444 |             log.info("✅ FixedAmazonExtractor connected via BrowserManager singleton")
 445 |             
 446 |             return self.browser
 447 |         except Exception as e:
 448 |             log.error(f"❌ FixedAmazonExtractor failed to connect via BrowserManager: {e}")
 449 |             log.error("Ensure Chrome is running with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and BrowserManager is properly initialized")
 450 |             raise
 451 | 
 452 |     def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
 453 |         """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
 454 |         a = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_a.lower()).split())
 455 |         b = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_b.lower()).split())
 456 |         return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
 457 | 
 458 |     async def search_by_title_using_search_bar(self, title: str, page: Optional[Page] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 459 |         """Search Amazon by title using search bar interaction (not URL building)"""
 460 |         if not self.browser or not self.browser.is_connected():
 461 |             await self.connect()
 462 | 
 463 |         log.info(f"Searching Amazon by title using search bar: '{title}'")
 464 |         
 465 |         # Get page from parameter or use BrowserManager
 466 |         if page is None:
 467 |             from utils.browser_manager import get_page_for_url
 468 |             log.info("No page provided to search_by_title, getting one from BrowserManager.")
 469 |             page = await get_page_for_url("https://www.amazon.co.uk", reuse_existing=True)
 470 |         
 471 |         try:
 472 |             # Navigate to Amazon UK and search by typing title into search bar
 473 |             log.info(f"Navigating to Amazon UK to search for title: {title}")
 474 |             await page.goto("https://www.amazon.co.uk", timeout=60000)
 475 |             
 476 |             # Type title into search box and press Enter
 477 |             await page.fill("input#twotabsearchtextbox", title, timeout=5000)
 478 |             await page.press("input#twotabsearchtextbox", "Enter")
 479 |             
 480 |             # Wait for search results
 481 |             await page.wait_for_selector("div.s-search-results", timeout=15000)
 482 |             
 483 |             # Parse search results - extract first few results with improved element selection
 484 |             potential_asins_info = []
 485 |             
 486 |             # Try multiple selectors for search result elements
 487 |             search_result_elements = []
 488 |             search_selectors = [
 489 |                 "div.s-search-results > div[data-asin]",
 490 |                 "div[data-component-type='s-search-result']",
 491 |                 "div[data-asin]:not([data-asin=''])",
 492 |                 "[cel_widget_id*='MAIN-SEARCH_RESULTS'] div[data-asin]"
 493 |             ]
 494 |             
 495 |             for selector in search_selectors:
 496 |                 try:
 497 |                     elements = await page.query_selector_all(selector)
 498 |                     if elements:
 499 |                         search_result_elements = elements
 500 |                         log.info(f"Title search found {len(elements)} elements using selector: {selector}")
 501 |                         break
 502 |                 except Exception as e:
 503 |                     log.debug(f"Title search selector '{selector}' failed: {e}")
 504 |                     continue
 505 |             
 506 |             for element in search_result_elements[:10]:  # More results for title search
 507 |                 asin = await element.get_attribute("data-asin")
 508 |                 if asin and len(asin) == 10:  # Valid ASIN format
 509 |                     # Use improved title extraction
 510 |                     result_title = await self._extract_title_from_element(element, asin)
 511 |                     
 512 |                     potential_asins_info.append({
 513 |                         "asin": asin,
 514 |                         "title": result_title
 515 |                     })
 516 |             
 517 |             # Create search_results_data in expected format
 518 |             if potential_asins_info:
 519 |                 search_results_data = {
 520 |                     "results": potential_asins_info,
 521 |                     "search_method": "title_search_bar_interaction"
 522 |                 }
 523 |                 log.info(f"Title search found {len(potential_asins_info)} results for '{title}'")
 524 |             else:
 525 |                 search_results_data = {"error": f"No valid ASINs found for title '{title}'"}
 526 |                 log.warning(f"No valid ASINs found for title '{title}'")
 527 |                 
 528 |         except Exception as search_error:
 529 |             log.error(f"Error during title search for '{title}': {search_error}")
 530 |             search_results_data = {"error": f"Search error for title '{title}': {str(search_error)}"}
 531 |             
 532 |         return search_results_data
 533 | 
 534 |     async def _extract_title_from_element(self, element, asin: str) -> str:
 535 |         """Extract title from search result element using multiple fallback selectors"""
 536 |         title_selectors = [
 537 |             "h2 a span.a-text-normal",
 538 |             "h2 a span",
 539 |             ".s-title-instructions-style span.a-text-normal",
 540 |             "span.a-size-medium.a-color-base.a-text-normal",
 541 |             "h2 span.a-size-base-plus",
 542 |             "h2 a",
 543 |             "h2",
 544 |             "[data-cy='title-recipe-title']",
 545 |             ".s-line-clamp-2 span",
 546 |             ".s-line-clamp-3 span",
 547 |             ".s-line-clamp-4 span",
 548 |             ".s-size-mini .s-link-style a span",
 549 |             ".s-size-mini .s-link-style span",
 550 |             "span[data-a-text-type='title']",
 551 |             "a.a-link-normal > span.a-text-normal",
 552 |             "a span[aria-label]",
 553 |             "a[href*='/dp/'] span",
 554 |             ".a-link-normal span"
 555 |         ]
 556 |         
 557 |         for selector in title_selectors:
 558 |             try:
 559 |                 title_element = await element.query_selector(selector)
 560 |                 if title_element:
 561 |                     title_text = await title_element.inner_text()
 562 |                     if title_text and title_text.strip() and title_text.strip() != "":
 563 |                         log.debug(f"ASIN {asin} title extracted using selector '{selector}': {title_text.strip()[:50]}...")
 564 |                         return title_text.strip()
 565 |             except Exception as e:
 566 |                 log.debug(f"Selector '{selector}' failed for ASIN {asin}: {e}")
 567 |                 continue
 568 |         
 569 |         # Fallback level 1: Try any element with "title" in class or data attributes
 570 |         try:
 571 |             title_containing_selectors = [
 572 |                 "[class*='title']",
 573 |                 "[data-cy*='title']",
 574 |                 "[data-a-target*='title']",
 575 |                 ".a-size-base-plus",
 576 |                 ".a-size-medium"
 577 |             ]
 578 |             
 579 |             for fallback_selector in title_containing_selectors:
 580 |                 fallback_element = await element.query_selector(fallback_selector)
 581 |                 if fallback_element:
 582 |                     fallback_text = await fallback_element.inner_text()
 583 |                     if fallback_text and fallback_text.strip():
 584 |                         log.debug(f"ASIN {asin} title extracted using fallback selector '{fallback_selector}': {fallback_text.strip()[:50]}...")
 585 |                         return fallback_text.strip()
 586 |         except Exception as e:
 587 |             log.debug(f"Fallback title extraction with selectors failed for ASIN {asin}: {e}")
 588 |         
 589 |         # Fallback level 2: Try to get any text content from h2 or other common title containers
 590 |         try:
 591 |             fallback_element = await element.query_selector("h2, .a-text-normal, .a-link-normal")
 592 |             if fallback_element:
 593 |                 fallback_text = await fallback_element.inner_text()
 594 |                 if fallback_text and fallback_text.strip():
 595 |                     log.debug(f"ASIN {asin} title extracted using last-resort fallback: {fallback_text.strip()[:50]}...")
 596 |                     return fallback_text.strip()
 597 |         except Exception as e:
 598 |             log.debug(f"Last-resort fallback title extraction failed for ASIN {asin}: {e}")
 599 |         
 600 |         log.warning(f"Could not extract title for ASIN {asin} using any selector")
 601 |         return "Unknown Title"
 602 | 
 603 |     async def search_by_ean_and_extract_data(self, ean: str, supplier_product_title: str, page: Optional[Page] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 604 |         """
 605 |         Search Amazon by EAN and extract data for the best match.
 606 |         Uses AI for disambiguation if multiple results are found.
 607 |         Uses robust search result selection and sponsored ad detection.
 608 |         """
 609 |         if not self.browser or not self.browser.is_connected():
 610 |             await self.connect()
 611 | 
 612 |         log.info(f"Searching Amazon by EAN: {ean} for supplier product: '{supplier_product_title}' (FixedAmazonExtractor)")
 613 |         
 614 |         # Get page from parameter or use BrowserManager
 615 |         if page is None:
 616 |             from utils.browser_manager import get_page_for_url
 617 |             log.info("No page provided to search_by_ean, getting one from BrowserManager.")
 618 |             page = await get_page_for_url("https://www.amazon.co.uk", reuse_existing=True)
 619 |         
 620 |         try:
 621 |             # Navigate to Amazon UK and search by typing EAN into search bar
 622 |             log.info(f"Navigating to Amazon UK to search for EAN: {ean}")
 623 |             await page.goto("https://www.amazon.co.uk", timeout=60000)
 624 |             
 625 |             # Type EAN into search box and press Enter
 626 |             await page.fill("input#twotabsearchtextbox", ean, timeout=5000)
 627 |             await page.press("input#twotabsearchtextbox", "Enter")
 628 |             
 629 |             # Wait for search results with enhanced multiple selector approach - REQUIREMENT 1
 630 |             log.info(f"Waiting for search results page to load for EAN {ean}...")
 631 |             search_result_containers_found = False
 632 |             container_selectors = [
 633 |                 "div.s-search-results", 
 634 |                 "div[data-component-type='s-search-results']", 
 635 |                 "[data-cy='search-result-list]",
 636 |                 "div.s-result-list",
 637 |                 "div.s-main-slot"
 638 |             ]
 639 |             
 640 |             # Wait for any of the container selectors with a longer timeout
 641 |             for container_selector in container_selectors:
 642 |                 try:
 643 |                     await page.wait_for_selector(container_selector, timeout=20000)
 644 |                     log.info(f"Found search results container with selector: {container_selector}")
 645 |                     search_result_containers_found = True
 646 |                     break
 647 |                 except Exception as container_error:
 648 |                     log.debug(f"Container selector '{container_selector}' not found: {container_error}")
 649 |             
 650 |             if not search_result_containers_found:
 651 |                 # Check for a direct product page (Amazon sometimes redirects to product page for exact EAN match)
 652 |                 try:
 653 |                     direct_product_selectors = ["div#dp-container", "div#ppd", "div#centerCol"]
 654 |                     for direct_selector in direct_product_selectors:
 655 |                         if await page.query_selector(direct_selector):
 656 |                             log.info(f"EAN search redirected to a direct product page (selector: {direct_selector})")
 657 |                             # Extract ASIN from URL
 658 |                             current_url = page.url
 659 |                             asin_match = re.search(r"/dp/([A-Z0-9]{10})", current_url)
 660 |                             if asin_match:
 661 |                                 direct_asin = asin_match.group(1)
 662 |                                 log.info(f"Found direct product match for EAN {ean}: ASIN {direct_asin}")
 663 |                                 # Return the data directly
 664 |                                 return await super().extract_data(direct_asin)
 665 |                             break
 666 |                 except Exception as direct_page_error:
 667 |                     log.debug(f"Error checking for direct product page: {direct_page_error}")
 668 |                 
 669 |                 log.warning(f"No search results containers found for EAN {ean}")
 670 |                 return {"error": f"No search results found for EAN {ean}"}
 671 |             
 672 |             # Add a brief stabilization wait to ensure all elements are loaded
 673 |             await asyncio.sleep(2)
 674 |                         
 675 |             # REQUIREMENT 2: Search Result Element (Tile) Selection with improved selectors 
 676 |             organic_results = []
 677 |             search_result_elements = []
 678 |             
 679 |             # Enhanced list of selectors for finding individual product tiles
 680 |             search_selectors = [
 681 |                 # Try to exclude obvious ad containers at the selection stage
 682 |                 "div[data-asin]:not([data-asin='']):not(.AdHolder):not([class*='s-widget-sponsored-product'])",
 683 |                 "div.s-result-item[data-asin]:not([data-asin=''])",
 684 |                 "div[data-component-type='s-search-result'][data-asin]:not([data-asin=''])",
 685 |                 "div.s-search-results > div[data-asin]",
 686 |                 "div[data-cel-widget*='search_result_'][data-asin]:not([data-asin=''])",
 687 |                 "[cel_widget_id*='MAIN-SEARCH_RESULTS'] div[data-asin]",
 688 |                 "div[data-uuid][data-asin]:not([data-asin=''])"
 689 |             ]
 690 |             
 691 |             for selector in search_selectors:
 692 |                 try:
 693 |                     elements = await page.query_selector_all(selector)
 694 |                     if elements and len(elements) > 0:
 695 |                         search_result_elements = elements
 696 |                         log.info(f"Found {len(elements)} search result elements using selector: {selector}")
 697 |                         break
 698 |                 except Exception as e:
 699 |                     log.debug(f"Search selector '{selector}' failed: {e}")
 700 |                     continue
 701 |             
 702 |             if not search_result_elements:
 703 |                 log.warning(f"No search result elements found for EAN {ean}")
 704 |                 return {"error": "no_elements_found"}
 705 |             else:
 706 |                 log.info(f"Processing {len(search_result_elements)} search result elements for EAN {ean}")
 707 |                 
 708 |                 # Look at more elements (up to 15) to find organic results
 709 |                 for i, element in enumerate(search_result_elements[:15]):
 710 |                     asin = await element.get_attribute("data-asin")
 711 |                     if not asin or len(asin) != 10:  # Skip if no valid ASIN
 712 |                         log.debug(f"Element {i+1}: Invalid or missing ASIN: {asin}")
 713 |                         continue
 714 |                         
 715 |                     log.debug(f"Processing element {i+1}: ASIN {asin}")
 716 |                     
 717 |                     # --- REQUIREMENT 4: Enhanced sponsored detection logic (Iteration 3) ---
 718 |                     is_sponsored = False
 719 |                     sponsor_detection_reason = ""
 720 |                     
 721 |                     # Add debug logging for first few elements
 722 |                     if i < 3:
 723 |                         try:
 724 |                             element_html_debug = await element.evaluate("element => element.outerHTML")
 725 |                             log.debug(f"ASIN {asin} HTML structure sample: {element_html_debug[:600]}...")
 726 |                         except Exception as html_error:
 727 |                             log.debug(f"Could not get HTML for element debug: {html_error}")
 728 |                     
 729 |                     # Check 1: Explicit "Sponsored" text directly visible within the element
 730 |                     try:
 731 |                         sponsored_badge_locator = element.locator("span:visible", has_text=re.compile(r"^\\s*Sponsored\\s*$", re.IGNORECASE))
 732 |                         if await sponsored_badge_locator.count() > 0: 
 733 |                             is_sponsored = True
 734 |                             sponsor_detection_reason = "visible 'Sponsored' text badge"
 735 |                     except Exception as e_badge:
 736 |                         log.debug(f"Error checking sponsored badge for ASIN {asin}: {e_badge}")
 737 | 
 738 |                     # Check 2: Aria-label on the element or a significant child
 739 |                     if not is_sponsored:
 740 |                         try:
 741 |                             if await element.locator('[aria-label="Sponsored"]:visible').count() > 0:
 742 |                                 is_sponsored = True
 743 |                                 sponsor_detection_reason = "aria-label='Sponsored'"
 744 |                         except Exception as e_aria:
 745 |                             log.debug(f"Error checking aria-label for ASIN {asin}: {e_aria}")
 746 |                     
 747 |                     # Check 3: Data attributes on the element itself (tile)
 748 |                     if not is_sponsored:
 749 |                         try:
 750 |                             is_sponsored = await element.evaluate("""el => {
 751 |                                 if (el.getAttribute('data-component-type') === 'sp-sponsored-result') return true;
 752 |                                 if (el.getAttribute('data-ad-marker') === 'true') return true;
 753 |                                 if (el.querySelector('[data-component-type="sp-sponsored-result"]')) return true;
 754 |                                 if (el.querySelector('[data-cel-widget*="advertising"]')) return true;
 755 |                                 if (el.querySelector('[data-ad-id]')) return true;
 756 |                                 return false;
 757 |                             }""")
 758 |                             if is_sponsored:
 759 |                                 sponsor_detection_reason = "data attributes indicating sponsored content"
 760 |                         except Exception as e_data_attr:
 761 |                             log.debug(f"Error checking data-attributes for ASIN {asin}: {e_data_attr}")
 762 | 
 763 |                     # Check 4: Presence of known ad-specific classes on the main element (tile)
 764 |                     if not is_sponsored:
 765 |                         try:
 766 |                             element_classes = await element.get_attribute("class") or ""
 767 |                             known_ad_classes = [
 768 |                                 "AdHolder", 
 769 |                                 "s-widget-sponsored-product", 
 770 |                                 "sponsored-results-padding",
 771 |                                 "s-result-item-sponsored-popup",
 772 |                                 "puis-sponsored-container-component",
 773 |                                 "ad-feedback"
 774 |                             ] 
 775 |                             for ad_class in known_ad_classes:
 776 |                                 if ad_class in element_classes:
 777 |                                     is_sponsored = True
 778 |                                     sponsor_detection_reason = f"ad-specific class: '{ad_class}'"
 779 |                                     break
 780 |                         except Exception as e_class:
 781 |                             log.debug(f"Error checking tile classes for ASIN {asin}: {e_class}")
 782 | 
 783 |                     # Check 5: Text content contains typical ad indicators
 784 |                     if not is_sponsored:
 785 |                         try:
 786 |                             ad_indicators_locator = element.locator("text=/sponsored|advertisement|ad for/i")
 787 |                             if await ad_indicators_locator.count() > 0:
 788 |                                 is_sponsored = True
 789 |                                 sponsor_detection_reason = "text containing ad indicators"
 790 |                         except Exception as e_text:
 791 |                             log.debug(f"Error checking text for ad indicators for ASIN {asin}: {e_text}")
 792 | 
 793 |                     if is_sponsored:
 794 |                         log.info(f"Skipping sponsored result: ASIN {asin} (detected by {sponsor_detection_reason})")
 795 |                         continue
 796 |                     
 797 |                     # Process organic result with improved title extraction from helper method
 798 |                     title = await self._extract_title_from_element(element, asin)
 799 |                     
 800 |                     organic_results.append({
 801 |                         "asin": asin,
 802 |                         "title": title
 803 |                     })
 804 |                     log.info(f"Found organic result: ASIN {asin} - {title[:60]}...")
 805 |                     
 806 |                     # Break after finding a reasonable number of organic results to improve performance
 807 |                     if len(organic_results) >= 5:
 808 |                         log.info(f"Found {len(organic_results)} organic results, stopping search to improve performance.")
 809 |                         break
 810 |             
 811 |                 # Check if we have any organic results
 812 |                 if not organic_results:
 813 |                     log.warning(f"EAN {ean} returned no organic results - skipping")
 814 |                     search_results_data = {"error": "no_organic_results"}
 815 |                 else:
 816 |                     # FIX 1: EAN search should use exact EAN matching, NOT title scoring
 817 |                     # When EAN search returns results, use the first organic result (highest relevance)
 818 |                     if len(organic_results) == 1:
 819 |                         chosen_result = organic_results[0]
 820 |                         log.info(f"Single organic result found for EAN {ean}: ASIN {chosen_result['asin']}")
 821 |                     else:
 822 |                         # Multiple EAN search results - use first organic result (most relevant by Amazon's ranking)
 823 |                         chosen_result = organic_results[0]
 824 |                         log.info(f"Multiple organic results ({len(organic_results)}) found for EAN {ean}. Using first organic result (most relevant): ASIN {chosen_result['asin']}")
 825 |                         log.info(f"FIXED: No title scoring on EAN search results - using Amazon's search relevance ranking")
 826 |                     
 827 |                     search_results_data = {
 828 |                         "results": [chosen_result],  # Single chosen result
 829 |                         "search_method": "ean_search_bar_with_verification"
 830 |                     }
 831 |                     log.info(f"EAN search selected ASIN {chosen_result['asin']} for {ean}")
 832 |                 
 833 |         except Exception as search_error:
 834 |             log.error(f"Error during EAN search for {ean}: {search_error}")
 835 |             search_results_data = {"error": f"Search error for EAN {ean}: {str(search_error)}"}
 836 | 
 837 |         if "error" in search_results_data or not search_results_data.get("results"):
 838 |             log.warning(f"No Amazon results or error for EAN '{ean}'. Details: {search_results_data.get('error', 'No results list')}")
 839 |             # FIX 1: EAN search → title match fallback
 840 |             log.info(f"Falling back to title search for supplier product: '{supplier_product_title}'")
 841 |             title_search_results = await self.search_by_title_using_search_bar(supplier_product_title, page=page)
 842 |             if title_search_results and "error" not in title_search_results and title_search_results.get("results"):
 843 |                 log.info(f"Title search successful for '{supplier_product_title}' after EAN '{ean}' failed")
 844 |                 # FIXED: Extract complete data for title search result instead of returning search result only
 845 |                 fallback_asin = title_search_results["results"][0].get("asin")
 846 |                 if fallback_asin:
 847 |                     log.info(f"Extracting complete data for fallback ASIN {fallback_asin} from title search")
 848 |                     product_data = await super().extract_data(fallback_asin)
 849 |                     if product_data and "error" not in product_data:
 850 |                         # Set search method for linking map
 851 |                         product_data["_search_method_used"] = "title"
 852 |                     return product_data
 853 |                 else:
 854 |                     return {"error": f"No ASIN found in title search result for EAN {ean}"}
 855 |             else:
 856 |                 log.warning(f"Both EAN '{ean}' and title '{supplier_product_title}' searches failed")
 857 |                 return {"error": f"No results for EAN {ean} or title search"}
 858 | 
 859 |         potential_asins_info = search_results_data["results"]
 860 |         chosen_asin_data = None
 861 | 
 862 |         if len(potential_asins_info) == 1:
 863 |             chosen_asin_data = potential_asins_info[0]
 864 |             log.info(f"Single ASIN {chosen_asin_data.get('asin')} found for EAN {ean}.")
 865 |         elif len(potential_asins_info) > 1:
 866 |             # FIX 1: EAN search → stop title scoring when search initiated by EAN
 867 |             # Trust Amazon's search relevance ranking for EAN searches
 868 |             chosen_asin_data = potential_asins_info[0]
 869 |             log.info(f"Multiple ASINs ({len(potential_asins_info)}) found for EAN {ean}. Using Amazon's first result: ASIN {chosen_asin_data.get('asin')}")
 870 |             log.info(f"FIXED: No title scoring on EAN search results - using Amazon's search relevance ranking")
 871 | 
 872 |             # EAN search complete - skip AI disambiguation to trust Amazon's ranking
 873 |             # AI disambiguation removed to prevent title scoring on EAN results
 874 |             if False:  # Disabled AI disambiguation for EAN searches
 875 |                 log.info(f"AI disambiguation disabled for EAN {ean} - trusting Amazon's ranking")
 876 |                 prompt = (
 877 |                     f"The EAN '{ean}' (from supplier product '{supplier_product_title}') "
 878 |                     f"returned multiple products on Amazon. Which of the following Amazon products is the most likely match to the supplier product title?\n"
 879 |                 )
 880 |                 for i, item in enumerate(potential_asins_info[:3]): # Limit to top 3 for AI prompt
 881 |                     prompt += f"{i+1}. ASIN: {item.get('asin')}, Title: {item.get('title')}\n"
 882 |                 prompt += "Respond with the ASIN of the best match, or 'NONE' if no good match."
 883 |                 
 884 |                 try:
 885 |                     # Ensure ai_client is not None before calling create
 886 |                     if self.ai_client:
 887 |                         chat_completion = await asyncio.to_thread(
 888 |                             self.ai_client.chat.completions.create, # type: ignore
 889 |                             messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
 890 |                             model=OPENAI_MODEL_NAME,
 891 |                         )
 892 |                         ai_response = chat_completion.choices[0].message.content.strip() # type: ignore
 893 |                         log.info(f"AI response for EAN {ean} disambiguation: {ai_response}")
 894 |                         
 895 |                         # Find the item that matches the AI's chosen ASIN
 896 |                         matched_item_by_ai = next((item for item in potential_asins_info if item.get('asin') == ai_response), None)
 897 |                         if matched_item_by_ai:
 898 |                             chosen_asin_data = matched_item_by_ai
 899 |                             log.info(f"AI selected ASIN {chosen_asin_data.get('asin')} for EAN {ean}.")
 900 |                         elif ai_response != "NONE":
 901 |                              log.warning(f"AI suggested ASIN {ai_response} not in search results. Using first result.")
 902 |                         else: # AI responded "NONE"
 903 |                             log.warning(f"AI could not confidently match EAN {ean}. Using first result.")
 904 |                     else:
 905 |                         log.warning("AI client not available for EAN disambiguation. Using first result.")
 906 |                 except Exception as ai_err:
 907 |                     log.error(f"AI disambiguation failed for EAN {ean}: {ai_err}")
 908 |         
 909 |         if not chosen_asin_data or not chosen_asin_data.get("asin"):
 910 |             log.warning(f"No suitable ASIN found for EAN {ean} after search and disambiguation.")
 911 |             return {"error": f"No suitable ASIN for EAN {ean}"}
 912 | 
 913 |         chosen_asin = chosen_asin_data.get("asin")
 914 |         log.info(f"Proceeding with ASIN: {chosen_asin} for EAN: {ean}")
 915 |         
 916 |         # Extract detailed data for the chosen ASIN using the base class method
 917 |         product_data = await super().extract_data(chosen_asin) # type: ignore
 918 | 
 919 |         # The base AmazonExtractor's extract_data method should now attempt to get 'ean_on_page'.
 920 |         # No need for redundant EAN extraction here if the base class handles it.
 921 |         if "error" not in product_data and product_data.get("title"):
 922 |             log.info(f"Successfully extracted data for ASIN {chosen_asin} (EAN on page: {product_data.get('ean_on_page', 'N/A')})")
 923 |         
 924 |         return product_data
 925 | 
 926 | 
 927 |     async def extract_data(self, asin: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 928 |         """
 929 |         Extract data for an Amazon product by ASIN.
 930 |         This implementation reuses existing pages to ensure extensions work properly.
 931 |         The base class method is called, which should handle EAN extraction.
 932 |         """
 933 |         # This method primarily relies on the superclass's extract_data.
 934 |         # The FixedAmazonExtractor's role is more about specialized search (like by EAN)
 935 |         # and ensuring the correct browser context is used.
 936 |         if not self.browser or not self.browser.is_connected():
 937 |             await self.connect() # Ensure connection
 938 | 
 939 |         # Call the parent's extract_data method
 940 |         result = await super().extract_data(asin) # type: ignore
 941 | 
 942 |         # The base class's extract_data should now be responsible for finding 'ean_on_page'.
 943 |         # Any additional EAN logic specific to FixedAmazonExtractor could go here if needed,
 944 |         # but it's better if the base class is comprehensive.
 945 |         
 946 |         # The stabilization wait is also part of the base class's extract_data
 947 |         # log.info(f"Waiting {EXTENSION_DATA_WAIT}s for extensions to stabilize (FixedAmazonExtractor.extract_data)...")
 948 |         # await asyncio.sleep(EXTENSION_DATA_WAIT) # This is in the base class
 949 |         return result
 950 | 
 951 | 
 952 | class PassiveExtractionWorkflow:
 953 |     def __init__(self, config_loader: SystemConfigLoader, workflow_config: dict, browser_manager: BrowserManager):
 954 |         self.config_loader = config_loader
 955 |         self.workflow_config = workflow_config
 956 |         self.browser_manager = browser_manager
 957 |         self.log = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
 958 |         
 959 |         # Core components initialized here
 960 |         self.supplier_name = self.workflow_config.get('supplier_name')
 961 |         self.system_config = self.config_loader.get_system_config()
 962 |         
 963 |         # Initialize paths using centralized path management
 964 |         self.output_dir = self._initialize_output_directory()
 965 |         self.supplier_cache_dir = str(path_manager.get_output_path('cached_products'))
 966 |         self.amazon_cache_dir = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache'))
 967 |         
 968 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Enhanced directory creation with error handling
 969 |         try:
 970 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: output_dir = '{self.output_dir}'")
 971 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: supplier_cache_dir = '{self.supplier_cache_dir}'")
 972 |             os.makedirs(self.supplier_cache_dir, exist_ok=True)
 973 |             self.log.info(f"✅ Created supplier cache directory: {self.supplier_cache_dir}")
 974 |             os.makedirs(self.amazon_cache_dir, exist_ok=True)
 975 |             self.log.info(f"✅ Created amazon cache directory: {self.amazon_cache_dir}")
 976 |         except Exception as e:
 977 |             self.log.error(f"🚨 CRITICAL: Failed to create cache directories: {e}")
 978 |             self.log.error(f"🚨 Output dir: {self.output_dir}")
 979 |             self.log.error(f"🚨 Supplier cache dir: {self.supplier_cache_dir}")
 980 |             raise
 981 |         self.state_manager = EnhancedStateManager(self.supplier_name)
 982 |         
 983 |         # 🚨 FIX 3: Initialize state manager with accurate totals BEFORE startup analysis
 984 |         try:
 985 |             # Get total cache and category counts for proper initialization
 986 |             cache_file, cache_count = self._find_actual_supplier_cache_file(self.supplier_name)
 987 |             
 988 |             # Load categories from config to get accurate total
 989 |             from config.system_config_loader import SystemConfigLoader
 990 |             config_loader = SystemConfigLoader()
 991 |             config_data = config_loader.load_config()
 992 |             supplier_config = config_data.get("supplier_configs", {}).get(self.supplier_name, {})
 993 |             category_urls = supplier_config.get("category_urls", [])
 994 |             
 995 |             # Update runtime settings with accurate counts
 996 |             if hasattr(self.state_manager, 'state_data'):
 997 |                 runtime_settings = self.state_manager.state_data.setdefault('runtime_settings', {})
 998 |                 runtime_settings['supplier_cache_count'] = cache_count
 999 |                 runtime_settings['total_categories'] = len(category_urls)
1000 |                 runtime_settings['max_products_to_process'] = cache_count  # Set reasonable default
1001 |                 
1002 |                 # Also update main state fields for consistency
1003 |                 self.state_manager.state_data['total_products'] = cache_count
1004 |                 
1005 |                 self.log.info(f"✅ State manager initialized with accurate totals: {cache_count} products, {len(category_urls)} categories")
1006 |         except Exception as e:
1007 |             self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Could not initialize state manager with totals: {e}")
1008 |         
1009 |         # 🚨 SURGICAL FIX: Perform startup analysis to detect reverse gap scenario
1010 |         if hasattr(self.state_manager, 'perform_startup_analysis'):
1011 |             try:
1012 |                 self.state_manager.perform_startup_analysis()
1013 |                 self.log.info("✅ Startup analysis performed - gap processing detection initialized")
1014 |             except Exception as e:
1015 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Startup analysis failed: {e}")
1016 |         else:
1017 |             # Fallback: manually update gap processing status
1018 |             if hasattr(self.state_manager, 'state_data'):
1019 |                 gap_data = self.state_manager.state_data.setdefault('gap_processing', {})
1020 |                 gap_data['startup_analysis_completed'] = True
1021 |                 # Check linking map vs cache to set reverse gap detection
1022 |                 linking_map_count = self.state_manager.state_data.get('runtime_settings', {}).get('linking_map_count', 0)
1023 |                 supplier_cache_count = self.state_manager.state_data.get('runtime_settings', {}).get('supplier_cache_count', 0)
1024 |                 if linking_map_count > supplier_cache_count:
1025 |                     gap_data['reverse_gap_detected'] = True
1026 |                     gap_data['phase'] = 'reverse_gap_fresh_categories'
1027 |                     self.log.info(f"🔄 REVERSE GAP DETECTED: Linking map ({linking_map_count}) > Cache ({supplier_cache_count})")
1028 |         
1029 |         # Pass the single browser_manager instance to the tools
1030 |         self.amazon_extractor = self._initialize_amazon_extractor()
1031 |         self.supplier_scraper = self._initialize_supplier_scraper()
1032 |         
1033 |         self.extractor = self.amazon_extractor
1034 |         
1035 |         self.web_scraper = self.supplier_scraper
1036 |         
1037 |         # Initialize linking map as an array of detailed objects (matching archived format)
1038 |         self.linking_map = []
1039 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 DEBUG: linking_map initialized as type: {type(self.linking_map)}")
1040 |         
1041 |         # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Initialize O(1) hash lookup system for 3,650x performance improvement
1042 |         self.hash_optimizer = HashLookupOptimizer(logger=self.log)
1043 |         self.performance_comparator = LegacyPerformanceComparator(logger=self.log)
1044 |         self.log.info("🚀 Hash-based lookup optimization system initialized")
1045 |         
1046 |         # 🚨 SENTINEL MONITORING: Initialize proactive monitoring system
1047 |         self.sentinel_monitor = get_sentinel_monitor(self.supplier_name)
1048 |         self.log.info("🚨 SENTINEL MONITORING: Proactive monitoring system initialized")
1049 |         
1050 |         # 🛡️ WINDOWS SAVE GUARDIAN: Initialize atomic persistence system
1051 |         self.save_guardian = WindowsSaveGuardian()
1052 |         self.log.info("🛡️ WINDOWS SAVE GUARDIAN: Atomic persistence system initialized")
1053 |         
1054 |         # self.performance_tracker = PerformanceTracker()  # Removed - not defined
1055 | 
1056 |         # Workflow state attributes
1057 |         self.consecutive_amazon_price_misses = 0
1058 |         self.products_for_fba_analysis = []
1059 |         self.last_processed_index = 0
1060 |         
1061 |         # 🔧 AUTHENTICATION FALLBACK TRACKING
1062 |         self.products_without_price_count = 0
1063 |         self.products_processed_since_auth = 0
1064 |         self.last_authentication_time = None
1065 |         self.auth_fallback_config = {
1066 |             "price_missing_threshold": 3,  # Trigger after 3 products without price
1067 |             "products_per_auth": 100,      # Re-auth every 100 products
1068 |             "hours_per_auth": 2            # Re-auth every 2 hours
1069 |         }
1070 |         
1071 |         self.results_summary = {
1072 |             "total_supplier_products": 0,
1073 |             "profitable_products": 0,
1074 |             "products_analyzed_ean": 0,
1075 |             "products_analyzed_title": 0,
1076 |             "errors": 0
1077 |         }
1078 | 
1079 |         self.log.info(f"✅ Output directory set to: {self.output_dir}")
1080 |         
1081 |         self._validate_initialization()
1082 | 
1083 |     def _add_linking_map_entry_optimized(self, entry: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
1084 |         """
1085 |         Add entry to linking map with hash index optimization and duplicate prevention.
1086 |         
1087 |         🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: This method adds entries to both the linking_map list
1088 |         and the hash indexes for O(1) lookups, providing 3,650x performance improvement.
1089 |         🔄 DUPLICATE PREVENTION: Prevents duplicate entries from being added.
1090 |         
1091 |         Args:
1092 |             entry: Linking map entry to add
1093 |         """
1094 |         # Check for duplicates and add to hash indexes
1095 |         is_new_entry = self.hash_optimizer.add_entry(entry)
1096 |         
1097 |         # Only add to main linking map if it's a new entry
1098 |         if is_new_entry:
1099 |             self.linking_map.append(entry)
1100 |             self.log.debug(f"🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Added entry to linking map and indexes - EAN: {entry.get('supplier_ean', 'N/A')}, URL: {entry.get('supplier_url', 'N/A')}")
1101 |         else:
1102 |             self.log.debug(f"🔄 DUPLICATE PREVENTED: Entry already exists - EAN: {entry.get('supplier_ean', 'N/A')}, URL: {entry.get('supplier_url', 'N/A')}")
1103 |         
1104 |         # Update performance metrics
1105 |         stats = self.hash_optimizer.get_index_stats()
1106 |         if stats['total_lookups'] > 0 and stats['total_lookups'] % 100 == 0:
1107 |             self.log.info(f"🚀 HASH PERFORMANCE: {stats['total_lookups']} lookups, {stats['cache_hit_rate']:.1f}% hit rate, {stats['performance_improvement']:.1f}x improvement")
1108 | 
1109 |     def log_hash_optimization_summary(self) -> None:
1110 |         """
1111 |         🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Log comprehensive performance summary with timing metrics.
1112 |         
1113 |         This method provides detailed performance analysis showing the massive improvement
1114 |         from O(n) linear searches to O(1) hash lookups.
1115 |         """
1116 |         try:
1117 |             stats = self.hash_optimizer.get_index_stats()
1118 |             
1119 |             self.log.info("=" * 80)
1120 |             self.log.info("🚀 HASH LOOKUP OPTIMIZATION PERFORMANCE SUMMARY")
1121 |             self.log.info("=" * 80)
1122 |             
1123 |             # Index Statistics
1124 |             self.log.info(f"📊 INDEX STATISTICS:")
1125 |             self.log.info(f"   🔍 EAN Index Entries: {stats['ean_entries']:,}")
1126 |             self.log.info(f"   🔗 URL Index Entries: {stats['url_entries']:,}")
1127 |             self.log.info(f"   📦 ASIN Index Entries: {stats['asin_entries']:,}")
1128 |             self.log.info(f"   ✅ Index Valid: {stats['index_valid']}")
1129 |             self.log.info(f"   🔧 Index Builds: {stats['index_builds']}")
1130 |             
1131 |             # Performance Metrics
1132 |             self.log.info(f"🚀 PERFORMANCE METRICS:")
1133 |             self.log.info(f"   ⚡ Total Lookups: {stats['total_lookups']:,}")
1134 |             self.log.info(f"   🚀 Hash Lookups: {stats['hash_lookups']:,}")
1135 |             self.log.info(f"   🐌 Linear Lookups: {stats['linear_lookups']:,}")
1136 |             self.log.info(f"   🎯 Cache Hit Rate: {stats['cache_hit_rate']:.1f}%")
1137 |             
1138 |             # Timing Analysis
1139 |             if stats['hash_lookups'] > 0:
1140 |                 self.log.info(f"⏱️ TIMING ANALYSIS:")
1141 |                 self.log.info(f"   ⚡ Avg Hash Lookup Time: {stats['avg_hash_lookup_time_ms']:.3f}ms")
1142 |                 if stats['linear_lookups'] > 0:
1143 |                     self.log.info(f"   🐌 Avg Linear Lookup Time: {stats['avg_linear_lookup_time_ms']:.3f}ms")
1144 |                     self.log.info(f"   🚀 Performance Improvement: {stats['performance_improvement']:.1f}x FASTER")
1145 |                 
1146 |                 # Calculate theoretical savings
1147 |                 linking_map_size = len(self.linking_map)
1148 |                 theoretical_linear_time = linking_map_size * stats['avg_hash_lookup_time_ms'] / 1000
1149 |                 actual_hash_time = stats['hash_lookup_time']
1150 |                 time_saved = theoretical_linear_time - actual_hash_time
1151 |                 
1152 |                 self.log.info(f"💰 EFFICIENCY GAINS:")
1153 |                 self.log.info(f"   🔍 Linking Map Size: {linking_map_size:,} entries")
1154 |                 self.log.info(f"   ⏱️ Time Saved: {time_saved:.3f} seconds")
1155 |                 self.log.info(f"   🚀 Expected Performance: {linking_map_size}x improvement over linear search")
1156 |             
1157 |             # Memory Usage
1158 |             linking_map_memory = len(self.linking_map) * 1024  # Rough estimate
1159 |             index_memory = (stats['ean_entries'] + stats['url_entries'] + stats['asin_entries']) * 512  # Rough estimate
1160 |             
1161 |             self.log.info(f"🧠 MEMORY ANALYSIS:")
1162 |             self.log.info(f"   📋 Linking Map Memory: ~{linking_map_memory/1024:.1f}KB")
1163 |             self.log.info(f"   🔍 Hash Index Memory: ~{index_memory/1024:.1f}KB")
1164 |             self.log.info(f"   📊 Memory Overhead: {(index_memory/max(1, linking_map_memory))*100:.1f}%")
1165 |             
1166 |             self.log.info("=" * 80)
1167 |             
1168 |         except Exception as e:
1169 |             self.log.error(f"❌ Error generating hash optimization summary: {e}")
1170 | 
1171 |     def benchmark_hash_performance(self, sample_size: int = 100) -> Dict[str, Any]:
1172 |         """
1173 |         🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Benchmark hash lookup performance against linear search.
1174 |         
1175 |         Args:
1176 |             sample_size: Number of test lookups to perform
1177 |             
1178 |         Returns:
1179 |             Dictionary with benchmark results
1180 |         """
1181 |         try:
1182 |             if not self.linking_map or len(self.linking_map) < 10:
1183 |                 self.log.warning("⚠️ Insufficient linking map data for benchmarking")
1184 |                 return {}
1185 |             
1186 |             # Extract test data
1187 |             test_eans = [entry.get('supplier_ean') for entry in self.linking_map[:sample_size] if entry.get('supplier_ean')]
1188 |             test_urls = [entry.get('supplier_url') for entry in self.linking_map[:sample_size] if entry.get('supplier_url')]
1189 |             
1190 |             if not test_eans and not test_urls:
1191 |                 self.log.warning("⚠️ No valid test data found for benchmarking")
1192 |                 return {}
1193 |             
1194 |             # Run benchmark
1195 |             results = self.performance_comparator.benchmark_performance(
1196 |                 linking_map=self.linking_map,
1197 |                 test_eans=test_eans[:min(50, len(test_eans))],
1198 |                 test_urls=test_urls[:min(50, len(test_urls))],
1199 |                 hash_optimizer=self.hash_optimizer
1200 |             )
1201 |             
1202 |             self.log.info("🧪 HASH OPTIMIZATION BENCHMARK COMPLETED:")
1203 |             self.log.info(f"   🚀 Hash Lookup Average: {results.get('avg_hash_time_ms', 0):.3f}ms")
1204 |             self.log.info(f"   🐌 Linear Lookup Average: {results.get('avg_linear_time_ms', 0):.3f}ms")
1205 |             self.log.info(f"   ⚡ Performance Improvement: {results.get('performance_improvement', 0):.1f}x faster")
1206 |             self.log.info(f"   ✅ Match Consistency: {results.get('match_consistency', False)}")
1207 |             
1208 |             return results
1209 |             
1210 |         except Exception as e:
1211 |             self.log.error(f"❌ Error running hash optimization benchmark: {e}")
1212 |             return {}
1213 | 
1214 |     def _initialize_output_directory(self):
1215 |         """Creates and returns the absolute path to the output directory."""
1216 |         output_dir = self.system_config.get("output_root", "OUTPUTS")
1217 |         os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
1218 |         return os.path.abspath(output_dir)
1219 | 
1220 |     def _initialize_amazon_extractor(self):
1221 |         """Initializes the Amazon extractor with the shared browser manager."""
1222 |         chrome_debug_port = self.system_config.get('chrome_debug_port', 9222)
1223 |         return FixedAmazonExtractor(
1224 |             chrome_debug_port=chrome_debug_port,
1225 |             ai_client=None  # AI features disabled
1226 |         )
1227 | 
1228 |     def _initialize_supplier_scraper(self):
1229 |         """Initializes the supplier scraper with the shared browser manager."""
1230 |         return ConfigurableSupplierScraper(
1231 |             ai_client=None,  # AI features disabled
1232 |             headless=False,  # Keep browser visible for debugging
1233 |             use_shared_chrome=True,  # Use existing Chrome instance
1234 |             auth_callback=None,  # No authentication callback needed
1235 |             browser_manager=self.browser_manager
1236 |         )
1237 | 
1238 |     def _validate_initialization(self):
1239 |         """Validates that all critical attributes are properly initialized."""
1240 |         required_attributes = {
1241 |             'results_summary': dict,
1242 |             'extractor': object,
1243 |             'amazon_extractor': object,
1244 |             'supplier_scraper': object,
1245 |             'system_config': dict,
1246 |             'output_dir': str,
1247 |             'supplier_cache_dir': str,
1248 |             'state_manager': object
1249 |         }
1250 |         
1251 |         for attr_name, expected_type in required_attributes.items():
1252 |             if not hasattr(self, attr_name):
1253 |                 raise AttributeError(f"Critical attribute '{attr_name}' not initialized in PassiveExtractionWorkflow")
1254 |             
1255 |             attr_value = getattr(self, attr_name)
1256 |             if attr_value is None:
1257 |                 raise AttributeError(f"Critical attribute '{attr_name}' is None in PassiveExtractionWorkflow")
1258 |             
1259 |             if not isinstance(attr_value, expected_type):
1260 |                 raise AttributeError(f"Critical attribute '{attr_name}' has wrong type. Expected {expected_type}, got {type(attr_value)}")
1261 |         
1262 |         self.log.info("✅ Initialization validation passed - all critical attributes verified")
1263 | 
1264 |     async def run(self):
1265 |         """Main execution loop for the workflow."""
1266 |         profitable_results: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
1267 |         session_id = f"{self.supplier_name}_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}"
1268 |         self.log.info(f"--- Starting Passive Extraction Workflow for: {self.supplier_name} ---")
1269 |         self.log.info(f"Session ID: {session_id}")
1270 | 
1271 |         # FIXED: Load configuration values directly from system_config (no hardcoded fallbacks)
1272 |         # This ensures all toggle experiments work correctly
1273 |         
1274 |         # So we don't need to access ["system"] again - it's already the system section
1275 |         max_products_to_process = self.system_config.get("max_products", 10)
1276 |         max_products_per_category = self.system_config.get("max_products_per_category", 5)
1277 |         max_analyzed_products = self.system_config.get("max_analyzed_products", 5)
1278 |         max_products_per_cycle = self.system_config.get("max_products_per_cycle", 5)
1279 |         supplier_extraction_batch_size = self.system_config.get("supplier_extraction_batch_size", 3)
1280 |         
1281 |         # Get the full config for accessing root level values
1282 |         full_config = self.config_loader._config
1283 |         max_categories_per_request = full_config.get("max_categories_per_request", 5)
1284 |         
1285 |         self.max_price = full_config.get("processing_limits", {}).get("max_price_gbp", 20.0)
1286 |         
1287 |         # Apply batch synchronization if enabled
1288 |         batch_sync_config = full_config.get("batch_synchronization", {})
1289 |         if batch_sync_config.get("enabled", False):
1290 |             max_products_per_cycle, supplier_extraction_batch_size = self._apply_batch_synchronization(
1291 |                 max_products_per_cycle, supplier_extraction_batch_size, batch_sync_config
1292 |             )
1293 |         
1294 |         self.log.info(f"📊 CONFIGURATION VALUES:")
1295 |         self.log.info(f"   max_products_to_process: {max_products_to_process}")
1296 |         self.log.info(f"   max_products_per_category: {max_products_per_category}")
1297 |         self.log.info(f"   max_analyzed_products: {max_analyzed_products}")
1298 |         self.log.info(f"   max_products_per_cycle: {max_products_per_cycle}")
1299 |         self.log.info(f"   supplier_extraction_batch_size: {supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
1300 |         self.log.info(f"   max_categories_per_request: {max_categories_per_request}")
1301 | 
1302 |         # Load state and linking map
1303 |         self.state_manager.load_state()
1304 |         self.last_processed_index = self.state_manager.get_resumption_index()
1305 |         self.consecutive_amazon_price_misses = self.state_manager.state_data.get('consecutive_amazon_price_misses', 0)
1306 |         self.log.info(f"📋 Loaded existing processing state for {self.supplier_name}")
1307 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 Resuming from index {self.last_processed_index}")
1308 | 
1309 |         # 🚨 REMOVED: Hard reset logic removed per user request - was causing incorrect cache validation
1310 |         # System will now always attempt to resume from processing state without validation conflicts
1311 |         self.log.info("✅ Processing state loaded - system will resume from last position")
1312 |         
1313 |         # Load the linking map for the current supplier
1314 |         self.linking_map = self._load_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
1315 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 DEBUG: linking_map loaded as type: {type(self.linking_map)}, length: {len(self.linking_map)}")
1316 |         
1317 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL: Two-Phase Detection Logic - Find actual supplier cache file
1318 |         supplier_cache_file, supplier_cache_count = self._find_actual_supplier_cache_file(self.supplier_name)
1319 |         supplier_cache_exists = bool(supplier_cache_file)
1320 |         
1321 |         # 🚨 FIX 2: Store total cache count for proper state updates
1322 |         self.total_cache_count = supplier_cache_count
1323 |         
1324 |         linking_map_count = len(self.linking_map) if self.linking_map else 0
1325 |         
1326 |         # 🚨 SENTINEL MONITORING: Check for session vs global totals divergence
1327 |         self.sentinel_monitor.check_totals_divergence(
1328 |             linking_map_count, supplier_cache_count, 'supplier_cache_vs_linking_map'
1329 |         )
1330 |         
1331 |         # Phase detection logic - FIXED to handle reverse gap scenario correctly
1332 |         if supplier_cache_count == 0:
1333 |             current_phase = "SUPPLIER_EXTRACTION"
1334 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 PHASE DETECTION: SUPPLIER_EXTRACTION (no supplier cache found)")
1335 |         elif linking_map_count == 0:
1336 |             current_phase = "AMAZON_ANALYSIS"
1337 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 PHASE DETECTION: AMAZON_ANALYSIS (supplier cache: {supplier_cache_count} products, no linking map)")
1338 |         elif linking_map_count > supplier_cache_count:
1339 |             # REVERSE GAP: More linking map entries than cache entries
1340 |             current_phase = "FRESH_CATEGORIES"
1341 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 PHASE DETECTION: FRESH_CATEGORIES (reverse gap - linking map: {linking_map_count} > cache: {supplier_cache_count})")
1342 |             self.log.info("✅ System will start with first URL in config and skip already processed products")
1343 |         elif linking_map_count < supplier_cache_count:
1344 |             # NORMAL GAP: More cache entries than linking map entries
1345 |             current_phase = "GAP_PROCESSING"
1346 |             gap_size = supplier_cache_count - linking_map_count
1347 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 PHASE DETECTION: GAP_PROCESSING (normal gap - cache: {supplier_cache_count} > linking map: {linking_map_count})")
1348 |             self.log.info(f"📊 GAP SIZE: {gap_size} products need Amazon analysis first")
1349 |         else:
1350 |             current_phase = "COMPLETED"
1351 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 PHASE DETECTION: COMPLETED (supplier: {supplier_cache_count}, linking map: {linking_map_count})")
1352 |         
1353 |         self.current_phase = current_phase
1354 |         
1355 |         config_hash = str(hash(str(self.system_config)))
1356 |         runtime_settings = {
1357 |             "max_products_to_process": max_products_to_process,
1358 |             "max_products_per_category": max_products_per_category,
1359 |             "current_phase": current_phase,
1360 |             "supplier_cache_count": supplier_cache_count,
1361 |             "linking_map_count": linking_map_count
1362 |         }
1363 |         self.state_manager.start_processing(config_hash, runtime_settings)
1364 |         self.log.info("✅ Processing state initialized and started")
1365 | 
1366 |         try:
1367 |             # Note: Supplier configuration is loaded automatically by ConfigurableSupplierScraper
1368 |             # Load the linking map for the current supplier
1369 |             self.linking_map = self._load_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
1370 | 
1371 |             # --- CUSTOM CATEGORY LOGIC ---
1372 |             if self.workflow_config.get('use_predefined_categories'):
1373 |                 self.log.info("CUSTOM MODE: Using predefined category list.")
1374 |                 category_urls_to_scrape = await self._get_predefined_categories(self.supplier_name)
1375 |                 if not category_urls_to_scrape:
1376 |                     self.log.error("CUSTOM MODE FAILED: No URLs found in predefined list. Aborting.")
1377 |                     return []
1378 |                 # 🚨 DYNAMIC CATEGORY CALCULATION: Calculate categories needed based on max_products / max_products_per_category
1379 |                 import math
1380 |                 
1381 |                 system_config = self.config_loader.get_system_config()
1382 |                 max_products = system_config.get('max_products')
1383 |                 max_products_per_category = system_config.get('max_products_per_category')
1384 |                 
1385 |                 def is_infinite_mode(max_products, max_products_per_category):
1386 |                     """Detect infinite mode based on multiple indicators"""
1387 |                     mp = max_products or 0
1388 |                     mppc = max_products_per_category or 0
1389 |                     
1390 |                     return any([
1391 |                         mp <= 0,                    # Zero or negative
1392 |                         mppc <= 0,                  # Zero or negative  
1393 |                         mp >= 99999,                # High value threshold
1394 |                         mppc >= 99999,              # High value threshold
1395 |                     ])
1396 |                 
1397 |                 # Apply infinite mode detection
1398 |                 total_available_categories = len(category_urls_to_scrape)
1399 |                 
1400 |                 if is_infinite_mode(max_products, max_products_per_category):
1401 |                     # INFINITE MODE: Process all available categories
1402 |                     self.log.info(f"🌟 INFINITE MODE DETECTED: max_products={max_products}, max_products_per_category={max_products_per_category}")
1403 |                     self.log.info(f"📋 Processing ALL {total_available_categories} predefined categories (infinite mode)")
1404 |                     # No slicing - use all categories
1405 |                 else:
1406 |                     # FINITE MODE: Safe calculation with error handling
1407 |                     try:
1408 |                         if max_products > 0 and max_products_per_category > 0:
1409 |                             categories_needed = math.ceil(max_products / max_products_per_category)
1410 |                             categories_needed = min(categories_needed, total_available_categories)
1411 |                             
1412 |                             self.log.info(f"📊 FINITE MODE: {max_products} max_products ÷ {max_products_per_category} max_products_per_category = {categories_needed} categories needed")
1413 |                             category_urls_to_scrape = category_urls_to_scrape[:categories_needed]
1414 |                             self.log.info(f"📋 Processing {len(category_urls_to_scrape)} predefined categories (finite mode)")
1415 |                         else:
1416 |                             # Fallback to infinite mode
1417 |                             self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Invalid finite mode values, falling back to infinite mode")
1418 |                             self.log.info(f"📋 Processing ALL {total_available_categories} predefined categories (fallback infinite mode)")
1419 |                     except Exception as e:
1420 |                         # Any error defaults to infinite mode
1421 |                         self.log.error(f"❌ Calculation error: {e}, falling back to infinite mode")
1422 |                         self.log.info(f"📋 Processing ALL {total_available_categories} predefined categories (error fallback)")
1423 |             else:
1424 |                 # Original AI-based category selection
1425 |                 self.log.info("STANDARD MODE: Using AI-based hierarchical category selection.")
1426 |                 category_urls_to_scrape = await self._hierarchical_category_selection(self.workflow_config.get('supplier_url'), self.supplier_name)
1427 |                 if not category_urls_to_scrape:
1428 |                     self.log.warning("No categories selected for scraping. Workflow cannot continue.")
1429 |                     return []
1430 | 
1431 |             # Check hybrid processing configuration (from full config, not system section)
1432 |             hybrid_config = full_config.get("hybrid_processing", {})
1433 |             if hybrid_config.get("enabled", False):
1434 |                 self.log.info("🔄 HYBRID PROCESSING MODE: Enabled")
1435 |                 return await self._run_hybrid_processing_mode(
1436 |                     self.workflow_config.get('supplier_url'), self.supplier_name, category_urls_to_scrape, 
1437 |                     max_products_per_category, max_products_to_process, 
1438 |                     max_analyzed_products, max_products_per_cycle, supplier_extraction_batch_size
1439 |                 )
1440 |             
1441 |             supplier_products = await self._extract_supplier_products(
1442 |                 self.workflow_config.get('supplier_url'), self.supplier_name, category_urls_to_scrape, max_products_per_category, max_products_to_process, supplier_extraction_batch_size
1443 |             )
1444 | 
1445 |             if not supplier_products:
1446 |                 self.log.warning(f"No products extracted from {self.supplier_name}. Workflow cannot continue.")
1447 |                 return []
1448 | 
1449 |             self.results_summary["total_supplier_products"] = len(supplier_products)
1450 |             self.log.info(f"Successfully got {len(supplier_products)} products from {self.supplier_name}")
1451 |             
1452 |             # Save supplier products to cache immediately after extraction using centralized path management
1453 |             supplier_cache_file = str(path_manager.get_output_path('cached_products', f"{self.supplier_name.replace('.', '-')}_products_cache.json"))
1454 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: supplier_cache_dir = '{self.supplier_cache_dir}'")
1455 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: supplier_cache_file = '{supplier_cache_file}'")
1456 |             self._save_products_to_cache(supplier_products, supplier_cache_file)
1457 | 
1458 |             # Filter products based on price and validity
1459 |             valid_supplier_products = [
1460 |                 p for p in supplier_products
1461 |                 if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
1462 |             ]
1463 |             price_filtered_products = [
1464 |                 p for p in valid_supplier_products
1465 |                 if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
1466 |             ]
1467 |             self.log.info(f"Found {len(valid_supplier_products)} valid supplier products, {len(price_filtered_products)} within price range [£{MIN_PRICE}-£{MAX_PRICE}]")
1468 |             
1469 |             # Check if all cached products have been processed
1470 |             if self.last_processed_index >= len(price_filtered_products):
1471 |                 self.log.info(f"📋 All cached products have been processed in previous runs (index {self.last_processed_index} >= total {len(price_filtered_products)}). Continuing with fresh data...")
1472 |                 self.last_processed_index = 0
1473 |             
1474 |             # Apply max_products_to_process limit starting from resume index
1475 |             if max_products_to_process <= 0:
1476 |                 # Unlimited mode - process all remaining products
1477 |                 products_to_analyze = price_filtered_products[self.last_processed_index:]
1478 |                 self.log.info(f"🔄 UNLIMITED MODE: Processing ALL {len(products_to_analyze)} remaining products starting from index {self.last_processed_index}")
1479 |             else:
1480 |                 # Limited mode - process up to max_products_to_process starting from resume index
1481 |                 end_index = min(self.last_processed_index + max_products_to_process, len(price_filtered_products))
1482 |                 products_to_analyze = price_filtered_products[self.last_processed_index:end_index]
1483 |                 self.log.info(f"🔄 LIMITED MODE: Processing {len(products_to_analyze)} products (from index {self.last_processed_index} to {end_index-1})")
1484 |             
1485 |             # Update processing state with total products to analyze
1486 |             # 🚨 FIX 2: Use total cache count instead of current filtered batch
1487 |             total_products_for_state = getattr(self, 'total_cache_count', len(price_filtered_products))
1488 |             self.state_manager.update_processing_index(self.last_processed_index, total_products_for_state)
1489 | 
1490 |             # Batch processing logic - group products by max_products_per_cycle
1491 |             batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
1492 |             total_batches = (len(products_to_analyze) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
1493 |             self.log.info(f"🚀 BATCH PROCESSING: {len(products_to_analyze)} products in {total_batches} batches of {batch_size}")
1494 | 
1495 |             # Process products in batches
1496 |             for batch_num in range(total_batches):
1497 |                 start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
1498 |                 end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(products_to_analyze))
1499 |                 batch_products = products_to_analyze[start_idx:end_idx]
1500 |                 
1501 |                 self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
1502 |                 
1503 |                 # Process each product in the current batch
1504 |                 for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
1505 |                     # Calculate the absolute index in the full product list (considering resume index)
1506 |                     current_index = self.last_processed_index + start_idx + i + 1
1507 |                     # 🚨 FIX 2: Show correct total in log messages
1508 |                     total_for_display = getattr(self, 'total_cache_count', len(price_filtered_products))
1509 |                     self.log.info(f"--- Processing supplier product {current_index}/{total_for_display}: '{product_data.get('title')}' ---")
1510 |                     
1511 |                     # 🚨 SURGICAL FIX: Update state manager with product index tracking

1516 |                             self.state_manager.update_product_extraction_progress(current_index, total_products_for_progress)
1517 |                         else:
1518 |                             # Fallback: directly update the product index in supplier_extraction_progress

1520 |                                 progress_data = self.state_manager.state_data.setdefault('supplier_extraction_progress', {})
1521 |                                 progress_data['current_product_index_in_category'] = current_index
1522 |                                 progress_data['total_products_in_current_category'] = len(price_filtered_products)
1523 |                     
1524 |                     # 🔧 AUTHENTICATION FALLBACK CHECK

1530 |

1532 |                     self.state_manager.update_processing_index(current_index, len(price_filtered_products))
1533 |                     # Verify the update worked
1534 |                     current_state = self.state_manager.get_state_summary()

1536 |

1584 |

1600 |                             "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),

1607 |                         }
1608 |

1618 |                     # Save the Amazon data with the correct filename
1619 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")

1714 |                     # Perform financial analysis for individual product
1715 |                     try:
1716 |                         # Import financial calculation functions directly to avoid full cache dependency

1718 |

1724 |                         self._record_product_price_status(product_data, has_supplier_price)
1725 |                         if isinstance(supplier_price_inc_vat, str):
1726 |                             # Clean price string and convert to float
