1735 |                         
1736 |                         if not financials:
1737 |                             self.log.warning(f"Financial calculation returned empty for '{product_data.get('title')}'")
1738 |                             financials = {}
1739 |                             
1740 |                     except Exception as e:
1741 |                         self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed for '{product_data.get('title')}': {e}")
1742 |                         self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
1743 |                         # Continue with empty financials rather than failing completely
1744 |                         financials = {}
1745 | 
1746 |                     # Combine all data
1747 |                     combined_data = {**product_data, "amazon_data": amazon_data, "financials": financials}
1748 |                                 
1749 |                     # 🚨 NEW: Update Amazon analysis progression tracking
1750 |                     if hasattr(self, 'state_manager') and self.state_manager:
1751 |                         try:
1752 |                             # Update Amazon analysis progress with new system progression
1753 |                             self.state_manager.update_amazon_analysis_progress_new(
1754 |                                 product_url=product_data.get("url", ""),
1755 |                                 increment=1
1756 |                             )
1757 |                             self.log.debug(f"📊 AMAZON ANALYSIS: Updated progression for product {current_index}")
1758 |                         except Exception as e:
1759 |                             self.log.error(f"❌ AMAZON PROGRESSION UPDATE FAILED: {e}")
1760 | 
1761 |                     # Check for profitability
1762 |                     if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
1763 |                         self.log.info(f"✅ Profitable product found: '{product_data.get('title')}' (ROI: {financials.get('ROI'):.2f}%, Profit: £{financials.get('NetProfit'):.2f})")
1764 |                         profitable_results.append(combined_data)
1765 |                         self.results_summary["profitable_products"] += 1
1766 |                         self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "profitable")
1767 |                     else:
1768 |                         self.log.info(f"Product not profitable: '{product_data.get('title')}' (ROI: {financials.get('ROI', 0):.2f}%, Profit: £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f})")
1769 |                         self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "not_profitable")
1770 | 
1771 |                     # Save state periodically using configurable batch sizes
1772 |                     overall_product_index = start_idx + i + 1
1773 |                     linking_map_batch = self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1)
1774 |                     
1775 |                     if overall_product_index % linking_map_batch == 0:
1776 |                         self.state_manager.save_state(preserve_interruption_state=True)
1777 |                         self._save_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
1778 |                         self.log.info(f"📊 Periodic save at product {overall_product_index} (linking_map_batch_size: {linking_map_batch})")
1779 |                         
1780 |                         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX #4: SMART MEMORY MANAGEMENT
1781 |                         # Implement periodic memory clearing with sliding window approach
1782 |                         self._perform_smart_memory_management(overall_product_index, batch_products)
1783 | 
1784 |             # Final save and completion
1785 |             self.log.info("🔍 DEBUG: Starting final save and completion phase...")
1786 |             try:
1787 |                 self.log.info("🔍 DEBUG: Calling state_manager.complete_processing()...")
1788 |                 self.state_manager.complete_processing()
1789 |                 self.log.info("✅ State manager processing completed")
1790 |                 
1791 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: Calling _save_linking_map with supplier_name='{self.supplier_name}'...")
1792 |                 self._save_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
1793 |                 self.log.info("✅ Linking map save completed")
1794 |                 
1795 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: Calling _save_final_report with {len(profitable_results)} profitable results...")
1796 |                 self._save_final_report(profitable_results, self.supplier_name)
1797 |                 self.log.info("✅ Final report save completed")
1798 |                 
1799 |             except Exception as final_save_error:
1800 |                 self.log.error(f"❌ CRITICAL: Error during final save phase: {final_save_error}", exc_info=True)
1801 |                 self.log.error("This explains why linking map and financial reports are not being saved!")
1802 |             # This must happen regardless of whether products were processed or skipped
1803 |             self.log.info("🔍 DEBUG: Starting mandatory final save and completion phase...")
1804 |             try:
1805 |                 self.log.info("🔍 DEBUG: Calling state_manager.complete_processing()...")
1806 |                 self.state_manager.complete_processing()
1807 |                 self.log.info("✅ State manager processing completed")
1808 |                 
1809 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: Calling _save_linking_map with supplier_name='{self.supplier_name}'...")
1810 |                 self._save_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
1811 |                 self.log.info("✅ Linking map save completed")
1812 |                 
1813 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: Calling _save_final_report with {len(profitable_results)} profitable results...")
1814 |                 self._save_final_report(profitable_results, self.supplier_name)
1815 |                 self.log.info("✅ Final report save completed")
1816 |                 
1817 |             except Exception as final_save_error:
1818 |                 self.log.error(f"❌ CRITICAL: Error during mandatory final save phase: {final_save_error}", exc_info=True)
1819 |                 self.log.error("This explains why linking map and financial reports are not being saved!")
1820 |             
1821 |             try:
1822 |                 self.log.info("🧮 Generating comprehensive financial report...")
1823 |                 # Fix: Use correct config path for financial_report_batch_size
1824 |                 financial_batch_size_config = self.system_config.get("system", {}).get("financial_report_batch_size", 50)
1825 |                 self.log.info(f"📊 Using financial report batch size: {financial_batch_size_config}")
1826 |                 financial_results = run_calculations(self.supplier_name, max_products=financial_batch_size_config)
1827 |                 if financial_results and financial_results.get('statistics', {}).get('output_file'):
1828 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Financial report generated: {financial_results['statistics']['output_file']}")
1829 |                 else:
1830 |                     self.log.warning("⚠️ Financial report generated but no file path returned")
1831 |             except ImportError as ie:
1832 |                 self.log.error(f"❌ Could not import FBA_Financial_calculator: {ie}")
1833 |             except Exception as e:
1834 |                 self.log.error(f"❌ Error generating financial report: {e}")
1835 |             
1836 |             self.log.info(f"📊 Processing state file saved: {self.state_manager.state_file_path}")
1837 |             self.log.info(f"📊 Final state summary: {self.state_manager.get_state_summary()}")
1838 |             
1839 |             # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Log comprehensive performance summary
1840 |             self.log_hash_optimization_summary()
1841 |             
1842 |             # 🧪 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Run performance benchmark
1843 |             if len(self.linking_map) > 50:  # Only benchmark if we have sufficient data
1844 |                 benchmark_results = self.benchmark_hash_performance(sample_size=min(100, len(self.linking_map)//2))
1845 |                 if benchmark_results:
1846 |                     self.log.info(f"🧪 BENCHMARK: Hash optimization validated with {benchmark_results.get('performance_improvement', 0):.1f}x improvement")
1847 |

1849 |             self.sentinel_monitor.finalize_monitoring()
1850 |             
1851 |             self.log.info("--- Passive Extraction Workflow Finished ---")
1852 |             self.log.info(f"Summary: {self.results_summary}")
1853 |         
1854 |             return profitable_results
1855 | 
1856 |         except Exception as e:
1857 |             self.log.error(f"Unexpected error occurred during workflow execution: {e}", exc_info=True)
1858 |             return []

1860 |     async def _run_hybrid_processing_mode(self, supplier_url: str, supplier_name: str, category_urls_to_scrape: List[str], 
1861 |                                         max_products_per_category: int, max_products_to_process: int, 
1862 |                                         max_analyzed_products: int, max_products_per_cycle: int, 
1863 |                                         supplier_extraction_batch_size: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
1864 |         """
1865 |         HYBRID PROCESSING MODE: Process categories sequentially, only processing NEW products from each category.
1866 |         This fixes the issue where the system was processing ALL cached products instead of just new ones.
1867 |         """
1868 |         # Set hybrid processing mode flag for category-by-category processing
1869 |         self._hybrid_processing_mode = True
1870 |         self.log.info("🔄 HYBRID PROCESSING MODE: Starting sequential category processing")
1871 |         
1872 |         profitable_results = []
1873 |         
1874 |         # Get current counts to determine if we're in gap processing scenario
1875 |         supplier_cache_file, supplier_cache_count = self._find_actual_supplier_cache_file(supplier_name)
1876 |         linking_map_count = len(self.linking_map) if self.linking_map else 0
1877 |         
1878 |         self.log.info(f"📊 HYBRID MODE STATE: Cache={supplier_cache_count}, Linking Map={linking_map_count}")
1879 |         
1880 |         # Check for reverse gap scenario (linking map > cache)
1881 |         if linking_map_count > supplier_cache_count:
1882 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 REVERSE GAP DETECTED: Linking map ({linking_map_count}) > Cache ({supplier_cache_count})")
1883 |             self.log.info("✅ Starting directly with fresh category processing (no gap processing needed)")
1884 |         elif supplier_cache_count > linking_map_count:
1885 |             gap_size = supplier_cache_count - linking_map_count
1886 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 NORMAL GAP DETECTED: Cache ({supplier_cache_count}) > Linking map ({linking_map_count})")
1887 |             self.log.info(f"📊 GAP SIZE: {gap_size} products need Amazon analysis")
1888 |             
1889 |             # Initialize gap processing in state manager
1890 |             self.state_manager.start_gap_processing(gap_size, linking_map_count, supplier_cache_count)
1891 |             
1892 |             # Process the gap first - load cached products and process unmatched ones
1893 |             profitable_results.extend(await self._process_gap_products(gap_size, supplier_cache_count, linking_map_count))
1894 |             
1895 |             # Complete gap processing
1896 |             self.state_manager.complete_gap_processing()
1897 |             self.log.info("✅ Gap processing completed, starting fresh category processing")
1898 |         
1899 |         # Process categories sequentially - only NEW products from each category
1900 |         chunk_size = supplier_extraction_batch_size
1901 |         total_chunks = (len(category_urls_to_scrape) + chunk_size - 1) // chunk_size
1902 |         
1903 |         for chunk_index in range(total_chunks):
1904 |             start_idx = chunk_index * chunk_size
1905 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + chunk_size, len(category_urls_to_scrape))
1906 |             chunk_category_urls = category_urls_to_scrape[start_idx:end_idx]
1907 |             
1908 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing chunk {chunk_index + 1}/{total_chunks}: categories {start_idx + 1}-{end_idx}")
1909 |             
1910 |             # Extract NEW products from current chunk of categories
1911 |             chunk_products = await self._extract_supplier_products(
1912 |                 supplier_url, supplier_name, chunk_category_urls, 
1913 |                 max_products_per_category, max_products_to_process, chunk_size
1914 |             )
1915 |             
1916 |             if not chunk_products:
1917 |                 self.log.info(f"📋 No NEW products extracted from chunk {chunk_index + 1}")
1918 |                 continue
1919 |             
1920 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 EXTRACTION RESULT: {len(chunk_products)} NEW products extracted from {len(chunk_category_urls)} categories")
1921 |             
1922 |             # CRITICAL FIX: Process only NEW products, not all cached products
1923 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 Processing {len(chunk_products)} products with main workflow logic")
1924 |             
1925 |             # Filter and process only the NEW products from this chunk
1926 |             # Apply the corrected filtering logic to hybrid processing mode
1927 |             filtered_products = self._filter_unprocessed_products_with_hash_lookup(chunk_products, supplier_name)
1928 |             
1929 |             if len(filtered_products) != len(chunk_products):
1930 |                 skipped_count = len(chunk_products) - len(filtered_products)
1931 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 HYBRID FILTERING: {skipped_count} products already processed, {len(filtered_products)} need Amazon analysis")
1932 |             
1933 |             if not filtered_products:
1934 |                 self.log.info(f"📋 All products in chunk {chunk_index + 1} already processed")
1935 |                 continue
1936 |             
1937 |             # Process the filtered products through Amazon analysis
1938 |             chunk_profitable_results = await self._process_chunk_with_main_workflow_logic(
1939 |                 filtered_products, max_products_per_cycle
1940 |             )
1941 |             
1942 |             profitable_results.extend(chunk_profitable_results)
1943 |             self.log.info(f"✅ Chunk {chunk_index + 1} completed: {len(chunk_profitable_results)} profitable products found")
1944 |         
1945 |         self.log.info(f"🎯 HYBRID PROCESSING COMPLETED: {len(profitable_results)} total profitable products found")
1946 |         return profitable_results
1947 |             
1948 |     async def _process_gap_products(self, gap_size: int, supplier_cache_count: int, linking_map_count: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
1949 |         """
1950 |         Process the gap between cache and linking map entries.
1951 |         This processes cached products that haven't been analyzed on Amazon yet.
1952 |         """
1953 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 Starting gap processing: {gap_size} products need Amazon analysis")
1954 |         
1955 |         profitable_results = []
1956 |         
1957 |         # Load cached products
1958 |         supplier_cache_file, _ = self._find_actual_supplier_cache_file(self.supplier_name)
1959 |         if not supplier_cache_file:
1960 |             self.log.error("❌ No supplier cache file found for gap processing")
1961 |             return profitable_results
1962 |         
1963 |         try:
1964 |             with open(supplier_cache_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
1965 |                 all_cached_products = json.load(f)
1966 |         except Exception as e:
1967 |             self.log.error(f"❌ Failed to load cached products: {e}")
1968 |             return profitable_results
1969 |         
1970 |         # Get products that need processing (from linking_map_count to supplier_cache_count)
1971 |         gap_products = all_cached_products[linking_map_count:supplier_cache_count]
1972 |         
1973 |         self.log.info(f"📊 Gap processing: Processing products {linking_map_count+1} to {supplier_cache_count}")
1974 |         
1975 |         # Process each gap product
1976 |         for i, product_data in enumerate(gap_products):
1977 |             current_gap_index = i + 1
1978 |             overall_product_index = linking_map_count + current_gap_index
1979 |             
1980 |             self.log.info(f"--- Processing gap product {current_gap_index}/{gap_size}: '{product_data.get('title')}' ---")
1981 |             
1982 |             # Update gap processing progress
1983 |             self.state_manager.update_gap_processing_progress(
1984 |                 current_gap_index, 
1985 |                 len([r for r in profitable_results if r.get('profitable', False)]),
1986 |                 product_data.get('url', '')
1987 |             )
1988 |             
1989 |             # Check if already processed
1990 |             supplier_url = product_data.get("url")
1991 |             if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(supplier_url):
1992 |                 self.log.info("Product already processed: skipping")
1993 |                 continue
1994 |             
1995 |             # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Check if already in linking map using O(1) lookup
1996 |             supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
1997 |             already_in_linking_map, existing_entry = self.hash_optimizer.check_product_in_linking_map(
1998 |                 supplier_ean=supplier_ean, supplier_url=supplier_url
1999 |             )
2000 |             if already_in_linking_map:
2001 |                 self.log.info(f"✅ Product already in linking map - skipping (O(1) hash lookup)")
2002 |                 self.log.debug(f"🚀 HASH LOOKUP: Found existing entry with ASIN: {existing_entry.get('amazon_asin') if existing_entry else 'N/A'}")
2003 |             
2004 |             if already_in_linking_map:
2005 |                 continue
2006 |             
2007 |             # Process through Amazon analysis
2008 |             try:
2009 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
2010 |                 
2011 |                 if amazon_data and amazon_data.get("asin"):
2012 |                     # Create successful linking map entry
2013 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
2014 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
2015 |                         supplier_ean = None
2016 |                     
2017 |                     actual_search_method = amazon_data.get("_search_method_used", "unknown")
2018 |                     if actual_search_method == "EAN":
2019 |                         confidence = "high"
2020 |                     elif actual_search_method == "title":
2021 |                         confidence = "medium"
2022 |                     else:
2023 |                         confidence = "low"
2024 |                     
2025 |                     linking_entry = {
2026 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
2027 |                         "amazon_asin": amazon_data.get("asin"),
2028 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
2029 |                         "amazon_title": amazon_data.get("title"),
2030 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
2031 |                         "amazon_price": amazon_data.get("current_price"),
2032 |                         "match_method": actual_search_method,
2033 |                         "confidence": confidence,
2034 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
2035 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url")
2036 |                     }
2037 |                     # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Use optimized method to add entry and update indexes
2038 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(linking_entry)
2039 |                     
2040 |                     # Run financial analysis
2041 |                     financial_result = await self._run_financial_analysis(product_data, amazon_data)
2042 |                     
2043 |                     if financial_result and financial_result.get('profitable', False):
2044 |                         profitable_results.append(financial_result)
2045 |                         self.log.info(f"✅ Profitable gap product found: {product_data.get('title')}")
2046 |                     else:
2047 |                         self.log.info(f"❌ Not profitable gap product: {product_data.get('title')}")
2048 |                 else:
2049 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for gap processing (same as main processing)
2050 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
2051 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
2052 |                         supplier_ean = None
2053 |                         
2054 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
2055 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
2056 |                     no_match_reason = f"Gap processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
2057 |                     
2058 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
2059 |                     no_match_entry = {
2060 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
2061 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
2062 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
2063 |                         "amazon_title": None,
2064 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
2065 |                         "amazon_price": None,
2066 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
2067 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
2068 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
2069 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
2070 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
2071 |                     }
2072 |                     
2073 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
2074 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
2075 |                     self.log.info(f"❌ No Amazon match for gap product: {product_data.get('title')} - Added no-match linking entry")
2076 |                 
2077 |                 # Mark as processed
2078 |                 self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(supplier_url, "completed_gap_processing")
2079 |                 
2080 |                 # Save periodically
2081 |                 if current_gap_index % 5 == 0:
2082 |                     self._save_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
2083 |                     self.state_manager.save_state(preserve_interruption_state=True)
2084 |                     self.log.info(f"📊 Gap processing checkpoint: {current_gap_index}/{gap_size}")
2085 |                 
2086 |             except Exception as e:
2087 |                 self.log.error(f"❌ Error processing gap product {current_gap_index}: {e}")
2088 |                 continue
2089 |         
2090 |         # Final save
2091 |         self._save_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
2092 |         self.state_manager.save_state(preserve_interruption_state=True)
2093 |         
2094 |         self.log.info(f"✅ Gap processing completed: {len(profitable_results)} profitable products found")
2095 |         return profitable_results
2096 |         """Process the gap products (products in cache but not in linking map)"""
2097 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 Starting gap processing for {gap_size} products")
2098 |         
2099 |         profitable_results = []
2100 |         
2101 |         # Load cached products
2102 |         supplier_cache_file, _ = self._find_actual_supplier_cache_file(self.supplier_name)
2103 |         if not supplier_cache_file:
2104 |             self.log.error("❌ No supplier cache file found for gap processing")
2105 |             return profitable_results
2106 |         
2107 |         try:
2108 |             with open(supplier_cache_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
2109 |                 all_cached_products = json.load(f)
2110 |         except Exception as e:
2111 |             self.log.error(f"❌ Error loading cached products: {e}")
2112 |             return profitable_results
2113 |         
2114 |         # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Get products that need processing using O(1) hash lookup
2115 |         # Replace O(n) URL set creation with O(1) hash-based processing
2116 |         processed_urls_set = self.hash_optimizer.get_processed_urls_set()
2117 |         
2118 |         gap_products = []
2119 |         for product in all_cached_products:
2120 |             product_url = product.get("url")
2121 |             if product_url and product_url not in processed_urls_set:
2122 |                 gap_products.append(product)
2123 |         
2124 |         self.log.info(f"🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Gap processing using O(1) lookups on {len(processed_urls_set)} processed URLs")
2125 |         
2126 |         self.log.info(f"📊 Found {len(gap_products)} products to process in gap")
2127 |         
2128 |         # Process gap products
2129 |         for i, product_data in enumerate(gap_products[:gap_size]):
2130 |             self.log.info(f"--- Processing gap product {i + 1}/{gap_size}: '{product_data.get('title')}' ---")
2131 |             
2132 |             try:
2133 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
2134 |                 if amazon_data and amazon_data.get("asin"):
2135 |                     # Create linking map entry
2136 |                     linking_entry = self._create_linking_map_entry(product_data, amazon_data)
2137 |                     # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Use optimized method to add entry and update indexes
2138 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(linking_entry)
2139 |                     
2140 |                     # Check profitability
2141 |                     if self._is_profitable(product_data, amazon_data):
2142 |                         profitable_results.append({
2143 |                             "supplier_data": product_data,
2144 |                             "amazon_data": amazon_data,
2145 |                             "linking_entry": linking_entry
2146 |                         })
2147 |                         self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE GAP: {product_data.get('title')}")
2148 |                     
2149 |                     # Update gap processing progress
2150 |                     self.state_manager.update_gap_processing_progress(
2151 |                         i + 1, len(profitable_results), product_data.get("url", "")
2152 |                     )
2153 |                 
2154 |             except Exception as e:
2155 |                 self.log.error(f"❌ Error processing gap product: {e}")
2156 |         
2157 |         return profitable_results
2158 | 
2159 |     def _load_linking_map(self, supplier_name: str) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
2160 |         """Load linking map from supplier-specific JSON file using centralized path management"""
2161 |         # Use path_manager for standardized, canonical dotted path construction
2162 |         linking_map_path = str(get_linking_map_path(supplier_name=supplier_name))
2163 |         
2164 |         # Only use canonical path - no fallback patterns
2165 |         if os.path.exists(linking_map_path):
2166 |             try:
2167 |                 with open(linking_map_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
2168 |                     raw_data = json.load(f)
2169 |                     
2170 |                     # Handle both formats: convert old dict format to new array format
2171 |                     if isinstance(raw_data, dict):
2172 |                         # Old simple format: {"EAN": "ASIN", "EAN2": "ASIN2"} -> convert to array
2173 |                         linking_map = []
2174 |                         for ean, asin in raw_data.items():
2175 |                             linking_map.append({
2176 |                                 "supplier_product_identifier": f"EAN_{ean}",
2177 |                                 "supplier_title_snippet": "",
2178 |                                 "chosen_amazon_asin": asin,
2179 |                                 "amazon_title_snippet": "",
2180 |                                 "amazon_ean_on_page": ean,
2181 |                                 "match_method": "EAN_cached"
2182 |                             })
2183 |                         self.log.info(f"✅ Converted linking map from dict format to array format from {linking_map_path} with {len(linking_map)} entries")
2184 |                     elif isinstance(raw_data, list):
2185 |                         # New detailed format: [{"supplier_product_identifier": "EAN_123", "chosen_amazon_asin": "ABC", ...}]
2186 |                         linking_map = raw_data
2187 |                         self.log.info(f"✅ Loaded linking map (array format) from {linking_map_path} with {len(linking_map)} entries")
2188 |                     else:
2189 |                         self.log.error(f"Unexpected linking map format: {type(raw_data)} - Creating new map")
2190 |                         return []
2191 |                         
2192 |                     # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Build hash indexes for O(1) lookups
2193 |                     self.linking_map = linking_map
2194 |                     self.hash_optimizer.build_indexes(linking_map)
2195 |                     self.log.info(f"🚀 HASH INDEXES BUILT: Ready for O(1) lookups on {len(linking_map)} entries")
2196 |                     
2197 |                     return linking_map
2198 |             except (json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError, Exception) as e:
2199 |                 self.log.error(f"Error loading linking map from {linking_map_path}: {e}")
2200 |                 return []  # Return empty list on error
2201 |         
2202 |         # No valid linking map found at canonical path
2203 |         self.log.info(f"✅ No existing linking map found at {linking_map_path} - Creating new map")
2204 |         
2205 |         # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Initialize empty hash indexes
2206 |         self.linking_map = []
2207 |         self.hash_optimizer.build_indexes([])
2208 |         
2209 |         return []
2210 | 
2211 |     def _save_linking_map(self, supplier_name: str):
2212 |         """Save linking map to supplier-specific JSON file using Windows atomic save"""
2213 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: _save_linking_map called with {len(self.linking_map)} entries for supplier {supplier_name}")
2214 |         
2215 |         # 🚨 SENTINEL MONITORING: Track linking map size before save
2216 |         previous_size = 0
2217 |         linking_map_path = get_linking_map_path(supplier_name)
2218 |         # 🚨 SENTINEL MONITORING: Check for path variants
2219 |         self.sentinel_monitor.check_path_variants(str(linking_map_path), "linking_map_access")
2220 |         if linking_map_path.exists():
2221 |             try:
2222 |                 with open(linking_map_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
2223 |                     existing_data = json.load(f)
2224 |                     previous_size = len(existing_data) if isinstance(existing_data, list) else 0
2225 |             except Exception as e:
2226 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Could not read existing linking map for size comparison: {e}")
2227 |         
2228 |         sample_entry = self.linking_map[0] if self.linking_map else {}
2229 |         truncated_sample = {k: str(v)[:50] + '...' if len(str(v)) > 50 else v for k, v in sample_entry.items()}
2230 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: linking_map type: {type(self.linking_map)}, entries: {len(self.linking_map)}, sample: {truncated_sample}")
2231 |         
2232 |         if not self.linking_map:
2233 |             self.log.warning("⚠️ CRITICAL: Empty linking map - nothing to save. This suggests linking map entries are not being created!")
2234 |             # 🚨 SENTINEL MONITORING: Alert on empty linking map
2235 |             self.sentinel_monitor.check_linking_map_shrinkage(0, previous_size)
2236 |             return
2237 |             
2238 |         # Use consistent path naming with get_linking_map_path
2239 |         try:
2240 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 DEBUG: Target file path: {linking_map_path}")
2241 |             
2242 |         except Exception as path_error:
2243 |             self.log.error(f"❌ CRITICAL: Failed to create linking map path: {path_error}")
2244 |             return
2245 |         
2246 |         # 🔧 ATOMIC SAVE: Use Windows Save Guardian with alt-temp strategy
2247 |         self.log.info(f"🔧 ATOMIC SAVE: Attempting to save {len(self.linking_map)} entries using atomic strategy...")
2248 |         
2249 |         try:
2250 |             # 🚨 SENTINEL MONITORING: Track save attempt
2251 |             current_size = len(self.linking_map)
2252 |             save_success = False
2253 |             retry_count = 1
2254 |             
2255 |             # 🛡️ Use Windows Save Guardian with anti-truncation protection
2256 |             success = self.save_guardian.save_json_atomic(
2257 |                 linking_map_path, 
2258 |                 self.linking_map, 
2259 |                 min_entries_guard=1000
2260 |             )
2261 |             
2262 |             if success:
2263 |                 save_success = True
2264 |                 self.log.info(f"✅ Successfully saved linking map with {len(self.linking_map)} entries to {linking_map_path}")
2265 |                 
2266 |                 # Verify the file was actually created and has content
2267 |                 if linking_map_path.exists():
2268 |                     file_size = linking_map_path.stat().st_size
2269 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ VERIFICATION: File exists at {linking_map_path} with size {file_size} bytes")
2270 |                     
2271 |                     # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Rebuild hash indexes after saving linking map
2272 |                     self.hash_optimizer.build_indexes(self.linking_map)
2273 |                     self.log.info(f"🚀 HASH INDEXES REBUILT: Updated indexes for {len(self.linking_map)} entries")
2274 |                     
2275 |                     # 🚨 SENTINEL MONITORING: Check for linking map shrinkage
2276 |                     self.sentinel_monitor.check_linking_map_shrinkage(current_size, previous_size)
2277 |                     self.sentinel_monitor.track_save_retry("windows_save_guardian", True, retry_count)
2278 |                 else:
2279 |                     self.log.error(f"❌ CRITICAL: File was not created at {linking_map_path}")
2280 |                     self.sentinel_monitor.track_save_retry("windows_save_guardian", False, retry_count)
2281 |             else:
2282 |                 self.log.error(f"❌ CRITICAL: Windows Save Guardian failed for {linking_map_path}")
2283 |                 self.sentinel_monitor.track_save_retry("windows_save_guardian", False, retry_count)
2284 |                 
2285 |         except Exception as e:
2286 |             self.log.error(f"❌ CRITICAL: Error in Windows Save Guardian: {e}", exc_info=True)
2287 |             self.sentinel_monitor.track_save_retry("windows_save_guardian", False, retry_count)
2288 | 
2289 |     def _classify_url(self, url: str) -> str:
2290 |         """return 'friendly' | 'avoid' | 'neutral' based on patterns (priority order)."""
2291 |         path = url.lower()
2292 |         for kws in FBA_FRIENDLY_PATTERNS.values():
2293 |             if any(k in path for k in kws):
2294 |                 return "friendly"
2295 |         for kws in FBA_AVOID_PATTERNS.values():
2296 |             if any(k in path for k in kws):
2297 |                 return "avoid"
2298 |         return "neutral"
2299 | 
2300 |     # ──────────────────────── ③  Enhanced State tracking helpers ──────────────────────
2301 |     def _load_history(self):
2302 |         """Load comprehensive scraping history to prevent duplicate processing"""
2303 |         if self.state_path and Path(self.state_path).exists(): # MODIFIED HERE
2304 |             try:
2305 |                 history = json.loads(Path(self.state_path).read_text()) # AND HERE for consistency
2306 |                 # Ensure all required keys exist with backward compatibility
2307 |                 default_history = {
2308 |                     "categories_scraped": [],
2309 |                     "products_processed": [],
2310 |                     "pages_visited": [],
2311 |                     "subpages_scraped": [],
2312 |                     "ai_suggested_categories": [],
2313 |                     "failed_categories": [],
2314 |                     "last_scrape_timestamp": None,
2315 |                     "scrape_sessions": [],
2316 |                     "category_performance": {},
2317 |                     "url_hash_cache": {},
2318 |                     "ai_decision_history": []  # New field for AI decisions
2319 |                 }
2320 |                 # Merge with defaults to handle missing keys
2321 |                 for key, default_value in default_history.items():
2322 |                     if key not in history:
2323 |                         history[key] = default_value
2324 |                 return history
2325 |             except (json.JSONDecodeError, Exception) as e:
2326 |                 log.warning(f"Failed to load history, creating new: {e}")
2327 |                 return self._get_default_history()
2328 |         return self._get_default_history()
2329 | 
2330 |     def _load_ai_memory(self, supplier_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2331 |         """
2332 |         🧠 Load AI memory from AI cache file to prevent re-suggesting same categories.
2333 | 
2334 |         Args:
2335 |             supplier_name: Name of the supplier
2336 | 
2337 |         Returns:
2338 |             Dictionary containing AI memory data
2339 |         """
2340 |         try:
2341 |             ai_cache_file = os.path.join(AI_CATEGORY_CACHE_DIR, f"{supplier_name.replace('.', '_')}_ai_category_cache.json")
2342 | 
2343 |             if os.path.exists(ai_cache_file):
2344 |                 with open(ai_cache_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
2345 |                     content = f.read().strip()
2346 |                     if not content or content == "{}":  # File is empty or just empty JSON
2347 |                         log.info(f"🧠 AI cache file is empty for {supplier_name} - starting fresh")
2348 |                         # Don't raise error - return empty memory but let the system continue
2349 |                         return {
2350 |                             "previously_suggested_urls": [],
2351 |                             "total_products_processed": 0,
2352 |                             "ai_history": [],
2353 |                             "total_ai_calls": 0,
2354 |                             "price_phase": "low"
2355 |                         }
2356 |                     ai_cache_data = json.loads(content)
2357 | 
2358 |                 # Extract all previously suggested URLs from AI history
2359 |                 previously_suggested_urls = set()
2360 |                 total_products_processed = 0
2361 | 
2362 |                 ai_history = ai_cache_data.get("ai_suggestion_history", [])
2363 |                 for entry in ai_history:
2364 |                     ai_suggestions = entry.get("ai_suggestions", {})
2365 | 
2366 |                     # Collect all suggested URLs
2367 |                     top_urls = ai_suggestions.get("top_3_urls", [])
2368 |                     secondary_urls = ai_suggestions.get("secondary_urls", [])
2369 | 
2370 |                     previously_suggested_urls.update(top_urls)
2371 |                     previously_suggested_urls.update(secondary_urls)
2372 | 
2373 |                     # Track products processed
2374 |                     session_context = entry.get("session_context", {})
2375 |                     total_products_processed += session_context.get("total_products_processed", 0)
2376 | 
2377 |                 log.info(f"🧠 AI Memory loaded: {len(previously_suggested_urls)} previously suggested URLs, {total_products_processed} products processed")
2378 |                 log.debug(f"🧠 Previously suggested URLs: {list(previously_suggested_urls)}")
2379 | 
2380 |                 return {
2381 |                     "previously_suggested_urls": list(previously_suggested_urls),
2382 |                     "total_products_processed": total_products_processed,
2383 |                     "ai_history": ai_history,
2384 |                     "total_ai_calls": ai_cache_data.get("total_ai_calls", 0),
2385 |                     "price_phase": ai_cache_data.get("price_phase", "low")  # Default to Phase 1
2386 |                 }
2387 |             else:
2388 |                 log.info(f"🧠 No AI cache file found for {supplier_name} - starting fresh")
2389 | 
2390 |         except Exception as e:
2391 |             log.error(f"Error loading AI memory for {supplier_name}: {e}")
2392 | 
2393 |         # Return default empty memory
2394 |         return {
2395 |             "previously_suggested_urls": [],
2396 |             "total_products_processed": 0,
2397 |             "ai_history": [],
2398 |             "total_ai_calls": 0,
2399 |             "price_phase": "low"  # Default to Phase 1
2400 |         }
2401 | 
2402 |     def _get_default_history(self):
2403 |         """Get default history structure"""
2404 |         return {
2405 |             "categories_scraped": [],
2406 |             "products_processed": [],
2407 |             "pages_visited": [],
2408 |             "subpages_scraped": [],
2409 |             "ai_suggested_categories": [],
2410 |             "failed_categories": [],
2411 |             "last_scrape_timestamp": None,
2412 |             "scrape_sessions": [],
2413 |             "category_performance": {},
2414 |             "url_hash_cache": {},
2415 |             "ai_decision_history": []  # New field for AI decisions
2416 |         }
2417 | 
2418 |     async def _get_predefined_categories(self, supplier_name: str) -> list:
2419 |         """Load predefined category URLs from a custom JSON file."""
2420 |         log.info(f"Attempting to load predefined categories for {supplier_name}")
2421 |         try:
2422 |             # Robust path construction using pathlib
2423 |             config_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "config" / f"{supplier_name.replace('.co.uk', '')}_categories.json"
2424 |             
2425 |             if config_path.exists():
2426 |                 with open(config_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
2427 |                     data = json.load(f)
2428 |                     category_urls = data.get("category_urls", [])
2429 |                     log.info(f"✅ Successfully loaded {len(category_urls)} predefined category URLs from {config_path}")
2430 |                     return category_urls
2431 |             else:
2432 |                 log.warning(f"⚠️ Predefined category file not found at {config_path}")
2433 |                 return []
2434 |         except FileNotFoundError:
2435 |             log.error(f"❌ Configuration file not found: {config_path}")
2436 |             return []
2437 |         except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
2438 |             log.error(f"❌ Invalid JSON in configuration file: {e}")
2439 |             return []
2440 |         except Exception as e:
2441 |             log.error(f"❌ Unexpected error loading predefined categories: {e}")
2442 |             return []
2443 | 
2444 |     def _save_history(self, hist: dict):
2445 |         """Save comprehensive scraping history with atomic write"""
2446 |         if self.state_path:
2447 |             # Add current timestamp
2448 |             hist["last_scrape_timestamp"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
2449 |             
2450 |             # Use atomic write to prevent corruption
2451 |             # Ensure self.state_path is a Path object for .with_suffix and os.replace
2452 |             state_path_obj = Path(self.state_path)
2453 |             temp_path = state_path_obj.with_suffix(".tmp")
2454 |             try:
2455 |                 with open(temp_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
2456 |                     json.dump(hist, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
2457 |                 os.replace(temp_path, state_path_obj) # Use Path object here too
2458 |                 log.debug(f"History saved with {len(hist.get('categories_scraped', []))} categories, {len(hist.get('pages_visited', []))} pages")
2459 |             except Exception as e:
2460 |                 log.error(f"Failed to save history: {e}")
2461 |                 if os.path.exists(temp_path): # os.path.exists is fine for string or Path
2462 |                     try:
2463 |                         os.remove(temp_path)
2464 |                     except:
2465 |                         pass
2466 | 
2467 |     def _record_ai_decision(self, hist: dict, ai_response: dict):
2468 |         """Record AI decision for future reference and learning"""
2469 |         hist.setdefault("ai_decision_history", []).append({
2470 |             "timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
2471 |             "categories_suggested": ai_response["top_3_urls"],
2472 |             "skip_urls": ai_response["skip_urls"],
2473 |             "reasoning": ai_response.get("detailed_reasoning", {}),
2474 |             "progression_strategy": ai_response.get("progression_strategy", "unknown")
2475 |         })
2476 |         # DEPRECATED: Replaced by EnhancedStateManager to prevent state conflicts
2477 |         # self._save_history(hist)
2478 | 
2479 |     def _is_url_previously_scraped(self, url: str, hist: dict) -> bool:
2480 |         """Check if URL has been previously scraped using multiple tracking methods"""
2481 |         if not url:
2482 |             return False
2483 |             
2484 |         # Direct URL check
2485 |         if url in hist.get("categories_scraped", []):
2486 |             return True
2487 |         if url in hist.get("pages_visited", []):
2488 |             return True
2489 |         if url in hist.get("subpages_scraped", []):
2490 |             return True
2491 |             
2492 |         # URL hash check for similar URLs (handles pagination, query params)
2493 |         import hashlib
2494 |         url_hash = hashlib.md5(url.encode()).hexdigest()
2495 |         if url_hash in hist.get("url_hash_cache", {}):
2496 |             cached_url = hist["url_hash_cache"][url_hash]
2497 |             log.debug(f"URL hash match found: {url} matches previously scraped {cached_url}")
2498 |             return True
2499 |             
2500 |         # Normalize URL and check (remove trailing slashes, query params for base comparison)
2501 |         from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs
2502 |         parsed = urlparse(url)
2503 |         base_url = f"{parsed.scheme}://{parsed.netloc}{parsed.path.rstrip('/')}"
2504 |         
2505 |         for scraped_url in hist.get("categories_scraped", []) + hist.get("pages_visited", []):
2506 |             scraped_parsed = urlparse(scraped_url)
2507 |             scraped_base = f"{scraped_parsed.scheme}://{scraped_parsed.netloc}{scraped_parsed.path.rstrip('/')}"
2508 |             if base_url == scraped_base:
2509 |                 log.debug(f"Base URL match: {url} matches {scraped_url}")
2510 |                 return True
2511 |                 
2512 |         return False
2513 | 
2514 |     def _add_url_to_history(self, url: str, hist: dict, url_type: str = "page"):
2515 |         """Add URL to appropriate history list and hash cache"""
2516 |         if not url:
2517 |             return
2518 |             
2519 |         # Add to appropriate list
2520 |         if url_type == "category":
2521 |             if url not in hist.get("categories_scraped", []):
2522 |                 hist.setdefault("categories_scraped", []).append(url)
2523 |         elif url_type == "subpage":
2524 |             if url not in hist.get("subpages_scraped", []):
2525 |                 hist.setdefault("subpages_scraped", []).append(url)
2526 |         else:  # default to pages_visited
2527 |             if url not in hist.get("pages_visited", []):
2528 |                 hist.setdefault("pages_visited", []).append(url)
2529 |         
2530 |         # Add to hash cache
2531 |         import hashlib
2532 |         url_hash = hashlib.md5(url.encode()).hexdigest()
2533 |         hist.setdefault("url_hash_cache", {})[url_hash] = url
2534 |         
2535 |         log.debug(f"Added {url_type} URL to history: {url}")
2536 | 
2537 |     def _record_category_performance(self, category_url: str, products_found: int, hist: dict):
2538 |         """Record performance metrics for categories to improve future AI suggestions"""
2539 |         hist.setdefault("category_performance", {})[category_url] = {
2540 |             "products_found": products_found,
2541 |             "last_scraped": datetime.now().isoformat(),
2542 |             "performance_score": min(products_found / 10.0, 1.0)  # Normalize to 0-1 scale
2543 |         }
2544 | 
2545 |     def _get_category_performance_summary(self, hist: dict) -> str:
2546 |         """Get summary of category performance for AI context"""
2547 |         performance = hist.get("category_performance", {})
2548 |         if not performance:
2549 |             return "No previous category performance data available."
2550 |         
2551 |         summary_lines = ["Previous category performance:"]
2552 |         sorted_categories = sorted(performance.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]["performance_score"], reverse=True)
2553 |         
2554 |         for url, metrics in sorted_categories[:5]:  # Top 5 performing categories
2555 |             summary_lines.append(f"- {url}: {metrics['products_found']} products (score: {metrics['performance_score']:.2f})")
2556 |         
2557 |         if len(sorted_categories) > 5:
2558 |             summary_lines.append(f"... and {len(sorted_categories) - 5} more categories")
2559 |             
2560 |         return "\n".join(summary_lines)
2561 | 
2562 |     # ──────────────────────── PHASE 3: SUBCATEGORY DEDUPLICATION ──────────────────────────
2563 |     def _detect_parent_child_urls(self, urls: List[str]) -> Dict[str, List[str]]:
2564 |         """
2565 |         Detect parent-child URL relationships to prevent double processing.
2566 |         Returns dict where keys are parent URLs and values are lists of child URLs.
2567 |         
2568 |         Example:
2569 |         Input: ['/health-beauty.html', '/health-beauty/cosmetics.html', '/gifts-toys.html']
2570 |         Output: {'/health-beauty.html': ['/health-beauty/cosmetics.html'], '/gifts-toys.html': []}
2571 |         """
2572 |         from urllib.parse import urlparse
2573 |         
2574 |         parent_child_map = {}
2575 |         processed_urls = set()
2576 |         
2577 |         # Sort URLs by path depth (shorter paths first)
2578 |         sorted_urls = sorted(urls, key=lambda url: urlparse(url).path.count('/'))
2579 |         
2580 |         for url in sorted_urls:
2581 |             if url in processed_urls:
2582 |                 continue
2583 |                 
2584 |             parsed_url = urlparse(url)
2585 |             url_path = parsed_url.path.rstrip('/')
2586 |             
2587 |             # Find potential child URLs
2588 |             child_urls = []
2589 |             for other_url in sorted_urls:
2590 |                 if other_url == url or other_url in processed_urls:
2591 |                     continue
2592 |                     
2593 |                 other_parsed = urlparse(other_url)
2594 |                 other_path = other_parsed.path.rstrip('/')
2595 |                 
2596 |                 # Check if other_url is a child of current url
2597 |                 # Child URL should start with parent path + '/'
2598 |                 if other_path.startswith(url_path + '/') and other_path != url_path:
2599 |                     # Additional validation: ensure it's direct child, not grandchild
2600 |                     remaining_path = other_path[len(url_path + '/'):]
2601 |                     if '/' not in remaining_path or remaining_path.count('/') <= 1:
2602 |                         child_urls.append(other_url)
2603 |                         processed_urls.add(other_url)
2604 |             
2605 |             parent_child_map[url] = child_urls
2606 |             processed_urls.add(url)
2607 |             
2608 |         log.info(f"Parent-child URL analysis: {len(parent_child_map)} parent categories, "
2609 |                 f"{sum(len(children) for children in parent_child_map.values())} child categories")
2610 |         
2611 |         return parent_child_map
2612 | 
2613 |     async def _filter_urls_by_subcategory_deduplication(self, category_urls: List[str]) -> List[str]:
2614 |         """
2615 |         Apply subcategory deduplication logic: only include subcategories if parent category has <2 products.
2616 |         
2617 |         This prevents double processing of URLs like:
2618 |         - /health-beauty.html AND /health-beauty/cosmetics.html
2619 |         - /gifts-toys.html AND /gifts-toys/toys-games.html
2620 |         """
2621 |         if not category_urls:
2622 |             return category_urls
2623 |             
2624 |         # Detect parent-child relationships
2625 |         parent_child_map = self._detect_parent_child_urls(category_urls)
2626 |         
2627 |         # Validate each parent category for product count
2628 |         filtered_urls = []
2629 |         validation_cache = {}  # Cache validation results to avoid duplicate checks
2630 |         
2631 |         for parent_url, child_urls in parent_child_map.items():
2632 |             # Check parent category product count (use cache if available)
2633 |             if parent_url not in validation_cache:
2634 |                 validation_result = await self._validate_category_productivity(parent_url)
2635 |                 validation_cache[parent_url] = validation_result
2636 |             else:
2637 |                 validation_result = validation_cache[parent_url]
2638 |             
2639 |             parent_product_count = validation_result.get("product_count", 0)
2640 |             
2641 |             # CORE LOGIC: Apply subcategory deduplication rule
2642 |             if parent_product_count >= 2:
2643 |                 # Parent has sufficient products - skip subcategories, use pagination only
2644 |                 filtered_urls.append(parent_url)
2645 |                 log.info(f"✅ PARENT CATEGORY SUFFICIENT: {parent_url} ({parent_product_count} products) "
2646 |                         f"- SKIPPING {len(child_urls)} subcategories: {child_urls}")
2647 |             else:
2648 |                 # Parent has <2 products - include subcategories for more products
2649 |                 filtered_urls.append(parent_url)
2650 |                 filtered_urls.extend(child_urls)
2651 |                 log.info(f"⚠️  PARENT CATEGORY INSUFFICIENT: {parent_url} ({parent_product_count} products) "
2652 |                         f"- INCLUDING {len(child_urls)} subcategories: {child_urls}")
2653 |         
2654 |         log.info(f"SUBCATEGORY DEDUPLICATION: Reduced {len(category_urls)} URLs to {len(filtered_urls)} "
2655 |                 f"(eliminated {len(category_urls) - len(filtered_urls)} redundant subcategories)")
2656 |         
2657 |         return filtered_urls
2658 | 
2659 |     # ──────────────────────── ②  Enhanced AI method ──────────────────────────
2660 |     async def _get_ai_suggested_categories_enhanced(
2661 |         self,
2662 |         supplier_url: str,
2663 |         supplier_name: str,
2664 |         discovered_categories: list[dict],
2665 |         previous_categories: list[str] | None = None,
2666 |         processed_products: int | None = None,
2667 |     ) -> dict:
2668 |         """🧠 FBA-aware AI selection with UK business intelligence & enhanced memory."""
2669 |         prev_cats = previous_categories or []
2670 | 
2671 |         # 🧠 ENHANCED: Filter out previously suggested categories more thoroughly
2672 |         available_categories = []
2673 |         for c in discovered_categories:
2674 |             if (
2675 |                 self._classify_url(c["url"]) == "friendly" and
2676 |                 c["url"] not in prev_cats and
2677 |                 not any(prev_url in c["url"] or c["url"] in prev_url for prev_url in prev_cats)
2678 |             ):
2679 |                 available_categories.append(c)
2680 | 
2681 |         # Limit to reasonable number for AI processing
2682 |         category_limit = self.system_config.get("ai_features", {}).get("category_selection", {}).get("max_categories_per_request", 3)
2683 |         friendly = available_categories[:category_limit]
2684 |         formatted = "\n".join(f'- {c["name"]}: {c["url"]}' for c in friendly)
2685 | 
2686 |         # 🧠 ENHANCED: Include comprehensive memory context with failure tracking
2687 |         memory_context = ""
2688 |         if prev_cats:
2689 |             memory_context = f"\n\n🧠 IMPORTANT - PREVIOUSLY SUGGESTED CATEGORIES (DO NOT REPEAT):\n"
2690 |             for i, prev_cat in enumerate(prev_cats[-10:], 1):  # Show last 10 to avoid token limit
2691 |                 memory_context += f"{i}. {prev_cat}\n"
2692 |             memory_context += "\n⚠️ You MUST suggest DIFFERENT categories that have NOT been suggested before!"
2693 | 
2694 |         # Add failure tracking from AI memory
2695 |         ai_memory = self._load_ai_memory(supplier_name)
2696 |         failed_urls = []
2697 |         failed_errors = {}
2698 | 
2699 |         # Extract failed URLs from AI history
2700 |         for entry in ai_memory.get("ai_history", []):
2701 |             if isinstance(entry, dict) and "ai_suggestions" in entry:
2702 |                 suggestions = entry["ai_suggestions"]
2703 |                 if "failed_urls" in suggestions:
2704 |                     failed_urls.extend(suggestions["failed_urls"])
2705 |                 if "failed_url_errors" in suggestions:
2706 |                     failed_errors.update(suggestions["failed_url_errors"])
2707 | 
2708 |         # Add failure context to prompt
2709 |         if failed_urls:
2710 |             memory_context += f"\n\n❌ FAILED CATEGORIES (DO NOT SUGGEST THESE):\n"
2711 |             for i, failed_url in enumerate(set(failed_urls[-15:]), 1):  # Show last 15 failures
2712 |                 error_msg = failed_errors.get(failed_url, "Unknown error")
2713 |                 memory_context += f"{i}. {failed_url} (Error: {error_msg})\n"
2714 |             memory_context += "\n⚠️ These URLs failed validation - DO NOT suggest them again!"
2715 |         prompt = f"""
2716 | AMAZON FBA UK CATEGORY ANALYSIS FOR: {supplier_name}
2717 | 
2718 | You are an expert Amazon FBA consultant specializing in UK marketplace product sourcing and category analysis.
2719 | 
2720 | DISCOVERED CATEGORIES FROM WEBSITE HOMEPAGE:
2721 | {formatted}
2722 | 
2723 | {memory_context}
2724 | 
2725 | PREVIOUSLY PROCESSED CATEGORIES: {prev_cats or "None"}
2726 | PREVIOUSLY PROCESSED PRODUCTS: {processed_products or "None"}
2727 | 
2728 | 🚨 CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS - READ CAREFULLY:
2729 | 1. **YOU MUST ONLY SELECT URLs FROM THE "DISCOVERED CATEGORIES" LIST ABOVE**
2730 | 2. **DO NOT INVENT OR CREATE NEW URLs - ONLY USE THE PROVIDED ONES**
2731 | 3. **DO NOT SUGGEST URLs THAT ARE NOT IN THE DISCOVERED CATEGORIES LIST**
2732 | 4. ONLY select URLs that appear to be PRODUCT LISTING PAGES, not search forms or filters
2733 | 5. Avoid URLs containing: "search", "advanced", "filter", "sort", "login", "account"
2734 | 6. Prioritize URLs with clear category names that suggest product listings
2735 | 7. Avoid URLs that look like individual product pages (containing specific product names)
2736 | 
2737 | URL SELECTION RULES:
2738 | - **ONLY SELECT FROM THE DISCOVERED CATEGORIES LIST ABOVE**
2739 | - **DO NOT CREATE NEW URLs OR MODIFY EXISTING ONES**
2740 | - GOOD patterns from the list: URLs ending in category names like "/household.html", "/baby-kids.html"
2741 | - BAD patterns from the list: URLs containing "search", "advanced", "filter", "catalogsearch"
2742 | 
2743 | Based on your FBA expertise, select categories from the DISCOVERED CATEGORIES list that are most likely to contain profitable, scrapeable products for Amazon FBA UK.
2744 | 
2745 | ⚠️ **IMPORTANT: ALL URLs in your response MUST come from the DISCOVERED CATEGORIES list above. Do not invent new URLs.**
2746 | 
2747 | Return a JSON object with EXACTLY these keys:
2748 | {{
2749 |     "top_3_urls": [list of 3 best PRODUCT LISTING category URLs],
2750 |     "secondary_urls": [list of 3-5 backup category URLs],
2751 |     "skip_urls": [list of category URLs to avoid - include search/filter pages],
2752 |     "detailed_reasoning": {{\"category_name\": \"detailed reason for selection/skipping including URL pattern analysis\"}},
2753 |     "progression_strategy": "description of your category selection strategy focusing on product listing identification",
2754 |     "url_pattern_confidence": {{\"high_confidence\": [\"urls you're very confident contain products\"], \"medium_confidence\": [\"urls that might contain products\"], \"low_confidence\": [\"urls unlikely to contain products\"]}}
2755 | }}
2756 | 
2757 | PRIORITIZE CATEGORIES LIKELY TO CONTAIN:
2758 | HIGH PRIORITY:
2759 | - Home & Kitchen products (high profit margins, consistent demand)
2760 | - Pet Supplies (growing market, good margins)
2761 | - Beauty & Personal Care (repeat purchases, brand loyalty)
2762 | - Sports & Outdoors (seasonal opportunities)
2763 | - Office & Stationery (business demand)
2764 | - DIY & Tools (practical demand)
2765 | - Baby & Nursery products (premium pricing potential)
2766 | - Toys & Games (consistent demand)
2767 | - Kids Books (coloring books, sticker books, activity books, picture books)
2768 | 
2769 | MEDIUM PRIORITY:
2770 | - Clearance/Value categories (pound lines, 50p & under, clearance, sale, discount)
2771 | - Crafts & Hobbies (creative supplies, art materials)
2772 | - Seasonal items (Christmas, Halloween, party supplies)
2773 | - Automotive accessories (small car accessories)
2774 | 
2775 | AVOID CATEGORIES WITH:
2776 | - Electronics (high competition, warranty issues)
2777 | - Clothing/Fashion (size/fit issues, returns)
2778 | - Medical/Pharmaceutical (regulatory restrictions)
2779 | - Food/Beverages (expiry dates, regulations)
2780 | - Large/bulky items (shipping costs, storage issues)
2781 | - High-value jewelry (authentication, insurance)
2782 | - Dangerous goods (batteries, flammables, restricted)
2783 | - Adult Books (novels, fiction, textbooks, academic books - AVOID these)
2784 | - Search/filter pages (no actual products)
2785 | 
2786 | IMPORTANT BOOK DISTINCTION:
2787 | - INCLUDE: Kids books, coloring books, sticker books, activity books, picture books
2788 | - EXCLUDE: Adult novels, fiction, textbooks, academic books, biographies
2789 | 
2790 | REASONING REQUIREMENTS:
2791 | For each selected URL, explain:
2792 | 1. Why the URL pattern suggests it contains product listings
2793 | 2. What type of products you expect to find
2794 | 3. Why those products are suitable for FBA
2795 | 4. Estimated profit potential (High/Medium/Low)
2796 | 
2797 | Return ONLY valid JSON, no additional text."""
2798 |         # ---------- AI CALL ----------
2799 |         # 🔧 FIXED: Use regular model since search-enabled model doesn't support json_object format
2800 |         model_to_use = "gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14"  # Use gpt-4.1-mini for consistency
2801 | 
2802 |         # Log API call details for debugging
2803 |         log.info(f"🤖 OpenAI API Call - Model: {model_to_use}, Max Tokens: 1200")
2804 |         log.info(f"🤖 Prompt Length: {len(prompt)} characters")
2805 |         log.debug(f"🤖 Full Prompt: {prompt[:500]}..." if len(prompt) > 500 else f"🤖 Full Prompt: {prompt}")
2806 | 
2807 |         raw = await asyncio.to_thread(
2808 |             self.ai_client.chat.completions.create,
2809 |             model=model_to_use,
2810 |             messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
2811 |             response_format={"type": "json_object"},
2812 |             max_tokens=1200,  # Increased for better reasoning
2813 |         )
2814 | 
2815 |         # Log token usage
2816 |         if hasattr(raw, 'usage') and raw.usage:
2817 |             log.info(f"🤖 Token Usage - Input: {raw.usage.prompt_tokens}, Output: {raw.usage.completion_tokens}, Total: {raw.usage.total_tokens}")
2818 | 
2819 |         # Save detailed API call log
2820 |         self._save_api_call_log(prompt, raw, model_to_use, "category_suggestion")
2821 | 
2822 |         # ---------- STRICT VALIDATION ----------
2823 |         try:
2824 |             ai = json.loads(raw.choices[0].message.content.strip())
2825 |             # Fix: Add missing keys with default values instead of failing
2826 |             required = {"top_3_urls", "secondary_urls", "skip_urls"}
2827 |             if not required.issubset(ai):
2828 |                 self.log.warning(f"AI JSON missing keys: {required - set(ai)} - adding defaults")
2829 |                 # Add missing keys with defaults
2830 |                 if "top_3_urls" not in ai:
2831 |                     ai["top_3_urls"] = [c["url"] for c in discovered_categories
2832 |                                       if self._classify_url(c["url"]) == "friendly"
2833 |                                       and c["url"] not in (previous_categories or [])][:3]
2834 |                 if "secondary_urls" not in ai:
2835 |                     ai["secondary_urls"] = []
2836 |                 if "skip_urls" not in ai:
2837 |                     ai["skip_urls"] = []
2838 |             
2839 |             # Ensure lists are actually lists
2840 |             for key in ["top_3_urls", "secondary_urls", "skip_urls"]:
2841 |                 if not isinstance(ai[key], list):
2842 |                     self.log.warning(f"AI JSON '{key}' is not a list - fixing")
2843 |                     ai[key] = [ai[key]] if ai[key] else []
2844 |                     
2845 |         except Exception as e:
2846 |             self.log.error("AI JSON invalid → %s – falling back to heuristic list", e)
2847 |             friendly_urls = [c["url"] for c in discovered_categories
2848 |                              if self._classify_url(c["url"]) == "friendly"
2849 |                              and c["url"] not in (previous_categories or [])][:3]
2850 |             ai = {"top_3_urls": friendly_urls,
2851 |                   "secondary_urls": [],
2852 |                   "skip_urls": [],
2853 |                   "detailed_reasoning": {"fallback": "heuristic-friendly"},
2854 |                   "progression_strategy": "simple-first-3"}
2855 | 
2856 |         # hard filter avoid patterns
2857 |         ai["top_3_urls"] = [u for u in ai["top_3_urls"] if self._classify_url(u) != "avoid"]
2858 |         ai["skip_urls"]  = list(set(ai.get("skip_urls",[]) + [u for u in ai["top_3_urls"] if self._classify_url(u)=="avoid"]))
2859 |         return ai
2860 | 
2861 |     async def _validate_category_productivity(self, url: str) -> dict:
2862 |         """Check if category URL actually contains products (Solution 3)"""
2863 |         try:
2864 |             log.info(f"Validating category productivity for: {url}")
2865 | 
2866 |             # Quick scrape to count products on first page
2867 |             session = await self.web_scraper._get_session()
2868 |             async with session.get(url, timeout=aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=15)) as response:
2869 |                 if response.status != 200:
2870 |                     return {
2871 |                         "url": url,
2872 |                         "product_count": 0,
2873 |                         "is_productive": False,
2874 |                         "error": f"HTTP {response.status}"
2875 |                     }
2876 | 
2877 |                 html_content = await response.text()
2878 | 
2879 |             # Use the same product detection logic as the scraper
2880 |             from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
2881 |             soup = BeautifulSoup(html_content, 'html.parser')
2882 | 
2883 |             # Try configured selectors first
2884 |             product_elements = []
2885 |             try:
2886 |                 supplier_config = self.web_scraper.get_supplier_config(url)
2887 |                 if supplier_config and supplier_config.get("selectors", {}).get("product_container"):
2888 |                     product_selector = supplier_config["selectors"]["product_container"]
2889 |                     product_elements = soup.select(product_selector)
2890 |             except Exception as e:
2891 |                 log.debug(f"Failed to use configured selectors for {url}: {e}")
2892 | 
2893 |             # Fallback to generic selectors if no configured ones work
2894 |             if not product_elements:
2895 |                 generic_selectors = [
2896 |                     'div.product',
2897 |                     '.product-item',
2898 |                     '.product-container',
2899 |                     '[class*="product"]',
2900 |                     '.item'
2901 |                 ]
2902 |                 for selector in generic_selectors:
2903 |                     product_elements = soup.select(selector)
2904 |                     if product_elements:
2905 |                         break
2906 | 
2907 |             product_count = len(product_elements)
2908 |             is_productive = product_count >= 2  # Minimum 2 products required
2909 | 
2910 |             log.info(f"Category validation: {url} -> {product_count} products (productive: {is_productive})")
2911 | 
2912 |             return {
2913 |                 "url": url,
2914 |                 "product_count": product_count,
2915 |                 "is_productive": is_productive,
2916 |                 "validation_method": "product_count_check"
2917 |             }
2918 | 
2919 |         except Exception as e:
2920 |             log.warning(f"Category productivity validation failed for {url}: {e}")
2921 |             return {
2922 |                 "url": url,
2923 |                 "product_count": 0,
2924 |                 "is_productive": False,
2925 |                 "error": str(e)
2926 |             }
2927 | 
2928 |     def _optimize_category_urls(self, urls: list, price_range: str = "low") -> list:
2929 |         """DEPRECATED: URL optimization is now handled by supplier-specific extraction scripts"""
2930 |         optimized_urls = []
2931 | 
2932 |         for url in urls:
2933 |             # Base parameters for better product retrieval (generic, no price filtering)
2934 |             # ❌ COMMENTED OUT - hardcoded for different website, not compatible with poundwholesale.co.uk
2935 |             # base_params = "product_list_limit=64&product_list_order=price&product_list_dir=asc"
2936 | 
2937 |             # Add parameters to URL
2938 |             # ❌ COMMENTED OUT - using raw URLs for poundwholesale.co.uk compatibility
2939 |             # if '?' in url:
2940 |             #     optimized_url = f"{url}&{base_params}"
2941 |             # else:
2942 |             #     optimized_url = f"{url}?{base_params}"
2943 | 
2944 |             # ✅ USE RAW URL for poundwholesale.co.uk
2945 |             optimized_url = url
2946 |             optimized_urls.append(optimized_url)
2947 |             log.info(f"Using raw URL (poundwholesale.co.uk): {url}")
2948 | 
2949 |         return optimized_urls
2950 | 
2951 |     def _save_api_call_log(self, prompt: str, response, model: str, call_type: str):
2952 |         """Save detailed OpenAI API call logs for debugging and token tracking"""
2953 |         try:
2954 |             from pathlib import Path
2955 | 
2956 |             # Create API logs directory using proper path management (claude.md standards)
2957 |             from utils.path_manager import get_api_log_path
2958 |             log_file = get_api_log_path("openai")  # This creates the proper path
2959 | 
2960 |             # Create log entry
2961 |             log_entry = {
2962 |                 "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
2963 |                 "call_type": call_type,
2964 |                 "model": model,
2965 |                 "prompt_length": len(prompt),
2966 |                 "prompt": prompt,
2967 |                 "response_content": response.choices[0].message.content if response.choices else "No response",
2968 |                 "usage": {
2969 |                     "prompt_tokens": response.usage.prompt_tokens if hasattr(response, 'usage') and response.usage else 0,
2970 |                     "completion_tokens": response.usage.completion_tokens if hasattr(response, 'usage') and response.usage else 0,
2971 |                     "total_tokens": response.usage.total_tokens if hasattr(response, 'usage') and response.usage else 0
2972 |                 }
2973 |             }
2974 | 
2975 |             # Save to daily log file (already created by get_api_log_path)
2976 |             with open(log_file, 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f:
2977 |                 f.write(json.dumps(log_entry, ensure_ascii=False) + '\n')
2978 | 
2979 |             log.info(f"💾 API call logged to {log_file}")
2980 | 
2981 |         except Exception as e:
2982 |             log.warning(f"Failed to save API call log: {e}")
2983 | 
2984 |     def _determine_price_phase(self, ai_memory: dict) -> str:
2985 |         """Determine current price phase based on AI memory"""
2986 |         # Check if Phase 2 has been initiated
2987 |         if ai_memory.get("price_phase") == "medium":
2988 |             return "medium"
2989 | 
2990 |         # Default to Phase 1 (low price range)
2991 |         return "low"
2992 | 
2993 |     def _store_phase_2_continuation_point(self, category_url: str, page_num: int, products_scraped: int):
2994 |         """Store pagination state for Phase 2 continuation"""
2995 |         try:
2996 |             from utils.path_manager import get_phase_continuation_path
2997 | 
2998 |             # Create continuation points file using path_manager (claude.md standards)
2999 |             continuation_file = get_phase_continuation_path()
3000 |             continuation_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
3001 | 
3002 |             # Load existing continuation points
3003 |             continuation_data = {}
3004 |             if continuation_file.exists():
3005 |                 try:
3006 |                     with open(continuation_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
3007 |                         continuation_data = json.load(f)
3008 |                 except Exception as e:
3009 |                     log.warning(f"Could not load continuation points: {e}")
3010 | 
3011 |             # Store continuation point for this category
3012 |             base_url = category_url.split('?')[0]  # Remove any existing parameters
3013 |             continuation_data[base_url] = {
3014 |                 "last_page": page_num,
3015 |                 "products_scraped": products_scraped,
3016 |                 "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
3017 |                 "phase_1_complete": True
3018 |             }
3019 | 
3020 |             # Save updated continuation points using atomic save
3021 |             success = self.save_guardian.save_json_atomic(continuation_file, continuation_data)
3022 |             if not success:
3023 |                 self.log.error(f"❌ Failed to save continuation data to {continuation_file}")
3024 | 
3025 |             log.info(f"📍 Stored Phase 2 continuation point for {base_url}: page {page_num}, {products_scraped} products")
3026 | 
3027 |         except Exception as e:
3028 |             log.error(f"Failed to store Phase 2 continuation point: {e}")
3029 | 
3030 |     def _reset_ai_memory_for_phase_2(self, supplier_name: str, current_memory: dict):
3031 |         """Reset AI memory for Phase 2 while preserving Phase 1 history"""
3032 |         try:
3033 |             from pathlib import Path
3034 | 
3035 |             cache_file_path = Path(AI_CATEGORY_CACHE_DIR) / f"{supplier_name.replace('.', '_')}_ai_category_cache.json"
3036 | 
3037 |             if cache_file_path.exists():
3038 |                 with open(cache_file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
3039 |                     cache_data = json.load(f)
3040 | 
3041 |                 # Add Phase 2 marker to cache
3042 |                 phase_2_entry = {
3043 |                     "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
3044 |                     "phase_transition": f"Phase 1 (£{self.min_price}-£{self.price_midpoint}) complete, starting Phase 2 (£{self.price_midpoint}-£{MAX_PRICE})",
3045 |                     "phase_1_summary": {
3046 |                         "total_categories_processed": len(current_memory.get("previously_suggested_urls", [])),
3047 |                         "total_products_processed": current_memory.get("total_products_processed", 0)
3048 |                     }
3049 |                 }
3050 | 
3051 |                 cache_data["ai_suggestion_history"].append(phase_2_entry)
3052 |                 cache_data["last_updated"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
3053 |                 cache_data["price_phase"] = "medium"  # Mark as Phase 2
3054 | 
3055 |                 # Reset category suggestions for Phase 2 but keep history
3056 |                 cache_data["phase_2_started"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
3057 | 
3058 |                 with open(cache_file_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
3059 |                     json.dump(cache_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
3060 | 
3061 |                 log.info(f"🔄 Phase 2 transition recorded in AI cache: {cache_file_path}")
3062 | 
3063 |         except Exception as e:
3064 |             log.error(f"Failed to reset AI memory for Phase 2: {e}")
3065 |

3112 |     def _classify_category_type(self, url: str, name: str = "") -> str:
3113 |         """Classify category as friendly, avoid, or neutral for FBA"""
3114 |         url_lower = url.lower()
3115 |         name_lower = name.lower()
3116 |         combined_text = f"{url_lower} {name_lower}"
3117 | 
3118 |         # Check FBA friendly patterns
3119 |         for category_type, keywords in FBA_FRIENDLY_PATTERNS.items():
3120 |             if any(keyword in combined_text for keyword in keywords):
3121 |                 return "friendly"
3122 | 
3123 |         # Check FBA avoid patterns
3124 |         for category_type, keywords in FBA_AVOID_PATTERNS.items():
3125 |             if any(keyword in combined_text for keyword in keywords):
3126 |                 return "avoid"
3127 | 
3128 |         # Check neutral patterns
3129 |         for category_type, keywords in FBA_NEUTRAL_PATTERNS.items():
3130 |             if any(keyword in combined_text for keyword in keywords):
3131 |                 return "neutral"
3132 | 
3133 |         return "unknown"
3134 | 
3135 |     def _save_products_to_cache(self, products: list, cache_file_path: str):
