4285 |                             chunk_products = await self._extract_supplier_products(
4286 |                                 supplier_url, supplier_name, chunk_categories, 
4287 |                                 max_products_per_category, max_products_to_process, supplier_extraction_batch_size
4288 |                             )
4289 |                     except Exception as e:
4290 |                         self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Could not read supplier cache: {e} - proceeding with extraction")
4291 |                         # Extract from this chunk of categories
4292 |                         chunk_products = await self._extract_supplier_products(
4293 |                             supplier_url, supplier_name, chunk_categories, 
4294 |                             max_products_per_category, max_products_to_process, supplier_extraction_batch_size
4295 |                         )
4296 |                 else:
4297 |                     self.log.info(f"📝 SUPPLIER PHASE: No existing cache found - starting fresh extraction")
4298 |                     # Extract from this chunk of categories
4299 |                     chunk_products = await self._extract_supplier_products(
4300 |                         supplier_url, supplier_name, chunk_categories, 
4301 |                         max_products_per_category, max_products_to_process, supplier_extraction_batch_size
4302 |                     )
4303 |                 
4304 |                 if chunk_products:
4305 |                     # Immediately analyze these products
4306 |                     # Use the same detailed processing logic as main workflow
4307 |                     chunk_results = await self._process_chunk_with_main_workflow_logic(
4308 |                         chunk_products, max_products_per_cycle
4309 |                     )
4310 |                     profitable_results.extend(chunk_results)
4311 |                     
4312 |                     # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Financial Report Triggering Mechanism - Monitor linking map count as TRIGGER
4313 |                     financial_batch_size = self.system_config.get("financial_report_batch_size", 2)
4314 |                     if not financial_batch_size:
4315 |                         self.log.error("❌ CONFIGURATION ERROR: financial_report_batch_size not found in system config")
4316 |                         continue
4317 |                     
4318 |                     current_linking_map_count = len(self.linking_map)
4319 |                     
4320 |                     # ✅ CORRECTED LOGIC: financial_report_batch_size is TRIGGER MECHANISM (every N linking map entries)
4321 |                     if current_linking_map_count > 0 and current_linking_map_count % financial_batch_size == 0:
4322 |                         try:
4323 |                             self.log.info(f"🚨 FINANCIAL REPORT TRIGGER: Reached {current_linking_map_count} linking map entries (trigger every {financial_batch_size})")
4324 |                             from tools.FBA_Financial_calculator import run_calculations
4325 |                             financial_results = run_calculations(supplier_name)
4326 |                             if financial_results and financial_results.get('statistics', {}).get('output_file'):
4327 |                                 self.log.info(f"✅ Financial report EXECUTED: {financial_results['statistics']['output_file']}")
4328 |                             else:
4329 |                                 self.log.warning("⚠️ Financial report executed but no file path returned")
4330 |                         except ImportError as ie:
4331 |                             self.log.error(f"❌ Could not import FBA_Financial_calculator: {ie}")
4332 |                         except Exception as e:
4333 |                             self.log.error(f"❌ Error executing financial report: {e}")
4334 |                     elif current_linking_map_count > 0:
4335 |                         next_trigger_count = ((current_linking_map_count // financial_batch_size) + 1) * financial_batch_size
4336 |                         self.log.info(f"💡 FINANCIAL REPORT TRIGGER: Next trigger at {next_trigger_count} linking map entries (current: {current_linking_map_count}, trigger frequency: {financial_batch_size})")
4337 |                     
4338 |                     # Check memory management
4339 |                     memory_config = hybrid_config.get("memory_management", {})
4340 |                     if memory_config.get("clear_cache_between_phases", False):
4341 |                         self.log.info("🧹 Clearing cache between processing phases")
4342 |                         # Add cache clearing logic here if needed
4343 |                 
4344 |         elif processing_modes.get("balanced", {}).get("enabled", False):
4345 |             # Balanced mode: Extract in batches, analyze each batch
4346 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 HYBRID MODE: Balanced processing")
4347 |             
4348 |             # Extract all products first
4349 |             all_products = await self._extract_supplier_products(
4350 |                 supplier_url, supplier_name, category_urls_to_scrape, 
4351 |                 max_products_per_category, max_products_to_process
4352 |             )
4353 |             
4354 |             if all_products:
4355 |                 # Process in analysis batches if enabled
4356 |                 if processing_modes.get("balanced", {}).get("analysis_after_extraction_batch", True):
4357 |                     batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
4358 |                     for batch_start in range(0, len(all_products), batch_size):
4359 |                         batch_end = min(batch_start + batch_size, len(all_products))
4360 |                         batch_products = all_products[batch_start:batch_end]
4361 |                         
4362 |                         self.log.info(f"🔄 Analyzing batch {batch_start//batch_size + 1}: products {batch_start+1}-{batch_end}")
4363 |                         # Use the same detailed processing logic as main workflow
4364 |                         batch_results = await self._process_chunk_with_main_workflow_logic(
4365 |                             batch_products, max_products_per_cycle
4366 |                         )
4367 |                         profitable_results.extend(batch_results)
4368 |                 else:
4369 |                     # Analyze all products at once
4370 |                     # Use the same detailed processing logic as main workflow
4371 |                     profitable_results = await self._process_chunk_with_main_workflow_logic(
4372 |                         all_products, max_products_per_cycle
4373 |                     )
4374 |         else:
4375 |             # Sequential mode (default): Complete supplier extraction, then Amazon analysis
4376 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 HYBRID MODE: Sequential processing (extract all, then analyze all)")
4377 |             
4378 |             # Standard sequential processing - extract all first
4379 |             all_products = await self._extract_supplier_products(
4380 |                 supplier_url, supplier_name, category_urls_to_scrape, 
4381 |                 max_products_per_category, max_products_to_process
4382 |             )
4383 |             
4384 |             if all_products:
4385 |                 profitable_results = await self._analyze_products_batch(
4386 |                     all_products, supplier_name, max_products_per_cycle
4387 |                 )
4388 |         
4389 |         try:
4390 |             # Save linking map with all collected entries
4391 |             self._save_linking_map(supplier_name)
4392 |             self.log.info("✅ Hybrid mode: Linking map save completed")
4393 |             
4394 |             # Save final report of profitable products  
4395 |             self._save_final_report(profitable_results, supplier_name)
4396 |             self.log.info("✅ Hybrid mode: Final report save completed")
4397 |             
4398 |             # Save processing state
4399 |             self.state_manager.save_state(preserve_interruption_state=True)
4400 |             self.log.info("--- Hybrid Processing Mode Finished ---")
4401 |             
4402 |         except Exception as save_error:
4403 |             self.log.error(f"❌ CRITICAL: Error during final save operations in hybrid mode: {save_error}", exc_info=True)
4404 |         
4405 |         return profitable_results
4406 | 
4407 |     async def _analyze_products_batch(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], 
4408 |                                     supplier_name: str, max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
4409 |         """Analyze a batch of supplier products for Amazon matching and profitability."""
4410 |         profitable_results = []
4411 |         
4412 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis
4413 |         valid_products = [
4414 |             p for p in products
4415 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
4416 |         ]
4417 |         
4418 |         price_filtered_products = [
4419 |             p for p in valid_products
4420 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
4421 |         ]
4422 |         
4423 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(price_filtered_products)} products in batch mode")
4424 |         
4425 |         # Process products in cycles
4426 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
4427 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
4428 |         
4429 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
4430 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
4431 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
4432 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
4433 |             
4434 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing analysis batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
4435 |             
4436 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
4437 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
4438 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing product {current_index}: '{product_data.get('title')}'")
4439 |                 
4440 |                 # Check if already processed
4441 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
4442 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
4443 |                     continue
4444 |                 
4445 |                 # Amazon data extraction and analysis
4446 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
4447 |                 if not amazon_data or "error" in amazon_data:
4448 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
4449 |                     
4450 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for batch processing
4451 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
4452 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
4453 |                         supplier_ean = None
4454 |                         
4455 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
4456 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
4457 |                     no_match_reason = f"Batch processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
4458 |                     
4459 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
4460 |                     no_match_entry = {
4461 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
4462 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
4463 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
4464 |                         "amazon_title": None,
4465 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
4466 |                         "amazon_price": None,
4467 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
4468 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
4469 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
4470 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
4471 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
4472 |                     }
4473 |                     
4474 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
4475 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
4476 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for batch product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
4477 |                     
4478 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
4479 |                     continue
4480 |                 
4481 |                 # Save Amazon cache and create linking map
4482 |                 supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
4483 |                 asin = amazon_data.get("asin", "NO_ASIN")
4484 |                 
4485 |                 # Save Amazon data
4486 |                 filename_identifier = supplier_ean if supplier_ean else re.sub(r'[<>:"/\\|?*\s]+', '_', product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50])
4487 |                 amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
4488 |                 with open(amazon_cache_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
4489 |                     json.dump(amazon_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
4490 |                 
4491 |                 # Financial analysis
4492 |                 try:
4493 |                     # Already imported at top of file: from FBA_Financial_calculator import financials as calc_financials
4494 |                     supplier_price = float(product_data.get("price", 0))
4495 |                     current_price = amazon_data.get("current_price", 0)
4496 |                     
4497 |                     if supplier_price > 0 and current_price > 0:
4498 |                         financials = calc_financials(
4499 |                             supplier_price=supplier_price,
4500 |                             amazon_price=current_price,
4501 |                             amazon_sales_rank=amazon_data.get("sales_rank", 999999),
4502 |                             amazon_rating=amazon_data.get("rating", 0),
4503 |                             amazon_review_count=amazon_data.get("reviews", 0)
4504 |                         )
4505 |                         
4506 |                         # Check profitability
4507 |                         if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
4508 |                             self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE: ROI {financials['ROI']:.1f}%, Profit £{financials['NetProfit']:.2f}")
4509 |                             
4510 |                             combined_data = self._combine_supplier_amazon_data(product_data, amazon_data, "hybrid_batch")
4511 |                             combined_data["financials"] = financials
4512 |                             profitable_results.append(combined_data)
4513 |                             
4514 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, True, financials.get("NetProfit", 0))
4515 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
4516 |                         else:
4517 |                             self.log.info(f"Not profitable: ROI {financials.get('ROI', 0):.1f}%, Profit £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f}")
4518 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, False)
4519 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
4520 |                             
4521 |                 except Exception as e:
4522 |                     self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed: {e}")
4523 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
4524 |         
4525 |         return profitable_results
4526 | 
4527 |     def _get_cached_products_path(self, category_url: str):
4528 |         """Helper to get the path for a category's product cache file."""
4529 |         category_filename = f"{self.supplier_name}_{category_url.split('/')[-1]}_products.json"
4530 |         return str(path_manager.get_cache_path('supplier_cache', category_filename))
4531 | 
4532 |     def _combine_data(self, supplier_data: Dict[str, Any], amazon_data: Dict[str, Any], session_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
4533 |         """Combine supplier and Amazon data and calculate financial metrics."""
4534 |         combined = {
4535 |             "supplier_product_info": supplier_data,
4536 |             "amazon_product_info": amazon_data,
4537 |             "analysis_timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
4538 |             "session_id": session_id
4539 |         }
4540 |         # Financial calculation is now handled within the main run loop before this function is called
4541 |         # This function is primarily for combining data and adding match validation
4542 |         match_validation = self._validate_product_match(supplier_data, amazon_data)
4543 |         combined["match_validation"] = match_validation
4544 |         return combined
4545 | 
4546 |     def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
4547 |         """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
4548 |         a = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_a.lower()).split())
4549 |         b = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_b.lower()).split())
4550 |         return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
4551 |     
4552 |     def _sanitize_filename(self, title: str) -> str:
4553 |         """Sanitize product title for use in filename"""
4554 |         if not title:
4555 |             return "unknown_title"
4556 |         # Remove or replace problematic characters
4557 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', title)
4558 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'\s+', '_', sanitized)
4559 |         return sanitized[:50]  # Limit length to 50 chars
4560 | 
4561 |     def _validate_product_match(self, supplier_product: Dict[str, Any], amazon_product: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
4562 |         """Validate the match between supplier and Amazon products using configurable thresholds."""
4563 |         matching_thresholds = self.system_config.get("performance", {}).get("matching_thresholds", {})
4564 |         
4565 |         # Get configurable thresholds with fallback to more conservative defaults
4566 |         title_similarity_threshold = matching_thresholds.get("title_similarity", 0.25)
4567 |         medium_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("medium_title_similarity", 0.5) 
4568 |         high_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("high_title_similarity", 0.75)
4569 |         
4570 |         title_overlap_score = self._overlap_score(supplier_product.get("title", ""), amazon_product.get("title", ""))
4571 | 
4572 |         match_quality = "low"
4573 |         confidence = 0.0
4574 |         
4575 |         # Use configurable thresholds - much more strict than previous hardcoded values
4576 |         if title_overlap_score >= high_title_similarity:
4577 |             match_quality = "high"
4578 |             confidence = 0.9
4579 |         elif title_overlap_score >= medium_title_similarity:
4580 |             match_quality = "medium"
4581 |             confidence = 0.6
4582 |         elif title_overlap_score >= title_similarity_threshold:
4583 |             match_quality = "low"
4584 |             confidence = 0.3
4585 |         else:
4586 |             match_quality = "very_low"
4587 |             confidence = 0.1
4588 | 
4589 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 MATCH VALIDATION: '{supplier_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' vs '{amazon_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' = {title_overlap_score:.2f} ({match_quality}, {confidence:.1%})")
4590 |         
4591 |         return {"match_quality": match_quality, "confidence": confidence, "title_overlap_score": title_overlap_score}
4592 | 
4593 |     def _is_product_meeting_criteria(self, combined_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
4594 |         """Check if a product meets all defined business criteria."""
4595 |         # This function is now largely redundant as profitability checks are done in the main loop.
4596 |         # However, it can be used for other criteria like sales rank, reviews, etc.
4597 |         amazon_data = combined_data.get("amazon_product_info", {})
4598 |         financials = combined_data.get("financials", {})
4599 |         # Check profitability (already done, but for completeness)
4600 |         is_profitable = financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT
4601 |         # Check Amazon listing quality
4602 |         meets_rating = amazon_data.get("rating", 0) >= MIN_RATING
4603 |         meets_reviews = amazon_data.get("reviews", 0) >= MIN_REVIEWS
4604 |         meets_sales_rank = amazon_data.get("sales_rank", MAX_SALES_RANK + 1) <= MAX_SALES_RANK
4605 |         # Check for battery products (using the helper function)
4606 |         is_battery = is_battery_title(amazon_data.get("title", "")) or is_battery_title(combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", ""))
4607 |         # Check for other non-FBA friendly keywords
4608 |         is_non_fba_friendly = any(k in amazon_data.get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS) or \
4609 |                               any(k in combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS)
4610 |         # All criteria must be met
4611 |         return is_profitable and meets_rating and meets_reviews and meets_sales_rank and not is_battery and not is_non_fba_friendly
4612 | 
4613 |     def _apply_batch_synchronization(self, max_products_per_cycle: int, 
4614 |                                    supplier_extraction_batch_size: int,
4615 |                                    batch_sync_config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[int, int]:
4616 |         """
4617 |         Apply batch synchronization to align all batch sizes consistently.
4618 |         Returns updated max_products_per_cycle and supplier_extraction_batch_size.
4619 |         """
4620 |         target_batch_size = batch_sync_config.get("target_batch_size", 3)
4621 |         synchronize_all = batch_sync_config.get("synchronize_all_batch_sizes", False)
4622 |         validation_config = batch_sync_config.get("validation", {})
4623 |         
4624 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 BATCH SYNCHRONIZATION: Enabled")
4625 |         self.log.info(f"   target_batch_size: {target_batch_size}")
4626 |         self.log.info(f"   synchronize_all_batch_sizes: {synchronize_all}")
4627 |         
4628 |         original_values = {
4629 |             "max_products_per_cycle": max_products_per_cycle,
4630 |             "supplier_extraction_batch_size": supplier_extraction_batch_size,
4631 |             "linking_map_batch_size": self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1),
4632 |             "financial_report_batch_size": self.system_config.get("financial_report_batch_size", 2)
4633 |         }
4634 |         
4635 |         # Check for mismatched sizes and warn if configured
4636 |         if validation_config.get("warn_on_mismatched_sizes", True):
4637 |             unique_sizes = set(original_values.values())
4638 |             if len(unique_sizes) > 1:
4639 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ BATCH SYNC WARNING: Mismatched batch sizes detected: {original_values}")
4640 |                 self.log.warning(f"   Unique sizes found: {sorted(unique_sizes)}")
4641 |         
4642 |         if synchronize_all:
4643 |             # Synchronize all batch sizes to target
4644 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
4645 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
4646 |             
4647 |             # Also update system config values in memory for consistency
4648 |             if "system" in self.system_config:
4649 |                 self.system_config["max_products_per_cycle"] = target_batch_size
4650 |                 self.system_config["supplier_extraction_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
4651 |                 self.system_config["linking_map_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
4652 |                 self.system_config["financial_report_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
4653 |             
4654 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: All batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
4655 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
4656 |             
4657 |         else:
4658 |             # Only synchronize the two main processing batch sizes
4659 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
4660 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
4661 |             
4662 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: Main processing batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
4663 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
4664 |         
4665 |         return new_max_products_per_cycle, new_supplier_extraction_batch_size
4666 | 
4667 |     async def _analyze_products_batch(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], 
4668 |                                     supplier_name: str, max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
4669 |         """Analyze a batch of supplier products for Amazon matching and profitability."""
4670 |         profitable_results = []
4671 |         
4672 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis
4673 |         valid_products = [
4674 |             p for p in products
4675 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
4676 |         ]
4677 |         
4678 |         price_filtered_products = [
4679 |             p for p in valid_products
4680 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
4681 |         ]
4682 |         
4683 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(price_filtered_products)} products in batch mode")
4684 |         
4685 |         # Process products in cycles
4686 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
4687 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
4688 |         
4689 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
4690 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
4691 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
4692 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
4693 |             
4694 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing analysis batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
4695 |             
4696 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
4697 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
4698 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing product {current_index}: '{product_data.get('title')}'")
4699 |                 
4700 |                 # Check if already processed
4701 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
4702 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
4703 |                     continue
4704 |                 
4705 |                 # Amazon data extraction and analysis
4706 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
4707 |                 if not amazon_data or "error" in amazon_data:
4708 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
4709 |                     
4710 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for batch processing
4711 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
4712 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
4713 |                         supplier_ean = None
4714 |                         
4715 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
4716 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
4717 |                     no_match_reason = f"Batch processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
4718 |                     
4719 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
4720 |                     no_match_entry = {
4721 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
4722 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
4723 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
4724 |                         "amazon_title": None,
4725 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
4726 |                         "amazon_price": None,
4727 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
4728 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
4729 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
4730 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
4731 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
4732 |                     }
4733 |                     
4734 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
4735 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
4736 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for batch product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
4737 |                     
4738 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
4739 |                     continue
4740 |                 
4741 |                 # Save Amazon cache and create linking map
4742 |                 supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
4743 |                 asin = amazon_data.get("asin", "NO_ASIN")
4744 |                 
4745 |                 # Save Amazon data
4746 |                 filename_identifier = supplier_ean if supplier_ean else re.sub(r'[<>:"/\\|?*\s]+', '_', product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50])
4747 |                 amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
4748 |                 with open(amazon_cache_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
4749 |                     json.dump(amazon_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
4750 |                 
4751 |                 # Financial analysis
4752 |                 try:
4753 |                     # Already imported at top of file: from FBA_Financial_calculator import financials as calc_financials
4754 |                     supplier_price = float(product_data.get("price", 0))
4755 |                     current_price = amazon_data.get("current_price", 0)
4756 |                     
4757 |                     if supplier_price > 0 and current_price > 0:
4758 |                         financials = calc_financials(
4759 |                             supplier_price=supplier_price,
4760 |                             amazon_price=current_price,
4761 |                             amazon_sales_rank=amazon_data.get("sales_rank", 999999),
4762 |                             amazon_rating=amazon_data.get("rating", 0),
4763 |                             amazon_review_count=amazon_data.get("reviews", 0)
4764 |                         )
4765 |                         
4766 |                         # Check profitability
4767 |                         if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
4768 |                             self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE: ROI {financials['ROI']:.1f}%, Profit £{financials['NetProfit']:.2f}")
4769 |                             
4770 |                             combined_data = self._combine_supplier_amazon_data(product_data, amazon_data, "hybrid_batch")
4771 |                             combined_data["financials"] = financials
4772 |                             profitable_results.append(combined_data)
4773 |                             
4774 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, True, financials.get("NetProfit", 0))
4775 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
4776 |                         else:
4777 |                             self.log.info(f"Not profitable: ROI {financials.get('ROI', 0):.1f}%, Profit £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f}")
4778 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, False)
4779 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
4780 |                             
4781 |                 except Exception as e:
4782 |                     self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed: {e}")
4783 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
4784 |         
4785 |         return profitable_results
4786 | 
4787 |     def _get_cached_products_path(self, category_url: str):
4788 |         """Helper to get the path for a category's product cache file."""
4789 |         category_filename = f"{self.supplier_name}_{category_url.split('/')[-1]}_products.json"
4790 |         return str(path_manager.get_cache_path('supplier_cache', category_filename))
4791 | 
4792 |     def _combine_data(self, supplier_data: Dict[str, Any], amazon_data: Dict[str, Any], session_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
4793 |         """Combine supplier and Amazon data and calculate financial metrics."""
4794 |         combined = {
4795 |             "supplier_product_info": supplier_data,
4796 |             "amazon_product_info": amazon_data,
4797 |             "analysis_timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
4798 |             "session_id": session_id
4799 |         }
4800 |         # Financial calculation is now handled within the main run loop before this function is called
4801 |         # This function is primarily for combining data and adding match validation
4802 |         match_validation = self._validate_product_match(supplier_data, amazon_data)
4803 |         combined["match_validation"] = match_validation
4804 |         return combined
4805 | 
4806 |     def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
4807 |         """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
4808 |         a = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_a.lower()).split())
4809 |         b = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_b.lower()).split())
4810 |         return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
4811 |     
4812 |     def _sanitize_filename(self, title: str) -> str:
4813 |         """Sanitize product title for use in filename"""
4814 |         if not title:
4815 |             return "unknown_title"
4816 |         # Remove or replace problematic characters
4817 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', title)
4818 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'\s+', '_', sanitized)
4819 |         return sanitized[:50]  # Limit length to 50 chars
4820 | 
4821 |     def _validate_product_match(self, supplier_product: Dict[str, Any], amazon_product: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
4822 |         """Validate the match between supplier and Amazon products using configurable thresholds."""
4823 |         matching_thresholds = self.system_config.get("performance", {}).get("matching_thresholds", {})
4824 |         
4825 |         # Get configurable thresholds with fallback to more conservative defaults
4826 |         title_similarity_threshold = matching_thresholds.get("title_similarity", 0.25)
4827 |         medium_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("medium_title_similarity", 0.5) 
4828 |         high_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("high_title_similarity", 0.75)
4829 |         
4830 |         title_overlap_score = self._overlap_score(supplier_product.get("title", ""), amazon_product.get("title", ""))
4831 | 
4832 |         match_quality = "low"
4833 |         confidence = 0.0
4834 |         
4835 |         # Use configurable thresholds - much more strict than previous hardcoded values
4836 |         if title_overlap_score >= high_title_similarity:
4837 |             match_quality = "high"
4838 |             confidence = 0.9
4839 |         elif title_overlap_score >= medium_title_similarity:
4840 |             match_quality = "medium"
4841 |             confidence = 0.6
4842 |         elif title_overlap_score >= title_similarity_threshold:
4843 |             match_quality = "low"
4844 |             confidence = 0.3
4845 |         else:
4846 |             match_quality = "very_low"
4847 |             confidence = 0.1
4848 | 
4849 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 MATCH VALIDATION: '{supplier_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' vs '{amazon_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' = {title_overlap_score:.2f} ({match_quality}, {confidence:.1%})")
4850 |         
4851 |         return {"match_quality": match_quality, "confidence": confidence, "title_overlap_score": title_overlap_score}
4852 | 
4853 |     def _is_product_meeting_criteria(self, combined_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
4854 |         """Check if a product meets all defined business criteria."""
4855 |         # This function is now largely redundant as profitability checks are done in the main loop.
4856 |         # However, it can be used for other criteria like sales rank, reviews, etc.
4857 |         amazon_data = combined_data.get("amazon_product_info", {})
4858 |         financials = combined_data.get("financials", {})
4859 |         # Check profitability (already done, but for completeness)
4860 |         is_profitable = financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT
4861 |         # Check Amazon listing quality
4862 |         meets_rating = amazon_data.get("rating", 0) >= MIN_RATING
4863 |         meets_reviews = amazon_data.get("reviews", 0) >= MIN_REVIEWS
4864 |         meets_sales_rank = amazon_data.get("sales_rank", MAX_SALES_RANK + 1) <= MAX_SALES_RANK
4865 |         # Check for battery products (using the helper function)
4866 |         is_battery = is_battery_title(amazon_data.get("title", "")) or is_battery_title(combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", ""))
4867 |         # Check for other non-FBA friendly keywords
4868 |         is_non_fba_friendly = any(k in amazon_data.get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS) or \
4869 |                               any(k in combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS)
4870 |         # All criteria must be met
4871 |         return is_profitable and meets_rating and meets_reviews and meets_sales_rank and not is_battery and not is_non_fba_friendly
4872 | 
4873 |     def _apply_batch_synchronization(self, max_products_per_cycle: int, 
4874 |                                    supplier_extraction_batch_size: int,
4875 |                                    batch_sync_config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[int, int]:
4876 |         """
4877 |         Apply batch synchronization to align all batch sizes consistently.
4878 |         Returns updated max_products_per_cycle and supplier_extraction_batch_size.
4879 |         """
4880 |         target_batch_size = batch_sync_config.get("target_batch_size", 3)
4881 |         synchronize_all = batch_sync_config.get("synchronize_all_batch_sizes", False)
4882 |         validation_config = batch_sync_config.get("validation", {})
4883 |         
4884 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 BATCH SYNCHRONIZATION: Enabled")
4885 |         self.log.info(f"   target_batch_size: {target_batch_size}")
4886 |         self.log.info(f"   synchronize_all_batch_sizes: {synchronize_all}")
4887 |         
4888 |         original_values = {
4889 |             "max_products_per_cycle": max_products_per_cycle,
4890 |             "supplier_extraction_batch_size": supplier_extraction_batch_size,
4891 |             "linking_map_batch_size": self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1),
4892 |             "financial_report_batch_size": self.system_config.get("financial_report_batch_size", 2)
4893 |         }
4894 |         
4895 |         # Check for mismatched sizes and warn if configured
4896 |         if validation_config.get("warn_on_mismatched_sizes", True):
4897 |             unique_sizes = set(original_values.values())
4898 |             if len(unique_sizes) > 1:
4899 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ BATCH SYNC WARNING: Mismatched batch sizes detected: {original_values}")
4900 |                 self.log.warning(f"   Unique sizes found: {sorted(unique_sizes)}")
4901 |         
4902 |         if synchronize_all:
4903 |             # Synchronize all batch sizes to target
4904 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
4905 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
4906 |             
4907 |             # Also update system config values in memory for consistency
4908 |             if "system" in self.system_config:
4909 |                 self.system_config["max_products_per_cycle"] = target_batch_size
4910 |                 self.system_config["supplier_extraction_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
4911 |                 self.system_config["linking_map_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
4912 |                 self.system_config["financial_report_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
4913 |             
4914 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: All batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
4915 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
4916 |             
4917 |         else:
4918 |             # Only synchronize the two main processing batch sizes
4919 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
4920 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
4921 |             
4922 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: Main processing batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
4923 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
4924 |         
4925 |         return new_max_products_per_cycle, new_supplier_extraction_batch_size
4926 | 
4927 |     async def _analyze_products_batch(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], 
4928 |                                     supplier_name: str, max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
4929 |         """Analyze a batch of supplier products for Amazon matching and profitability."""
4930 |         profitable_results = []
4931 |         
4932 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis
4933 |         valid_products = [
4934 |             p for p in products
4935 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
4936 |         ]
4937 |         
4938 |         price_filtered_products = [
4939 |             p for p in valid_products
4940 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
4941 |         ]
4942 |         
4943 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(price_filtered_products)} products in batch mode")
4944 |         
4945 |         # Process products in cycles
4946 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
4947 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
4948 |         
4949 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
4950 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
4951 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
4952 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
4953 |             
4954 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing analysis batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
4955 |             
4956 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
4957 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
4958 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing product {current_index}: '{product_data.get('title')}'")
4959 |                 
4960 |                 # Check if already processed
4961 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
4962 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
4963 |                     continue
4964 |                 
4965 |                 # Amazon data extraction and analysis
4966 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
4967 |                 if not amazon_data or "error" in amazon_data:
4968 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
4969 |                     
4970 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for batch processing
4971 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
4972 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
4973 |                         supplier_ean = None
4974 |                         
4975 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
4976 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
4977 |                     no_match_reason = f"Batch processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
4978 |                     
4979 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
4980 |                     no_match_entry = {
4981 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
4982 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
4983 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
4984 |                         "amazon_title": None,
4985 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
4986 |                         "amazon_price": None,
4987 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
4988 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
4989 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
4990 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
4991 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
4992 |                     }
4993 |                     
4994 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
4995 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
4996 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for batch product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
4997 |                     
4998 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
4999 |                     continue
5000 |                 
5001 |                 # Save Amazon cache and create linking map
5002 |                 supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
5003 |                 asin = amazon_data.get("asin", "NO_ASIN")
5004 |                 
5005 |                 # Save Amazon data
5006 |                 filename_identifier = supplier_ean if supplier_ean else re.sub(r'[<>:"/\\|?*\s]+', '_', product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50])
5007 |                 amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
5008 |                 with open(amazon_cache_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
5009 |                     json.dump(amazon_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
5010 |                 
5011 |                 # Financial analysis
5012 |                 try:
5013 |                     # Already imported at top of file: from FBA_Financial_calculator import financials as calc_financials
5014 |                     supplier_price = float(product_data.get("price", 0))
5015 |                     current_price = amazon_data.get("current_price", 0)
5016 |                     
5017 |                     if supplier_price > 0 and current_price > 0:
5018 |                         financials = calc_financials(
5019 |                             supplier_price=supplier_price,
5020 |                             amazon_price=current_price,
5021 |                             amazon_sales_rank=amazon_data.get("sales_rank", 999999),
5022 |                             amazon_rating=amazon_data.get("rating", 0),
5023 |                             amazon_review_count=amazon_data.get("reviews", 0)
5024 |                         )
5025 |                         
5026 |                         # Check profitability
5027 |                         if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
5028 |                             self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE: ROI {financials['ROI']:.1f}%, Profit £{financials['NetProfit']:.2f}")
5029 |                             
5030 |                             combined_data = self._combine_supplier_amazon_data(product_data, amazon_data, "hybrid_batch")
5031 |                             combined_data["financials"] = financials
5032 |                             profitable_results.append(combined_data)
5033 |                             
5034 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, True, financials.get("NetProfit", 0))
5035 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
5036 |                         else:
5037 |                             self.log.info(f"Not profitable: ROI {financials.get('ROI', 0):.1f}%, Profit £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f}")
5038 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, False)
5039 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
5040 |                             
5041 |                 except Exception as e:
5042 |                     self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed: {e}")
5043 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
5044 |         
5045 |         return profitable_results
5046 | 
5047 |     def _get_cached_products_path(self, category_url: str):
5048 |         """Helper to get the path for a category's product cache file."""
5049 |         category_filename = f"{self.supplier_name}_{category_url.split('/')[-1]}_products.json"
5050 |         return str(path_manager.get_cache_path('supplier_cache', category_filename))
5051 | 
5052 |     def _combine_data(self, supplier_data: Dict[str, Any], amazon_data: Dict[str, Any], session_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
5053 |         """Combine supplier and Amazon data and calculate financial metrics."""
5054 |         combined = {
5055 |             "supplier_product_info": supplier_data,
5056 |             "amazon_product_info": amazon_data,
5057 |             "analysis_timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
5058 |             "session_id": session_id
5059 |         }
5060 |         # Financial calculation is now handled within the main run loop before this function is called
5061 |         # This function is primarily for combining data and adding match validation
5062 |         match_validation = self._validate_product_match(supplier_data, amazon_data)
5063 |         combined["match_validation"] = match_validation
5064 |         return combined
5065 | 
5066 |     def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
5067 |         """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
5068 |         a = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_a.lower()).split())
5069 |         b = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_b.lower()).split())
5070 |         return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
5071 |     
5072 |     def _sanitize_filename(self, title: str) -> str:
5073 |         """Sanitize product title for use in filename"""
5074 |         if not title:
5075 |             return "unknown_title"
5076 |         # Remove or replace problematic characters
5077 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', title)
5078 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'\s+', '_', sanitized)
5079 |         return sanitized[:50]  # Limit length to 50 chars
5080 | 
5081 |     def _validate_product_match(self, supplier_product: Dict[str, Any], amazon_product: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
5082 |         """Validate the match between supplier and Amazon products using configurable thresholds."""
5083 |         matching_thresholds = self.system_config.get("performance", {}).get("matching_thresholds", {})
5084 |         
5085 |         # Get configurable thresholds with fallback to more conservative defaults
5086 |         title_similarity_threshold = matching_thresholds.get("title_similarity", 0.25)
5087 |         medium_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("medium_title_similarity", 0.5) 
5088 |         high_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("high_title_similarity", 0.75)
5089 |         
5090 |         title_overlap_score = self._overlap_score(supplier_product.get("title", ""), amazon_product.get("title", ""))
5091 | 
5092 |         match_quality = "low"
5093 |         confidence = 0.0
5094 |         
5095 |         # Use configurable thresholds - much more strict than previous hardcoded values
5096 |         if title_overlap_score >= high_title_similarity:
5097 |             match_quality = "high"
5098 |             confidence = 0.9
5099 |         elif title_overlap_score >= medium_title_similarity:
5100 |             match_quality = "medium"
5101 |             confidence = 0.6
5102 |         elif title_overlap_score >= title_similarity_threshold:
5103 |             match_quality = "low"
5104 |             confidence = 0.3
5105 |         else:
5106 |             match_quality = "very_low"
5107 |             confidence = 0.1
5108 | 
5109 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 MATCH VALIDATION: '{supplier_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' vs '{amazon_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' = {title_overlap_score:.2f} ({match_quality}, {confidence:.1%})")
5110 |         
5111 |         return {"match_quality": match_quality, "confidence": confidence, "title_overlap_score": title_overlap_score}
5112 | 
5113 |     def _is_product_meeting_criteria(self, combined_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
5114 |         """Check if a product meets all defined business criteria."""
5115 |         # This function is now largely redundant as profitability checks are done in the main loop.
5116 |         # However, it can be used for other criteria like sales rank, reviews, etc.
5117 |         amazon_data = combined_data.get("amazon_product_info", {})
5118 |         financials = combined_data.get("financials", {})
5119 |         # Check profitability (already done, but for completeness)
5120 |         is_profitable = financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT
5121 |         # Check Amazon listing quality
5122 |         meets_rating = amazon_data.get("rating", 0) >= MIN_RATING
5123 |         meets_reviews = amazon_data.get("reviews", 0) >= MIN_REVIEWS
5124 |         meets_sales_rank = amazon_data.get("sales_rank", MAX_SALES_RANK + 1) <= MAX_SALES_RANK
5125 |         # Check for battery products (using the helper function)
5126 |         is_battery = is_battery_title(amazon_data.get("title", "")) or is_battery_title(combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", ""))
5127 |         # Check for other non-FBA friendly keywords
5128 |         is_non_fba_friendly = any(k in amazon_data.get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS) or \
5129 |                               any(k in combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS)
5130 |         # All criteria must be met
5131 |         return is_profitable and meets_rating and meets_reviews and meets_sales_rank and not is_battery and not is_non_fba_friendly
5132 | 
5133 |     def _apply_batch_synchronization(self, max_products_per_cycle: int, 
5134 |                                    supplier_extraction_batch_size: int,
5135 |                                    batch_sync_config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[int, int]:
5136 |         """
5137 |         Apply batch synchronization to align all batch sizes consistently.
5138 |         Returns updated max_products_per_cycle and supplier_extraction_batch_size.
5139 |         """
5140 |         target_batch_size = batch_sync_config.get("target_batch_size", 3)
5141 |         synchronize_all = batch_sync_config.get("synchronize_all_batch_sizes", False)
5142 |         validation_config = batch_sync_config.get("validation", {})
5143 |         
5144 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 BATCH SYNCHRONIZATION: Enabled")
5145 |         self.log.info(f"   target_batch_size: {target_batch_size}")
5146 |         self.log.info(f"   synchronize_all_batch_sizes: {synchronize_all}")
5147 |         
5148 |         original_values = {
5149 |             "max_products_per_cycle": max_products_per_cycle,
5150 |             "supplier_extraction_batch_size": supplier_extraction_batch_size,
5151 |             "linking_map_batch_size": self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1),
5152 |             "financial_report_batch_size": self.system_config.get("financial_report_batch_size", 2)
5153 |         }
5154 |         
5155 |         # Check for mismatched sizes and warn if configured
5156 |         if validation_config.get("warn_on_mismatched_sizes", True):
5157 |             unique_sizes = set(original_values.values())
5158 |             if len(unique_sizes) > 1:
5159 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ BATCH SYNC WARNING: Mismatched batch sizes detected: {original_values}")
5160 |                 self.log.warning(f"   Unique sizes found: {sorted(unique_sizes)}")
5161 |         
5162 |         if synchronize_all:
5163 |             # Synchronize all batch sizes to target
5164 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
5165 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
5166 |             
5167 |             # Also update system config values in memory for consistency
5168 |             if "system" in self.system_config:
5169 |                 self.system_config["max_products_per_cycle"] = target_batch_size
5170 |                 self.system_config["supplier_extraction_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5171 |                 self.system_config["linking_map_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5172 |                 self.system_config["financial_report_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5173 |             
5174 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: All batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
5175 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
5176 |             
5177 |         else:
5178 |             # Only synchronize the two main processing batch sizes
5179 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
5180 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
5181 |             
5182 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: Main processing batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
5183 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
5184 |         
5185 |         return new_max_products_per_cycle, new_supplier_extraction_batch_size
5186 | 
5187 |     async def _analyze_products_batch(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], 
5188 |                                     supplier_name: str, max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
5189 |         """Analyze a batch of supplier products for Amazon matching and profitability."""
5190 |         profitable_results = []
5191 |         
5192 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis
5193 |         valid_products = [
5194 |             p for p in products
5195 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
5196 |         ]
5197 |         
5198 |         price_filtered_products = [
5199 |             p for p in valid_products
5200 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
5201 |         ]
5202 |         
5203 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(price_filtered_products)} products in batch mode")
5204 |         
5205 |         # Process products in cycles
5206 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
5207 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
5208 |         
5209 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
5210 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
5211 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
5212 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
5213 |             
5214 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing analysis batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
5215 |             
5216 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
5217 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
5218 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing product {current_index}: '{product_data.get('title')}'")
5219 |                 
5220 |                 # Check if already processed
5221 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
5222 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
5223 |                     continue
5224 |                 
5225 |                 # Amazon data extraction and analysis
5226 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
5227 |                 if not amazon_data or "error" in amazon_data:
5228 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
5229 |                     
5230 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for batch processing
5231 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
5232 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
5233 |                         supplier_ean = None
5234 |                         
5235 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
5236 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
5237 |                     no_match_reason = f"Batch processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
5238 |                     
5239 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
5240 |                     no_match_entry = {
5241 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
5242 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
5243 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
5244 |                         "amazon_title": None,
5245 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
5246 |                         "amazon_price": None,
5247 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
5248 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
5249 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
5250 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
5251 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
5252 |                     }
5253 |                     
5254 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
5255 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
5256 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for batch product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
5257 |                     
5258 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
5259 |                     continue
5260 |                 
5261 |                 # Save Amazon cache and create linking map
5262 |                 supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
5263 |                 asin = amazon_data.get("asin", "NO_ASIN")
5264 |                 
5265 |                 # Save Amazon data
5266 |                 filename_identifier = supplier_ean if supplier_ean else re.sub(r'[<>:"/\\|?*\s]+', '_', product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50])
5267 |                 amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
5268 |                 with open(amazon_cache_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
5269 |                     json.dump(amazon_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
5270 |                 
5271 |                 # Financial analysis
5272 |                 try:
5273 |                     # Already imported at top of file: from FBA_Financial_calculator import financials as calc_financials
5274 |                     supplier_price = float(product_data.get("price", 0))
5275 |                     current_price = amazon_data.get("current_price", 0)
5276 |                     
5277 |                     if supplier_price > 0 and current_price > 0:
5278 |                         financials = calc_financials(
5279 |                             supplier_price=supplier_price,
5280 |                             amazon_price=current_price,
5281 |                             amazon_sales_rank=amazon_data.get("sales_rank", 999999),
5282 |                             amazon_rating=amazon_data.get("rating", 0),
5283 |                             amazon_review_count=amazon_data.get("reviews", 0)
5284 |                         )
5285 |                         
5286 |                         # Check profitability
5287 |                         if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
5288 |                             self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE: ROI {financials['ROI']:.1f}%, Profit £{financials['NetProfit']:.2f}")
5289 |                             
5290 |                             combined_data = self._combine_supplier_amazon_data(product_data, amazon_data, "hybrid_batch")
5291 |                             combined_data["financials"] = financials
5292 |                             profitable_results.append(combined_data)
5293 |                             
5294 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, True, financials.get("NetProfit", 0))
5295 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
5296 |                         else:
5297 |                             self.log.info(f"Not profitable: ROI {financials.get('ROI', 0):.1f}%, Profit £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f}")
5298 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, False)
5299 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
5300 |                             
5301 |                 except Exception as e:
5302 |                     self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed: {e}")
5303 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
5304 |         
5305 |         return profitable_results
5306 | 
5307 |     def _get_cached_products_path(self, category_url: str):
5308 |         """Helper to get the path for a category's product cache file."""
5309 |         category_filename = f"{self.supplier_name}_{category_url.split('/')[-1]}_products.json"
5310 |         return str(path_manager.get_cache_path('supplier_cache', category_filename))
5311 | 
5312 |     def _combine_data(self, supplier_data: Dict[str, Any], amazon_data: Dict[str, Any], session_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
5313 |         """Combine supplier and Amazon data and calculate financial metrics."""
5314 |         combined = {
5315 |             "supplier_product_info": supplier_data,
5316 |             "amazon_product_info": amazon_data,
5317 |             "analysis_timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
5318 |             "session_id": session_id
5319 |         }
5320 |         # Financial calculation is now handled within the main run loop before this function is called
5321 |         # This function is primarily for combining data and adding match validation
5322 |         match_validation = self._validate_product_match(supplier_data, amazon_data)
5323 |         combined["match_validation"] = match_validation
5324 |         return combined
5325 | 
5326 |     def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
5327 |         """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
5328 |         a = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_a.lower()).split())
5329 |         b = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_b.lower()).split())
5330 |         return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
5331 |     
5332 |     def _sanitize_filename(self, title: str) -> str:
5333 |         """Sanitize product title for use in filename"""
5334 |         if not title:
5335 |             return "unknown_title"
5336 |         # Remove or replace problematic characters
5337 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', title)
5338 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'\s+', '_', sanitized)
5339 |         return sanitized[:50]  # Limit length to 50 chars
5340 | 
5341 |     def _validate_product_match(self, supplier_product: Dict[str, Any], amazon_product: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
5342 |         """Validate the match between supplier and Amazon products using configurable thresholds."""
5343 |         matching_thresholds = self.system_config.get("performance", {}).get("matching_thresholds", {})
5344 |         
5345 |         # Get configurable thresholds with fallback to more conservative defaults
5346 |         title_similarity_threshold = matching_thresholds.get("title_similarity", 0.25)
5347 |         medium_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("medium_title_similarity", 0.5) 
5348 |         high_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("high_title_similarity", 0.75)
5349 |         
5350 |         title_overlap_score = self._overlap_score(supplier_product.get("title", ""), amazon_product.get("title", ""))
5351 | 
5352 |         match_quality = "low"
5353 |         confidence = 0.0
5354 |         
5355 |         # Use configurable thresholds - much more strict than previous hardcoded values
5356 |         if title_overlap_score >= high_title_similarity:
5357 |             match_quality = "high"
5358 |             confidence = 0.9
5359 |         elif title_overlap_score >= medium_title_similarity:
5360 |             match_quality = "medium"
5361 |             confidence = 0.6
5362 |         elif title_overlap_score >= title_similarity_threshold:
5363 |             match_quality = "low"
5364 |             confidence = 0.3
5365 |         else:
5366 |             match_quality = "very_low"
5367 |             confidence = 0.1
5368 | 
5369 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 MATCH VALIDATION: '{supplier_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' vs '{amazon_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' = {title_overlap_score:.2f} ({match_quality}, {confidence:.1%})")
5370 |         
5371 |         return {"match_quality": match_quality, "confidence": confidence, "title_overlap_score": title_overlap_score}
5372 | 
5373 |     def _is_product_meeting_criteria(self, combined_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
5374 |         """Check if a product meets all defined business criteria."""
5375 |         # This function is now largely redundant as profitability checks are done in the main loop.
5376 |         # However, it can be used for other criteria like sales rank, reviews, etc.
5377 |         amazon_data = combined_data.get("amazon_product_info", {})
5378 |         financials = combined_data.get("financials", {})
5379 |         # Check profitability (already done, but for completeness)
5380 |         is_profitable = financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT
5381 |         # Check Amazon listing quality
5382 |         meets_rating = amazon_data.get("rating", 0) >= MIN_RATING
5383 |         meets_reviews = amazon_data.get("reviews", 0) >= MIN_REVIEWS
5384 |         meets_sales_rank = amazon_data.get("sales_rank", MAX_SALES_RANK + 1) <= MAX_SALES_RANK
5385 |         # Check for battery products (using the helper function)
5386 |         is_battery = is_battery_title(amazon_data.get("title", "")) or is_battery_title(combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", ""))
5387 |         # Check for other non-FBA friendly keywords
5388 |         is_non_fba_friendly = any(k in amazon_data.get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS) or \
5389 |                               any(k in combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS)
5390 |         # All criteria must be met
5391 |         return is_profitable and meets_rating and meets_reviews and meets_sales_rank and not is_battery and not is_non_fba_friendly
5392 | 
5393 |     def _apply_batch_synchronization(self, max_products_per_cycle: int, 
5394 |                                    supplier_extraction_batch_size: int,
5395 |                                    batch_sync_config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[int, int]:
5396 |         """
5397 |         Apply batch synchronization to align all batch sizes consistently.
5398 |         Returns updated max_products_per_cycle and supplier_extraction_batch_size.
5399 |         """
5400 |         target_batch_size = batch_sync_config.get("target_batch_size", 3)
5401 |         synchronize_all = batch_sync_config.get("synchronize_all_batch_sizes", False)
5402 |         validation_config = batch_sync_config.get("validation", {})
5403 |         
5404 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 BATCH SYNCHRONIZATION: Enabled")
5405 |         self.log.info(f"   target_batch_size: {target_batch_size}")
5406 |         self.log.info(f"   synchronize_all_batch_sizes: {synchronize_all}")
5407 |         
5408 |         original_values = {
5409 |             "max_products_per_cycle": max_products_per_cycle,
5410 |             "supplier_extraction_batch_size": supplier_extraction_batch_size,
5411 |             "linking_map_batch_size": self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1),
5412 |             "financial_report_batch_size": self.system_config.get("financial_report_batch_size", 2)
5413 |         }
5414 |         
5415 |         # Check for mismatched sizes and warn if configured
5416 |         if validation_config.get("warn_on_mismatched_sizes", True):
5417 |             unique_sizes = set(original_values.values())
5418 |             if len(unique_sizes) > 1:
5419 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ BATCH SYNC WARNING: Mismatched batch sizes detected: {original_values}")
5420 |                 self.log.warning(f"   Unique sizes found: {sorted(unique_sizes)}")
5421 |         
5422 |         if synchronize_all:
5423 |             # Synchronize all batch sizes to target
5424 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
5425 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
5426 |             
5427 |             # Also update system config values in memory for consistency
5428 |             if "system" in self.system_config:
5429 |                 self.system_config["max_products_per_cycle"] = target_batch_size
5430 |                 self.system_config["supplier_extraction_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5431 |                 self.system_config["linking_map_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5432 |                 self.system_config["financial_report_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5433 |             
5434 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: All batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
5435 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
5436 |             
5437 |         else:
5438 |             # Only synchronize the two main processing batch sizes
5439 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
5440 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
5441 |             
5442 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: Main processing batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
5443 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
5444 |         
5445 |         return new_max_products_per_cycle, new_supplier_extraction_batch_size
5446 | 
5447 |     async def _analyze_products_batch(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], 
5448 |                                     supplier_name: str, max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
5449 |         """Analyze a batch of supplier products for Amazon matching and profitability."""
5450 |         profitable_results = []
5451 |         
5452 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis
5453 |         valid_products = [
5454 |             p for p in products
5455 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
5456 |         ]
5457 |         
5458 |         price_filtered_products = [
5459 |             p for p in valid_products
5460 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
5461 |         ]
5462 |         
5463 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(price_filtered_products)} products in batch mode")
5464 |         
5465 |         # Process products in cycles
5466 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
5467 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
5468 |         
5469 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
5470 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
5471 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
5472 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
5473 |             
5474 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing analysis batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
