5475 |             
5476 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
5477 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
5478 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing product {current_index}: '{product_data.get('title')}'")
5479 |                 
5480 |                 # Check if already processed
5481 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
5482 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
5483 |                     continue
5484 |                 
5485 |                 # Amazon data extraction and analysis
5486 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
5487 |                 if not amazon_data or "error" in amazon_data:
5488 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
5489 |                     
5490 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for batch processing
5491 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
5492 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
5493 |                         supplier_ean = None
5494 |                         
5495 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
5496 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
5497 |                     no_match_reason = f"Batch processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
5498 |                     
5499 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
5500 |                     no_match_entry = {
5501 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
5502 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
5503 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
5504 |                         "amazon_title": None,
5505 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
5506 |                         "amazon_price": None,
5507 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
5508 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
5509 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
5510 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
5511 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
5512 |                     }
5513 |                     
5514 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
5515 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
5516 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for batch product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
5517 |                     
5518 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
5519 |                     continue
5520 |                 
5521 |                 # Save Amazon cache and create linking map
5522 |                 supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
5523 |                 asin = amazon_data.get("asin", "NO_ASIN")
5524 |                 
5525 |                 # Save Amazon data
5526 |                 filename_identifier = supplier_ean if supplier_ean else re.sub(r'[<>:"/\\|?*\s]+', '_', product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50])
5527 |                 amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
5528 |                 with open(amazon_cache_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
5529 |                     json.dump(amazon_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
5530 |                 
5531 |                 # Financial analysis
5532 |                 try:
5533 |                     # Already imported at top of file: from FBA_Financial_calculator import financials as calc_financials
5534 |                     supplier_price = float(product_data.get("price", 0))
5535 |                     current_price = amazon_data.get("current_price", 0)
5536 |                     
5537 |                     if supplier_price > 0 and current_price > 0:
5538 |                         financials = calc_financials(
5539 |                             supplier_price=supplier_price,
5540 |                             amazon_price=current_price,
5541 |                             amazon_sales_rank=amazon_data.get("sales_rank", 999999),
5542 |                             amazon_rating=amazon_data.get("rating", 0),
5543 |                             amazon_review_count=amazon_data.get("reviews", 0)
5544 |                         )
5545 |                         
5546 |                         # Check profitability
5547 |                         if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
5548 |                             self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE: ROI {financials['ROI']:.1f}%, Profit £{financials['NetProfit']:.2f}")
5549 |                             
5550 |                             combined_data = self._combine_supplier_amazon_data(product_data, amazon_data, "hybrid_batch")
5551 |                             combined_data["financials"] = financials
5552 |                             profitable_results.append(combined_data)
5553 |                             
5554 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, True, financials.get("NetProfit", 0))
5555 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
5556 |                         else:
5557 |                             self.log.info(f"Not profitable: ROI {financials.get('ROI', 0):.1f}%, Profit £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f}")
5558 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, False)
5559 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
5560 |                             
5561 |                 except Exception as e:
5562 |                     self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed: {e}")
5563 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
5564 |         
5565 |         return profitable_results
5566 | 
5567 |     def _get_cached_products_path(self, category_url: str):
5568 |         """Helper to get the path for a category's product cache file."""
5569 |         category_filename = f"{self.supplier_name}_{category_url.split('/')[-1]}_products.json"
5570 |         return str(path_manager.get_cache_path('supplier_cache', category_filename))
5571 | 
5572 |     def _combine_data(self, supplier_data: Dict[str, Any], amazon_data: Dict[str, Any], session_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
5573 |         """Combine supplier and Amazon data and calculate financial metrics."""
5574 |         combined = {
5575 |             "supplier_product_info": supplier_data,
5576 |             "amazon_product_info": amazon_data,
5577 |             "analysis_timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
5578 |             "session_id": session_id
5579 |         }
5580 |         # Financial calculation is now handled within the main run loop before this function is called
5581 |         # This function is primarily for combining data and adding match validation
5582 |         match_validation = self._validate_product_match(supplier_data, amazon_data)
5583 |         combined["match_validation"] = match_validation
5584 |         return combined
5585 | 
5586 |     def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
5587 |         """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
5588 |         a = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_a.lower()).split())
5589 |         b = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_b.lower()).split())
5590 |         return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
5591 |     
5592 |     def _sanitize_filename(self, title: str) -> str:
5593 |         """Sanitize product title for use in filename"""
5594 |         if not title:
5595 |             return "unknown_title"
5596 |         # Remove or replace problematic characters
5597 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', title)
5598 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'\s+', '_', sanitized)
5599 |         return sanitized[:50]  # Limit length to 50 chars
5600 | 
5601 |     def _validate_product_match(self, supplier_product: Dict[str, Any], amazon_product: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
5602 |         """Validate the match between supplier and Amazon products using configurable thresholds."""
5603 |         matching_thresholds = self.system_config.get("performance", {}).get("matching_thresholds", {})
5604 |         
5605 |         # Get configurable thresholds with fallback to more conservative defaults
5606 |         title_similarity_threshold = matching_thresholds.get("title_similarity", 0.25)
5607 |         medium_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("medium_title_similarity", 0.5) 
5608 |         high_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("high_title_similarity", 0.75)
5609 |         
5610 |         title_overlap_score = self._overlap_score(supplier_product.get("title", ""), amazon_product.get("title", ""))
5611 | 
5612 |         match_quality = "low"
5613 |         confidence = 0.0
5614 |         
5615 |         # Use configurable thresholds - much more strict than previous hardcoded values
5616 |         if title_overlap_score >= high_title_similarity:
5617 |             match_quality = "high"
5618 |             confidence = 0.9
5619 |         elif title_overlap_score >= medium_title_similarity:
5620 |             match_quality = "medium"
5621 |             confidence = 0.6
5622 |         elif title_overlap_score >= title_similarity_threshold:
5623 |             match_quality = "low"
5624 |             confidence = 0.3
5625 |         else:
5626 |             match_quality = "very_low"
5627 |             confidence = 0.1
5628 | 
5629 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 MATCH VALIDATION: '{supplier_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' vs '{amazon_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' = {title_overlap_score:.2f} ({match_quality}, {confidence:.1%})")
5630 |         
5631 |         return {"match_quality": match_quality, "confidence": confidence, "title_overlap_score": title_overlap_score}
5632 | 
5633 |     def _is_product_meeting_criteria(self, combined_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
5634 |         """Check if a product meets all defined business criteria."""
5635 |         # This function is now largely redundant as profitability checks are done in the main loop.
5636 |         # However, it can be used for other criteria like sales rank, reviews, etc.
5637 |         amazon_data = combined_data.get("amazon_product_info", {})
5638 |         financials = combined_data.get("financials", {})
5639 |         # Check profitability (already done, but for completeness)
5640 |         is_profitable = financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT
5641 |         # Check Amazon listing quality
5642 |         meets_rating = amazon_data.get("rating", 0) >= MIN_RATING
5643 |         meets_reviews = amazon_data.get("reviews", 0) >= MIN_REVIEWS
5644 |         meets_sales_rank = amazon_data.get("sales_rank", MAX_SALES_RANK + 1) <= MAX_SALES_RANK
5645 |         # Check for battery products (using the helper function)
5646 |         is_battery = is_battery_title(amazon_data.get("title", "")) or is_battery_title(combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", ""))
5647 |         # Check for other non-FBA friendly keywords
5648 |         is_non_fba_friendly = any(k in amazon_data.get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS) or \
5649 |                               any(k in combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS)
5650 |         # All criteria must be met
5651 |         return is_profitable and meets_rating and meets_reviews and meets_sales_rank and not is_battery and not is_non_fba_friendly
5652 | 
5653 |     def _apply_batch_synchronization(self, max_products_per_cycle: int, 
5654 |                                    supplier_extraction_batch_size: int,
5655 |                                    batch_sync_config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[int, int]:
5656 |         """
5657 |         Apply batch synchronization to align all batch sizes consistently.
5658 |         Returns updated max_products_per_cycle and supplier_extraction_batch_size.
5659 |         """
5660 |         target_batch_size = batch_sync_config.get("target_batch_size", 3)
5661 |         synchronize_all = batch_sync_config.get("synchronize_all_batch_sizes", False)
5662 |         validation_config = batch_sync_config.get("validation", {})
5663 |         
5664 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 BATCH SYNCHRONIZATION: Enabled")
5665 |         self.log.info(f"   target_batch_size: {target_batch_size}")
5666 |         self.log.info(f"   synchronize_all_batch_sizes: {synchronize_all}")
5667 |         
5668 |         original_values = {
5669 |             "max_products_per_cycle": max_products_per_cycle,
5670 |             "supplier_extraction_batch_size": supplier_extraction_batch_size,
5671 |             "linking_map_batch_size": self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1),
5672 |             "financial_report_batch_size": self.system_config.get("financial_report_batch_size", 2)
5673 |         }
5674 |         
5675 |         # Check for mismatched sizes and warn if configured
5676 |         if validation_config.get("warn_on_mismatched_sizes", True):
5677 |             unique_sizes = set(original_values.values())
5678 |             if len(unique_sizes) > 1:
5679 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ BATCH SYNC WARNING: Mismatched batch sizes detected: {original_values}")
5680 |                 self.log.warning(f"   Unique sizes found: {sorted(unique_sizes)}")
5681 |         
5682 |         if synchronize_all:
5683 |             # Synchronize all batch sizes to target
5684 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
5685 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
5686 |             
5687 |             # Also update system config values in memory for consistency
5688 |             if "system" in self.system_config:
5689 |                 self.system_config["max_products_per_cycle"] = target_batch_size
5690 |                 self.system_config["supplier_extraction_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5691 |                 self.system_config["linking_map_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5692 |                 self.system_config["financial_report_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5693 |             
5694 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: All batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
5695 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
5696 |             
5697 |         else:
5698 |             # Only synchronize the two main processing batch sizes
5699 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
5700 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
5701 |             
5702 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: Main processing batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
5703 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
5704 |         
5705 |         return new_max_products_per_cycle, new_supplier_extraction_batch_size
5706 | 
5707 |     async def _analyze_products_batch(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], 
5708 |                                     supplier_name: str, max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
5709 |         """Analyze a batch of supplier products for Amazon matching and profitability."""
5710 |         profitable_results = []
5711 |         
5712 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis
5713 |         valid_products = [
5714 |             p for p in products
5715 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
5716 |         ]
5717 |         
5718 |         price_filtered_products = [
5719 |             p for p in valid_products
5720 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
5721 |         ]
5722 |         
5723 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(price_filtered_products)} products in batch mode")
5724 |         
5725 |         # Process products in cycles
5726 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
5727 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
5728 |         
5729 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
5730 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
5731 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
5732 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
5733 |             
5734 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing analysis batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
5735 |             
5736 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
5737 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
5738 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing product {current_index}: '{product_data.get('title')}'")
5739 |                 
5740 |                 # Check if already processed
5741 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
5742 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
5743 |                     continue
5744 |                 
5745 |                 # Amazon data extraction and analysis
5746 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
5747 |                 if not amazon_data or "error" in amazon_data:
5748 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
5749 |                     
5750 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for batch processing
5751 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
5752 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
5753 |                         supplier_ean = None
5754 |                         
5755 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
5756 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
5757 |                     no_match_reason = f"Batch processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
5758 |                     
5759 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
5760 |                     no_match_entry = {
5761 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
5762 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
5763 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
5764 |                         "amazon_title": None,
5765 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
5766 |                         "amazon_price": None,
5767 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
5768 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
5769 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
5770 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
5771 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
5772 |                     }
5773 |                     
5774 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
5775 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
5776 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for batch product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
5777 |                     
5778 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
5779 |                     continue
5780 |                 
5781 |                 # Save Amazon cache and create linking map
5782 |                 supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
5783 |                 asin = amazon_data.get("asin", "NO_ASIN")
5784 |                 
5785 |                 # Save Amazon data
5786 |                 filename_identifier = supplier_ean if supplier_ean else re.sub(r'[<>:"/\\|?*\s]+', '_', product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50])
5787 |                 amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
5788 |                 with open(amazon_cache_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
5789 |                     json.dump(amazon_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
5790 |                 
5791 |                 # Financial analysis
5792 |                 try:
5793 |                     # Already imported at top of file: from FBA_Financial_calculator import financials as calc_financials
5794 |                     supplier_price = float(product_data.get("price", 0))
5795 |                     current_price = amazon_data.get("current_price", 0)
5796 |                     
5797 |                     if supplier_price > 0 and current_price > 0:
5798 |                         financials = calc_financials(
5799 |                             supplier_price=supplier_price,
5800 |                             amazon_price=current_price,
5801 |                             amazon_sales_rank=amazon_data.get("sales_rank", 999999),
5802 |                             amazon_rating=amazon_data.get("rating", 0),
5803 |                             amazon_review_count=amazon_data.get("reviews", 0)
5804 |                         )
5805 |                         
5806 |                         # Check profitability
5807 |                         if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
5808 |                             self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE: ROI {financials['ROI']:.1f}%, Profit £{financials['NetProfit']:.2f}")
5809 |                             
5810 |                             combined_data = self._combine_supplier_amazon_data(product_data, amazon_data, "hybrid_batch")
5811 |                             combined_data["financials"] = financials
5812 |                             profitable_results.append(combined_data)
5813 |                             
5814 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, True, financials.get("NetProfit", 0))
5815 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
5816 |                         else:
5817 |                             self.log.info(f"Not profitable: ROI {financials.get('ROI', 0):.1f}%, Profit £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f}")
5818 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, False)
5819 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
5820 |                             
5821 |                 except Exception as e:
5822 |                     self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed: {e}")
5823 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
5824 |         
5825 |         return profitable_results
5826 | 
5827 |     def _get_cached_products_path(self, category_url: str):
5828 |         """Helper to get the path for a category's product cache file."""
5829 |         category_filename = f"{self.supplier_name}_{category_url.split('/')[-1]}_products.json"
5830 |         return str(path_manager.get_cache_path('supplier_cache', category_filename))
5831 | 
5832 |     def _combine_data(self, supplier_data: Dict[str, Any], amazon_data: Dict[str, Any], session_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
5833 |         """Combine supplier and Amazon data and calculate financial metrics."""
5834 |         combined = {
5835 |             "supplier_product_info": supplier_data,
5836 |             "amazon_product_info": amazon_data,
5837 |             "analysis_timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
5838 |             "session_id": session_id
5839 |         }
5840 |         # Financial calculation is now handled within the main run loop before this function is called
5841 |         # This function is primarily for combining data and adding match validation
5842 |         match_validation = self._validate_product_match(supplier_data, amazon_data)
5843 |         combined["match_validation"] = match_validation
5844 |         return combined
5845 | 
5846 |     def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
5847 |         """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
5848 |         a = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_a.lower()).split())
5849 |         b = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_b.lower()).split())
5850 |         return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
5851 |     
5852 |     def _sanitize_filename(self, title: str) -> str:
5853 |         """Sanitize product title for use in filename"""
5854 |         if not title:
5855 |             return "unknown_title"
5856 |         # Remove or replace problematic characters
5857 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', title)
5858 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'\s+', '_', sanitized)
5859 |         return sanitized[:50]  # Limit length to 50 chars
5860 | 
5861 |     def _validate_product_match(self, supplier_product: Dict[str, Any], amazon_product: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
5862 |         """Validate the match between supplier and Amazon products using configurable thresholds."""
5863 |         matching_thresholds = self.system_config.get("performance", {}).get("matching_thresholds", {})
5864 |         
5865 |         # Get configurable thresholds with fallback to more conservative defaults
5866 |         title_similarity_threshold = matching_thresholds.get("title_similarity", 0.25)
5867 |         medium_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("medium_title_similarity", 0.5) 
5868 |         high_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("high_title_similarity", 0.75)
5869 |         
5870 |         title_overlap_score = self._overlap_score(supplier_product.get("title", ""), amazon_product.get("title", ""))
5871 | 
5872 |         match_quality = "low"
5873 |         confidence = 0.0
5874 |         
5875 |         # Use configurable thresholds - much more strict than previous hardcoded values
5876 |         if title_overlap_score >= high_title_similarity:
5877 |             match_quality = "high"
5878 |             confidence = 0.9
5879 |         elif title_overlap_score >= medium_title_similarity:
5880 |             match_quality = "medium"
5881 |             confidence = 0.6
5882 |         elif title_overlap_score >= title_similarity_threshold:
5883 |             match_quality = "low"
5884 |             confidence = 0.3
5885 |         else:
5886 |             match_quality = "very_low"
5887 |             confidence = 0.1
5888 | 
5889 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 MATCH VALIDATION: '{supplier_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' vs '{amazon_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' = {title_overlap_score:.2f} ({match_quality}, {confidence:.1%})")
5890 |         
5891 |         return {"match_quality": match_quality, "confidence": confidence, "title_overlap_score": title_overlap_score}
5892 | 
5893 |     def _is_product_meeting_criteria(self, combined_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
5894 |         """Check if a product meets all defined business criteria."""
5895 |         # This function is now largely redundant as profitability checks are done in the main loop.
5896 |         # However, it can be used for other criteria like sales rank, reviews, etc.
5897 |         amazon_data = combined_data.get("amazon_product_info", {})
5898 |         financials = combined_data.get("financials", {})
5899 |         # Check profitability (already done, but for completeness)
5900 |         is_profitable = financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT
5901 |         # Check Amazon listing quality
5902 |         meets_rating = amazon_data.get("rating", 0) >= MIN_RATING
5903 |         meets_reviews = amazon_data.get("reviews", 0) >= MIN_REVIEWS
5904 |         meets_sales_rank = amazon_data.get("sales_rank", MAX_SALES_RANK + 1) <= MAX_SALES_RANK
5905 |         # Check for battery products (using the helper function)
5906 |         is_battery = is_battery_title(amazon_data.get("title", "")) or is_battery_title(combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", ""))
5907 |         # Check for other non-FBA friendly keywords
5908 |         is_non_fba_friendly = any(k in amazon_data.get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS) or \
5909 |                               any(k in combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS)
5910 |         # All criteria must be met
5911 |         return is_profitable and meets_rating and meets_reviews and meets_sales_rank and not is_battery and not is_non_fba_friendly
5912 | 
5913 |     def _apply_batch_synchronization(self, max_products_per_cycle: int, 
5914 |                                    supplier_extraction_batch_size: int,
5915 |                                    batch_sync_config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[int, int]:
5916 |         """
5917 |         Apply batch synchronization to align all batch sizes consistently.
5918 |         Returns updated max_products_per_cycle and supplier_extraction_batch_size.
5919 |         """
5920 |         target_batch_size = batch_sync_config.get("target_batch_size", 3)
5921 |         synchronize_all = batch_sync_config.get("synchronize_all_batch_sizes", False)
5922 |         validation_config = batch_sync_config.get("validation", {})
5923 |         
5924 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 BATCH SYNCHRONIZATION: Enabled")
5925 |         self.log.info(f"   target_batch_size: {target_batch_size}")
5926 |         self.log.info(f"   synchronize_all_batch_sizes: {synchronize_all}")
5927 |         
5928 |         original_values = {
5929 |             "max_products_per_cycle": max_products_per_cycle,
5930 |             "supplier_extraction_batch_size": supplier_extraction_batch_size,
5931 |             "linking_map_batch_size": self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1),
5932 |             "financial_report_batch_size": self.system_config.get("financial_report_batch_size", 2)
5933 |         }
5934 |         
5935 |         # Check for mismatched sizes and warn if configured
5936 |         if validation_config.get("warn_on_mismatched_sizes", True):
5937 |             unique_sizes = set(original_values.values())
5938 |             if len(unique_sizes) > 1:
5939 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ BATCH SYNC WARNING: Mismatched batch sizes detected: {original_values}")
5940 |                 self.log.warning(f"   Unique sizes found: {sorted(unique_sizes)}")
5941 |         
5942 |         if synchronize_all:
5943 |             # Synchronize all batch sizes to target
5944 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
5945 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
5946 |             
5947 |             # Also update system config values in memory for consistency
5948 |             if "system" in self.system_config:
5949 |                 self.system_config["max_products_per_cycle"] = target_batch_size
5950 |                 self.system_config["supplier_extraction_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5951 |                 self.system_config["linking_map_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5952 |                 self.system_config["financial_report_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
5953 |             
5954 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: All batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
5955 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
5956 |             
5957 |         else:
5958 |             # Only synchronize the two main processing batch sizes
5959 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
5960 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
5961 |             
5962 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: Main processing batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
5963 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
5964 |         
5965 |         return new_max_products_per_cycle, new_supplier_extraction_batch_size
5966 | 
5967 |     async def _analyze_products_batch(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], 
5968 |                                     supplier_name: str, max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
5969 |         """Analyze a batch of supplier products for Amazon matching and profitability."""
5970 |         profitable_results = []
5971 |         
5972 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis
5973 |         valid_products = [
5974 |             p for p in products
5975 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
5976 |         ]
5977 |         
5978 |         price_filtered_products = [
5979 |             p for p in valid_products
5980 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
5981 |         ]
5982 |         
5983 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(price_filtered_products)} products in batch mode")
5984 |         
5985 |         # Process products in cycles
5986 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
5987 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
5988 |         
5989 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
5990 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
5991 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
5992 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
5993 |             
5994 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing analysis batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
5995 |             
5996 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
5997 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
5998 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing product {current_index}: '{product_data.get('title')}'")
5999 |                 
6000 |                 # Check if already processed
6001 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
6002 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
6003 |                     continue
6004 |                 
6005 |                 # Amazon data extraction and analysis
6006 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
6007 |                 if not amazon_data or "error" in amazon_data:
6008 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
6009 |                     
6010 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for batch processing
6011 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
6012 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
6013 |                         supplier_ean = None
6014 |                         
6015 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
6016 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
6017 |                     no_match_reason = f"Batch processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
6018 |                     
6019 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
6020 |                     no_match_entry = {
6021 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
6022 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
6023 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
6024 |                         "amazon_title": None,
6025 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
6026 |                         "amazon_price": None,
6027 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
6028 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
6029 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
6030 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
6031 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
6032 |                     }
6033 |                     
6034 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
6035 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
6036 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for batch product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
6037 |                     
6038 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
6039 |                     continue
6040 |                 
6041 |                 # Save Amazon cache and create linking map
6042 |                 supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
6043 |                 asin = amazon_data.get("asin", "NO_ASIN")
6044 |                 
6045 |                 # Save Amazon data
6046 |                 filename_identifier = supplier_ean if supplier_ean else re.sub(r'[<>:"/\\|?*\s]+', '_', product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50])
6047 |                 amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
6048 |                 with open(amazon_cache_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
6049 |                     json.dump(amazon_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
6050 |                 
6051 |                 # Financial analysis
6052 |                 try:
6053 |                     # Already imported at top of file: from FBA_Financial_calculator import financials as calc_financials
6054 |                     supplier_price = float(product_data.get("price", 0))
6055 |                     current_price = amazon_data.get("current_price", 0)
6056 |                     
6057 |                     if supplier_price > 0 and current_price > 0:
6058 |                         financials = calc_financials(
6059 |                             supplier_price=supplier_price,
6060 |                             amazon_price=current_price,
6061 |                             amazon_sales_rank=amazon_data.get("sales_rank", 999999),
6062 |                             amazon_rating=amazon_data.get("rating", 0),
6063 |                             amazon_review_count=amazon_data.get("reviews", 0)
6064 |                         )
6065 |                         
6066 |                         # Check profitability
6067 |                         if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
6068 |                             self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE: ROI {financials['ROI']:.1f}%, Profit £{financials['NetProfit']:.2f}")
6069 |                             
6070 |                             combined_data = self._combine_supplier_amazon_data(product_data, amazon_data, "hybrid_batch")
6071 |                             combined_data["financials"] = financials
6072 |                             profitable_results.append(combined_data)
6073 |                             
6074 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, True, financials.get("NetProfit", 0))
6075 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
6076 |                         else:
6077 |                             self.log.info(f"Not profitable: ROI {financials.get('ROI', 0):.1f}%, Profit £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f}")
6078 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, False)
6079 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
6080 |                             
6081 |                 except Exception as e:
6082 |                     self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed: {e}")
6083 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
6084 |         
6085 |         return profitable_results
6086 | 
6087 |     def _get_cached_products_path(self, category_url: str):
6088 |         """Helper to get the path for a category's product cache file."""
6089 |         category_filename = f"{self.supplier_name}_{category_url.split('/')[-1]}_products.json"
6090 |         return str(path_manager.get_cache_path('supplier_cache', category_filename))
6091 | 
6092 |     def _combine_data(self, supplier_data: Dict[str, Any], amazon_data: Dict[str, Any], session_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
6093 |         """Combine supplier and Amazon data and calculate financial metrics."""
6094 |         combined = {
6095 |             "supplier_product_info": supplier_data,
6096 |             "amazon_product_info": amazon_data,
6097 |             "analysis_timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
6098 |             "session_id": session_id
6099 |         }
6100 |         # Financial calculation is now handled within the main run loop before this function is called
6101 |         # This function is primarily for combining data and adding match validation
6102 |         match_validation = self._validate_product_match(supplier_data, amazon_data)
6103 |         combined["match_validation"] = match_validation
6104 |         return combined
6105 | 
6106 |     def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
6107 |         """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
6108 |         a = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_a.lower()).split())
6109 |         b = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_b.lower()).split())
6110 |         return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
6111 |     
6112 |     def _sanitize_filename(self, title: str) -> str:
6113 |         """Sanitize product title for use in filename"""
6114 |         if not title:
6115 |             return "unknown_title"
6116 |         # Remove or replace problematic characters
6117 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', title)
6118 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'\s+', '_', sanitized)
6119 |         return sanitized[:50]  # Limit length to 50 chars
6120 | 
6121 |     def _validate_product_match(self, supplier_product: Dict[str, Any], amazon_product: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
6122 |         """Validate the match between supplier and Amazon products using configurable thresholds."""
6123 |         matching_thresholds = self.system_config.get("performance", {}).get("matching_thresholds", {})
6124 |         
6125 |         # Get configurable thresholds with fallback to more conservative defaults
6126 |         title_similarity_threshold = matching_thresholds.get("title_similarity", 0.25)
6127 |         medium_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("medium_title_similarity", 0.5) 
6128 |         high_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("high_title_similarity", 0.75)
6129 |         
6130 |         title_overlap_score = self._overlap_score(supplier_product.get("title", ""), amazon_product.get("title", ""))
6131 | 
6132 |         match_quality = "low"
6133 |         confidence = 0.0
6134 |         
6135 |         # Use configurable thresholds - much more strict than previous hardcoded values
6136 |         if title_overlap_score >= high_title_similarity:
6137 |             match_quality = "high"
6138 |             confidence = 0.9
6139 |         elif title_overlap_score >= medium_title_similarity:
6140 |             match_quality = "medium"
6141 |             confidence = 0.6
6142 |         elif title_overlap_score >= title_similarity_threshold:
6143 |             match_quality = "low"
6144 |             confidence = 0.3
6145 |         else:
6146 |             match_quality = "very_low"
6147 |             confidence = 0.1
6148 | 
6149 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 MATCH VALIDATION: '{supplier_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' vs '{amazon_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' = {title_overlap_score:.2f} ({match_quality}, {confidence:.1%})")
6150 |         
6151 |         return {"match_quality": match_quality, "confidence": confidence, "title_overlap_score": title_overlap_score}
6152 | 
6153 |     def _is_product_meeting_criteria(self, combined_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
6154 |         """Check if a product meets all defined business criteria."""
6155 |         # This function is now largely redundant as profitability checks are done in the main loop.
6156 |         # However, it can be used for other criteria like sales rank, reviews, etc.
6157 |         amazon_data = combined_data.get("amazon_product_info", {})
6158 |         financials = combined_data.get("financials", {})
6159 |         # Check profitability (already done, but for completeness)
6160 |         is_profitable = financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT
6161 |         # Check Amazon listing quality
6162 |         meets_rating = amazon_data.get("rating", 0) >= MIN_RATING
6163 |         meets_reviews = amazon_data.get("reviews", 0) >= MIN_REVIEWS
6164 |         meets_sales_rank = amazon_data.get("sales_rank", MAX_SALES_RANK + 1) <= MAX_SALES_RANK
6165 |         # Check for battery products (using the helper function)
6166 |         is_battery = is_battery_title(amazon_data.get("title", "")) or is_battery_title(combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", ""))
6167 |         # Check for other non-FBA friendly keywords
6168 |         is_non_fba_friendly = any(k in amazon_data.get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS) or \
6169 |                               any(k in combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS)
6170 |         # All criteria must be met
6171 |         return is_profitable and meets_rating and meets_reviews and meets_sales_rank and not is_battery and not is_non_fba_friendly
6172 | 
6173 |     def _apply_batch_synchronization(self, max_products_per_cycle: int, 
6174 |                                    supplier_extraction_batch_size: int,
6175 |                                    batch_sync_config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[int, int]:
6176 |         """
6177 |         Apply batch synchronization to align all batch sizes consistently.
6178 |         Returns updated max_products_per_cycle and supplier_extraction_batch_size.
6179 |         """
6180 |         target_batch_size = batch_sync_config.get("target_batch_size", 3)
6181 |         synchronize_all = batch_sync_config.get("synchronize_all_batch_sizes", False)
6182 |         validation_config = batch_sync_config.get("validation", {})
6183 |         
6184 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 BATCH SYNCHRONIZATION: Enabled")
6185 |         self.log.info(f"   target_batch_size: {target_batch_size}")
6186 |         self.log.info(f"   synchronize_all_batch_sizes: {synchronize_all}")
6187 |         
6188 |         original_values = {
6189 |             "max_products_per_cycle": max_products_per_cycle,
6190 |             "supplier_extraction_batch_size": supplier_extraction_batch_size,
6191 |             "linking_map_batch_size": self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1),
6192 |             "financial_report_batch_size": self.system_config.get("financial_report_batch_size", 2)
6193 |         }
6194 |         
6195 |         # Check for mismatched sizes and warn if configured
6196 |         if validation_config.get("warn_on_mismatched_sizes", True):
6197 |             unique_sizes = set(original_values.values())
6198 |             if len(unique_sizes) > 1:
6199 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ BATCH SYNC WARNING: Mismatched batch sizes detected: {original_values}")
6200 |                 self.log.warning(f"   Unique sizes found: {sorted(unique_sizes)}")
6201 |         
6202 |         if synchronize_all:
6203 |             # Synchronize all batch sizes to target
6204 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
6205 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
6206 |             
6207 |             # Also update system config values in memory for consistency
6208 |             if "system" in self.system_config:
6209 |                 self.system_config["max_products_per_cycle"] = target_batch_size
6210 |                 self.system_config["supplier_extraction_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
6211 |                 self.system_config["linking_map_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
6212 |                 self.system_config["financial_report_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
6213 |             
6214 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: All batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
6215 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
6216 |             
6217 |         else:
6218 |             # Only synchronize the two main processing batch sizes
6219 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
6220 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
6221 |             
6222 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: Main processing batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
6223 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
6224 |         
6225 |         return new_max_products_per_cycle, new_supplier_extraction_batch_size
6226 | 
6227 |     async def _analyze_products_batch(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], 
6228 |                                     supplier_name: str, max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
6229 |         """Analyze a batch of supplier products for Amazon matching and profitability."""
6230 |         profitable_results = []
6231 |         
6232 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis
6233 |         valid_products = [
6234 |             p for p in products
6235 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
6236 |         ]
6237 |         
6238 |         price_filtered_products = [
6239 |             p for p in valid_products
6240 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
6241 |         ]
6242 |         
6243 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(price_filtered_products)} products in batch mode")
6244 |         
6245 |         # Process products in cycles
6246 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
6247 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
6248 |         
6249 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
6250 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
6251 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
6252 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
6253 |             
6254 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing analysis batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
6255 |             
6256 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
6257 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
6258 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing product {current_index}: '{product_data.get('title')}'")
6259 |                 
6260 |                 # Check if already processed
6261 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
6262 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
6263 |                     continue
6264 |                 
6265 |                 # Amazon data extraction and analysis
6266 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
6267 |                 if not amazon_data or "error" in amazon_data:
6268 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
6269 |                     
6270 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for batch processing
6271 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
6272 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
6273 |                         supplier_ean = None
6274 |                         
6275 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
6276 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
6277 |                     no_match_reason = f"Batch processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
6278 |                     
6279 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
6280 |                     no_match_entry = {
6281 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
6282 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
6283 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
6284 |                         "amazon_title": None,
6285 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
6286 |                         "amazon_price": None,
6287 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
6288 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
6289 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
6290 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
6291 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
6292 |                     }
6293 |                     
6294 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
6295 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
6296 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for batch product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
6297 |                     
6298 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
6299 |                     continue
6300 |                 
6301 |                 # Save Amazon cache and create linking map
6302 |                 supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
6303 |                 asin = amazon_data.get("asin", "NO_ASIN")
6304 |                 
6305 |                 # Save Amazon data
6306 |                 filename_identifier = supplier_ean if supplier_ean else re.sub(r'[<>:"/\\|?*\s]+', '_', product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50])
6307 |                 amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
6308 |                 with open(amazon_cache_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
6309 |                     json.dump(amazon_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
6310 |                 
6311 |                 # Financial analysis
6312 |                 try:
6313 |                     # Already imported at top of file: from FBA_Financial_calculator import financials as calc_financials
6314 |                     supplier_price = float(product_data.get("price", 0))
6315 |                     current_price = amazon_data.get("current_price", 0)
6316 |                     
6317 |                     if supplier_price > 0 and current_price > 0:
6318 |                         financials = calc_financials(
6319 |                             supplier_price=supplier_price,
6320 |                             amazon_price=current_price,
6321 |                             amazon_sales_rank=amazon_data.get("sales_rank", 999999),
6322 |                             amazon_rating=amazon_data.get("rating", 0),
6323 |                             amazon_review_count=amazon_data.get("reviews", 0)
6324 |                         )
6325 |                         
6326 |                         # Check profitability
6327 |                         if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
6328 |                             self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE: ROI {financials['ROI']:.1f}%, Profit £{financials['NetProfit']:.2f}")
6329 |                             
6330 |                             combined_data = self._combine_supplier_amazon_data(product_data, amazon_data, "hybrid_batch")
6331 |                             combined_data["financials"] = financials
6332 |                             profitable_results.append(combined_data)
6333 |                             
6334 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, True, financials.get("NetProfit", 0))
6335 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
6336 |                         else:
6337 |                             self.log.info(f"Not profitable: ROI {financials.get('ROI', 0):.1f}%, Profit £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f}")
6338 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, False)
6339 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
6340 |                             
6341 |                 except Exception as e:
6342 |                     self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed: {e}")
6343 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
6344 |         
6345 |         return profitable_results
6346 | 
6347 |     def _get_cached_products_path(self, category_url: str):
6348 |         """Helper to get the path for a category's product cache file."""
6349 |         category_filename = f"{self.supplier_name}_{category_url.split('/')[-1]}_products.json"
6350 |         return str(path_manager.get_cache_path('supplier_cache', category_filename))
6351 | 
6352 |     def _combine_data(self, supplier_data: Dict[str, Any], amazon_data: Dict[str, Any], session_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
6353 |         """Combine supplier and Amazon data and calculate financial metrics."""
6354 |         combined = {
6355 |             "supplier_product_info": supplier_data,
6356 |             "amazon_product_info": amazon_data,
6357 |             "analysis_timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
6358 |             "session_id": session_id
6359 |         }
6360 |         # Financial calculation is now handled within the main run loop before this function is called
6361 |         # This function is primarily for combining data and adding match validation
6362 |         match_validation = self._validate_product_match(supplier_data, amazon_data)
6363 |         combined["match_validation"] = match_validation
6364 |         return combined
6365 | 
6366 |     def _overlap_score(self, title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
6367 |         """Calculate word overlap score between two titles"""
6368 |         a = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_a.lower()).split())
6369 |         b = set(re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', title_b.lower()).split())
6370 |         return len(a & b) / max(1, len(a))
6371 |     
6372 |     def _sanitize_filename(self, title: str) -> str:
6373 |         """Sanitize product title for use in filename"""
6374 |         if not title:
6375 |             return "unknown_title"
6376 |         # Remove or replace problematic characters
6377 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', title)
6378 |         sanitized = re.sub(r'\s+', '_', sanitized)
6379 |         return sanitized[:50]  # Limit length to 50 chars
6380 | 
6381 |     def _validate_product_match(self, supplier_product: Dict[str, Any], amazon_product: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
6382 |         """Validate the match between supplier and Amazon products using configurable thresholds."""
6383 |         matching_thresholds = self.system_config.get("performance", {}).get("matching_thresholds", {})
6384 |         
6385 |         # Get configurable thresholds with fallback to more conservative defaults
6386 |         title_similarity_threshold = matching_thresholds.get("title_similarity", 0.25)
6387 |         medium_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("medium_title_similarity", 0.5) 
6388 |         high_title_similarity = matching_thresholds.get("high_title_similarity", 0.75)
6389 |         
6390 |         title_overlap_score = self._overlap_score(supplier_product.get("title", ""), amazon_product.get("title", ""))
6391 | 
6392 |         match_quality = "low"
6393 |         confidence = 0.0
6394 |         
6395 |         # Use configurable thresholds - much more strict than previous hardcoded values
6396 |         if title_overlap_score >= high_title_similarity:
6397 |             match_quality = "high"
6398 |             confidence = 0.9
6399 |         elif title_overlap_score >= medium_title_similarity:
6400 |             match_quality = "medium"
6401 |             confidence = 0.6
6402 |         elif title_overlap_score >= title_similarity_threshold:
6403 |             match_quality = "low"
6404 |             confidence = 0.3
6405 |         else:
6406 |             match_quality = "very_low"
6407 |             confidence = 0.1
6408 | 
6409 |         self.log.debug(f"🔍 MATCH VALIDATION: '{supplier_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' vs '{amazon_product.get('title', 'Unknown')}' = {title_overlap_score:.2f} ({match_quality}, {confidence:.1%})")
6410 |         
6411 |         return {"match_quality": match_quality, "confidence": confidence, "title_overlap_score": title_overlap_score}
6412 | 
6413 |     def _is_product_meeting_criteria(self, combined_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
6414 |         """Check if a product meets all defined business criteria."""
6415 |         # This function is now largely redundant as profitability checks are done in the main loop.
6416 |         # However, it can be used for other criteria like sales rank, reviews, etc.
6417 |         amazon_data = combined_data.get("amazon_product_info", {})
6418 |         financials = combined_data.get("financials", {})
6419 |         # Check profitability (already done, but for completeness)
6420 |         is_profitable = financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT
6421 |         # Check Amazon listing quality
6422 |         meets_rating = amazon_data.get("rating", 0) >= MIN_RATING
6423 |         meets_reviews = amazon_data.get("reviews", 0) >= MIN_REVIEWS
6424 |         meets_sales_rank = amazon_data.get("sales_rank", MAX_SALES_RANK + 1) <= MAX_SALES_RANK
6425 |         # Check for battery products (using the helper function)
6426 |         is_battery = is_battery_title(amazon_data.get("title", "")) or is_battery_title(combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", ""))
6427 |         # Check for other non-FBA friendly keywords
6428 |         is_non_fba_friendly = any(k in amazon_data.get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS) or \
6429 |                               any(k in combined_data["supplier_product_info"].get("title", "").lower() for k in NON_FBA_FRIENDLY_KEYWORDS)
6430 |         # All criteria must be met
6431 |         return is_profitable and meets_rating and meets_reviews and meets_sales_rank and not is_battery and not is_non_fba_friendly
6432 | 
6433 |     def _apply_batch_synchronization(self, max_products_per_cycle: int, 
6434 |                                    supplier_extraction_batch_size: int,
6435 |                                    batch_sync_config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[int, int]:
6436 |         """
6437 |         Apply batch synchronization to align all batch sizes consistently.
6438 |         Returns updated max_products_per_cycle and supplier_extraction_batch_size.
6439 |         """
6440 |         target_batch_size = batch_sync_config.get("target_batch_size", 3)
6441 |         synchronize_all = batch_sync_config.get("synchronize_all_batch_sizes", False)
6442 |         validation_config = batch_sync_config.get("validation", {})
6443 |         
6444 |         self.log.info(f"🔄 BATCH SYNCHRONIZATION: Enabled")
6445 |         self.log.info(f"   target_batch_size: {target_batch_size}")
6446 |         self.log.info(f"   synchronize_all_batch_sizes: {synchronize_all}")
6447 |         
6448 |         original_values = {
6449 |             "max_products_per_cycle": max_products_per_cycle,
6450 |             "supplier_extraction_batch_size": supplier_extraction_batch_size,
6451 |             "linking_map_batch_size": self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1),
6452 |             "financial_report_batch_size": self.system_config.get("financial_report_batch_size", 2)
6453 |         }
6454 |         
6455 |         # Check for mismatched sizes and warn if configured
6456 |         if validation_config.get("warn_on_mismatched_sizes", True):
6457 |             unique_sizes = set(original_values.values())
6458 |             if len(unique_sizes) > 1:
6459 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ BATCH SYNC WARNING: Mismatched batch sizes detected: {original_values}")
6460 |                 self.log.warning(f"   Unique sizes found: {sorted(unique_sizes)}")
6461 |         
6462 |         if synchronize_all:
6463 |             # Synchronize all batch sizes to target
6464 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
6465 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
6466 |             
6467 |             # Also update system config values in memory for consistency
6468 |             if "system" in self.system_config:
6469 |                 self.system_config["max_products_per_cycle"] = target_batch_size
6470 |                 self.system_config["supplier_extraction_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
6471 |                 self.system_config["linking_map_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
6472 |                 self.system_config["financial_report_batch_size"] = target_batch_size
6473 |             
6474 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: All batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
6475 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
6476 |             
6477 |         else:
6478 |             # Only synchronize the two main processing batch sizes
6479 |             new_max_products_per_cycle = target_batch_size
6480 |             new_supplier_extraction_batch_size = target_batch_size
6481 |             
6482 |             self.log.info(f"✅ BATCH SYNC: Main processing batch sizes synchronized to {target_batch_size}")
6483 |             self.log.info(f"   Updated values: max_products_per_cycle={new_max_products_per_cycle}, supplier_extraction_batch_size={new_supplier_extraction_batch_size}")
6484 |         
6485 |         return new_max_products_per_cycle, new_supplier_extraction_batch_size
6486 | 
6487 |     async def _analyze_products_batch(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], 
6488 |                                     supplier_name: str, max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
6489 |         """Analyze a batch of supplier products for Amazon matching and profitability."""
6490 |         profitable_results = []
6491 |         
6492 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis
6493 |         valid_products = [
6494 |             p for p in products
6495 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
6496 |         ]
6497 |         
6498 |         price_filtered_products = [
6499 |             p for p in valid_products
6500 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
6501 |         ]
6502 |         
6503 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(price_filtered_products)} products in batch mode")
6504 |         
6505 |         # Process products in cycles
6506 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
6507 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
6508 |         
6509 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
6510 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
6511 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
6512 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
6513 |             
6514 |             self.log.info(f"🔄 Processing analysis batch {batch_num + 1}/{total_batches} ({len(batch_products)} products)")
6515 |             
6516 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
6517 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
6518 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 Analyzing product {current_index}: '{product_data.get('title')}'")
6519 |                 
6520 |                 # Check if already processed
6521 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
6522 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
6523 |                     continue
6524 |                 
6525 |                 # Amazon data extraction and analysis
6526 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
6527 |                 if not amazon_data or "error" in amazon_data:
6528 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
6529 |                     
6530 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Create no-match linking entry for batch processing
6531 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
6532 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
6533 |                         supplier_ean = None
6534 |                         
6535 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
6536 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
6537 |                     no_match_reason = f"Batch processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
6538 |                     
6539 |                     # Create no-match linking entry
6540 |                     no_match_entry = {
6541 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
6542 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
6543 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title"),
6544 |                         "amazon_title": None,
6545 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
6546 |                         "amazon_price": None,
6547 |                         "match_method": "none",  # NEW: Indicates no match found
6548 |                         "confidence": 0,    # NEW: No confidence for failed matches
6549 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
6550 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
6551 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason  # NEW: Audit trail explaining why no match
6552 |                     }
6553 |                     
6554 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
6555 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
6556 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for batch product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
6557 |                     
6558 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
6559 |                     continue
6560 |                 
6561 |                 # Save Amazon cache and create linking map
6562 |                 supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
6563 |                 asin = amazon_data.get("asin", "NO_ASIN")
6564 |                 
6565 |                 # Save Amazon data
6566 |                 filename_identifier = supplier_ean if supplier_ean else re.sub(r'[<>:"/\\|?*\s]+', '_', product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50])
6567 |                 amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
6568 |                 with open(amazon_cache_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
6569 |                     json.dump(amazon_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
6570 |                 
6571 |                 # Financial analysis
6572 |                 try:
6573 |                     # Already imported at top of file: from FBA_Financial_calculator import financials as calc_financials
6574 |                     supplier_price = float(product_data.get("price", 0))
6575 |                     current_price = amazon_data.get("current_price", 0)
6576 |                     
6577 |                     if supplier_price > 0 and current_price > 0:
6578 |                         financials = calc_financials(
6579 |                             supplier_price=supplier_price,
6580 |                             amazon_price=current_price,
6581 |                             amazon_sales_rank=amazon_data.get("sales_rank", 999999),
6582 |                             amazon_rating=amazon_data.get("rating", 0),
6583 |                             amazon_review_count=amazon_data.get("reviews", 0)
6584 |                         )
6585 |                         
6586 |                         # Check profitability
6587 |                         if financials.get("ROI", 0) > MIN_ROI_PERCENT and financials.get("NetProfit", 0) > MIN_PROFIT_PER_UNIT:
6588 |                             self.log.info(f"✅ PROFITABLE: ROI {financials['ROI']:.1f}%, Profit £{financials['NetProfit']:.2f}")
6589 |                             
6590 |                             combined_data = self._combine_supplier_amazon_data(product_data, amazon_data, "hybrid_batch")
6591 |                             combined_data["financials"] = financials
6592 |                             profitable_results.append(combined_data)
6593 |                             
6594 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, True, financials.get("NetProfit", 0))
6595 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
6596 |                         else:
6597 |                             self.log.info(f"Not profitable: ROI {financials.get('ROI', 0):.1f}%, Profit £{financials.get('NetProfit', 0):.2f}")
6598 |                             self.state_manager.update_success_metrics(True, False)
6599 |                             self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
6600 |                             
6601 |                 except Exception as e:
6602 |                     self.log.error(f"Financial calculation failed: {e}")
6603 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
6604 |         
6605 |         return profitable_results
6606 | 
6607 |     def _get_cached_products_path(self, category_url: str):
6608 |         """Helper to get the path for a category's product cache file."""
6609 |         category_filename = f"{self.supplier_name}_{category_url.split('/')[-1]}_products.json"
6610 |         return str(path_manager.get_cache_path('supplier_cache', category_filename))
6611 | 
6612 |     # Removed duplicate _load_linking_map method - using the one at line 1354
6613 |
