6616 |     async def _process_chunk_with_main_workflow_logic(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
6617 |         """Process products using the same detailed logic as main workflow (not simplified batch processing)"""
6618 |         profitable_results = []
6619 |         
6620 |         # Filter and prepare products for analysis (same as main workflow)
6621 |         valid_products = [
6622 |             p for p in products
6623 |             if p.get("title") and isinstance(p.get("price"), (float, int)) and p.get("price", 0) > 0 and p.get("url")
6624 |         ]
6625 |         
6626 |         price_filtered_products = [
6627 |             p for p in valid_products
6628 |             if MIN_PRICE <= p.get("price", 0) <= MAX_PRICE
6629 |         ]
6630 |         
6631 |         self.log.info(f"🔍 Processing {len(price_filtered_products)} products with main workflow logic")
6632 |         
6633 |         # Use the same logic as the main workflow
6634 |         batch_size = max_products_per_cycle
6635 |         total_batches = (len(price_filtered_products) + batch_size - 1) // batch_size
6636 |         
6637 |         for batch_num in range(total_batches):
6638 |             start_idx = batch_num * batch_size
6639 |             end_idx = min(start_idx + batch_size, len(price_filtered_products))
6640 |             batch_products = price_filtered_products[start_idx:end_idx]
6641 |             
6642 |             # Process each product in the current batch using main workflow logic
6643 |             for i, product_data in enumerate(batch_products):
6644 |                 current_index = start_idx + i + 1
6645 |                 self.log.info(f"--- Processing supplier product {current_index}/{len(price_filtered_products)}: '{product_data.get('title')}' ---")
6646 |                 
6647 |                 # 🚨 SURGICAL FIX: Update state manager with product index tracking (gap processing)
6648 |                 if hasattr(self, 'state_manager') and self.state_manager:
6649 |                     if hasattr(self.state_manager, 'update_product_extraction_progress'):
6650 |                         self.state_manager.update_product_extraction_progress(current_index, len(price_filtered_products))
6651 |                     else:
6652 |                         # Fallback: directly update the product index in supplier_extraction_progress
6653 |                         if hasattr(self.state_manager, 'state_data'):
6654 |                             progress_data = self.state_manager.state_data.setdefault('supplier_extraction_progress', {})
6655 |                             progress_data['current_product_index_in_category'] = current_index
6656 |                             progress_data['total_products_in_current_category'] = len(price_filtered_products)
6657 |                 
6658 |                 # 🔄 UPDATE PROCESSING STATE: Update detailed progress tracking for hybrid mode
6659 |                 if hasattr(self, 'state_manager') and self.state_manager:
6660 |                     # Calculate current category and subcategory indexes
6661 |                     # For hybrid mode, we process by chunks, so category index = batch_num + 1
6662 |                     self.state_manager.update_supplier_extraction_progress(
6663 |                         category_index=batch_num + 1,
6664 |                         total_categories=total_batches,
6665 |                         subcategory_index=i + 1,
6666 |                         total_subcategories=len(batch_products),
6667 |                         batch_number=batch_num + 1,
6668 |                         total_batches=total_batches,
6669 |                         category_url=product_data.get('source_url', 'unknown'),
6670 |                         extraction_phase="amazon_analysis"
6671 |                     )
6672 |                     # Also update the main processing index
6673 |                     self.state_manager.update_processing_index(current_index, len(price_filtered_products))
6674 |                 
6675 |                 # Check if product has been previously processed
6676 |                 if self.state_manager.is_product_processed(product_data.get("url")):
6677 |                     self.log.info(f"Product already processed: {product_data.get('url')}. Skipping.")
6678 |                     continue
6679 | 
6680 |                 # Extract Amazon data using the same logic as main workflow
6681 |                 amazon_data = await self._get_amazon_data(product_data)
6682 |                 
6683 |                 if amazon_data:
6684 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
6685 |                     amazon_asin = amazon_data.get("asin") or amazon_data.get("asin_extracted_from_page")
6686 |                     
6687 |                     # Save Amazon data to cache file  
6688 |                     # 🚨 FIX: Sanitize supplier_ean to handle multiple EANs or invalid characters
6689 |                     if supplier_ean:
6690 |                         # Take first EAN if multiple exist, sanitize invalid filename characters
6691 |                         filename_identifier = str(supplier_ean).split('/')[0].split('\\')[0].replace(" ", "_").replace(":", "_").replace("?", "_").replace("*", "_").replace("<", "_").replace(">", "_").replace("|", "_").replace('"', "_")
6692 |                     else:
6693 |                         filename_identifier = product_data.get("title", "NO_TITLE")[:50].replace(" ", "_").replace("/", "_").replace("\\", "_").replace(":", "_").replace("?", "_").replace("*", "_").replace("<", "_").replace(">", "_").replace("|", "_").replace('"', "_")
6694 |                     amazon_cache_path = str(path_manager.get_output_path('FBA_ANALYSIS', 'amazon_cache', f"amazon_{amazon_asin}_{filename_identifier}.json"))
6695 |                     success = self.save_guardian.save_json_atomic(amazon_cache_path, amazon_data)
6696 |                     if success:
6697 |                         self.log.info(f"💾 Saved Amazon data to {amazon_cache_path}")
6698 |                     else:
6699 |                         self.log.error(f"❌ Failed to save Amazon data to {amazon_cache_path}")
6700 |                     
6701 |                     # Create linking map entry
6702 |                     
6703 |                     if amazon_asin:  # Only require ASIN, EAN can be None for title matches
6704 |                         # DEBUG: Check linking_map type before assignment
6705 |                         self.log.debug(f"🔍 DEBUG: linking_map type: {type(self.linking_map)}, size: {len(self.linking_map)} entries")
6706 |                         
6707 |                         # Create detailed linking map entry (matching working pre-minor-fix format)
6708 |                         # Get the actual search method used from Amazon data
6709 |                         actual_search_method = amazon_data.get("_search_method_used", "unknown")
6710 |                         
6711 |                         linking_entry = {
6712 |                             "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
6713 |                             "amazon_asin": amazon_asin,
6714 |                             "supplier_title": product_data.get("title", ""),
6715 |                             "amazon_title": amazon_data.get("title", ""),
6716 |                             "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
6717 |                             "amazon_price": amazon_data.get("current_price"),
6718 |                             "match_method": actual_search_method,
6719 |                             "confidence": "high" if actual_search_method == "EAN" else "medium" if actual_search_method == "title" else "low",
6720 |                             "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
6721 |                             "supplier_url": product_data.get("url")
6722 |                         }
6723 |                         # 🚀 HASH OPTIMIZATION: Use optimized method to add entry and update indexes
6724 |                         self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(linking_entry)
6725 |                         
6726 |                         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Check financial report trigger after each linking map entry
6727 |                         # Fix: Use correct config path from system config file
6728 |                         financial_batch_size = self.system_config.get("system", {}).get("financial_report_batch_size", 50)
6729 |                         current_linking_map_count = len(self.linking_map)
6730 |                         
6731 |                         if current_linking_map_count > 0 and current_linking_map_count % financial_batch_size == 0:
6732 |                             try:
6733 |                                 self.log.info(f"🚨 FINANCIAL REPORT TRIGGER: Reached {current_linking_map_count} linking map entries (trigger every {financial_batch_size})")
6734 |                                 from tools.FBA_Financial_calculator import run_calculations
6735 |                                 self.log.info(f"📊 Using financial report batch size from config: {financial_batch_size}")
6736 |                                 financial_results = run_calculations(self.supplier_name, max_products=financial_batch_size)
6737 |                                 if financial_results and financial_results.get('statistics', {}).get('output_file'):
6738 |                                     self.log.info(f"✅ Financial report EXECUTED: {financial_results['statistics']['output_file']}")
6739 |                                 else:
6740 |                                     self.log.warning("⚠️ Financial report executed but no file path returned")
6741 |                             except ImportError as ie:
6742 |                                 self.log.error(f"❌ Could not import FBA_Financial_calculator: {ie}")
6743 |                             except Exception as e:
6744 |                                 self.log.error(f"❌ Error executing financial report: {e}")
6745 |                         elif current_linking_map_count > 0:
6746 |                             next_trigger_count = ((current_linking_map_count // financial_batch_size) + 1) * financial_batch_size
6747 |                             self.log.info(f"💡 FINANCIAL REPORT TRIGGER: Next trigger at {next_trigger_count} linking map entries (current: {current_linking_map_count}, trigger frequency: {financial_batch_size})")
6748 |                     
6749 |                     # Combine supplier and Amazon data
6750 |                     combined_data = {**product_data, **amazon_data}
6751 |                     
6752 |                     # Run financial analysis using same logic as main workflow
6753 |                     try:
6754 |                         # Extract supplier price with validation
6755 |                         supplier_price_inc_vat = combined_data.get("price", 0)
6756 |                         if isinstance(supplier_price_inc_vat, str):
6757 |                             # Clean price string and convert to float
6758 |                             import re
6759 |                             price_clean = re.sub(r'[^0-9.]', '', supplier_price_inc_vat)
6760 |                             supplier_price_inc_vat = float(price_clean) if price_clean else 0
6761 |                         elif supplier_price_inc_vat is None:
6762 |                             supplier_price_inc_vat = 0
6763 |                             
6764 |                         # Calculate financial metrics for this specific product
6765 |                         financials = calc_financials(combined_data, combined_data, supplier_price_inc_vat)
6766 |                         
6767 |                         if not financials:
6768 |                             self.log.warning(f"Financial calculation returned empty for '{combined_data.get('title')}'")
6769 |                             financial_result = {"error": "Financial calculation returned empty", "is_profitable": False}
6770 |                         else:
6771 |                             # Extract profitability from financials result
6772 |                             is_profitable = financials.get("is_profitable", False)
6773 |                             financial_result = {
6774 |                                 **financials,
6775 |                                 "is_profitable": is_profitable
6776 |                             }
6777 |                             
6778 |                     except Exception as calc_error:
6779 |                         self.log.error(f"Financial calculation error: {calc_error}")
6780 |                         financial_result = {"error": str(calc_error), "is_profitable": False}
6781 |                     
6782 |                     if financial_result and financial_result.get("is_profitable"):
6783 |                         profitable_results.append(financial_result)
6784 |                         self.log.info(f"✅ Profitable product found: {product_data.get('title')}")
6785 |                         self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_profitable")
6786 |                     elif financial_result and financial_result.get("error"):
6787 |                         self.log.info(f"❌ Financial analysis failed: {financial_result.get('error')}")
6788 |                         self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_financial_calculation")
6789 |                     else:
6790 |                         self.log.info(f"❌ Not profitable product: {product_data.get('title')}")
6791 |                         self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "completed_not_profitable")
6792 |                 
6793 |                 else:
6794 |                     # Handle no Amazon data or error cases
6795 |                     self.log.warning(f"Could not retrieve Amazon data for '{product_data.get('title')}'. Creating no-match linking entry.")
6796 |                     
6797 |                     # Create no-match linking entry for chunk processing
6798 |                     supplier_ean = product_data.get("ean") or product_data.get("barcode")
6799 |                     if supplier_ean == "None" or supplier_ean is None:
6800 |                         supplier_ean = None
6801 |                         
6802 |                     # Determine the reason for no match
6803 |                     error_info = amazon_data.get("error") if isinstance(amazon_data, dict) else "No Amazon data returned"
6804 |                     no_match_reason = f"Chunk processing - Amazon search failed: {error_info}"
6805 |                     
6806 |                     no_match_entry = {
6807 |                         "supplier_ean": supplier_ean,
6808 |                         "amazon_asin": None,
6809 |                         "supplier_title": product_data.get("title", ""),
6810 |                         "amazon_title": None,
6811 |                         "supplier_price": product_data.get("price"),
6812 |                         "amazon_price": None,
6813 |                         "match_method": "none",
6814 |                         "confidence": 0,
6815 |                         "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
6816 |                         "supplier_url": product_data.get("url"),
6817 |                         "no_match_reason": no_match_reason
6818 |                     }
6819 |                     
6820 |                     # Add no-match entry to linking map using optimized method
6821 |                     self._add_linking_map_entry_optimized(no_match_entry)
6822 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Added NO-MATCH linking entry for chunk product: {supplier_ean or 'NO_EAN'} → NO MATCH")
6823 |                     
6824 |                     self.state_manager.mark_product_processed(product_data.get("url"), "failed_amazon_extraction")
6825 |                 
6826 |                 # Save state periodically using configurable batch size
6827 |                 linking_map_batch = self.system_config.get("linking_map_batch_size", 1)
6828 |                 if current_index % linking_map_batch == 0:
6829 |                     self.state_manager.save_state(preserve_interruption_state=True)
6830 |                     self._save_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
6831 |                     self.log.info(f"📊 Periodic save at product {current_index} (linking_map_batch_size: {linking_map_batch})")
6832 |         
6833 |         try:
6834 |             # Final save of linking map with all entries
6835 |             self._save_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
6836 |             self.log.info("✅ Chunk processing: Final linking map save completed")
6837 |             
6838 |             # Final save of financial results
6839 |             if profitable_results:
6840 |                 # Note: _save_final_report expects supplier_name as second parameter
6841 |                 self._save_final_report(profitable_results, self.supplier_name)
6842 |                 self.log.info("✅ Chunk processing: Final report save completed")
6843 |             
6844 |             self.log.info("--- Chunk Processing with Main Workflow Logic Finished ---")
6845 |             
6846 |         except Exception as save_error:
6847 |             self.log.error(f"❌ CRITICAL: Error during final save operations in chunk processing: {save_error}", exc_info=True)
6848 |         
6849 |         return profitable_results
6850 |     
6851 |     def _check_authentication_fallback_needed(self) -> bool:
6852 |         """
6853 |         🔧 AUTHENTICATION FALLBACK: Check if re-authentication is needed based on various triggers
6854 |         """
6855 |         # Trigger 1: Too many products without prices
6856 |         if self.products_without_price_count >= self.auth_fallback_config["price_missing_threshold"]:
6857 |             self.log.warning(f"🔐 AUTH TRIGGER: {self.products_without_price_count} products without price (threshold: {self.auth_fallback_config['price_missing_threshold']})")
6858 |             return True
6859 |             
6860 |         # Trigger 2: Product count-based re-authentication
6861 |         if self.products_processed_since_auth >= self.auth_fallback_config["products_per_auth"]:
6862 |             self.log.warning(f"🔐 AUTH TRIGGER: {self.products_processed_since_auth} products processed since last auth (threshold: {self.auth_fallback_config['products_per_auth']})")
6863 |             return True
6864 |             
6865 |         # Trigger 3: Time-based re-authentication
6866 |         if self.last_authentication_time:
6867 |             from datetime import datetime, timedelta
6868 |             hours_since_auth = (datetime.now() - self.last_authentication_time).total_seconds() / 3600
6869 |             if hours_since_auth >= self.auth_fallback_config["hours_per_auth"]:
6870 |                 self.log.warning(f"🔐 AUTH TRIGGER: {hours_since_auth:.1f} hours since last auth (threshold: {self.auth_fallback_config['hours_per_auth']})")
6871 |                 return True
6872 |                 
6873 |         return False
6874 |     
6875 |     async def _perform_authentication_fallback(self) -> bool:
6876 |         """
6877 |         🔧 AUTHENTICATION FALLBACK: Perform re-authentication using SupplierAuthenticationService
6878 |         """
6879 |         try:
6880 |             self.log.info("🔐 Performing authentication fallback...")
6881 |             
6882 |             # Import and use the authentication service
6883 |             from tools.supplier_authentication_service import SupplierAuthenticationService
6884 |             
6885 |             # Get credentials from config
6886 |             supplier_config_path = os.path.join(
6887 |                 os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))),
6888 |                 "config", "supplier_configs", f"{self.supplier_name.replace('.', '-')}.json"
6889 |             )
6890 |             
6891 |             # Create authentication service
6892 |             auth_service = SupplierAuthenticationService(
6893 |                 supplier_name=self.supplier_name,
6894 |                 supplier_url=f"https://{self.supplier_name}",
6895 |                 config_path=supplier_config_path
6896 |             )
6897 |             
6898 |             # Get a page from browser manager
6899 |             page = None
6900 |             if hasattr(self, 'browser_manager') and self.browser_manager:
6901 |                 try:
6902 |                     page = await self.browser_manager.get_page()
6903 |                 except:
6904 |                     self.log.warning("Could not get page from browser manager for authentication")
6905 |             
6906 |             if page:
6907 |                 # Use hardcoded credentials (as shown in the auth service)
6908 |                 credentials = {
6909 |                     "email": "info@theblacksmithmarket.com",
6910 |                     "password": "0Dqixm9c&"
6911 |                 }
6912 |                 
6913 |                 success, method = await auth_service.ensure_authenticated_session(page, credentials, force_reauth=True)
6914 |                 
6915 |                 if success:
6916 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ Authentication fallback successful using {method}")
6917 |                     # Reset counters and update timestamp
6918 |                     self.products_without_price_count = 0
6919 |                     self.products_processed_since_auth = 0
6920 |                     self.last_authentication_time = datetime.now()
6921 |                     return True
6922 |                 else:
6923 |                     self.log.error("❌ Authentication fallback failed")
6924 |                     return False
6925 |             else:
6926 |                 self.log.error("❌ Could not get browser page for authentication")
6927 |                 return False
6928 |                 
6929 |         except Exception as e:
6930 |             self.log.error(f"❌ Authentication fallback error: {e}")
6931 |             return False
6932 |     
6933 |     def _record_product_price_status(self, product_data: dict, has_price: bool):
6934 |         """
6935 |         🔧 AUTHENTICATION FALLBACK: Record whether product has price for authentication monitoring
6936 |         """
6937 |         # Update product processing counter
6938 |         self.products_processed_since_auth += 1
6939 |         
6940 |         # Track price missing for authentication fallback
6941 |         if not has_price:
6942 |             self.products_without_price_count += 1
6943 |             self.log.info(f"🔍 PRICE MISSING: Product without price ({self.products_without_price_count}/{self.auth_fallback_config['price_missing_threshold']})")
6944 |         else:
6945 |             # Reset counter on successful price extraction
6946 |             if self.products_without_price_count > 0:
6947 |                 self.log.info(f"✅ PRICE FOUND: Resetting price missing counter (was {self.products_without_price_count})")
6948 |                 self.products_without_price_count = 0
6949 |     
6950 |     def _get_supplier_cache_path_fix(self) -> str:
6951 |         """Get the path to supplier cache file"""
6952 |         supplier_name_clean = self.supplier_name.replace('.', '-')
6953 |         return os.path.join(self.supplier_cache_dir, f"{supplier_name_clean}_products_cache.json")
6954 |     
6955 |     def _find_actual_supplier_cache_file(self, supplier_name: str) -> tuple[str, int]:
6956 |         """
6957 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Find actual supplier cache file including fallback files
6958 |         Returns: (file_path, product_count) or ("", 0) if not found
6959 |         """
6960 |         # Pattern 1: Expected filename (matches save method)
6961 |         expected_filename = f"{supplier_name.replace('.', '-')}_products_cache.json"
6962 |         expected_path = os.path.join(self.supplier_cache_dir, expected_filename)
6963 |         
6964 |         # Pattern 2: Alternative sanitized filename (legacy compatibility)
6965 |         sanitized_filename = f"{supplier_name.replace('.', '_')}_products_cache.json"
6966 |         sanitized_path = os.path.join(self.supplier_cache_dir, sanitized_filename)
6967 |         
6968 |         # Check expected patterns first
6969 |         for pattern_name, file_path in [("expected", expected_path), ("sanitized", sanitized_path)]:
6970 |             if os.path.exists(file_path):
6971 |                 try:
6972 |                     with open(file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
6973 |                         products = json.load(f)
6974 |                     count = len(products)
6975 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ CACHE FOUND: {pattern_name} pattern - {count} products in {os.path.basename(file_path)}")
6976 |                     return file_path, count
6977 |                 except Exception as e:
6978 |                     self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Could not read {pattern_name} cache file: {e}")
6979 |         
6980 |         # Pattern 3: Search for fallback files (cache_fallback_*.json)
6981 |         try:
6982 |             import glob
6983 |             fallback_pattern = os.path.join(self.supplier_cache_dir, "cache_fallback_*.json")
6984 |             fallback_files = glob.glob(fallback_pattern)
6985 |             
6986 |             if fallback_files:
6987 |                 # Use the most recent fallback file
6988 |                 latest_fallback = max(fallback_files, key=os.path.getmtime)
6989 |                 try:
6990 |                     with open(latest_fallback, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
6991 |                         products = json.load(f)
6992 |                     count = len(products)
6993 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ CACHE FOUND: fallback pattern - {count} products in {os.path.basename(latest_fallback)}")
6994 |                     return latest_fallback, count
6995 |                 except Exception as e:
6996 |                     self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Could not read fallback cache file: {e}")
6997 |         except Exception as e:
6998 |             self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Could not search for fallback files: {e}")
6999 |         
7000 |         self.log.info(f"📝 NO CACHE FOUND: No supplier cache file found for {supplier_name}")
7001 |         return "", 0
7002 | 
7003 |     # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX 1: Processing State Resumption Logic
7004 |     def _should_continue_supplier_scraping(self) -> bool:
7005 |         """Check if supplier scraping should continue from previous index"""
7006 |         try:
7007 |             # Check processing state for extraction progress
7008 |             if hasattr(self.state_manager, 'state_data') and self.state_manager.state_data:
7009 |                 # Get extraction progress from state
7010 |                 extraction_progress = self.state_manager.state_data.get('supplier_extraction_progress', {})
7011 |                 products_extracted = extraction_progress.get('products_extracted_total', 0)
7012 |                 
7013 |                 # Get the target from runtime settings
7014 |                 metadata = self.state_manager.state_data.get('metadata', {})
7015 |                 runtime_settings = metadata.get('runtime_settings', {})
7016 |                 target_products = runtime_settings.get('max_products_per_category', 8)
7017 |                 
7018 |                 self.log.info(f"🔍 SUPPLIER SCRAPING CHECK: extracted {products_extracted}/{target_products} products")
7019 |                 
7020 |                 # Continue scraping if we haven't reached the target
7021 |                 if products_extracted < target_products:
7022 |                     self.log.info(f"🔄 SUPPLIER SCRAPING NEEDED: {products_extracted} < {target_products}")
7023 |                     return True
7024 |                 else:
7025 |                     self.log.info(f"✅ SUPPLIER SCRAPING COMPLETE: {products_extracted} >= {target_products}")
7026 |                     return False
7027 |             else:
7028 |                 self.log.info("🔄 NO PROCESSING STATE: Proceeding with supplier scraping")
7029 |                 return True
7030 |                 
7031 |         except Exception as e:
7032 |             self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Error checking supplier scraping status: {e}, proceeding with scraping")
7033 |             return True
7034 | 
7035 |     # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX 2: 276-Chunk Skip Logic  
7036 |     def _should_skip_chunk_processing(self) -> bool:
7037 |         """Check if chunk processing should be skipped due to already processed products"""
7038 |         try:
7039 |             # Check if we have extracted products
7040 |             cache_path = self._get_supplier_cache_path_fix()
7041 |             if not os.path.exists(cache_path):
7042 |                 self.log.info("🔄 NO SUPPLIER CACHE: Cannot skip chunk processing")
7043 |                 return False
7044 |             
7045 |             # Check if we have processed products through Amazon (linking map exists)
7046 |             linking_map = self._load_linking_map(self.supplier_name)
7047 |             if not linking_map:
7048 |                 self.log.info("🔄 NO LINKING MAP: Cannot skip chunk processing")
7049 |                 return False
7050 |             
7051 |             # Check if financial reports already exist
7052 |             financial_reports_dir = Path(self.output_dir) / "FBA_ANALYSIS" / "financial_reports"
7053 |             if financial_reports_dir.exists():
7054 |                 report_files = list(financial_reports_dir.glob("fba_financial_report_*.csv"))
7055 |                 if report_files:
7056 |                     latest_report = max(report_files, key=lambda x: x.stat().st_mtime)
7057 |                     # Check if report is recent (within last hour)
7058 |                     import time
7059 |                     if (time.time() - latest_report.stat().st_mtime) < 3600:
7060 |                         self.log.info(f"✅ RECENT FINANCIAL REPORT EXISTS: {latest_report.name} - skipping chunk reprocessing")
7061 |                         return True
7062 |             
7063 |             self.log.info("🔄 CONDITIONS NOT MET: Proceeding with chunk processing")
7064 |             return False
7065 |             
7066 |         except Exception as e:
7067 |             self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Error checking chunk skip conditions: {e}, proceeding with processing")
7068 |             return False
7069 |     
7070 |     def _load_existing_profitable_results(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
7071 |         """Load existing profitable results from recent financial reports"""
7072 |         try:
7073 |             financial_reports_dir = Path(self.output_dir) / "FBA_ANALYSIS" / "financial_reports"
7074 |             if not financial_reports_dir.exists():
7075 |                 return []
7076 |             
7077 |             report_files = list(financial_reports_dir.glob("fba_financial_report_*.csv"))
7078 |             if not report_files:
7079 |                 return []
7080 |             
7081 |             latest_report = max(report_files, key=lambda x: x.stat().st_mtime)
7082 |             self.log.info(f"📊 Loading existing profitable results from: {latest_report.name}")
7083 |             
7084 |             # Read CSV and convert to results format
7085 |             import pandas as pd
7086 |             df = pd.read_csv(latest_report)
7087 |             profitable_results = []
7088 |             
7089 |             for _, row in df.iterrows():
7090 |                 if row.get('profit_gbp', 0) > 0:  # Only profitable products
7091 |                     result = {
7092 |                         'supplier_product': {
7093 |                             'title': row.get('supplier_title', ''),
7094 |                             'price': row.get('supplier_price_gbp', 0),
7095 |                             'ean': row.get('ean', ''),
7096 |                             'url': row.get('supplier_url', '')
7097 |                         },
7098 |                         'amazon_product': {
7099 |                             'asin': row.get('asin', ''),
7100 |                             'title': row.get('amazon_title', ''),
7101 |                             'price': row.get('amazon_price_gbp', 0)
7102 |                         },
7103 |                         'profit_gbp': row.get('profit_gbp', 0),
7104 |                         'roi_percentage': row.get('roi_percentage', 0)
7105 |                     }
7106 |                     profitable_results.append(result)
7107 |             
7108 |             self.log.info(f"✅ Loaded {len(profitable_results)} existing profitable results")
7109 |             return profitable_results
7110 |             
7111 |         except Exception as e:
7112 |             self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Error loading existing profitable results: {e}")
7113 |             return []
7114 | 
7115 |     # 🚨 LOGIN SCRIPT TRIGGER IMPLEMENTATION - Uses same authentication script as system startup
7116 |     async def _trigger_authentication_check(self, context: str = "category_processing") -> bool:
7117 |         """
7118 |         Trigger the same login script that runs at system startup before each category extraction.
7119 |         This ensures authentication is refreshed at workflow boundaries as required.
7120 |         """
7121 |         try:
7122 |             self.log.info(f"🔐 LOGIN SCRIPT TRIGGER: Checking authentication before {context}")
7123 |             
7124 |             # Import the same authentication service used at system startup
7125 |             from tools.supplier_authentication_service import SupplierAuthenticationService
7126 |             
7127 |             if hasattr(self, 'browser_manager') and self.browser_manager:
7128 |                 # Initialize authentication service using same pattern as run_custom_poundwholesale.py
7129 |                 auth_service = SupplierAuthenticationService(self.browser_manager)
7130 |                 
7131 |                 # Get a page from browser manager to check authentication
7132 |                 page = await self.browser_manager.get_page(
7133 |                     self.workflow_config.get('supplier_url', 'https://www.poundwholesale.co.uk')
7134 |                 )
7135 |                 
7136 |                 if page:
7137 |                     # Get supplier credentials from system config (hybrid processing enabled)
7138 |                     supplier_name = self.supplier_name or "poundwholesale.co.uk"
7139 |                     credentials_config = self.system_config.get("credentials", {}).get(supplier_name, {})
7140 |                     credentials = {
7141 |                         "email": credentials_config.get("username", "info@theblacksmithmarket.com"),
7142 |                         "password": credentials_config.get("password", "0Dqixm9c&")
7143 |                     }
7144 |                     
7145 |                     # Use the same authentication method as startup script
7146 |                     authenticated = await auth_service.ensure_authenticated_session(page, credentials)
7147 |                     
7148 |                     if authenticated:
7149 |                         self.log.info(f"✅ LOGIN SCRIPT SUCCESS: Authentication verified for {context}")
7150 |                         return True
7151 |                     else:
7152 |                         self.log.warning(f"⚠️ LOGIN SCRIPT FAILED: Authentication failed for {context}")
7153 |                         return False
7154 |                 else:
7155 |                     self.log.warning(f"⚠️ LOGIN SCRIPT ERROR: Could not get page for authentication check in {context}")
7156 |                     return True  # Continue processing even if can't check
7157 |                     
7158 |             else:
7159 |                 self.log.warning(f"⚠️ LOGIN SCRIPT UNAVAILABLE: No browser manager for authentication in {context}")
7160 |                 return True  # Continue processing if no browser manager
7161 |                 
7162 |         except Exception as e:
7163 |             self.log.warning(f"⚠️ LOGIN SCRIPT TRIGGER ERROR: {e} in {context}, continuing processing")
7164 |             return True  # Continue processing on error to avoid blocking workflow
7165 | 
7166 |     # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX 3: Authentication Fallback Integration (Deprecated - replaced by _trigger_authentication_check)
7167 |     async def _check_authentication_before_category(self) -> bool:
7168 |         """Check authentication before category and trigger fallback if needed"""
7169 |         try:
7170 |             # Import the authentication service
7171 |             from tools.supplier_authentication_service import SupplierAuthenticationService
7172 |             
7173 |             if hasattr(self, 'browser_manager') and self.browser_manager:
7174 |                 auth_service = SupplierAuthenticationService(self.browser_manager)
7175 |                 
7176 |                 # Check if authenticated
7177 |                 is_authenticated = await auth_service.is_authenticated()
7178 |                 
7179 |                 if not is_authenticated:
7180 |                     self.log.warning("⚠️ AUTHENTICATION LOST: Triggering login fallback")
7181 |                     
7182 |                     # Trigger standalone login script
7183 |                     try:
7184 |                         from tools.standalone_playwright_login import StandalonePlaywrightLogin
7185 |                         login_handler = StandalonePlaywrightLogin(self.browser_manager)
7186 |                         login_result = await login_handler.perform_login(self.supplier_name)
7187 |                         
7188 |                         if login_result:
7189 |                             self.log.info("✅ Authentication fallback successful")
7190 |                             return True
7191 |                         else:
7192 |                             self.log.error("❌ Authentication fallback failed")
7193 |                             return False
7194 |                     except Exception as login_error:
7195 |                         self.log.error(f"❌ Authentication fallback error: {login_error}")
7196 |                         return False
7197 |                 else:
7198 |                     self.log.info("✅ Authentication verified")
7199 |                     return True
7200 |             else:
7201 |                 self.log.warning("⚠️ No browser manager available for authentication check")
7202 |                 return True  # Assume authenticated if can't check
7203 |                 
7204 |         except Exception as e:
7205 |             self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Error checking authentication: {e}, proceeding")
7206 |             return True  # Assume authenticated on error

7224 |     async def _process_existing_products_with_amazon_extraction(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]], max_products_per_cycle: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
7225 |         """Process existing cached products with Amazon extraction only"""

7229 |     def _filter_unprocessed_products_with_hash_lookup(self, all_products: List[Dict[str, Any]], supplier_name: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
7230 |         """
7231 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX #1 & #3: Filter products using hash-based O(1) lookup against linking map AND product cache
7232 |         
7233 |         This method implements:
7234 |         - Hash-based lookup for O(1) performance instead of O(n) linear searches  
7235 |         - Linking map filtering to only return unprocessed products
7236 |         - 🚀 ENHANCEMENT: Product cache filtering to skip products already extracted  
7237 |         - Comprehensive logging for debugging gap processing
7238 |         
7239 |         Args:
7240 |             all_products: All cached supplier products (e.g., 2,335 products)
7241 |             supplier_name: Name of supplier for linking map path
7242 |             
7243 |         Returns:
7244 |             List of unprocessed products (e.g., 50-100 products that haven't been processed)
7245 |         """
7246 |         import json
7247 |         import os
7248 |         from typing import Set
7249 |         
7250 |         # 🔍 Step 1: Load linking map to identify already processed products
7251 |         linking_map_path = self._get_linking_map_path_for_supplier(supplier_name)
7252 |         processed_eans: Set[str] = set()
7253 |         processed_urls: Set[str] = set()
7254 |         
7255 |         if os.path.exists(linking_map_path):
7256 |             try:
7257 |                 with open(linking_map_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
7258 |                     linking_map_data = json.load(f)
7259 |

7392 |     def _load_supplier_cache(self, supplier_name: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
7393 |         """
7394 |         Load cached supplier products for hash indexing and duplicate prevention
7395 |         
7396 |         Args:
7397 |             supplier_name: Name of supplier to load cache for
7398 |             
7399 |         Returns:
7400 |             List of cached products, or empty list if no cache found
7401 |         """
7402 |         import json
7403 |         
7404 |         supplier_cache_file, _ = self._find_actual_supplier_cache_file(supplier_name)
7405 |         if not supplier_cache_file:
7406 |             self.log.debug(f"📊 No supplier cache file found for {supplier_name}")
7407 |             return []
7408 |         
7409 |         try:
7410 |             with open(supplier_cache_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
7411 |                 cached_products = json.load(f)
7412 |                 self.log.debug(f"📊 Loaded {len(cached_products)} products from supplier cache: {supplier_cache_file}")
7413 |                 return cached_products
7414 |         except Exception as e:
7415 |             self.log.warning(f"⚠️ Error loading supplier cache {supplier_cache_file}: {e}")
7416 |             return []
7417 |     
7418 |     def _build_product_hash_index(self, products: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Dict[str, bool]:
7419 |         """
7420 |         Build hash index for O(1) product lookups
7421 |         
7422 |         Args:
7423 |             products: List of products to index
7424 |             
7425 |         Returns:
7426 |             Dict mapping product identifiers to True (for fast existence checking)
7427 |         """
7428 |         hash_index = {}
7429 |         
7430 |         for product in products:
7431 |             # Index by EAN if available
7432 |             product_ean = product.get('ean', '')
7433 |             if product_ean:
7434 |                 hash_index[product_ean] = True
7435 |             
7436 |             # Index by URL if available
7437 |             product_url = product.get('url', '')
7438 |             if product_url:
7439 |                 hash_index[product_url] = True
7440 |         
7441 |         return hash_index
7442 |     
7443 |     def _get_linking_map_path_for_supplier(self, supplier_name: str) -> str:
7444 |         """Get the linking map file path for a given supplier using centralized path management"""
7445 |         # Use path_manager for standardized path construction - ensures canonical dotted path
7446 |         return str(get_linking_map_path(supplier_name=supplier_name))
7447 |     
7448 |     def _perform_smart_memory_management(self, current_product_index: int, batch_products: list):
7449 |         """
7450 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX #4: Smart Memory Management with Sliding Window Approach
7451 |         
7452 |         Implements periodic memory clearing with preservation of recent context for debugging continuity.
7453 |         Prevents memory accumulation while maintaining processing efficiency.
7454 |         
7455 |         Args:
7456 |             current_product_index: Current product being processed
7457 |             batch_products: Current batch of products being processed
7458 |         """
7459 |         import gc
7460 |         
7461 |         # Memory management configuration
7462 |         memory_config = self.system_config.get("memory_management", {})
7463 |         clear_frequency = memory_config.get("clear_frequency_products", 500)  # Clear every 500 products
7464 |         sliding_window_size = memory_config.get("sliding_window_size", 100)   # Keep last 100 for continuity
7465 |         enabled = memory_config.get("enabled", True)
7466 |         
7467 |         if not enabled:
7468 |             return
7469 |         
7470 |         # Check if it's time for memory clearing
7471 |         if current_product_index % clear_frequency == 0:
7472 |             self.log.info(f"🧹 SMART MEMORY MANAGEMENT: Starting memory clearing at product {current_product_index}")
7473 |             
7474 |             # Step 1: Clear large data structures if they exist
7475 |             cleared_items = 0
7476 |             
7477 |             # Clear any accumulated results lists (while preserving recent items)
7478 |             if hasattr(self, 'profitable_results') and len(self.profitable_results) > sliding_window_size:
7479 |                 original_count = len(self.profitable_results)
7480 |                 # Keep only the last N items for continuity
7481 |                 self.profitable_results = self.profitable_results[-sliding_window_size:]
7482 |                 cleared_count = original_count - len(self.profitable_results)
7483 |                 cleared_items += cleared_count
7484 |                 self.log.info(f"🧹 MEMORY: Cleared {cleared_count} old profitable results, kept {len(self.profitable_results)} recent items")
7485 |             
7486 |             # Clear product accumulation lists if they exist and are large
7487 |             if hasattr(self, '_current_all_products') and len(self._current_all_products) > sliding_window_size * 2:
7488 |                 # For product lists, we need to be more careful - only clear if we have persistent cache
7489 |                 cache_file_exists = False
7490 |                 try:
7491 |                     cache_file, _ = self._find_actual_supplier_cache_file(self.supplier_name)
7492 |                     cache_file_exists = cache_file and os.path.exists(cache_file)
7493 |                 except:
7494 |                     pass
7495 |                 
7496 |                 if cache_file_exists:
7497 |                     original_count = len(self._current_all_products)
7498 |                     # Keep recent products for continuity
7499 |                     self._current_all_products = self._current_all_products[-sliding_window_size:]
7500 |                     cleared_count = original_count - len(self._current_all_products)
7501 |                     cleared_items += cleared_count
7502 |                     self.log.info(f"🧹 MEMORY: Cleared {cleared_count} old cached products, kept {len(self._current_all_products)} recent items")
7503 |                     self.log.info(f"🧹 MEMORY: Product cache file available for recovery: {cache_file}")
7504 |             
7505 |             # Step 2: Clear any large temporary variables
7506 |             if hasattr(self, '_temp_amazon_data'):
7507 |                 delattr(self, '_temp_amazon_data')
7508 |                 cleared_items += 1
7509 |                 
7510 |             if hasattr(self, '_temp_search_results'):
7511 |                 delattr(self, '_temp_search_results')
7512 |                 cleared_items += 1
7513 |             
7514 |             # Step 3: Force garbage collection
7515 |             collected = gc.collect()
7516 |             
7517 |             # Step 4: Log memory management results
7518 |             self.log.info(f"🧹 SMART MEMORY CLEARED: {cleared_items} data structures, {collected} objects collected")
7519 |             self.log.info(f"🧹 MEMORY CONFIG: clear_frequency={clear_frequency}, sliding_window={sliding_window_size}")
7520 |             self.log.info(f"🧹 MEMORY CONTINUITY: Recent context preserved for debugging and state recovery")

  12 | - Separated resumption index from progress tracking

  91 |     def __init__(self):

  93 |     
  94 |     def inc(self, name: str, **labels) -> None:

  96 |         label_str = " ".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in labels.items())

 100 | class FixedEnhancedStateManager:
 101 |     """
 102 |     Fixed Enhanced State Manager - Solves critical processing state issues with thread safety
 103 | 
 104 |     Key Fixes:
 105 |     1. Separates resumption index from progress tracking
 106 |     2. Only performs reverse gap detection on startup, not every save
 107 |     3. Updates category totals with real-time scraping discoveries
 108 |     4. Fixes metric placement in processing state
 109 |     5. Preserves interruption state correctly
 110 |     6. Thread-safe atomic operations with file locking
 111 |     7. Phase-specific atomic commit methods
 112 |     """
 113 | 
 114 |     SCHEMA_VERSION = "1.2_THREAD_SAFE"
 115 |     _ALLOW_OVERWRITE_ENV = "ALLOW_DENOMINATOR_OVERWRITE"
 116 | 
 117 |     def __init__(self, supplier_name: str, base_path: Optional[str] = None, lock_timeout: float = 5.0):
 118 |         # Ensure instance logger for methods that use self.log
 119 |         self.log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 120 |         self.supplier_name = supplier_name
 121 |         self.lock_timeout = lock_timeout
 122 | 
 123 |         # Use relative base path if not provided
 124 |         if base_path is None:
 125 |             self.base_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent  # Go up to project root
 126 |         else:
 127 |             self.base_path = Path(base_path)
 128 | 
 129 |         self.state_file_path = get_processing_state_path(supplier_name)
 130 |         self.state_data = self._initialize_state()
 131 |         self._startup_completed = False  # Track if startup analysis is done
 132 |         self._last_save_time = 0.0
 133 |         self._drift_mirrored_once = False  # Mirror legacy from system at most once per run
 134 |         
 135 |         # Thread safety components (re-entrant to avoid self-deadlock on nested saves)
 136 |         self._write_lock = threading.RLock() if threading else None
 137 |         self._atomic_writer = ThreadSafeStateWriter(self.state_file_path) if ThreadSafeStateWriter else None
 138 |         
 139 |         # Legacy writer tracking for deprecation
 140 |         self._legacy_writer_calls = 0
 141 |         self._deprecated_methods_used = []
 142 |         
 143 |         # Metrics for observability
 144 |         self.metrics = SimpleMetrics()
 145 |         
 146 |         # one-time phase latches
 147 |         self._phase_first_after_resume_emitted: dict = {}
 148 |         self._phase_resume_honored_emitted: dict = {}
 149 |         
 150 |         # Single-writer proof 
 151 |         self._writer_session_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())
 152 |         
 153 |         # Load system config for toggles (best-effort)
 154 |         self.system_config: Dict[str, Any] = {}
 155 |         try:
 156 |             from config.system_config_loader import SystemConfigLoader
 157 | 
 158 |             self.system_config = SystemConfigLoader().get_system_config()
 159 |         except Exception:
 160 |             self.system_config = {}
 161 | 
 162 |     def enter_runtime_phase(self) -> None:
 163 |         """
 164 |         One-way latch indicating that startup initialization (freezing totals,
 165 |         manifest, config hash, etc.) has completed. After this point, NO save
 166 |         path may call file-grounded recomputation.
 167 |         """
 168 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
 169 |         self._validate_and_clamp_resume_ptr(sp)
 170 |         self._startup_completed = True
 171 |         log.debug("STATE: entered runtime phase")
 172 | 
 173 |     def _initialize_state(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 174 |         """Initialize state structure with all required fields and FIXED architecture"""
 175 |         return {
 176 |             "schema_version": self.SCHEMA_VERSION,
 177 |             "created_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 178 |             "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 179 |             "supplier_name": self.supplier_name,
 180 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX 1: Separate resumption from progress tracking
 181 |             "last_processed_index": 0,  # Legacy field for backward compatibility
 182 |             "resumption_index": 0,  # Where to resume after interruption
 183 |             "progress_index": 0,  # Current progress in active session
 184 |             "session_products_processed": 0,  # Products processed in current session
 185 |             "total_products": 0,
 186 |             "processing_status": "initialized",
 187 |             "is_fresh_start": True,  # 🚨 FIX 1: Add missing fresh start field (corrected by _detect_actual_fresh_start)
 188 |             "category_performance": {},
 189 |             "error_log": [],
 190 |             "successful_products": 0,
 191 |             "profitable_products": 0,
 192 |             "total_profit_found": 0.0,
 193 |             "processing_statistics": {
 194 |                 "start_time": None,
 195 |                 "end_time": None,
 196 |                 "total_runtime_seconds": 0,
 197 |                 "average_time_per_product": 0,
 198 |                 "products_per_hour": 0,
 199 |             },
 200 |             "metadata": {
 201 |                 "version": "3.8+_THREAD_SAFE",
 202 |                 "config_hash": "",
 203 |                 "runtime_settings": {},
 204 |                 "system_info": {},
 205 |                 "fix_applied": "legacy_writer_migration_20250906",
 206 |                 "thread_safety": "enabled",
 207 |                 "atomic_operations": "enabled",
 208 |             },
 209 |             # 🚨 REMOVED: processed_products section - hash search uses linking map + cached products only
 210 |             # Legacy section removed: single source of truth is system_progression only.
 211 |             # Keep empty dict for read-compat with old snapshots.
 212 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX 3: Enhanced gap processing with better tracking
 213 |             "gap_processing": {
 214 |                 "phase": "not_started",
 215 |                 "gap_products_total": 0,
 216 |                 "gap_products_processed": 0,
 217 |                 "gap_start_time": None,
 218 |                 "gap_end_time": None,
 219 |                 "gap_profitable_found": 0,
 220 |                 "gap_last_processed_url": "",
 221 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
 222 |                 "reverse_gap_detected": False,  # 🚨 NEW: Track reverse gap state
 223 |                 "startup_analysis_completed": False,  # 🚨 NEW: Track startup completion
 224 |             },
 225 |             # ✅ NEW: Separated progression metrics for precise resumption
 226 |             "system_progression": {
 227 |                 "current_phase": "supplier",
 228 |                 "current_category_index": 0,
 229 |                 "current_category_url": "",
 230 |                 "total_categories": 0,
 231 |                 "current_product_index_in_category": 0,
 232 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": 0,
 233 |                 "supplier_extraction_resumption_index": 0,
 234 |                 "amazon_analysis_resumption_index": 0,
 235 |             },
 236 |             # ✅ NEW: User-facing metrics (not used for resumption logic)
 237 |             "user_display_metrics": {
 238 |                 "total_products": 0,
 239 |                 "successful_products": 0,
 240 |                 "progress_count": 0,
 241 |                 "session_products_processed": 0,
 242 |             },
 243 |         }
 244 | 
 245 |     def _detect_actual_fresh_start(self):
 246 |         """Enhanced fresh start detection with processed product validation"""
 247 | 
 248 |         # Original flag-based logic
 249 |         flag_fresh_start = self.state_data.get("is_fresh_start", True)
 250 | 
 251 |         # NEW: Validate against actual processed data
 252 |         successful_products = self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
 253 |         global_counters = self.state_data.get("global_counters", {})
 254 |         total_processed = global_counters.get("total_products_processed", 0)
 255 |         system_progression = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 256 |         current_category = system_progression.get("current_category_index")
 257 | 
 258 |         # True fresh start criteria: No processed products AND no category progress
 259 |         actual_fresh_start = (
 260 |             successful_products == 0 and total_processed == 0 and current_category is None
 261 |         )
 262 | 
 263 |         # Detect and log contradictions
 264 |         if flag_fresh_start != actual_fresh_start:
 265 |             log.warning(
 266 |                 f"🚨 FRESH START CONTRADICTION DETECTED: "
 267 |                 f"flag={flag_fresh_start} actual={actual_fresh_start} "
 268 |                 f"products={successful_products} processed={total_processed} "
 269 |                 f"category={current_category}"
 270 |             )
 271 | 
 272 |             # Use actual state rather than flag
 273 |             self.state_data["is_fresh_start"] = actual_fresh_start
 274 | 
 275 |         return actual_fresh_start
 276 | 
 277 |     def load_state(self) -> bool:
 278 |         """
 279 |         Load existing state from file, with backward compatibility
 280 |         Returns True if state was loaded, False if starting fresh
 281 |         """
 282 |         if not self.state_file_path.exists():
 283 |             log.info(f"No existing state file found for {self.supplier_name}, starting fresh")
 284 |             return False
 285 | 
 286 |         try:
 287 |             with open(self.state_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 288 |                 loaded_data = json.load(f)
 289 | 
 290 |             # Handle backward compatibility
 291 |             if isinstance(loaded_data, dict) and "schema_version" not in loaded_data:
 292 |                 log.info("Converting legacy state format to fixed enhanced format")
 293 |                 self._migrate_legacy_state(loaded_data)
 294 |             else:
 295 |                 # Merge loaded data with initialized structure
 296 |                 self._merge_state_data(loaded_data)
 297 | 
 298 |             log.info(
 299 |                 f"Loaded state for {self.supplier_name} - resumption from index {self.state_data['resumption_index']}"
 300 |             )
 301 |             # 🧹 MIGRATION SCRUB: remove legacy subtree if present
 302 |             if isinstance(self.state_data, dict) and "supplier_extraction_progress" in self.state_data:
 303 |                 try:
 304 |                     del self.state_data["supplier_extraction_progress"]
 305 |                     log.info("🧹 Removed legacy 'supplier_extraction_progress' from state on load")
 306 |                     self.save_state_atomic()
 307 |                 except Exception as e:
 308 |                     log.warning(f"⚠️ Could not remove legacy subtree: {e}")
 309 |             return True
 310 | 
 311 |         except Exception as e:
 312 |             log.warning(f"Failed to load state file: {e}, starting fresh")
 313 |             return False
 314 | 
 315 |     def _migrate_legacy_state(self, legacy_data: Dict[str, Any]):
 316 |         """Migrate legacy state format to fixed enhanced format"""
 317 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Properly migrate legacy index to resumption_index
 318 |         legacy_index = legacy_data.get("last_processed_index", 0)
 319 |         self.state_data["last_processed_index"] = legacy_index
 320 |         self.state_data["resumption_index"] = legacy_index
 321 |         self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0  # Always start fresh progress
 322 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "migrated_from_legacy"
 323 | 
 324 |         # Migrate other legacy fields if they exist
 325 |         # Legacy subtree removed; no legacy-specific migration beyond indices
 326 |         log.info(f"Successfully migrated legacy state format - resumption index: {legacy_index}")
 327 | 
 328 |     def _merge_state_data(self, loaded_data: Dict[str, Any]):
 329 |         """Merge loaded data with initialized structure"""
 330 | 
 331 |         def deep_merge(base: Dict, overlay: Dict) -> Dict:
 332 |             result = base.copy()
 333 |             for key, value in overlay.items():
 334 |                 if key in result and isinstance(result[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
 335 |                     result[key] = deep_merge(result[key], value)
 336 |                 else:
 337 |                     result[key] = value
 338 |             return result
 339 | 
 340 |         self.state_data = deep_merge(self.state_data, loaded_data)
 341 |         self.state_data["last_updated"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
 342 | 
 343 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Ensure new fields exist even in merged data
 344 |         if "resumption_index" not in self.state_data:
 345 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0)
 346 |         if "progress_index" not in self.state_data:
 347 |             self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
 348 |         if "session_products_processed" not in self.state_data:
 349 |             self.state_data["session_products_processed"] = 0
 350 |         
 351 |         # 🚨 CROSS-RUN MONOTONICITY GUARD: Validate resumption pointer never decreases between runs
 352 |         self._validate_cross_run_monotonicity()
 353 | 
 354 |     def _validate_cross_run_monotonicity(self) -> None:
 355 |         """Validate that resumption indices never decrease between runs (prevents 4→1 rollbacks)."""
 356 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 357 |         
 358 |         # Get current indices
 359 |         current_cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
 360 |         current_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
 361 |         current_resumption = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 362 |         
 363 |         # Get stored previous run values (persistent across sessions)
 364 |         last_known_cat = self.state_data.get("_last_known_category_index", 0)
 365 |         last_known_prod = self.state_data.get("_last_known_product_index", 0)
 366 |         last_known_resumption = self.state_data.get("_last_known_resumption_index", 0)
 367 |         
 368 |         # Check for regression
 369 |         regression_detected = False
 370 |         
 371 |         if current_cat_idx < last_known_cat:
 372 |             log.error(f"🚨 CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Category index {current_cat_idx} < previous {last_known_cat}")
 373 |             regression_detected = True
 374 |             
 375 |         if current_cat_idx == last_known_cat and current_prod_idx < last_known_prod:
 376 |             log.error(f"🚨 CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Product index {current_prod_idx} < previous {last_known_prod}")
 377 |             regression_detected = True
 378 |             
 379 |         if current_resumption < last_known_resumption:
 380 |             log.error(f"🚨 CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Resumption index {current_resumption} < previous {last_known_resumption}")
 381 |             regression_detected = True
 382 |             
 383 |         if regression_detected:
 384 |             log.warning("🔧 CORRECTING CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Restoring previous indices")
 385 |             # Restore to safe previous values
 386 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max(current_cat_idx, last_known_cat)
 387 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = max(current_prod_idx, last_known_prod)
 388 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = max(current_resumption, last_known_resumption)
 389 |             
 390 |         # Update stored values for next run
 391 |         self.state_data["_last_known_category_index"] = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
 392 |         self.state_data["_last_known_product_index"] = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
 393 |         self.state_data["_last_known_resumption_index"] = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 394 | 
 395 |     def perform_startup_analysis(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 396 |         """
 397 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Perform reverse gap detection and category analysis ONLY on startup
 398 |         This method should be called ONCE at the beginning of a session, not on every save
 399 |         """
 400 |         if self._startup_completed:
 401 |             log.info("Startup analysis already completed for this session")
 402 |             return self.state_data.get("gap_processing", {}).get("category_completion_status", {})
 403 | 
 404 |         log.info("🔍 STARTUP ANALYSIS: Beginning comprehensive state analysis...")
 405 | 
 406 |         # Calculate file-grounded totals
 407 |         file_grounded_data = self._calculate_file_grounded_totals()
 408 | 
 409 |         # Toggle: allow disabling reverse gap heuristic entirely via config
 410 |         use_reverse_gap_heuristic = (
 411 |             self.system_config.get("pipeline_toggles", {})
 412 |             .get("resume", {})
 413 |             .get("use_reverse_gap_heuristic", False)
 414 |         )
 415 | 
 416 |         if not use_reverse_gap_heuristic:
 417 |             # Deterministic resume: use linking map count as resume index
 418 |             start_at = int(file_grounded_data.get("linking_map_count", 0))
 419 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = start_at
 420 |             self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
 421 |             self.state_data.setdefault("gap_processing", {})["reverse_gap_detected"] = False
 422 |             self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "system_progression"
 423 |             log.info(f"RESUME DECISION: START_AT_INDEX={start_at} (reason: system_progression)")
 424 |         # 🚨 REVERSE GAP POLICY FIX: Only perform reverse gap detection on startup
 425 |         elif file_grounded_data["linking_map_count"] > file_grounded_data["total_products"]:
 426 |             log.info(
 427 |                 f"🔄 REVERSE GAP DETECTED: Linking map ({file_grounded_data['linking_map_count']}) > Cache ({file_grounded_data['total_products']})"
 428 |             )
 429 | 
 430 |             # Check if we should preserve existing resume index or reset
 431 |             current_resumption_index = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 432 |             explicit_cache_rebuild = self.state_data.get("force_cache_rebuild", False)
 433 | 
 434 |             if explicit_cache_rebuild:
 435 |                 # Only reset to 0 if explicitly rebuilding cache
 436 |                 self.state_data["resumption_index"] = 0
 437 |                 self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "reverse_gap_cache_rebuild"
 438 |                 log.info(
 439 |                     f"✅ REVERSE GAP: Reset resumption_index = 0 (explicit cache rebuild requested)"
 440 |                 )
 441 |             elif current_resumption_index == 0:
 442 |                 # If resumption_index is 0, check if this is truly a fresh start or a restart
 443 |                 # 🚨 FIX 1: Use enhanced fresh start detection instead of manual logic
 444 |                 is_actual_fresh_start = self._detect_actual_fresh_start()
 445 | 
 446 |                 if not is_actual_fresh_start:
 447 |                     # This appears to be a restart, not a fresh start - preserve some progress
 448 |                     log.warning(
 449 |                         f"🔄 REVERSE GAP: Detected restart with resumption_index=0 but previous state exists - preserving index"
 450 |                     )
 451 |                     self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "reverse_gap_restart_preserved"
 452 |                 else:
 453 |                     # Truly fresh start
 454 |                     self.state_data["resumption_index"] = 0
 455 |                     self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "reverse_gap_fresh_start"
 456 |                     log.info(f"✅ REVERSE GAP: Fresh start confirmed - resumption_index = 0")
 457 |             else:
 458 |                 # Preserve existing resume index to avoid restarting from first category
 459 |                 log.warning(
 460 |                     f"🔄 REVERSE GAP: Preserving existing resumption_index = {current_resumption_index} (no explicit rebuild)"
 461 |                 )
 462 |                 self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "reverse_gap_preserved_resume"
 463 | 
 464 |             self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
 465 |             self.state_data["processing_status"] = "FRESH_CATEGORIES"
 466 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["reverse_gap_detected"] = True
 467 |         else:
 468 |             # Normal gap processing - resume from linking map count
 469 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = file_grounded_data["linking_map_count"]
 470 |             self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0  # Always start fresh progress tracking
 471 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["reverse_gap_detected"] = False
 472 | 
 473 |             # Track normal resume path
 474 |             self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "normal_startup"
 475 | 
 476 |             log.info(
 477 |                 f"✅ Normal startup - resumption_index = {file_grounded_data['linking_map_count']}"
 478 |             )
 479 | 
 480 |         # Log final resume decision for observability (if not already logged)
 481 |         if "resume_reason" in self.state_data and "resumption_index" in self.state_data:
 482 |             log.info(
 483 |                 f"RESUME DECISION: START_AT_INDEX={self.state_data['resumption_index']} (reason: {self.state_data['resume_reason']})"
 484 |             )
 485 | 
 486 |         # Update total products from file-grounded data
 487 |         self.state_data["total_products"] = file_grounded_data["total_products"]
 488 |         self.state_data["successful_products"] = file_grounded_data["processed_products"]
 489 | 
 490 |         # Update category completion status
 491 |         if file_grounded_data["category_completion_status"]:
 492 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"] = file_grounded_data[
 493 |                 "category_completion_status"
 494 |             ]
 495 | 
 496 |         # Legacy subtree deprecated by directive; do not update legacy sections
 497 | 
 498 |         # Mark startup analysis as completed
 499 |         self._startup_completed = True
 500 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["startup_analysis_completed"] = True
 501 | 
 502 |         # Save the startup analysis results
 503 |         self.save_state(preserve_interruption_state=False)
 504 | 
 505 |         log.info("✅ STARTUP ANALYSIS: Completed comprehensive state analysis")
 506 |         return self.state_data.get("gap_processing", {}).get("category_completion_status", {})
 507 | 
 508 |     def force_cache_rebuild(self, reason: str = "manual_request"):
 509 |         """
 510 |         🚨 REVERSE GAP POLICY: Explicitly force cache rebuild and reset resume index
 511 |         This should only be called when intentionally rebuilding the cache
 512 |         """
 513 |         log.info(f"🔄 FORCE CACHE REBUILD: {reason}")
 514 |         self.state_data["force_cache_rebuild"] = True
 515 |         self.state_data["resumption_index"] = 0
 516 |         self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
 517 |         self.state_data["resume_reason"] = f"force_rebuild_{reason}"
 518 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "FRESH_CATEGORIES"
 519 | 
 520 |         # Clear startup completion to trigger fresh analysis
 521 |         self._startup_completed = False
 522 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["startup_analysis_completed"] = False
 523 | 
 524 |         log.info("✅ Cache rebuild forced - resumption_index reset to 0")
 525 |     
 526 |     def _validate_cross_run_monotonicity(self):
 527 |         """
 528 |         🚨 CROSS-RUN MONOTONICITY GUARD: Ensures resumption pointer never decreases between runs
 529 |         
 530 |         This guard prevents non-monotonic resumption pointer regression that was observed
 531 |         in the rejection feedback (8→7 regression across runs). It validates that the
 532 |         loaded resumption pointer is not behind any previously recorded maximum.
 533 |         """
 534 |         current_resumption_index = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 535 |         
 536 |         # Get resumption pointer from system_progression if available
 537 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 538 |         resumption_ptr = sp.get("resumption_ptr", {})
 539 |         loaded_prod_idx = resumption_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0)
 540 |         
 541 |         # Check for cross-run regression
 542 |         if loaded_prod_idx > 0 and current_resumption_index < loaded_prod_idx:
 543 |             log.error(
 544 |                 f"🚨 CROSS-RUN MONOTONICITY VIOLATION: "
 545 |                 f"Loaded resumption_index ({current_resumption_index}) < "
 546 |                 f"previous resumption_ptr.prod_idx ({loaded_prod_idx}) - "
 547 |                 f"This violates monotonicity invariant!"
 548 |             )
 549 |             
 550 |             # Corrective action: Use the higher value to maintain monotonicity
 551 |             corrected_index = max(current_resumption_index, loaded_prod_idx)
 552 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = corrected_index
 553 |             
 554 |             # Log the correction and mark for attention
 555 |             log.warning(
 556 |                 f"🔧 MONOTONICITY REPAIR: Corrected resumption_index to {corrected_index} "
 557 |                 f"to maintain cross-run monotonicity invariant"
 558 |             )
 559 |             
 560 |             # Add violation tracking for audit purposes
 561 |             self.state_data.setdefault("monotonicity_violations", []).append({
 562 |                 "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 563 |                 "violation_type": "cross_run_regression", 
 564 |                 "loaded_index": current_resumption_index,
 565 |                 "previous_max": loaded_prod_idx,
 566 |                 "corrected_to": corrected_index,
 567 |                 "violation_evidence": f"{loaded_prod_idx}→{current_resumption_index} regression detected"
 568 |             })
 569 |             
 570 |             log.info(
 571 |                 f"✅ MONOTONICITY GUARD: Resumption pointer validated and corrected. "
 572 |                 f"Cross-run invariant maintained: {corrected_index} >= {loaded_prod_idx}"
 573 |             )
 574 |         else:
 575 |             log.info(
 576 |                 f"✅ MONOTONICITY GUARD: Cross-run validation passed. "
 577 |                 f"resumption_index={current_resumption_index} >= previous_max={loaded_prod_idx}"
 578 |             )
 579 | 
 580 |     def validate_and_repair_state(self) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
 581 |         """
 582 |         🚨 STATE VALIDATION: Validate state consistency and repair issues
 583 |         Returns (is_valid, repairs_made)
 584 |         """
 585 |         repairs_made = []
 586 | 
 587 |         # Ensure required keys exist
 588 |         required_keys = [
 589 |             "resumption_index",
 590 |             "progress_index",
 591 |             "total_products",
 592 |             "processing_status",
 593 |         ]
 594 |         for key in required_keys:
 595 |             if key not in self.state_data:
 596 |                 self.state_data[key] = (
 597 |                     0 if key.endswith("_index") or key == "total_products" else "initialized"
 598 |                 )
 599 |                 repairs_made.append(f"Added missing key: {key}")
 600 | 
 601 |         # Ensure resumption_index is within bounds and monotonic
 602 |         resumption_index = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 603 |         total_products = self.state_data.get("total_products", 0)
 604 | 
 605 |         if resumption_index < 0:
 606 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = 0
 607 |             repairs_made.append("Fixed negative resumption_index")
 608 | 
 609 |         if total_products > 0 and resumption_index > total_products:
 610 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = total_products
 611 |             repairs_made.append(
 612 |                 f"Fixed resumption_index bounds: {resumption_index} -> {total_products}"
 613 |             )
 614 | 
 615 |         # Ensure gap_processing structure exists
 616 |         if "gap_processing" not in self.state_data:
 617 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"] = {
 618 |                 "reverse_gap_detected": False,
 619 |                 "startup_analysis_completed": False,
 620 |             }
 621 |             repairs_made.append("Added missing gap_processing structure")
 622 | 
 623 |         # Ensure system_progression exists for breadcrumbs
 624 |         if "system_progression" not in self.state_data:
 625 |             self.state_data["system_progression"] = {
 626 |                 "current_phase": "supplier",
 627 |                 "current_category_index": 0,
 628 |                 "current_category_url": "",
 629 |                 "total_categories": 0,
 630 |                 "current_product_index_in_category": 0,
 631 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": 0,
 632 |                 "supplier_extraction_resumption_index": resumption_index,
 633 |                 "amazon_analysis_resumption_index": 0,
 634 |             }
 635 |             repairs_made.append("Added missing system_progression structure")
 636 | 
 637 |         # Log repairs if any were made
 638 |         if repairs_made:
 639 |             log.info(f"State repaired: {', '.join(repairs_made)}")
 640 | 
 641 |         return len(repairs_made) == 0, repairs_made
 642 | 
 643 |     def update_discovered_products_in_category(self, category_url: str, discovered_count: int):
 644 |         """
 645 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX 4: Update category totals with real-time scraping discoveries
 646 |         This method should be called when the scraper discovers more products than expected
 647 |         """
 648 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 649 |         current_total = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
 650 | 
 651 |         if discovered_count > current_total:
 652 |             log.info(
 653 |                 f"🔍 REAL-TIME DISCOVERY: Category {category_url[:50]}... discovered {discovered_count} products (was {current_total})"
 654 |             )
 655 | 
 656 |             # Use normalized URL for consistent key comparison
 657 |             from utils.normalization import normalize_url
 658 | 
 659 |             normalized_category_url = normalize_url(category_url)
 660 | 
 661 |             # Update the category completion status if it exists
 662 |             if (
 663 |                 "gap_processing" in self.state_data
 664 |                 and "category_completion_status" in self.state_data["gap_processing"]
 665 |             ):
 666 |                 if category_url in self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"]:
 667 |                     self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
 668 |                         "extracted"
 669 |                     ] = discovered_count
 670 |                     # Recalculate completion percentage
