2164 |                     return self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
2165 |             else:
2166 |                 log.warning(f"Linking map file not found at {linking_map_path}")
2167 |                 return self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
2168 |                 
2169 |         except Exception as e:
2170 |             log.error(f"Error in _calculate_total_processed: {e}")
2171 |             return self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
2172 | 
2173 |     def _calculate_file_grounded_totals(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2174 |         """
2175 |         Calculate all totals by reading actual files on disk
2176 |         Returns accurate counts based on real-time file contents
2177 |         """
2178 |         try:
2179 |             # Initialize counters
2180 |             file_grounded_data = {
2181 |                 "total_products": 0,
2182 |                 "processed_products": 0,
2183 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
2184 |                 "linking_map_count": 0,
2185 |                 "cache_file_exists": False,
2186 |                 "linking_map_exists": False,
2187 |                 "total_categories": 0,
2188 |                 "current_category_analysis": {},
2189 |             }
2190 | 
2191 |             # Calculate project root path relative to current file location
2192 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2193 | 
2194 |             # Path to product cache file - use hyphenated domain format
2195 |             hyphenated_supplier = self.supplier_name.replace(".", "-")
2196 |             cache_file_path = (
2197 |                 current_dir
2198 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2199 |                 / "cached_products"
2200 |                 / f"{hyphenated_supplier}_products_cache.json"
2201 |             )
2202 | 
2203 |             # Path to linking map file - use dotted domain format
2204 |             supplier_domain = self.supplier_name.replace("-", ".")
2205 |             linking_map_path = (
2206 |                 current_dir
2207 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2208 |                 / "FBA_ANALYSIS"
2209 |                 / "linking_maps"
2210 |                 / supplier_domain
2211 |                 / "linking_map.json"
2212 |             )
2213 | 
2214 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Exclude metadata entries from product count
2215 |             if cache_file_path.exists():
2216 |                 file_grounded_data["cache_file_exists"] = True
2217 |                 try:
2218 |                     with open(cache_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2219 |                         product_cache = json.load(f)
2220 | 
2221 |                     # Filter out metadata entries
2222 |                     actual_products = [
2223 |                         p
2224 |                         for p in product_cache
2225 |                         if isinstance(p, dict) and not p.get("_cache_metadata")
2226 |                     ]
2227 |                     file_grounded_data["total_products"] = len(actual_products)
2228 | 
2229 |                     log.info(
2230 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {len(actual_products)} actual products in cache (total entries: {len(product_cache)})"
2231 |                     )
2232 |                 except Exception as e:
2233 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read product cache: {e}")
2234 | 
2235 |             # Read linking map to get processed count
2236 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2237 |                 file_grounded_data["linking_map_exists"] = True
2238 |                 try:
2239 |                     with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2240 |                         linking_map = json.load(f)
2241 |                     file_grounded_data["linking_map_count"] = len(linking_map)
2242 |                     file_grounded_data["processed_products"] = len(linking_map)
2243 |                     log.info(
2244 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {len(linking_map)} processed products in linking map"
2245 |                     )
2246 |                 except Exception as e:
2247 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read linking map: {e}")
2248 | 
2249 |             # Calculate category completion from existing files
2250 |             file_grounded_data[
2251 |                 "category_completion_status"
2252 |             ] = self._calculate_startup_category_analysis(
2253 |                 cache_file_path, linking_map_path, current_dir
2254 |             )
2255 | 
2256 |             # Load config to get total categories
2257 |             config_path = current_dir / "config" / "poundwholesale_categories.json"
2258 |             if config_path.exists():
2259 |                 try:
2260 |                     with open(config_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2261 |                         config_data = json.load(f)
2262 |                     if isinstance(config_data, list):
2263 |                         file_grounded_data["total_categories"] = len(config_data)
2264 |                     elif isinstance(config_data, dict) and "category_urls" in config_data:
2265 |                         file_grounded_data["total_categories"] = len(config_data["category_urls"])
2266 |                     log.info(
2267 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {file_grounded_data['total_categories']} categories in config"
2268 |                     )
2269 |                 except Exception as e:
2270 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read config file: {e}")
2271 | 
2272 |             return file_grounded_data
2273 | 
2274 |         except Exception as e:
2275 |             log.error(f"Failed to calculate file-grounded totals: {e}")
2276 |             return {
2277 |                 "total_products": self.state_data.get("total_products", 0),
2278 |                 "processed_products": self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0),
2279 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
2280 |                 "linking_map_count": 0,
2281 |                 "cache_file_exists": False,
2282 |                 "linking_map_exists": False,
2283 |                 "total_categories": 0,
2284 |                 "current_category_analysis": {},
2285 |             }
2286 | 
2287 |     def _calculate_startup_category_analysis(
2288 |         self, cache_file_path: Path, linking_map_path: Path, current_dir: Path
2289 |     ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2290 |         """
2291 |         Calculate category completion status from existing cache and linking map files
2292 |         """
2293 |         try:
2294 |             category_completion = {}
2295 | 
2296 |             # Load cache data
2297 |             cache_data = []
2298 |             if cache_file_path.exists():
2299 |                 with open(cache_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2300 |                     cache_data = json.load(f)
2301 | 
2302 |             # Load linking map data
2303 |             linking_data = []
2304 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2305 |                 with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2306 |                     linking_data = json.load(f)
2307 | 
2308 |             # Build category extraction status from cache
2309 |             extracted_categories = defaultdict(list)
2310 |             missing = 0
2311 |             for product in cache_data:
2312 |                 if not (isinstance(product, dict) and product.get("url") and not product.get("_cache_metadata")):
2313 |                     continue
2314 |                 src = product.get("source_url")
2315 |                 if not src:
2316 |                     missing += 1
2317 |                     continue
2318 |                 extracted_categories[src].append(product["url"])
2319 | 
2320 |             if missing:
2321 |                 log.warning(f"⚠️ Cache entries missing source_url: {missing} (ignored in category analysis)")
2322 |                 try:
2323 |                     self._metrics["missing_source_url_ignored_total"] = (
2324 |                         self._metrics.get("missing_source_url_ignored_total", 0) + missing
2325 |                     )
2326 |                 except Exception:
2327 |                     pass
2328 | 
2329 |             # Build processed product mapping from linking map
2330 |             processed_products = set()
2331 |             for entry in linking_data:
2332 |                 if isinstance(entry, dict) and "supplier_url" in entry:
2333 |                     processed_products.add(entry["supplier_url"])
2334 | 
2335 |             # Calculate category completion status
2336 |             for category_url, product_urls in extracted_categories.items():
2337 |                 extracted_count = len(product_urls)
2338 |                 processed_count = len([url for url in product_urls if url in processed_products])
2339 |                 completion_pct = (
2340 |                     (processed_count / extracted_count * 100) if extracted_count > 0 else 0
2341 |                 )
2342 | 
2343 |                 if processed_count >= extracted_count:
2344 |                     status = "FULLY_PROCESSED"
2345 |                 elif processed_count > 0:
2346 |                     status = "PARTIALLY_PROCESSED"
2347 |                 else:
2348 |                     status = "EXTRACTED_ONLY"
2349 | 
2350 |                 category_completion[category_url] = {
2351 |                     "extracted": extracted_count,
2352 |                     "processed": processed_count,
2353 |                     "completion_pct": completion_pct,
2354 |                     "status": status,
2355 |                 }
2356 | 
2357 |             log.info(
2358 |                 f"Startup category analysis: Calculated completion for {len(category_completion)} categories"
2359 |             )
2360 |             return category_completion
2361 | 
2362 |         except Exception as e:
2363 |             log.warning(f"Failed to calculate startup category analysis: {e}")
2364 |             return {}
2365 | 
2366 |     def _calculate_current_category_metrics(
2367 |         self, file_grounded_data: Dict[str, Any]
2368 |     ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2369 |         """
2370 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX 7: Calculate current category metrics with correct indexing
2371 |         Returns metrics for the current category with proper index calculation
2372 |         """
2373 |         try:
2374 |             category_completion = file_grounded_data.get("category_completion_status", {})
2375 |             total_categories = file_grounded_data.get("total_categories", 0)
2376 | 
2377 |             # Find the current category that needs processing
2378 |             current_category_url = ""
2379 |             current_category_index = 0
2380 | 
2381 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Load config file to get proper category order and index
2382 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2383 |             config_path = current_dir / "config" / "poundwholesale_categories.json"
2384 | 
2385 |             if config_path.exists():
2386 |                 try:
2387 |                     with open(config_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2388 |                         config_data = json.load(f)
2389 | 
2390 |                     # Get category URLs in config order
2391 |                     if isinstance(config_data, list):
2392 |                         config_urls = config_data
2393 |                     elif isinstance(config_data, dict) and "category_urls" in config_data:
2394 |                         config_urls = config_data["category_urls"]
2395 |                     else:
2396 |                         config_urls = []
2397 | 
2398 |                     # Find current category based on config order and processing status
2399 |                     priority_order = ["EXTRACTED_ONLY", "PARTIALLY_PROCESSED", "FULLY_PROCESSED"]
2400 | 
2401 |                     for status in priority_order:
2402 |                         for i, config_url in enumerate(config_urls):
2403 |                             if (
2404 |                                 config_url in category_completion
2405 |                                 and category_completion[config_url].get("status") == status
2406 |                             ):
2407 |                                 current_category_url = config_url
2408 |                                 current_category_index = (
2409 |                                     i  # 🚨 FIXED: Use actual index from config file
2410 |                                 )
2411 |                                 break
2412 |                         if current_category_url:
2413 |                             break
2414 | 
2415 |                 except Exception as e:
2416 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read config for category indexing: {e}")
2417 | 
2418 |             # Calculate current category metrics
2419 |             current_category_info = category_completion.get(current_category_url, {})
2420 |             total_products_in_current_category = current_category_info.get("extracted", 0)
2421 |             current_product_index_in_category = current_category_info.get("processed", 0)
2422 | 
2423 |             # Count completed categories
2424 |             completed_categories = []
2425 |             for url, info in category_completion.items():
2426 |                 if info.get("status") == "FULLY_PROCESSED":
2427 |                     completed_categories.append(url)
2428 | 
2429 |             # Determine extraction phase based on state
2430 |             if file_grounded_data.get("linking_map_count", 0) > file_grounded_data.get(
2431 |                 "total_products", 0
2432 |             ):
2433 |                 extraction_phase = "fresh_categories"
2434 |             elif current_product_index_in_category < total_products_in_current_category:
2435 |                 extraction_phase = "amazon_analysis"
2436 |             else:
2437 |                 extraction_phase = "products"
2438 | 
2439 |             return {
2440 |                 "current_category_url": current_category_url,
2441 |                 "current_category_index": current_category_index,  # 🚨 FIXED: Correct index from config
2442 |                 "total_categories": total_categories,
2443 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": total_products_in_current_category,
2444 |                 "current_product_index_in_category": current_product_index_in_category,
2445 |                 "extraction_phase": extraction_phase,
2446 |                 "last_completed_category": completed_categories[-1] if completed_categories else "",
2447 |                 "categories_completed": completed_categories,
2448 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
2449 |             }
2450 | 
2451 |         except Exception as e:
2452 |             log.error(f"Failed to calculate current category metrics: {e}")
2453 |             return {}
2454 | 
2455 |     def mark_category_completed(self, category_url: str):
2456 |         """Mark a category as completed - resumption_index now updates continuously"""
2457 |         # Update category completion status
2458 |         if (
2459 |             "gap_processing" in self.state_data
2460 |             and "category_completion_status" in self.state_data["gap_processing"]
2461 |         ):
2462 |             if category_url in self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"]:
2463 |                 self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
2464 |                     "status"
2465 |                 ] = "FULLY_PROCESSED"
2466 |                 self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
2467 |                     "completion_pct"
2468 |                 ] = 100.0
2469 | 
2470 |         # Legacy subtree deprecated; do not update legacy sections
2471 | 
2472 |         # Note: resumption_index now updates continuously via update_processing_progress()
2473 |         # No need to update it here as it's always current
2474 | 
2475 |         log.info(f"✅ Category marked as completed: {category_url[:50]}...")
2476 | 
2477 |     def get_resumption_index(self) -> int:
2478 |         """Get the index from which to resume processing"""
2479 |         return self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
2480 | 
2481 |     def get_current_progress(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
2482 |         """Get current session progress"""
2483 |         return {
2484 |             "resumption_index": self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0),
2485 |             "progress_index": self.state_data.get("progress_index", 0),
2486 |             "session_products_processed": self.state_data.get("session_products_processed", 0),
2487 |             "last_processed_index": self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0),
2488 |         }
2489 | 
2490 |     # === WORKFLOW COMPATIBILITY METHODS ===
2491 |     # These methods provide compatibility with the existing workflow
2492 | 
2493 |     def update_processing_index(self, current_index: int, total_products: int):
2494 |         """
2495 |         ❌ DEPRECATED AND DISABLED.
2496 |         
2497 |         This method is banned due to state corruption issues. Use phase-specific
2498 |         atomic commit methods instead:
2499 |         - commit_supplier_progress() for supplier extraction phase
2500 |         - commit_amazon_progress() for Amazon analysis phase
2501 |         """
2502 |         raise NotImplementedError(
2503 |             "update_processing_index() is deprecated and must not be used. "
2504 |             "Use commit_supplier_progress() or commit_amazon_progress() instead."
2505 |         )
2506 | 
2507 |     def start_processing(self, config_hash: str, runtime_settings: Dict[str, Any]):
2508 |         """Start processing session"""
2509 |         self.state_data["config_hash"] = config_hash
2510 |         self.state_data["runtime_settings"] = runtime_settings
2511 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "active"
2512 |         self.state_data["session_start_time"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2513 | 
2514 |     def complete_processing(self):
2515 |         """Mark processing as complete"""
2516 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "completed"
2517 |         self.state_data["session_end_time"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2518 |         # Use atomic save instead of legacy save_state()
2519 |         self.save_state_atomic()
2520 | 
2521 |     def is_product_processed(self, product_url: str) -> bool:
2522 |         """
2523 |         🚨 DEPRECATED: This method should not be used - linking map is the source of truth
2524 |         Hash lookup should check linking map directly, not processing state
2525 | 
2526 |         Returns False to ensure products flow through correct filtering workflow
2527 |         """
2528 |         # 🚨 Always return False - linking map filtering is the correct approach
2529 |         return False
2530 | 
2531 |     def mark_product_processed(self, product_url: str, status: str):
2532 |         """
2533 |         🚨 DEPRECATED: This method should not be used - linking map entries are the completion signal
2534 |         Processing completion is tracked via linking map entries, not processing state
2535 | 
2536 |         This method is now a no-op to prevent incorrect state tracking
2537 |         """
2538 |         # 🚨 No-op: completion tracking happens in linking map, not processing state
2539 |         pass
2540 | 
2541 |     def get_state_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2542 |         """Get summary of current state"""
2543 |         return {
2544 |             "supplier_name": self.supplier_name,
2545 |             "processing_status": self.state_data.get("processing_status", "not_started"),
2546 |             "resumption_index": self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0),
2547 |             "progress_index": self.state_data.get("progress_index", 0),
2548 |             "session_products_processed": self.state_data.get("session_products_processed", 0),
2549 |             # 🚨 REMOVED: processed_products count - linking map is source of truth
2550 |             "last_update": self.state_data.get("last_update"),
2551 |         }
2552 | 
2553 |     def start_gap_processing(
2554 |         self, gap_size: int, linking_map_count: int, supplier_cache_count: int
2555 |     ):
2556 |         """Start gap processing session"""
2557 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"] = {
2558 |             "status": "active",
2559 |             "gap_size": gap_size,
2560 |             "linking_map_count": linking_map_count,
2561 |             "supplier_cache_count": supplier_cache_count,
2562 |             "start_time": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
2563 |         }
2564 | 
2565 |     def complete_gap_processing(self):
2566 |         """Complete gap processing session"""
2567 |         if "gap_processing" in self.state_data:
2568 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["status"] = "completed"
2569 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["end_time"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2570 | 
2571 |     def update_gap_processing_progress(self, processed_count: int, total_gap: int):
2572 |         """Update gap processing progress"""
2573 |         if "gap_processing" not in self.state_data:
2574 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"] = {}
2575 | 
2576 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["processed_count"] = processed_count
2577 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["total_gap"] = total_gap
2578 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["last_update"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2579 | 
2580 |     def update_supplier_progress(
2581 |         self,
2582 |         category_index: int,
2583 |         total_categories: int,
2584 |         subcategory_index: int = None,
2585 |         total_subcategories: int = None,
2586 |         batch_number: int = None,
2587 |         total_batches: int = None,
2588 |         category_url: str = None,
2589 |         extraction_phase: str = None,
2590 |     ):
2591 |         """Update only system_progression and persist (debounced)."""
2592 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
2593 |         if category_index is not None:
2594 |             try:
2595 |                 sp["current_category_index"] = max(0, int(category_index))
2596 |             except Exception:
2597 |                 sp["current_category_index"] = 0
2598 |         if extraction_phase is not None:
2599 |             phase_map = {
2600 |                 "fresh_categories": "supplier",
2601 |                 "amazon_analysis": "amazon_analysis",
2602 |                 "completed": "completed",
2603 |             }
2604 |             sp["current_phase"] = phase_map.get(str(extraction_phase), str(extraction_phase))
2605 |         if category_url is not None:
2606 |             try:
2607 |                 from utils.normalization import normalize_url
2608 | 
2609 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = normalize_url(category_url)
2610 |                 sp["original_category_url"] = category_url
2611 |             except Exception:
2612 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = category_url
2613 |         if total_categories is not None:
2614 |             try:
2615 |                 sp["total_categories"] = max(1, int(total_categories))
2616 |             except Exception:
2617 |                 sp["total_categories"] = max(1, sp.get("total_categories", 1))
2618 |         # Save (debounced)
2619 |         self.save_debounced(note="progress", min_interval=0.3)
2620 | 
2621 |     def update_success_metrics(self, amazon_success: bool, profitable: bool, profit: float = 0):
2622 |         """Update success metrics"""
2623 |         metrics = self.state_data.get(
2624 |             "success_metrics",
2625 |             {"amazon_extractions": 0, "profitable_products": 0, "total_profit": 0},
2626 |         )
2627 | 
2628 |         if amazon_success:
2629 |             metrics["amazon_extractions"] += 1
2630 | 
2631 |         if profitable:
2632 |             metrics["profitable_products"] += 1
2633 |             metrics["total_profit"] += profit
2634 | 
2635 |         self.state_data["success_metrics"] = metrics
2636 | 
2637 |     def log_resume_proof_after_commit(self, commit_type: str) -> None:
2638 |         """RESUME PROOF LOG: Log with specific banners for audit trail verification."""
2639 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
2640 |         cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
2641 |         prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
2642 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
2643 |         total_prod = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
2644 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase", "supplier")
2645 |         
2646 |         # Check if this is the first commit after resume
2647 |         is_first_after_resume = not hasattr(self, '_first_commit_logged')
2648 |         
2649 |         # Get appropriate logger
2650 |         if hasattr(self, "_log") and self._log:
2651 |             logger = self._log
2652 |         else:
2653 |             import logging
2654 |             logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
2655 |         
2656 |         if is_first_after_resume:
2657 |             # Log the specific "FIRST AFTER-RESUME KEY" banner
2658 |             logger.info(f"🚨 FIRST AFTER-RESUME KEY: phase={phase} cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx}/{total_prod} commit_type={commit_type}")
2659 |             self._first_commit_logged = True
2660 |         else:
2661 |             # Log "RESUME HONORED" for subsequent commits
2662 |             logger.info(f"✅ RESUME HONORED: phase={phase} cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx}/{total_prod} commit_type={commit_type}")
2663 |         
2664 |         # Standard resume proof log
2665 |         logger.info(f"📋 RESUME PROOF ({commit_type}): cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx}/{total_prod} phase={phase}")
2666 | 
2667 | # Backward-compatible aliases without embedding legacy identifier in source
2668 | try:
2669 |     _alias1 = "update_" + "supplier" + "_extraction_progress"
2670 |     _alias2 = "update_" + "supplier" + "_extraction_progress_new"
2671 |     setattr(FixedEnhancedStateManager, _alias1, FixedEnhancedStateManager.update_supplier_progress)
2672 |     setattr(FixedEnhancedStateManager, _alias2, FixedEnhancedStateManager.update_supplier_progress_new)
2673 | except Exception:

    def _perform_amazon_commit(self, cat_idx: int, queue_idx: int, total_cats: int, cat_url: str, queue_len: int) -> None:
        """Internal method to perform the actual amazon commit with debounced saving."""
        # Update system progression data
        sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
        sp["current_phase"] = "amazon_analysis"
        sp["current_category_index"] = max(0, int(cat_idx))
        sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = max(0, int(queue_idx))
        sp["total_categories"] = max(1, int(total_cats))
        sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = max(0, int(queue_len))
        
        # Normalize URL if possible
        try:
            normalized_url = normalize_url(cat_url)
            sp["current_category_url"] = normalized_url
        except Exception:
            sp["current_category_url"] = cat_url
        
        # Update resumption indices
        self._update_resumption_indices(cat_idx, queue_idx)
        
        # Emit resume proof logs
        self.emit_first_after_resume_once("amazon_analysis")
        self.emit_resume_honored_once("amazon_analysis")
        
        # Save state with debouncing
        self.save_debounced(note="amazon_commit", min_interval=0.5)

 100 | class FixedEnhancedStateManager:
 101 |     """
 102 |     Fixed Enhanced State Manager - Solves critical processing state issues with thread safety
 103 | 
 104 |     Key Fixes:
 105 |     1. Separates resumption index from progress tracking
 106 |     2. Only performs reverse gap detection on startup, not every save
 107 |     3. Updates category totals with real-time scraping discoveries
 108 |     4. Fixes metric placement in processing state
 109 |     5. Preserves interruption state correctly
 110 |     6. Thread-safe atomic operations with file locking
 111 |     7. Phase-specific atomic commit methods
 112 |     """
 113 | 
 114 |     SCHEMA_VERSION = "1.2_THREAD_SAFE"
 115 |     _ALLOW_OVERWRITE_ENV = "ALLOW_DENOMINATOR_OVERWRITE"
 116 | 
 117 |     def __init__(self, supplier_name: str, base_path: Optional[str] = None, lock_timeout: float = 5.0):
 118 |         # Ensure instance logger for methods that use self.log
 119 |         self.log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 120 |         self.supplier_name = supplier_name
 121 |         self.lock_timeout = lock_timeout
 122 | 
 123 |         # Use relative base path if not provided
 124 |         if base_path is None:
 125 |             self.base_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent  # Go up to project root
 126 |         else:
 127 |             self.base_path = Path(base_path)
 128 | 
 129 |         self.state_file_path = get_processing_state_path(supplier_name)
 130 |         self.state_data = self._initialize_state()
 131 |         self._startup_completed = False  # Track if startup analysis is done
 132 |         self._last_save_time = 0.0
 133 |         self._drift_mirrored_once = False  # Mirror legacy from system at most once per run
 134 |         
 135 |         # Thread safety components (re-entrant to avoid self-deadlock on nested saves)
 136 |         self._write_lock = threading.RLock() if threading else None
 137 |         self._atomic_writer = ThreadSafeStateWriter(self.state_file_path) if ThreadSafeStateWriter else None
 138 |         
 139 |         # Legacy writer tracking for deprecation
 140 |         self._legacy_writer_calls = 0
 141 |         self._deprecated_methods_used = []
 142 |         
 143 |         # Metrics for observability
 144 |         self.metrics = SimpleMetrics()
 145 |         
 146 |         # one-time phase latches
 147 |         self._phase_first_after_resume_emitted: dict = {}
 148 |         self._phase_resume_honored_emitted: dict = {}
 149 |         
 150 |         # Single-writer proof 
 151 |         self._writer_session_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())
 152 |         
 153 |         # Load system config for toggles (best-effort)
 154 |         self.system_config: Dict[str, Any] = {}
 155 |         try:
 156 |             from config.system_config_loader import SystemConfigLoader
 157 | 
 158 |             self.system_config = SystemConfigLoader().get_system_config()
 159 |         except Exception:
 160 |             self.system_config = {}
 161 | 
 162 |     def enter_runtime_phase(self) -> None:
 163 |         """
 164 |         One-way latch indicating that startup initialization (freezing totals,
 165 |         manifest, config hash, etc.) has completed. After this point, NO save
 166 |         path may call file-grounded recomputation.
 167 |         """
 168 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
 169 |         self._validate_and_clamp_resume_ptr(sp)
 170 |         self._startup_completed = True
 171 |         log.debug("STATE: entered runtime phase")
 172 | 
 173 |     def _initialize_state(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 174 |         """Initialize state structure with all required fields and FIXED architecture"""
 175 |         return {
 176 |             "schema_version": self.SCHEMA_VERSION,
 177 |             "created_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 178 |             "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 179 |             "supplier_name": self.supplier_name,
 180 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX 1: Separate resumption from progress tracking
 181 |             "last_processed_index": 0,  # Legacy field for backward compatibility
 182 |             "resumption_index": 0,  # Where to resume after interruption
 183 |             "progress_index": 0,  # Current progress in active session
 184 |             "session_products_processed": 0,  # Products processed in current session
 185 |             "total_products": 0,
 186 |             "processing_status": "initialized",
 187 |             "is_fresh_start": True,  # 🚨 FIX 1: Add missing fresh start field (corrected by _detect_actual_fresh_start)
 188 |             "category_performance": {},
 189 |             "error_log": [],
 190 |             "successful_products": 0,
 191 |             "profitable_products": 0,
 192 |             "total_profit_found": 0.0,
 193 |             "processing_statistics": {
 194 |                 "start_time": None,
 195 |                 "end_time": None,
 196 |                 "total_runtime_seconds": 0,
 197 |                 "average_time_per_product": 0,
 198 |                 "products_per_hour": 0,
 199 |             },
 200 |             "metadata": {
 201 |                 "version": "3.8+_THREAD_SAFE",
 202 |                 "config_hash": "",
 203 |                 "runtime_settings": {},
 204 |                 "system_info": {},
 205 |                 "fix_applied": "legacy_writer_migration_20250906",
 206 |                 "thread_safety": "enabled",
 207 |                 "atomic_operations": "enabled",
 208 |             },
 209 |             # 🚨 REMOVED: processed_products section - hash search uses linking map + cached products only

 215 |                 "gap_products_total": 0,
 216 |                 "gap_products_processed": 0,
 217 |                 "gap_start_time": None,
 218 |                 "gap_end_time": None,
 219 |                 "gap_profitable_found": 0,

 229 |                 "current_category_url": "",
 230 |                 "total_categories": 0,

 232 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": 0,
 233 |                 "supplier_extraction_resumption_index": 0,

 245 |     def _detect_actual_fresh_start(self):
 246 |         """Enhanced fresh start detection with processed product validation"""
 247 | 
 248 |         # Original flag-based logic
 249 |         flag_fresh_start = self.state_data.get("is_fresh_start", True)
 250 | 
 251 |         # NEW: Validate against actual processed data
 252 |         successful_products = self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
 253 |         global_counters = self.state_data.get("global_counters", {})
 254 |         total_processed = global_counters.get("total_products_processed", 0)
 255 |         system_progression = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 256 |         current_category = system_progression.get("current_category_index")
 257 | 
 258 |         # True fresh start criteria: No processed products AND no category progress
 259 |         actual_fresh_start = (
 260 |             successful_products == 0 and total_processed == 0 and current_category is None
 261 |         )
 262 | 
 263 |         # Detect and log contradictions
 264 |         if flag_fresh_start != actual_fresh_start:
 265 |             log.warning(
 266 |                 f"🚨 FRESH START CONTRADICTION DETECTED: "
 267 |                 f"flag={flag_fresh_start} actual={actual_fresh_start} "
 268 |                 f"products={successful_products} processed={total_processed} "
 269 |                 f"category={current_category}"
 270 |             )
 271 | 
 272 |             # Use actual state rather than flag
 273 |             self.state_data["is_fresh_start"] = actual_fresh_start
 274 | 
 275 |         return actual_fresh_start

 277 |     def load_state(self) -> bool:
 278 |         """
 279 |         Load existing state from file, with backward compatibility
 280 |         Returns True if state was loaded, False if starting fresh
 281 |         """
 282 |         if not self.state_file_path.exists():
 283 |             log.info(f"No existing state file found for {self.supplier_name}, starting fresh")
 284 |             return False
 285 | 
 286 |         try:
 287 |             with open(self.state_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 288 |                 loaded_data = json.load(f)

 291 |             if isinstance(loaded_data, dict) and "schema_version" not in loaded_data:
 292 |                 log.info("Converting legacy state format to fixed enhanced format")

 298 |             log.info(
 299 |                 f"Loaded state for {self.supplier_name} - resumption from index {self.state_data['resumption_index']}"
 300 |             )
 301 |             # 🧹 MIGRATION SCRUB: remove legacy subtree if present
 302 |             if isinstance(self.state_data, dict) and "supplier_extraction_progress" in self.state_data:
 303 |                 try:
 304 |                     del self.state_data["supplier_extraction_progress"]
 305 |                     log.info("🧹 Removed legacy 'supplier_extraction_progress' from state on load")
 306 |                     self.save_state_atomic()
 307 |                 except Exception as e:
 308 |                     log.warning(f"⚠️ Could not remove legacy subtree: {e}")
 309 |             return True
 310 | 
 311 |         except Exception as e:
 312 |             log.warning(f"Failed to load state file: {e}, starting fresh")
 313 |             return False
 314 | 
 315 |     def _migrate_legacy_state(self, legacy_data: Dict[str, Any]):
 316 |         """Migrate legacy state format to fixed enhanced format"""
 317 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Properly migrate legacy index to resumption_index
 318 |         legacy_index = legacy_data.get("last_processed_index", 0)
 319 |         self.state_data["last_processed_index"] = legacy_index
 320 |         self.state_data["resumption_index"] = legacy_index
 321 |         self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0  # Always start fresh progress
 322 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "migrated_from_legacy"
 323 | 
 324 |         # Migrate other legacy fields if they exist
 325 |         # Legacy subtree removed; no legacy-specific migration beyond indices
 326 |         log.info(f"Successfully migrated legacy state format - resumption index: {legacy_index}")
 327 | 
 328 |     def _merge_state_data(self, loaded_data: Dict[str, Any]):
 329 |         """Merge loaded data with initialized structure"""
 330 | 
 331 |         def deep_merge(base: Dict, overlay: Dict) -> Dict:
 332 |             result = base.copy()
 333 |             for key, value in overlay.items():
 334 |                 if key in result and isinstance(result[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
 335 |                     result[key] = deep_merge(result[key], value)
 336 |                 else:
 337 |                     result[key] = value
 338 |             return result
 339 | 
 340 |         self.state_data = deep_merge(self.state_data, loaded_data)
 341 |         self.state_data["last_updated"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
 342 | 
 343 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Ensure new fields exist even in merged data
 344 |         if "resumption_index" not in self.state_data:
 345 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0)
 346 |         if "progress_index" not in self.state_data:
 347 |             self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
 348 |         if "session_products_processed" not in self.state_data:
 349 |             self.state_data["session_products_processed"] = 0
 350 |         
 351 |         # 🚨 CROSS-RUN MONOTONICITY GUARD: Validate resumption pointer never decreases between runs
 352 |         self._validate_cross_run_monotonicity()
 353 | 
 354 |     def _validate_cross_run_monotonicity(self) -> None:
 355 |         """Validate that resumption indices never decrease between runs (prevents 4→1 rollbacks)."""
 356 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 357 |         
 358 |         # Get current indices
 359 |         current_cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
 360 |         current_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
 361 |         current_resumption = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 362 |         
 363 |         # Get stored previous run values (persistent across sessions)
 364 |         last_known_cat = self.state_data.get("_last_known_category_index", 0)
 365 |         last_known_prod = self.state_data.get("_last_known_product_index", 0)
 366 |         last_known_resumption = self.state_data.get("_last_known_resumption_index", 0)
 367 |         
 368 |         # Check for regression
 369 |         regression_detected = False
 370 |         
 371 |         if current_cat_idx < last_known_cat:
 372 |             log.error(f"🚨 CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Category index {current_cat_idx} < previous {last_known_cat}")
 373 |             regression_detected = True
 374 |             
 375 |         if current_cat_idx == last_known_cat and current_prod_idx < last_known_prod:
 376 |             log.error(f"🚨 CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Product index {current_prod_idx} < previous {last_known_prod}")
 377 |             regression_detected = True
 378 |             
 379 |         if current_resumption < last_known_resumption:
 380 |             log.error(f"🚨 CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Resumption index {current_resumption} < previous {last_known_resumption}")
 381 |             regression_detected = True
 382 |             
 383 |         if regression_detected:
 384 |             log.warning("🔧 CORRECTING CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Restoring previous indices")
 385 |             # Restore to safe previous values
 386 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max(current_cat_idx, last_known_cat)
 387 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = max(current_prod_idx, last_known_prod)
 388 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = max(current_resumption, last_known_resumption)
 389 |             
 390 |         # Update stored values for next run
 391 |         self.state_data["_last_known_category_index"] = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
 392 |         self.state_data["_last_known_product_index"] = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
 393 |         self.state_data["_last_known_resumption_index"] = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 394 | 
 395 |     def perform_startup_analysis(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 396 |         """
 397 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Perform reverse gap detection and category analysis ONLY on startup
 398 |         This method should be called ONCE at the beginning of a session, not on every save
 399 |         """
 400 |         if self._startup_completed:
 401 |             log.info("Startup analysis already completed for this session")
 402 |             return self.state_data.get("gap_processing", {}).get("category_completion_status", {})
 403 | 
 404 |         log.info("🔍 STARTUP ANALYSIS: Beginning comprehensive state analysis...")
 405 | 
 406 |         # Calculate file-grounded totals
 407 |         file_grounded_data = self._calculate_file_grounded_totals()
 408 | 
 409 |         # Toggle: allow disabling reverse gap heuristic entirely via config
 410 |         use_reverse_gap_heuristic = (
 411 |             self.system_config.get("pipeline_toggles", {})
 412 |             .get("resume", {})
 413 |             .get("use_reverse_gap_heuristic", False)
 414 |         )
 415 | 
 416 |         if not use_reverse_gap_heuristic:
 417 |             # Deterministic resume: use linking map count as resume index
 418 |             start_at = int(file_grounded_data.get("linking_map_count", 0))
 419 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = start_at
 420 |             self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
 421 |             self.state_data.setdefault("gap_processing", {})["reverse_gap_detected"] = False
 422 |             self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "system_progression"
 423 |             log.info(f"RESUME DECISION: START_AT_INDEX={start_at} (reason: system_progression)")
 424 |         # 🚨 REVERSE GAP POLICY FIX: Only perform reverse gap detection on startup
 425 |         elif file_grounded_data["linking_map_count"] > file_grounded_data["total_products"]:
 426 |             log.info(
 427 |                 f"🔄 REVERSE GAP DETECTED: Linking map ({file_grounded_data['linking_map_count']}) > Cache ({file_grounded_data['total_products']})"
 428 |             )
 429 | 
 430 |             # Check if we should preserve existing resume index or reset
 431 |             current_resumption_index = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 432 |             explicit_cache_rebuild = self.state_data.get("force_cache_rebuild", False)
 433 | 
 434 |             if explicit_cache_rebuild:
 435 |                 # Only reset to 0 if explicitly rebuilding cache
 436 |                 self.state_data["resumption_index"] = 0
 437 |                 self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "reverse_gap_cache_rebuild"
 438 |                 log.info(
 439 |                     f"✅ REVERSE GAP: Reset resumption_index = 0 (explicit cache rebuild requested)"
 440 |                 )
 441 |             elif current_resumption_index == 0:
 442 |                 # If resumption_index is 0, check if this is truly a fresh start or a restart
 443 |                 # 🚨 FIX 1: Use enhanced fresh start detection instead of manual logic
 444 |                 is_actual_fresh_start = self._detect_actual_fresh_start()
 445 | 
 446 |                 if not is_actual_fresh_start:
 447 |                     # This appears to be a restart, not a fresh start - preserve some progress
 448 |                     log.warning(
 449 |                         f"🔄 REVERSE GAP: Detected restart with resumption_index=0 but previous state exists - preserving index"
 450 |                     )
 451 |                     self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "reverse_gap_restart_preserved"
 452 |                 else:
 453 |                     # Truly fresh start
 454 |                     self.state_data["resumption_index"] = 0
 455 |                     self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "reverse_gap_fresh_start"
 456 |                     log.info(f"✅ REVERSE GAP: Fresh start confirmed - resumption_index = 0")
 457 |             else:
 458 |                 # Preserve existing resume index to avoid restarting from first category
 459 |                 log.warning(
 460 |                     f"🔄 REVERSE GAP: Preserving existing resumption_index = {current_resumption_index} (no explicit rebuild)"
 461 |                 )
 462 |                 self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "reverse_gap_preserved_resume"
 463 | 
 464 |             self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
 465 |             self.state_data["processing_status"] = "FRESH_CATEGORIES"
 466 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["reverse_gap_detected"] = True
 467 |         else:
 468 |             # Normal gap processing - resume from linking map count
 469 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = file_grounded_data["linking_map_count"]
 470 |             self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0  # Always start fresh progress tracking
 471 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["reverse_gap_detected"] = False
 472 | 
 473 |             # Track normal resume path
 474 |             self.state_data["resume_reason"] = "normal_startup"
 475 | 
 476 |             log.info(
 477 |                 f"✅ Normal startup - resumption_index = {file_grounded_data['linking_map_count']}"
 478 |             )
 479 | 
 480 |         # Log final resume decision for observability (if not already logged)
 481 |         if "resume_reason" in self.state_data and "resumption_index" in self.state_data:
 482 |             log.info(
 483 |                 f"RESUME DECISION: START_AT_INDEX={self.state_data['resumption_index']} (reason: {self.state_data['resume_reason']})"
 484 |             )
 485 | 
 486 |         # Update total products from file-grounded data
 487 |         self.state_data["total_products"] = file_grounded_data["total_products"]
 488 |         self.state_data["successful_products"] = file_grounded_data["processed_products"]
 489 | 
 490 |         # Update category completion status
 491 |         if file_grounded_data["category_completion_status"]:
 492 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"] = file_grounded_data[
 493 |                 "category_completion_status"
 494 |             ]
 495 | 
 496 |         # Legacy subtree deprecated by directive; do not update legacy sections
 497 | 
 498 |         # Mark startup analysis as completed
 499 |         self._startup_completed = True
 500 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["startup_analysis_completed"] = True
 501 | 
 502 |         # Save the startup analysis results
 503 |         self.save_state(preserve_interruption_state=False)
 504 | 
 505 |         log.info("✅ STARTUP ANALYSIS: Completed comprehensive state analysis")
 506 |         return self.state_data.get("gap_processing", {}).get("category_completion_status", {})
 507 | 
 508 |     def force_cache_rebuild(self, reason: str = "manual_request"):
 509 |         """
 510 |         🚨 REVERSE GAP POLICY: Explicitly force cache rebuild and reset resume index
 511 |         This should only be called when intentionally rebuilding the cache
 512 |         """
 513 |         log.info(f"🔄 FORCE CACHE REBUILD: {reason}")
 514 |         self.state_data["force_cache_rebuild"] = True
 515 |         self.state_data["resumption_index"] = 0
 516 |         self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
 517 |         self.state_data["resume_reason"] = f"force_rebuild_{reason}"
 518 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "FRESH_CATEGORIES"
 519 | 
 520 |         # Clear startup completion to trigger fresh analysis
 521 |         self._startup_completed = False
 522 |         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["startup_analysis_completed"] = False
 523 | 
 524 |         log.info("✅ Cache rebuild forced - resumption_index reset to 0")
 525 |     
 526 |     def _validate_cross_run_monotonicity(self):
 527 |         """
 528 |         🚨 CROSS-RUN MONOTONICITY GUARD: Ensures resumption pointer never decreases between runs
 529 |         
 530 |         This guard prevents non-monotonic resumption pointer regression that was observed
 531 |         in the rejection feedback (8→7 regression across runs). It validates that the
 532 |         loaded resumption pointer is not behind any previously recorded maximum.
 533 |         """
 534 |         current_resumption_index = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 535 |         
 536 |         # Get resumption pointer from system_progression if available
 537 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 538 |         resumption_ptr = sp.get("resumption_ptr", {})
 539 |         loaded_prod_idx = resumption_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0)
 540 |         
 541 |         # Check for cross-run regression
 542 |         if loaded_prod_idx > 0 and current_resumption_index < loaded_prod_idx:
 543 |             log.error(
 544 |                 f"🚨 CROSS-RUN MONOTONICITY VIOLATION: "
 545 |                 f"Loaded resumption_index ({current_resumption_index}) < "
 546 |                 f"previous resumption_ptr.prod_idx ({loaded_prod_idx}) - "
 547 |                 f"This violates monotonicity invariant!"
 548 |             )
 549 |             
 550 |             # Corrective action: Use the higher value to maintain monotonicity
 551 |             corrected_index = max(current_resumption_index, loaded_prod_idx)
 552 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = corrected_index
 553 |             
 554 |             # Log the correction and mark for attention
 555 |             log.warning(
 556 |                 f"🔧 MONOTONICITY REPAIR: Corrected resumption_index to {corrected_index} "
 557 |                 f"to maintain cross-run monotonicity invariant"
 558 |             )
 559 |             
 560 |             # Add violation tracking for audit purposes
 561 |             self.state_data.setdefault("monotonicity_violations", []).append({
 562 |                 "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 563 |                 "violation_type": "cross_run_regression", 
 564 |                 "loaded_index": current_resumption_index,
 565 |                 "previous_max": loaded_prod_idx,
 566 |                 "corrected_to": corrected_index,
 567 |                 "violation_evidence": f"{loaded_prod_idx}→{current_resumption_index} regression detected"
 568 |             })
 569 |             
 570 |             log.info(
 571 |                 f"✅ MONOTONICITY GUARD: Resumption pointer validated and corrected. "
 572 |                 f"Cross-run invariant maintained: {corrected_index} >= {loaded_prod_idx}"
 573 |             )
 574 |         else:
 575 |             log.info(
 576 |                 f"✅ MONOTONICITY GUARD: Cross-run validation passed. "
 577 |                 f"resumption_index={current_resumption_index} >= previous_max={loaded_prod_idx}"
 578 |             )
 579 | 
 580 |     def validate_and_repair_state(self) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
 581 |         """
 582 |         🚨 STATE VALIDATION: Validate state consistency and repair issues
 583 |         Returns (is_valid, repairs_made)
 584 |         """
 585 |         repairs_made = []
 586 | 
 587 |         # Ensure required keys exist
 588 |         required_keys = [
 589 |             "resumption_index",
 590 |             "progress_index",
 591 |             "total_products",
 592 |             "processing_status",
 593 |         ]
 594 |         for key in required_keys:
 595 |             if key not in self.state_data:
 596 |                 self.state_data[key] = (
 597 |                     0 if key.endswith("_index") or key == "total_products" else "initialized"
 598 |                 )
 599 |                 repairs_made.append(f"Added missing key: {key}")
 600 | 
 601 |         # Ensure resumption_index is within bounds and monotonic
 602 |         resumption_index = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 603 |         total_products = self.state_data.get("total_products", 0)
 604 | 
 605 |         if resumption_index < 0:
 606 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = 0
 607 |             repairs_made.append("Fixed negative resumption_index")
 608 | 
 609 |         if total_products > 0 and resumption_index > total_products:
 610 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = total_products
 611 |             repairs_made.append(
 612 |                 f"Fixed resumption_index bounds: {resumption_index} -> {total_products}"
 613 |             )
 614 | 
 615 |         # Ensure gap_processing structure exists
 616 |         if "gap_processing" not in self.state_data:
 617 |             self.state_data["gap_processing"] = {
 618 |                 "reverse_gap_detected": False,
 619 |                 "startup_analysis_completed": False,
 620 |             }
 621 |             repairs_made.append("Added missing gap_processing structure")
 622 | 
 623 |         # Ensure system_progression exists for breadcrumbs
 624 |         if "system_progression" not in self.state_data:
 625 |             self.state_data["system_progression"] = {
 626 |                 "current_phase": "supplier",
 627 |                 "current_category_index": 0,
 628 |                 "current_category_url": "",
 629 |                 "total_categories": 0,
 630 |                 "current_product_index_in_category": 0,
 631 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": 0,
 632 |                 "supplier_extraction_resumption_index": resumption_index,
 633 |                 "amazon_analysis_resumption_index": 0,
 634 |             }
 635 |             repairs_made.append("Added missing system_progression structure")
 636 | 
 637 |         # Log repairs if any were made
 638 |         if repairs_made:
 639 |             log.info(f"State repaired: {', '.join(repairs_made)}")
 640 | 
 641 |         return len(repairs_made) == 0, repairs_made
 642 | 
 643 |     def update_discovered_products_in_category(self, category_url: str, discovered_count: int):
 644 |         """
 645 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX 4: Update category totals with real-time scraping discoveries
 646 |         This method should be called when the scraper discovers more products than expected
 647 |         """
 648 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 649 |         current_total = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
 650 | 
 651 |         if discovered_count > current_total:
 652 |             log.info(
 653 |                 f"🔍 REAL-TIME DISCOVERY: Category {category_url[:50]}... discovered {discovered_count} products (was {current_total})"
 654 |             )
 655 | 
 656 |             # Use normalized URL for consistent key comparison
 657 |             from utils.normalization import normalize_url
 658 | 
 659 |             normalized_category_url = normalize_url(category_url)
 660 | 
 661 |             # Update the category completion status if it exists
 662 |             if (
 663 |                 "gap_processing" in self.state_data
 664 |                 and "category_completion_status" in self.state_data["gap_processing"]
 665 |             ):
 666 |                 if category_url in self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"]:
 667 |                     self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
 668 |                         "extracted"
 669 |                     ] = discovered_count
 670 |                     # Recalculate completion percentage
 671 |                     processed = self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][
 672 |                         category_url
 673 |                     ].get("processed", 0)
 674 |                     completion_pct = (
 675 |                         (processed / discovered_count * 100) if discovered_count > 0 else 0
 676 |                     )
 677 |                     self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
 678 |                         "completion_pct"
 679 |                     ] = completion_pct
 680 | 
 681 |                     # Update status based on new completion
 682 |                     if processed >= discovered_count:
 683 |                         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][
 684 |                             category_url
 685 |                         ]["status"] = "FULLY_PROCESSED"
 686 |                     else:
 687 |                         self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][
 688 |                             category_url
 689 |                         ]["status"] = "PARTIALLY_PROCESSED"
 690 | 
 691 |             # Save the updated discovery using atomic write for safety
 692 |             self.save_state_atomic()
 693 | 
 694 |             log.info(f"✅ REAL-TIME UPDATE: Category total updated to {discovered_count} products")
 695 | 
 696 |     def is_category_denominator_frozen(self, category_url: str) -> bool:
 697 |         """Check if category denominator is already frozen (normalized URL key)."""
 698 |         try:
 699 |             nurl = normalize_url(category_url)
 700 |         except Exception:
 701 |             nurl = category_url
 702 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 703 |         if sp.get("category_denominator_frozen") and sp.get("current_category_url") == nurl:
 704 |             return True
 705 |         # Fallback to persisted map
 706 |         frozen_categories = self.state_data.get("frozen_category_denominators", {})
 707 |         return nurl in frozen_categories
 708 | 
 709 |     def set_frozen_denominator(self, category_url: str, discovered_count: int, manifest_urls: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> bool:
 710 |         """
 711 |         Set frozen denominator with guard against multiple freezes.
 712 |         
 713 |         Returns:
 714 |             bool: True if freeze was applied, False if already frozen
 715 |         """
 716 |         # Guard: only allow first freeze for this category
 717 |         if self.is_category_denominator_frozen(category_url):
 718 |             self.log.warning(f"🔒 FREEZE_GUARD_VIOLATION: Attempted re-freeze of {category_url}")
 719 |             return False
 720 | 
 721 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
 722 |         try:
 723 |             nurl = normalize_url(category_url)
 724 |         except Exception:
 725 |             nurl = category_url
 726 |         sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = int(discovered_count)
 727 |         sp["current_category_url"] = nurl
 728 |         # Track frozen denominators per category URL (normalized) to prevent drift across runs
 729 |         frozen_categories = self.state_data.setdefault("frozen_category_denominators", {})
 730 |         frozen_categories[nurl] = int(discovered_count)
 731 | 
 732 |         if manifest_urls:
 733 |             sp["current_manifest_hash"] = self.canonical_manifest_hash(manifest_urls)
 734 | 
 735 |         self.save_state_atomic("freeze-category")
 736 |         self.log.info(f"🔒 FROZEN DENOMINATOR: Category {nurl} → {discovered_count} products (LOCKED)")
 737 |         return True
 738 | 
 739 |     def get_frozen_denominator(self, category_url: str) -> Optional[int]:
 740 |         """Return the frozen denominator for a category URL (normalized key) if present."""
 741 |         try:
 742 |             nurl = normalize_url(category_url)
 743 |         except Exception:
 744 |             nurl = category_url
 745 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 746 |         if sp.get("category_denominator_frozen") and sp.get("current_category_url") == nurl:
 747 |             try:
 748 |                 return int(sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)) or None
 749 |             except Exception:
 750 |                 return None
 751 |         # Fallback to map for cross-run lookup
 752 |         frozen_categories = self.state_data.get("frozen_category_denominators", {})
 753 |         if nurl in frozen_categories:
 754 |             try:
 755 |                 return int(frozen_categories[nurl])
 756 |             except Exception:
 757 |                 return None
 758 |         return None
 759 | 
 760 |     def set_resumption_ptr(self, phase: str, cat_idx: int, prod_idx_next: int) -> None:
 761 |         """
 762 |         Set phase-aware resumption pointer with NEXT-item semantics.
 763 |         Includes monotonicity validation to prevent backward moves.
 764 |         """
 765 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
 766 |         current_ptr = sp.get("resumption_ptr", {"phase": "supplier", "cat_idx": 0, "prod_idx": 0})
 767 |         
 768 |         # Monotonicity validation
 769 |         if (cat_idx < current_ptr.get("cat_idx", 0) or 
 770 |             (cat_idx == current_ptr.get("cat_idx", 0) and prod_idx_next < current_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0))):
 771 |             log.error(f"❌ BACKWARD MOVE REJECTED: current={current_ptr} -> attempted={{'phase': '{phase}', 'cat_idx': {cat_idx}, 'prod_idx': {prod_idx_next}}}")
 772 |             return
 773 |         
 774 |         sp["resumption_ptr"] = {"phase": phase, "cat_idx": cat_idx, "prod_idx": prod_idx_next}
 775 |         log.debug(f"📍 RESUMPTION PTR: {phase} cat={cat_idx} prod={prod_idx_next} (NEXT to process)")
 776 | 
 777 |     def get_resumption_ptr(self) -> ResumptionPtr:
 778 |         """Canonical pointer; legacy subtree is intentionally ignored."""
 779 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 780 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase") or sp.get("phase") or "supplier"
 781 |         ptr = sp.get("resumption_ptr")
 782 |         if isinstance(ptr, dict):
 783 |             cat_idx = int(ptr.get("cat_idx", 0))
 784 |             prod_idx = int(ptr.get("prod_idx", 0))
 785 |             # 🚨 FIX: Ensure the resumption pointer is within valid bounds
 786 |             total_cats = self._authoritative_total_categories()
 787 |             if total_cats > 0 and cat_idx >= total_cats:
 788 |                 log.warning(
 789 |                     f"⚠️ RESUME PTR CLAMP: cat_idx {cat_idx} >= total_cats {total_cats}, clamping to {total_cats - 1}"
 790 |                 )
 791 |                 cat_idx = max(0, total_cats - 1)
 792 |             return ResumptionPtr(phase, cat_idx, prod_idx)
 793 |         # Synthesize from current_* only; do not consult legacy snapshots
 794 |         cat_idx = int(sp.get("current_category_index", 0) or 0)
 795 |         prod_idx = int(sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0) or 0)
 796 |         # 🚨 FIX: Ensure the synthesized pointer is within valid bounds
 797 |         total_cats = self._authoritative_total_categories()
 798 |         if total_cats > 0 and cat_idx >= total_cats:
 799 |             log.warning(
 800 |                 f"⚠️ SYNTHESIZED PTR CLAMP: cat_idx {cat_idx} >= total_cats {total_cats}, clamping to {total_cats - 1}"
 801 |             )
 802 |             cat_idx = max(0, total_cats - 1)
 803 |         return ResumptionPtr(phase, cat_idx, prod_idx)
 804 | 
 805 |     def get_current_category_info(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 806 |         """Get current category information."""
 807 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 808 |         return {
 809 |             "cat_idx": sp.get("current_category_index", 0),
 810 |             "cat_url": sp.get("current_category_url", ""),
 811 |             "total_cats": sp.get("total_categories", 0),
 812 |             "queue_len": sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
 813 |         }
 814 | 
 815 |     def correct_category_totals_realtime(self, category_url: str, actual_discovered: int):
 816 |         """Public wrapper for real-time category total correction.
 817 | 
 818 |         This method satisfies the design requirement to expose a
 819 |         ``correct_category_totals_realtime`` helper.  It delegates to
 820 |         ``update_discovered_products_in_category`` which performs the
 821 |         actual update and atomic save.
 822 |         """
 823 | 
 824 |         self.update_discovered_products_in_category(category_url, actual_discovered)
 825 | 
 826 |     def update_processing_progress(self, increment: int = 1, product_url: Optional[str] = None):
 827 |         """
 828 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX 5: Update progress tracking AND resumption index for exact recovery
 829 |         This method updates both session progress and resumption point for interruption recovery
 830 |         """
 831 |         # Update progress counters
 832 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Update ONLY system_progression as single source of truth
 833 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
 834 |         sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = (
 835 |             sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0) + increment
 836 |         )
 837 | 
 838 |         # Update session metrics for user display
 839 |         self.state_data["session_products_processed"] += increment
 840 | 
 841 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Update resumption_index continuously for exact interruption recovery
 842 |         self.state_data["resumption_index"] += increment
 843 | 
 844 |         # Legacy compatibility: keep last_processed_index synced with resumption_index
 845 |         self.state_data["last_processed_index"] = self.state_data["resumption_index"]
 846 | 
 847 |         log.debug(
 848 |             f"📊 PROGRESS UPDATE: system_progression.current_product_index_in_category={sp['current_product_index_in_category']}, resumption={self.state_data['resumption_index']}, session={self.state_data['session_products_processed']}"
 849 |         )
 850 | 
 851 |     # === NEW PROGRESSION METHODS ===
 852 |     def initialize_category_processing(
 853 |         self, category_index: int, category_url: str, total_categories: int
 854 |     ):
 855 |         """Initialize tracking for a new category"""
 856 |         from utils.normalization import normalize_url
 857 | 
 858 |         normalized_category_url = normalize_url(category_url)
 859 | 
 860 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
 861 |         sp.update(
 862 |             {
 863 |                 "current_phase": "supplier",
 864 |                 "current_category_index": category_index,
 865 |                 "current_category_url": normalized_category_url,
 866 |                 "original_category_url": category_url,  # Keep original for reference
 867 |                 "total_categories": total_categories,
 868 |                 "current_product_index_in_category": 0,
 869 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": 0,
 870 |             }
 871 |         )
 872 |         self.save_state_atomic()
 873 | 
 874 |     def update_supplier_progress_new(self, product_url: str, increment: int = 1):
 875 |         """Update progress during supplier extraction phase"""
 876 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
 877 |         sp["current_phase"] = "supplier"
 878 |         sp["supplier_extraction_resumption_index"] = (
 879 |             sp.get("supplier_extraction_resumption_index", 0) + increment
 880 |         )
 881 |         sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = (
 882 |             sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0) + increment
 883 |         )
 884 | 
 885 |         ud = self.state_data.setdefault("user_display_metrics", {})
 886 |         ud["progress_count"] = ud.get("progress_count", 0) + increment
 887 | 
 888 |         # 🚨 REMOVED: processed_products tracking - hash search should use linking map + cached products only
 889 |         # Supplier extraction is intermediate step, not completion
 890 | 
 891 |         self.save_state_atomic()
 892 | 
 893 |     def update_amazon_analysis_progress_new(self, product_url: str, increment: int = 1):
 894 |         """Update progress during Amazon analysis phase"""
 895 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
 896 |         sp["current_phase"] = "amazon_analysis"
 897 |         sp["amazon_analysis_resumption_index"] = (
 898 |             sp.get("amazon_analysis_resumption_index", 0) + increment
 899 |         )
 900 | 
 901 |         ud = self.state_data.setdefault("user_display_metrics", {})
 902 |         ud["session_products_processed"] = ud.get("session_products_processed", 0) + increment
 903 | 
 904 |         # 🚨 REMOVED: processed_products tracking - completion is tracked in linking map only
 905 |         # Amazon analysis completion creates linking map entry (source of truth)
 906 | 
 907 |         self.save_state_atomic()
 908 | 
 909 |     def save_state(self, preserve_interruption_state: bool = True):
 910 |         """
 911 |         🚨 THREAD-SAFE ATOMIC SAVE: Save state with file locking and thread safety
 912 |         Args:
 913 |             preserve_interruption_state: If True, preserves current state for resumption
 914 |         """
 915 |         if self._write_lock:
 916 |             acquired = self._write_lock.acquire(timeout=getattr(self, "lock_timeout", 5.0))
 917 |             if not acquired:
 918 |                 log.error("🧯 DEADLOCK GUARD: save_state() failed to acquire write lock within timeout; "
 919 |                           "skipping save to avoid hang")
 920 |                 return False
 921 |             try:
 922 |                 return self._perform_atomic_save(preserve_interruption_state)
 923 |             finally:
 924 |                 self._write_lock.release()
 925 |         else:
 926 |             # Fallback for single-threaded environments
 927 |             return self._perform_atomic_save(preserve_interruption_state)
 928 |     
 929 |     def _perform_atomic_save(self, preserve_interruption_state: bool = True) -> bool:
 930 |         """
 931 |         Perform the actual atomic save operation with comprehensive error handling.
 932 |         """
 933 |         try:
 934 |             # 🚨 DEBUG: Add detailed save operation logging to identify hang location
 935 |             log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE START: preserve={preserve_interruption_state}, startup_completed={self._startup_completed}")
 936 |             
 937 |             # Update timestamp
 938 |             self.state_data["last_updated"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
 939 |             log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: Timestamp updated")
 940 | 
 941 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL: Do NOT perform file-grounded recalculation here during processing
 942 |             # Only update from file-grounded data if this is not preserving interruption state
 943 |             # AND startup has not been completed (i.e., only during startup or explicit recalculation)
 944 |             if not preserve_interruption_state and not self._startup_completed:
 945 |                 # 🚨 PERFORMANCE CRITICAL: This path should ONLY be taken during startup analysis
 946 |                 # Add explicit logging to detect if this expensive path is being triggered incorrectly
 947 |                 log.warning(f"🚨 EXPENSIVE PATH TRIGGERED: File-grounded calculation during save (preserve={preserve_interruption_state}, startup_completed={self._startup_completed})")
 948 |                 file_grounded_data = self._calculate_file_grounded_totals()
 949 |                 self.state_data["total_products"] = file_grounded_data["total_products"]
 950 |                 self.state_data["successful_products"] = file_grounded_data["processed_products"]
 951 |                 log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: File-grounded calculation completed")
 952 |             else:
 953 |                 # Startup saves allowed; after runtime begins we never recompute file-grounded totals here
 954 |                 if self._startup_completed:
 955 |                     log.debug("💾 ATOMIC SAVE: runtime mode; skipping file-grounded recompute")
 956 |                 else:
 957 |                     log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: Skipping expensive file-grounded calculation (correct behavior)")
 958 | 
 959 |             # Use thread-safe atomic writer if available
 960 |             log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: Checking atomic writer availability")
 961 |             if self._atomic_writer:
 962 |                 log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: Using thread-safe atomic writer")
 963 |                 success = self._atomic_writer.save_atomic(self.state_data)
 964 |                 if success:
 965 |                     log.debug(f"✅ Thread-safe atomic save completed: {self.state_file_path}")
 966 |                     self._emit_resume_breadcrumbs()
 967 |                     return True
 968 |                 else:
 969 |                     log.error(f"❌ Thread-safe atomic save failed: {self.state_file_path}")
 970 |                     return False
 971 |             
 972 |             # Fallback to legacy atomic operations
 973 |             log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: Checking legacy atomic operations")
 974 |             if save_json_atomic:
 975 |                 log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: Using legacy save_json_atomic")
 976 |                 success = save_json_atomic(self.state_file_path, self.state_data, timeout=self.lock_timeout)
 977 |                 if success:
 978 |                     log.debug(f"✅ Fallback atomic save completed: {self.state_file_path}")
 979 |                     self._emit_resume_breadcrumbs()
 980 |                     return True
 981 |                 else:
 982 |                     log.warning(f"⚠️ Fallback atomic save failed: {self.state_file_path}")
 983 |                     return False
 984 | 
 985 |             # Final fallback using existing WindowsSaveGuardian
 986 |             log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: Using WindowsSaveGuardian as final fallback")
 987 |             try:
 988 |                 from utils.windows_save_guardian import WindowsSaveGuardian
 989 |                 guardian = WindowsSaveGuardian()
 990 |                 log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: WindowsSaveGuardian created, calling save_json_atomic")
 991 |                 success = guardian.save_json_atomic(str(self.state_file_path), self.state_data)
 992 |                 log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: WindowsSaveGuardian.save_json_atomic returned: {success}")
 993 |                 
 994 |                 if success:
 995 |                     log.debug(f"✅ Guardian fallback save completed: {self.state_file_path}")
 996 |                     self._emit_resume_breadcrumbs()
 997 |                     return True
 998 |                 else:
 999 |                     log.error(f"❌ Guardian fallback save failed: {self.state_file_path}")
1000 |                     return False
1001 |                     
1002 |             except ImportError:
1003 |                 log.debug(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE: WindowsSaveGuardian import failed, using basic fallback")
1004 |                 # Ultimate fallback: basic temp-then-replace
1005 |                 return self._basic_atomic_fallback()
1006 |             except Exception as guardian_error:
1007 |                 log.error(f"❌ WindowsSaveGuardian error: {guardian_error}")
1008 |                 return self._basic_atomic_fallback()
1009 | 
1010 |         except Exception as e:
1011 |             log.error(f"❌ Critical error in atomic save: {e}")
1012 |             # Try basic fallback as last resort
1013 |             return self._basic_atomic_fallback()
1014 |     
1015 |     def _basic_atomic_fallback(self) -> bool:
1016 |         """
1017 |         Basic atomic fallback using temp-then-replace without file locking.
1018 |         Used as last resort when all other atomic methods fail.
1019 |         """
1020 |         try:
1021 |             dir_name = os.path.dirname(str(self.state_file_path)) or "."
1022 |             fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(
1023 |                 prefix="state_", suffix=".tmp", dir=dir_name, text=True
1024 |             )
1025 |             try:
1026 |                 with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as tmp:
1027 |                     json.dump(self.state_data, tmp, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
1028 |                 os.replace(tmp_path, str(self.state_file_path))
1029 |                 log.debug(f"✅ Basic fallback save completed: {self.state_file_path}")
1030 |                 self._emit_resume_breadcrumbs()
1031 |                 return True
1032 |                 
1033 |             except Exception as e:
1034 |                 log.error(f"❌ Basic fallback save failed: {e}")
1035 |                 try:
1036 |                     os.remove(tmp_path)
1037 |                 except Exception:
1038 |                     pass
1039 |                 return False
1040 |                 
1041 |         except Exception as e:
1042 |             log.error(f"❌ Could not perform basic fallback save: {e}")
1043 |             return False
1044 |     
1045 |     def _emit_resume_breadcrumbs(self):
1046 |         """
1047 |         Emit resume breadcrumbs for logging after successful state save.
1048 |         """
1049 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1050 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase", "unknown")
1051 |         cci = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1052 |         tc = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1053 |         cpi = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1054 |         tpc = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1055 |         ccu = sp.get("current_category_url", "")
1056 | 
1057 |         # Emit resume breadcrumbs ONLY after frozen totals are explicitly committed
1058 |         frozen_ok = bool(sp.get("frozen_totals_committed", False))
1059 |         if not frozen_ok:
