1060 |             log.debug("RESUME PTR suppressed: frozen_totals_committed=False")
1061 |             return
1062 |         
1063 |         # Additional gate: per-category denominator must be frozen
1064 |         if int(sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)) == 0:
1065 |             log.debug("RESUME PTR suppressed: category_denominator_unfrozen")
1066 |             return
1067 |             
1068 |         if tc > 0 and tpc > 0:
1069 |             log.info(f"RESUME PTR: phase={phase} cat_idx={cci}/{tc} url={ccu} prod_idx={cpi}/{tpc}")
1070 |         elif tc > 0:
1071 |             log.info(f"RESUME PTR: phase={phase} cat_idx={cci}/{tc} url={ccu} prod_idx={cpi}/pending")
1072 | 
1073 |     def emit_first_after_resume_if_needed(self, phase: str) -> None:
1074 |         """
1075 |         Emit FIRST_AFTER_RESUME_KEY banner once per phase.
1076 |         Stores flag in diagnostics section to avoid polluting control state.
1077 |         """
1078 |         diag = self.state_data.setdefault("diagnostics", {})
1079 |         key = f"first_after_resume_emitted_{phase}"
1080 |         
1081 |         if not diag.get(key) and self._frozen_ok():
1082 |             rp = self.get_resumption_ptr()
1083 |             log.info(f"FIRST_AFTER_RESUME_KEY phase={phase} cat={rp.cat_idx} prod={rp.prod_idx} denom={self.state_data['system_progression'].get('total_products_in_current_category', 0)}")
1084 |             diag[key] = True
1085 |             self.metrics.inc("resume_proof_emitted_total", phase=phase, type="first_after_resume")
1086 | 
1087 |     def emit_resume_honored_if_needed(self, phase: str) -> None:
1088 |         """
1089 |         Emit RESUME_HONORED banner once per phase.
1090 |         Stores flag in diagnostics section to avoid polluting control state.
1091 |         """
1092 |         diag = self.state_data.setdefault("diagnostics", {})
1093 |         key = f"resume_honored_emitted_{phase}"
1094 |         
1095 |         if not diag.get(key) and self._frozen_ok():
1096 |             rp = self.get_resumption_ptr()
1097 |             log.info(f"RESUME_HONORED phase={phase} cat={rp.cat_idx} prod={rp.prod_idx}")
1098 |             diag[key] = True
1099 |             self.metrics.inc("resume_proof_emitted_total", phase=phase, type="honored")
1100 | 
1101 |     def canonical_manifest_hash(self, urls: list[str]) -> str:
1102 |         """
1103 |         Compute deterministic manifest hash from normalized URLs.
1104 |         Uses canonical JSON with stable separators and UTF-8 encoding.
1105 |         """
1106 |         canon = [normalize_url(u) for u in urls]
1107 |         canon.sort()
1108 |         blob = json.dumps(canon, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False)
1109 |         return hashlib.sha1(blob.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:8]
1110 | 
1111 | 
1112 |     @staticmethod
1113 |     def compute_supplier_config_hash(category_urls: List[str]) -> str:
1114 |         joined = "".join(sorted(category_urls or []))
1115 |         return hashlib.sha256(joined.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
1116 | 
1117 |     def save(self, note: str = "") -> None:
1118 |         """Atomically save state with optional note."""
1119 |         st = self.state_data
1120 |         sp = st.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1121 |         sp["_writer_session_uuid"] = self._writer_session_uuid
1122 |         sp["_writer_seq"] = sp.get("_writer_seq", 0) + 1
1123 |         sp["_writer_note"] = note
1124 |         self.save_state(preserve_interruption_state=True)
1125 |         if note:
1126 |             log.info(f"💾 ATOMIC SAVE ({note})")
1127 |         else:
1128 |             log.info("💾 ATOMIC SAVE")
1129 | 
1130 |     def save_state_atomic(self, note: str = "") -> None:
1131 |         """
1132 |         Atomic save wrapper used by new progression methods.
1133 |         Accepts an optional note and forwards to the unified save path
1134 |         that emits atomic-save breadcrumbs and resume-proof hooks.
1135 |         """
1136 |         self.save(note=note)
1137 | 
1138 |     def save_debounced(self, note: str = "", min_interval: float = 2.0) -> None:
1139 |         # Startup saves are allowed; after runtime begins we must not recalc file-grounded totals
1140 |         if self._startup_completed:
1141 |             log.debug("💾 save_debounced: runtime mode (no file-grounded recompute)")
1142 |         
1143 |         now = time.time()
1144 |         if now - getattr(self, "_last_save_time", 0) < min_interval:
1145 |             return
1146 |         self._last_save_time = now
1147 |         self.save(note=note)
1148 |         self.log_resume_proof_after_commit(note or "debounced")
1149 | 
1150 |     def log_resume_proof_after_commit(self, context: str) -> None:
1151 |         """Log resume proof banner after atomic commits for audit trail."""
1152 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1153 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase", "unknown")
1154 |         cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1155 |         prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1156 |         
1157 |         # Check if this is the first commit after a resume
1158 |         if not getattr(self, '_first_resume_logged', False):
1159 |             log.info(f"🎆 FIRST AFTER-RESUME KEY: phase={phase} cat={cat_idx} prod={prod_idx} context={context}")
1160 |             self._first_resume_logged = True
1161 |         else:
1162 |             log.info(f"📝 RESUME HONORED: phase={phase} cat={cat_idx} prod={prod_idx} context={context}")
1163 |             
1164 |         log.debug(f"📝 RESUME PROOF ({context}): State committed successfully")
1165 | 
1166 |     def set_total_categories(self, total:int, manifest_hash:str) -> None:
1167 |         """Set frozen total categories and manifest hash; mirror into system_progression."""
1168 |         try:
1169 |             total_int = int(total)
1170 |         except Exception:
1171 |             total_int = 0
1172 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1173 |         sp["total_categories"] = total_int
1174 |         sp["current_manifest_hash"] = manifest_hash
1175 |         if sp.get("_last_manifest_hash") and sp["_last_manifest_hash"] != manifest_hash:
1176 |             log.warning(f"🧬 MANIFEST HASH CHANGED: {sp['_last_manifest_hash']} → {manifest_hash}")
1177 |         sp["_last_manifest_hash"] = manifest_hash
1178 |         self.save_debounced("manifest")
1179 | 
1180 |     def _validate_and_clamp_resume_ptr(self, sp: dict) -> None:
1181 |         """Validate and clamp resume pointer to be within bounds of frozen denominator."""
1182 |         rp = sp.get("resumption_ptr") or {}
1183 |         prod = int(rp.get("prod_idx", 0))
1184 |         total = int(sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0))
1185 |         if total > 0 and (prod < 0 or prod > total):
1186 |             new_prod = min(max(prod, 0), total)
1187 |             rp["prod_idx"] = new_prod
1188 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = rp
1189 |             if not sp.get("_resume_ptr_clamp_emitted"):
1190 |                 log.warning(f"🧯 CLAMPED RESUME PTR: prod={prod} → {new_prod} (total={total})")
1191 |                 sp["_resume_ptr_clamp_emitted"] = True
1192 | 
1193 |     def _freeze_category_denominator(self, sp: dict, *, cat_url: str, total: int) -> None:
1194 |         """Freeze per-category denominator with immutability guard."""
1195 |         if not sp.get("category_denominator_frozen", False):
1196 |             sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = int(total)
1197 |             sp["category_denominator_frozen"] = True
1198 |             log.info(f"🔒 DENOM FREEZE: url={cat_url} total={total}")
1199 |             return
1200 |         prev = int(sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0))
1201 |         if int(total) != prev:
1202 |             if os.getenv(self._ALLOW_OVERWRITE_ENV, "false").lower() == "true":
1203 |                 log.warning(f"⚠️ DENOM OVERWRITE (allowed via env): {prev} → {total} url={cat_url}")
1204 |                 sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = int(total)
1205 |             else:
1206 |                 log.error(f"🚫 DENOM CHANGE BLOCKED: frozen={prev} attempted={total} url={cat_url}")
1207 | 
1208 |     def mark_frozen_totals_committed(self) -> None:
1209 |         """Mark frozen totals as committed, enabling RESUME PTR logs."""
1210 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1211 |         sp["frozen_totals_committed"] = True
1212 |         self.save_state_atomic()
1213 | 
1214 |     def _frozen_ok(self) -> bool:
1215 |         """Check if frozen totals are committed and category denominator is frozen."""
1216 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1217 |         return bool(sp.get("frozen_totals_committed")) and int(sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)) > 0
1218 | 
1219 | 
1220 |     def emit_first_after_resume_once(self, phase: str) -> None:
1221 |         """Call exactly once per phase, right after denominator freeze is saved."""
1222 |         if self._phase_first_after_resume_emitted.get(phase):
1223 |             return
1224 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1225 |         log.info(
1226 |             f"FIRST_AFTER_RESUME_KEY phase={phase} "
1227 |             f"cat={sp.get('current_category_index')} "
1228 |             f"prod={sp.get('current_product_index_in_category')} "
1229 |             f"denom={sp.get('total_products_in_current_category')}"
1230 |         )
1231 |         self._phase_first_after_resume_emitted[phase] = True
1232 | 
1233 |     def emit_resume_honored_once(self, phase: str, commit_type: str) -> None:
1234 |         """May be called by many commit sites; will emit only once per phase."""
1235 |         if self._phase_resume_honored_emitted.get(phase):
1236 |             return
1237 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1238 |         log.info(
1239 |             f"✅ RESUME HONORED: phase={phase} "
1240 |             f"cat={sp.get('current_category_index')}/{sp.get('total_categories')} "
1241 |             f"prod={sp.get('current_product_index_in_category')}/{sp.get('total_products_in_current_category')} "
1242 |             f"commit_type={commit_type}"
1243 |         )
1244 |         log.info(
1245 |             f"📋 RESUME PROOF ({commit_type.upper()}): "
1246 |             f"cat={sp.get('current_category_index')}/{sp.get('total_categories')} "
1247 |             f"prod={sp.get('current_product_index_in_category')}/{sp.get('total_products_in_current_category')} "
1248 |             f"phase={phase}"
1249 |         )
1250 |         self._phase_resume_honored_emitted[phase] = True
1251 | 
1252 |     def commit_supplier_progress(self, *, cat_idx: int, prod_idx: int,
1253 |                                total_cats: int, cat_url: str, total_prod_in_cat: int) -> None:
1254 |         """Thread-safe atomic supplier-phase commit (category-relative cursor)."""
1255 |         # Emit a clear progress line with clamp preview and normalized URL
1256 |         original_idx = int(prod_idx)
1257 |         clamped_idx = original_idx
1258 |         if total_prod_in_cat is not None and total_prod_in_cat >= 0:
1259 |             if clamped_idx < 0:
1260 |                 clamped_idx = 0
1261 |             elif clamped_idx > total_prod_in_cat:
1262 |                 clamped_idx = total_prod_in_cat
1263 |         try:
1264 |             nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
1265 |         except Exception:
1266 |             nurl = str(cat_url)
1267 |         self.log.info(
1268 |             f"🔧 SUPPLIER PROGRESS: cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} "
1269 |             f"idx={original_idx}->{clamped_idx} (frozen_queue_len={total_prod_in_cat}) url={nurl}"
1270 |         )
1271 |         if self._write_lock:
1272 |             with self._write_lock:
1273 |                 self._perform_supplier_commit(cat_idx, clamped_idx, total_cats, cat_url, total_prod_in_cat)
1274 |         else:
1275 |             self._perform_supplier_commit(cat_idx, clamped_idx, total_cats, cat_url, total_prod_in_cat)
1276 |     
1277 |     def _perform_supplier_commit(self, cat_idx: int, prod_idx: int, total_cats: int, cat_url: str, total_prod_in_cat: int):
1278 |         """Internal supplier commit implementation."""
1279 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1280 | 
1281 |         # 🔒 CRITICAL: Per-category denominator freeze at category entry (normalized URL)
1282 |         try:
1283 |             nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
1284 |         except Exception:
1285 |             nurl = cat_url
1286 |         current_cat_url = sp.get("current_category_url")
1287 |         current_total_prod = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1288 | 
1289 |         if current_cat_url != nurl or current_total_prod == 0:
1290 |             # NEW CATEGORY ENTRY: Freeze denominator immediately
1291 |             sp["current_category_url"] = nurl
1292 |             sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = int(total_prod_in_cat)
1293 |             sp["category_denominator_frozen"] = True
1294 |             sp["category_freeze_timestamp"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1295 | 
1296 |             self.log.info(f"🔒 PER-CATEGORY FREEZE: {nurl} → {total_prod_in_cat} products")
1297 |             
1298 |             # Emit first-after-resume banner once per phase
1299 |             self.emit_first_after_resume_once("supplier")
1300 |         
1301 |         # Validate against frozen denominator
1302 |         frozen_total = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", total_prod_in_cat)
1303 |         if prod_idx > frozen_total:
1304 |             if hasattr(self, '_log') and self._log:
1305 |                 self._log.warning(f"⚠️ BOUNDS CLAMP: prod_idx={prod_idx} > frozen_total={frozen_total}, clamping to {frozen_total}")
1306 |             prod_idx = min(prod_idx, frozen_total)
1307 |         
1308 |         # Apply atomic state updates
1309 |         sp["current_phase"] = "supplier"
1310 |         sp["current_category_index"] = int(cat_idx)
1311 |         sp["total_categories"] = int(total_cats)
1312 |         try:
1313 |             nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
1314 |         except Exception:
1315 |             nurl = str(cat_url)
1316 |         sp["current_category_url"] = nurl
1317 |         sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = int(prod_idx)           # ✅ category-relative (last completed)
1318 |         
1319 |         # 🚨 IMPROVED: Set resumption pointer with detailed logging
1320 |         next_prod_idx = int(prod_idx) + 1
1321 |         self.log.info(
1322 |             f"📍 SETTING RESUMPTION PTR: cat={cat_idx}, current_prod={prod_idx}, next_prod={next_prod_idx}"
1323 |         )
1324 |         self.set_resumption_ptr(int(cat_idx), next_prod_idx)           # Store NEXT to process
1325 |         log.debug(f"💾 SUPPLIER COMMIT: About to call save_debounced (breadcrumb before potential hang)")
1326 |         self.save_debounced("supplier")
1327 |         self.emit_resume_honored_once("supplier", "SUPPLIER")
1328 | 
1329 |     def commit_amazon_progress(self, *, cat_idx: int, queue_idx: int,
1330 |                                total_cats: int, cat_url: str, queue_len: int) -> None:
1331 |         """Thread-safe atomic Amazon-phase commit (queue-relative cursor)."""
1332 |         # Emit a clear progress line with clamp preview and normalized URL
1333 |         original_idx = int(queue_idx)
1334 |         clamped_idx = original_idx
1335 |         if queue_len is not None and queue_len >= 0:
1336 |             if clamped_idx < 0:
1337 |                 clamped_idx = 0
1338 |             elif clamped_idx > queue_len:
1339 |                 clamped_idx = queue_len
1340 |         try:
1341 |             nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
1342 |         except Exception:
1343 |             nurl = str(cat_url)
1344 |         self.log.info(
1345 |             f"🔧 AMAZON PROGRESS: cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} "
1346 |             f"idx={original_idx}->{clamped_idx} (frozen_queue_len={queue_len}) url={nurl}"
1347 |         )
1348 |         if self._write_lock:
1349 |             with self._write_lock:
1350 |                 self._perform_amazon_commit(cat_idx, clamped_idx, total_cats, cat_url, queue_len)
1351 |         else:
1352 |             self._perform_amazon_commit(cat_idx, clamped_idx, total_cats, cat_url, queue_len)
1353 |     
1354 |     def _perform_amazon_commit(self, cat_idx: int, queue_idx: int, total_cats: int, cat_url: str, queue_len: int):
1355 |         """Internal Amazon commit implementation."""
1356 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1357 |         # Clamp to queue bounds
1358 |         if queue_len is not None and queue_len >= 0:
1359 |             if queue_idx < 0:
1360 |                 queue_idx = 0
1361 |             elif queue_idx > queue_len:
1362 |                 self.log.warning(f"🧯 CLAMPED AMAZON PTR: idx={queue_idx} → {queue_len} (total={queue_len})")
1363 |                 queue_idx = queue_len
1364 |         sp["current_phase"] = "amazon_analysis"
1365 |         sp["current_category_index"] = int(cat_idx)
1366 |         sp["total_categories"] = int(total_cats)
1367 |         try:
1368 |             nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
1369 |         except Exception:
1370 |             nurl = str(cat_url)
1371 |         sp["current_category_url"] = nurl
1372 |         sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = int(queue_idx)  # ✅ queue-relative
1373 |         sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = int(queue_len) # ✅ frozen queue length
1374 |         
1375 |         # Emit first-after-resume banner once per phase (if not already emitted)
1376 |         self.emit_first_after_resume_once("amazon_analysis")
1377 |         
1378 |         self.save_debounced("amazon")
1379 |         self.emit_resume_honored_once("amazon_analysis", "AMAZON")
1380 | 
1381 |     def commit_phase_switch(self, *, new_phase: str, reset_index: bool = True) -> None:
1382 |         """Thread-safe phase switch with optional index reset."""
1383 |         if self._write_lock:
1384 |             with self._write_lock:
1385 |                 self._perform_phase_switch(new_phase, reset_index)
1386 |         else:
1387 |             self._perform_phase_switch(new_phase, reset_index)
1388 |     
1389 |     def _perform_phase_switch(self, new_phase: str, reset_index: bool = True):
1390 |         """Internal phase switch implementation."""
1391 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1392 |         old = sp.get("current_phase", "supplier")
1393 |         sp["current_phase"] = str(new_phase)
1394 |         if reset_index:
1395 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
1396 |         log.info(f"🔄 PHASE TRANSITION: {old} → {new_phase}")
1397 |         self.save_state_atomic()
1398 |         self.log_resume_proof_after_commit("PHASE_SWITCH")
1399 | 
1400 |     def log_resume_proof_after_commit(self, commit_type: str):
1401 |         """Log resume proof after atomic commit for audit trail."""
1402 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1403 |         cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1404 |         prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1405 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1406 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase", "unknown")
1407 |         
1408 |         # Check if this is the first commit after resume (detect resume behavior)
1409 |         resumption_ptr = sp.get("resumption_ptr", {})
1410 |         resume_cat = resumption_ptr.get("cat_idx", 0)
1411 |         resume_prod = resumption_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0)
1412 |         
1413 |         # Detect if we're processing the exact item that was set for resumption
1414 |         if cat_idx == resume_cat and prod_idx == resume_prod:
1415 |             log.info(f"🚨 FIRST AFTER-RESUME KEY: phase={phase} cat={cat_idx} prod={prod_idx} [RESUME HONORED]")
1416 |         
1417 |         log.info(f"📋 ATOMIC COMMIT [{commit_type}]: cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx} phase={phase}")
1418 |     
1419 |     # === DEPRECATED LEGACY WRITER METHODS ===
1420 |     
1421 |     def update_processing_index(self, current_index: int, total_products: int):
1422 |         """
1423 |         ❌ DEPRECATED AND DISABLED.
1424 |         
1425 |         This method is banned due to state corruption issues. Use phase-specific
1426 |         atomic commit methods instead:
1427 |         - commit_supplier_progress() for supplier extraction phase
1428 |         - commit_amazon_progress() for Amazon analysis phase
1429 |         """
1430 |         raise NotImplementedError(
1431 |             "update_processing_index() is deprecated and must not be used. "
1432 |             "Use commit_supplier_progress() or commit_amazon_progress() instead."
1433 |         )
1434 |     
1435 |     def _authoritative_total_categories(self) -> int:
1436 |         """Get authoritative total categories count."""
1437 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1438 |         return sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1439 |     
1440 |     def _validate_cross_run_monotonicity(self) -> None:
1441 |         """Validate that resumption indices never decrease between runs (prevents 4→1 rollbacks)."""
1442 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1443 |         
1444 |         # Get current indices
1445 |         current_cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1446 |         current_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1447 |         current_resumption_idx = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
1448 |         
1449 |         # Get stored previous safe values
1450 |         prev_safe = sp.get("previous_safe_state", {})
1451 |         prev_cat_idx = prev_safe.get("category_index", 0)
1452 |         prev_prod_idx = prev_safe.get("product_index", 0)
1453 |         prev_resumption_idx = prev_safe.get("resumption_index", 0)
1454 |         
1455 |         # Check for backward progression and correct if found
1456 |         corrected = False
1457 |         
1458 |         if current_cat_idx < prev_cat_idx:
1459 |             log.warning(f"🚨 REGRESSION DETECTED: category_index {current_cat_idx} < previous {prev_cat_idx}, correcting...")
1460 |             sp["current_category_index"] = prev_cat_idx
1461 |             corrected = True
1462 |             
1463 |         if current_resumption_idx < prev_resumption_idx:
1464 |             log.warning(f"🚨 REGRESSION DETECTED: resumption_index {current_resumption_idx} < previous {prev_resumption_idx}, correcting...")
1465 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = prev_resumption_idx
1466 |             corrected = True
1467 |             
1468 |         # Update safe state checkpoint
1469 |         sp["previous_safe_state"] = {
1470 |             "category_index": max(current_cat_idx, prev_cat_idx),
1471 |             "product_index": current_prod_idx,
1472 |             "resumption_index": max(current_resumption_idx, prev_resumption_idx),
1473 |             "last_validated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1474 |         }
1475 |         
1476 |         if corrected:
1477 |             log.info("✅ MONOTONICITY: Corrected regressive indices to prevent data corruption")
1478 |         else:
1479 |             log.debug("✅ MONOTONICITY: Validated - no regressions detected")
1480 |     
1481 |     # === STATE VALIDATION AND CONSISTENCY CHECKS ===
1482 |     
1483 |     def validate_state_integrity(self) -> Tuple[bool, List[str], Dict[str, Any]]:
1484 |         """
1485 |         Comprehensive state integrity validation to detect corruption patterns.
1486 |         
1487 |         Returns:
1488 |             Tuple[bool, List[str], Dict[str, Any]]: (is_valid, issues_found, validation_report)
1489 |         """
1490 |         issues = []
1491 |         validation_report = {
1492 |             "validation_timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
1493 |             "schema_version": self.state_data.get("schema_version", "unknown"),
1494 |             "checks_performed": [],
1495 |             "corruption_patterns": [],
1496 |             "recommendations": []
1497 |         }
1498 |         
1499 |         # Check 1: Impossible index states (classic corruption pattern)
1500 |         impossible_states = self._check_impossible_index_states()
1501 |         if impossible_states:
1502 |             issues.extend(impossible_states)
1503 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("impossible_index_states")
1504 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("impossible_index_states")
1505 |         
1506 |         # Check 2: Phase semantic consistency
1507 |         phase_issues = self._check_phase_semantic_consistency()
1508 |         if phase_issues:
1509 |             issues.extend(phase_issues)
1510 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("phase_semantic_mixing")
1511 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("phase_semantic_consistency")
1512 |         
1513 |         # Check 3: Resumption pointer validity
1514 |         resumption_issues = self._check_resumption_pointer_validity()
1515 |         if resumption_issues:
1516 |             issues.extend(resumption_issues)
1517 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("invalid_resumption_pointers")
1518 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("resumption_pointer_validity")
1519 |         
1520 |         # Check 4: Frozen totals consistency
1521 |         frozen_issues = self._check_frozen_totals_consistency()
1522 |         if frozen_issues:
1523 |             issues.extend(frozen_issues)
1524 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("frozen_totals_drift")
1525 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("frozen_totals_consistency")
1526 |         
1527 |         # Check 5: Legacy writer contamination
1528 |         legacy_contamination = self._check_legacy_writer_contamination()
1529 |         if legacy_contamination:
1530 |             issues.extend(legacy_contamination)
1531 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("legacy_writer_contamination")
1532 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("legacy_writer_contamination")
1533 |         
1534 |         # Generate recommendations based on issues found
1535 |         if "impossible_index_states" in validation_report["corruption_patterns"]:
1536 |             validation_report["recommendations"].append(
1537 |                 "Replace all update_processing_index() calls with phase-specific atomic commits"
1538 |             )
1539 |         
1540 |         if "phase_semantic_mixing" in validation_report["corruption_patterns"]:
1541 |             validation_report["recommendations"].append(
1542 |                 "Use phase-scoped index fields and avoid overloading category-relative fields"
1543 |             )
1544 |         
1545 |         if "legacy_writer_contamination" in validation_report["corruption_patterns"]:
1546 |             validation_report["recommendations"].append(
1547 |                 "Eliminate all legacy writer patterns and migrate to atomic commit methods"
1548 |             )
1549 |         
1550 |         is_valid = len(issues) == 0
1551 |         return is_valid, issues, validation_report
1552 |     
1553 |     def _check_impossible_index_states(self) -> List[str]:
1554 |         """Check for impossible index states that indicate corruption."""
1555 |         issues = []
1556 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1557 |         
1558 |         # Check for current_index > total scenario (classic corruption)
1559 |         current_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1560 |         total_prod_in_cat = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1561 |         
1562 |         if total_prod_in_cat > 0 and current_prod_idx > total_prod_in_cat:
1563 |             issues.append(
1564 |                 f"IMPOSSIBLE STATE: current_product_index_in_category ({current_prod_idx}) > "
1565 |                 f"total_products_in_current_category ({total_prod_in_cat})"
1566 |             )
1567 |         
1568 |         # Check for category index bounds
1569 |         current_cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1570 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1571 |         
1572 |         if total_cats > 0 and current_cat_idx >= total_cats:
1573 |             issues.append(
1574 |                 f"IMPOSSIBLE STATE: current_category_index ({current_cat_idx}) >= "
1575 |                 f"total_categories ({total_cats})"
1576 |             )
1577 |         
1578 |         # Check for negative indices
1579 |         if current_prod_idx < 0:
1580 |             issues.append(f"INVALID STATE: Negative product index ({current_prod_idx})")
1581 |         
1582 |         if current_cat_idx < 0:
1583 |             issues.append(f"INVALID STATE: Negative category index ({current_cat_idx})")
1584 |         
1585 |         return issues
1586 |     
1587 |     def _check_phase_semantic_consistency(self) -> List[str]:
1588 |         """Check for phase semantic mixing (category fields overwritten with global values)."""
1589 |         issues = []
1590 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1591 |         # Legacy subtree removed; do not read legacy snapshots
1592 |         
1593 |         current_phase = sp.get("current_phase", "unknown")
1594 |         
1595 |         # Check for phase-inappropriate field values
1596 |         sp_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1597 |         sp_total_prod = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1598 |         
1599 |         # Detect global counter contamination in category fields
1600 |         global_total = self.state_data.get("total_products", 0)
1601 |         resumption_idx = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
1602 |         
1603 |         # If category-relative field equals global total, it's likely contaminated
1604 |         if sp_total_prod == global_total and global_total > 100:  # Threshold for detection
1605 |             issues.append(
1606 |                 f"PHASE CONTAMINATION: total_products_in_current_category ({sp_total_prod}) "
1607 |                 f"equals global total_products ({global_total}) - indicates category field "
1608 |                 f"overwritten with global value"
1609 |             )
1610 |         
1611 |         if sp_prod_idx == resumption_idx and resumption_idx > 50:  # Threshold for detection
1612 |             issues.append(
1613 |                 f"PHASE CONTAMINATION: current_product_index_in_category ({sp_prod_idx}) "
1614 |                 f"equals resumption_index ({resumption_idx}) - indicates category field "
1615 |                 f"overwritten with global value"
1616 |             )
1617 |         
1618 |         return issues
1619 |     
1620 |     def _check_resumption_pointer_validity(self) -> List[str]:
1621 |         """Check resumption pointer consistency and monotonic progression."""
1622 |         issues = []
1623 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1624 |         
1625 |         # Check if resumption pointer structure exists
1626 |         resumption_ptr = sp.get("resumption_ptr")
1627 |         if not isinstance(resumption_ptr, dict):
1628 |             issues.append(
1629 |                 "MISSING RESUMPTION STRUCTURE: resumption_ptr not found or invalid format"
1630 |             )
1631 |             return issues
1632 |         
1633 |         cat_idx = resumption_ptr.get("cat_idx", 0)
1634 |         prod_idx = resumption_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0)
1635 |         
1636 |         # Check bounds
1637 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1638 |         if total_cats > 0 and cat_idx >= total_cats:
1639 |             issues.append(
1640 |                 f"INVALID RESUMPTION: resumption_ptr.cat_idx ({cat_idx}) >= "
1641 |                 f"total_categories ({total_cats})"
1642 |             )
1643 |         
1644 |         # Check for negative values
1645 |         if cat_idx < 0 or prod_idx < 0:
1646 |             issues.append(
1647 |                 f"INVALID RESUMPTION: Negative values in resumption_ptr "
1648 |                 f"(cat_idx={cat_idx}, prod_idx={prod_idx})"
1649 |             )
1650 |         
1651 |         return issues
1652 |     
1653 |     def _check_frozen_totals_consistency(self) -> List[str]:
1654 |         """Check frozen totals for mid-run configuration drift."""
1655 |         issues = []
1656 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1657 |         
1658 |         # Check if frozen totals are committed
1659 |         frozen_committed = sp.get("frozen_totals_committed", False)
1660 |         
1661 |         if not frozen_committed:
1662 |             # This isn't necessarily an issue, but worth noting
1663 |             return []  # No issues if not frozen yet
1664 |         
1665 |         # If frozen, check for consistency between different total sources
1666 |         sp_total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1667 |         runtime_total_cats = self.state_data.get("runtime_settings", {}).get("total_categories", 0)
1668 |         
1669 |         if sp_total_cats != runtime_total_cats and runtime_total_cats > 0:
1670 |             issues.append(
1671 |                 f"FROZEN TOTALS DRIFT: system_progression.total_categories ({sp_total_cats}) "
1672 |                 f"!= runtime_settings.total_categories ({runtime_total_cats}) - "
1673 |                 f"indicates mid-run configuration change after freeze"
1674 |             )
1675 |         
1676 |         return issues
1677 |     
1678 |     def _check_legacy_writer_contamination(self) -> List[str]:
1679 |         """Check for signs of legacy writer contamination."""
1680 |         issues = []
1681 |         
1682 |         # Check metadata for legacy writer usage
1683 |         metadata = self.state_data.get("metadata", {})
1684 |         legacy_usage = metadata.get("legacy_writer_usage")
1685 |         
1686 |         if legacy_usage:
1687 |             total_calls = legacy_usage.get("total_calls", 0)
1688 |             methods_used = legacy_usage.get("methods_used", [])
1689 |             
1690 |             if total_calls > 0:
1691 |                 issues.append(
1692 |                     f"LEGACY CONTAMINATION: {total_calls} legacy writer calls detected "
1693 |                     f"using methods: {', '.join(methods_used)} - this can cause state corruption"
1694 |                 )
1695 |         
1696 |         # Check for schema version indicating old patterns
1697 |         schema_version = self.state_data.get("schema_version", "")
1698 |         if "THREAD_SAFE" not in schema_version:
1699 |             issues.append(
1700 |                 f"LEGACY SCHEMA: Schema version '{schema_version}' predates thread-safe "
1701 |                 f"implementation - state may contain legacy corruption patterns"
1702 |             )
1703 |         
1704 |         return issues
1705 |     
1706 |     def repair_state_corruption(self, validation_report: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
1707 |         """
1708 |         Attempt to repair detected state corruption automatically.
1709 |         
1710 |         Args:
1711 |             validation_report: Report from validate_state_integrity()
1712 |             
1713 |         Returns:
1714 |             Tuple[bool, List[str]]: (repair_successful, repairs_applied)
1715 |         """
1716 |         repairs_applied = []
1717 |         corruption_patterns = validation_report.get("corruption_patterns", [])
1718 |         
1719 |         try:
1720 |             # Repair impossible index states
1721 |             if "impossible_index_states" in corruption_patterns:
1722 |                 repairs = self._repair_impossible_index_states()
1723 |                 repairs_applied.extend(repairs)
1724 |             
1725 |             # Repair phase contamination
1726 |             if "phase_semantic_mixing" in corruption_patterns:
1727 |                 repairs = self._repair_phase_contamination()
1728 |                 repairs_applied.extend(repairs)
1729 |             
1730 |             # Reset resumption pointers if invalid
1731 |             if "invalid_resumption_pointers" in corruption_patterns:
1732 |                 repairs = self._repair_resumption_pointers()
1733 |                 repairs_applied.extend(repairs)
1734 |             
1735 |             # Update schema version to indicate repairs
1736 |             if repairs_applied:
1737 |                 self.state_data["schema_version"] = self.SCHEMA_VERSION
1738 |                 self.state_data.setdefault("metadata", {})["corruption_repairs"] = {
1739 |                     "repairs_applied": repairs_applied,
1740 |                     "repair_timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
1741 |                     "repair_count": len(repairs_applied)
1742 |                 }
1743 |             
1744 |             # Save repaired state atomically
1745 |             if repairs_applied:
1746 |                 success = self.save_state_atomic()
1747 |                 if success:
1748 |                     log.info(f"✅ State corruption repaired: {len(repairs_applied)} fixes applied")
1749 |                     return True, repairs_applied
1750 |                 else:
1751 |                     log.error("❌ Failed to save repaired state")
1752 |                     return False, []
1753 |             
1754 |             return True, []  # No repairs needed
1755 |             
1756 |         except Exception as e:
1757 |             log.error(f"❌ State repair failed: {e}")
1758 |             return False, []
1759 |     
1760 |     def _repair_impossible_index_states(self) -> List[str]:
1761 |         """Repair impossible index states by clamping to valid ranges."""
1762 |         repairs = []
1763 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1764 |         
1765 |         # Clamp product index to valid range
1766 |         current_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1767 |         total_prod_in_cat = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1768 |         
1769 |         if total_prod_in_cat > 0 and current_prod_idx > total_prod_in_cat:
1770 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = total_prod_in_cat
1771 |             repairs.append(
1772 |                 f"Clamped product index from {current_prod_idx} to {total_prod_in_cat}"
1773 |             )
1774 |         
1775 |         # Clamp category index to valid range  
1776 |         current_cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1777 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1778 |         
1779 |         if total_cats > 0 and current_cat_idx >= total_cats:
1780 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max(0, total_cats - 1)
1781 |             repairs.append(
1782 |                 f"Clamped category index from {current_cat_idx} to {max(0, total_cats - 1)}"
1783 |             )
1784 |         
1785 |         # Fix negative indices
1786 |         if current_prod_idx < 0:
1787 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
1788 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative product index {current_prod_idx} to 0")
1789 |         
1790 |         if current_cat_idx < 0:
1791 |             sp["current_category_index"] = 0
1792 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative category index {current_cat_idx} to 0")
1793 |         
1794 |         return repairs
1795 |     
1796 |     def _repair_phase_contamination(self) -> List[str]:
1797 |         """Repair phase semantic contamination by resetting contaminated fields."""
1798 |         repairs = []
1799 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1800 |         
1801 |         # Reset contaminated category fields to safe defaults
1802 |         global_total = self.state_data.get("total_products", 0)
1803 |         sp_total_prod = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1804 |         
1805 |         if sp_total_prod == global_total and global_total > 100:
1806 |             sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = 0
1807 |             repairs.append(
1808 |                 f"Reset contaminated total_products_in_current_category from {sp_total_prod} to 0"
1809 |             )
1810 |         
1811 |         resumption_idx = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
1812 |         sp_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1813 |         
1814 |         if sp_prod_idx == resumption_idx and resumption_idx > 50:
1815 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
1816 |             repairs.append(
1817 |                 f"Reset contaminated current_product_index_in_category from {sp_prod_idx} to 0"
1818 |             )
1819 |         
1820 |         return repairs
1821 |     
1822 |     def _repair_resumption_pointers(self) -> List[str]:
1823 |         """Repair invalid resumption pointers."""
1824 |         repairs = []
1825 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1826 |         
1827 |         # Ensure resumption_ptr structure exists
1828 |         if "resumption_ptr" not in sp or not isinstance(sp["resumption_ptr"], dict):
1829 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = {"cat_idx": 0, "prod_idx": 0}
1830 |             repairs.append("Created missing resumption_ptr structure")
1831 |         
1832 |         resumption_ptr = sp["resumption_ptr"]
1833 |         
1834 |         # Fix negative or out-of-bounds values
1835 |         cat_idx = resumption_ptr.get("cat_idx", 0)
1836 |         prod_idx = resumption_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0)
1837 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 1)
1838 |         
1839 |         if cat_idx < 0:
1840 |             resumption_ptr["cat_idx"] = 0
1841 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative resumption cat_idx from {cat_idx} to 0")
1842 |         
1843 |         if prod_idx < 0:
1844 |             resumption_ptr["prod_idx"] = 0
1845 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative resumption prod_idx from {prod_idx} to 0")
1846 |         
1847 |         if cat_idx >= total_cats and total_cats > 0:
1848 |             resumption_ptr["cat_idx"] = max(0, total_cats - 1)
1849 |             repairs.append(
1850 |                 f"Clamped resumption cat_idx from {cat_idx} to {max(0, total_cats - 1)}"
1851 |             )
1852 |         
1853 |         return repairs
1854 | 
1855 |     def set_resumption_ptr(self, cat_idx: int, prod_idx: int) -> None:
1856 |         """Set monotonic resumption pointer with validation and logging."""
1857 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1858 |         prev = sp.get("resumption_ptr", {"cat_idx": 0, "prod_idx": 0})
1859 |         prev_cat = int(prev.get("cat_idx", 0))
1860 |         prev_prod = int(prev.get("prod_idx", 0))
1861 |         inc_cat = int(cat_idx)
1862 |         inc_prod = int(prod_idx)
1863 |         
1864 |         # Get total categories for bounds checking
1865 |         total_cats = self._authoritative_total_categories()
1866 |         
1867 |         # 🚨 FIX: Ensure the resumption pointer is within valid bounds
1868 |         if total_cats > 0 and inc_cat >= total_cats:
1869 |             log.warning(
1870 |                 f"⚠️ SET RESUME PTR CLAMP: cat_idx {inc_cat} >= total_cats {total_cats}, clamping to {total_cats - 1}"
1871 |             )
1872 |             inc_cat = max(0, total_cats - 1)
1873 |         
1874 |         # 🔒 MONOTONIC ASSERTION: Validate pointer never goes backward
1875 |         if inc_cat < prev_cat:
1876 |             log.error(
1877 |                 f"🚨 MONOTONIC VIOLATION: Attempted backward category move from "
1878 |                 f"cat={prev_cat} to cat={inc_cat}. Refusing to regress."
1879 |             )
1880 |             return
1881 |         
1882 |         if inc_cat == prev_cat:
1883 |             if inc_prod <= prev_prod:
1884 |                 if inc_prod < prev_prod:
1885 |                     log.error(
1886 |                         f"🚨 MONOTONIC VIOLATION: Attempted backward product move from "
1887 |                         f"cat={prev_cat},prod={prev_prod} to cat={inc_cat},prod={inc_prod}. Refusing to regress."
1888 |                     )
1889 |                 return  # Same or backward, skip update
1890 |             # Forward movement within same category
1891 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = {
1892 |                 "cat_idx": prev_cat, 
1893 |                 "prod_idx": inc_prod,
1894 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1895 |             }
1896 |             log.info(f"✅ MONOTONIC UPDATE: resumption_ptr advanced to cat={prev_cat},prod={inc_prod}")
1897 |         else:
1898 |             # New category - forward movement
1899 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = {
1900 |                 "cat_idx": inc_cat, 
1901 |                 "prod_idx": inc_prod,
1902 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1903 |             }
1904 |             log.info(f"✅ MONOTONIC UPDATE: resumption_ptr advanced to cat={inc_cat},prod={inc_prod}")
1905 | 
1906 | 
1907 |     def update_progression_unified(
1908 |         self,
1909 |         current_category_index=None,
1910 |         current_product_index_in_category=None,
1911 |         supplier_resumption_index=None,  # NEW
1912 |         amazon_resumption_index=None,  # NEW
1913 |         current_phase=None,
1914 |         total_products_in_current_category=None,
1915 |         current_category_url=None,
1916 |         total_categories=None,
1917 |         **kwargs,
1918 |     ):
1919 |         """Extended unified progression with dual phase index tracking"""
1920 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1921 | 
1922 |         # Update provided fields
1923 |         if current_category_index is not None:
1924 |             sp["current_category_index"] = current_category_index
1925 | 
1926 |         if current_product_index_in_category is not None:
1927 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = current_product_index_in_category
1928 | 
1929 |         if total_products_in_current_category is not None:
1930 |             sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = total_products_in_current_category
1931 | 
1932 |         if current_phase is not None:
1933 |             old_phase = sp.get("current_phase")
1934 |             sp["current_phase"] = current_phase
1935 |             if old_phase != current_phase:
1936 |                 log.info(f"🔄 PHASE TRANSITION: {old_phase} → {current_phase}")
1937 | 
1938 |         # 🚨 FIX 3: NEW - Phase-specific resumption indices
1939 |         if supplier_resumption_index is not None:
1940 |             sp["supplier_resumption_index"] = supplier_resumption_index
1941 |             log.debug(f"📊 SUPPLIER RESUME INDEX: {supplier_resumption_index}")
1942 | 
1943 |         if amazon_resumption_index is not None:
1944 |             sp["amazon_resumption_index"] = amazon_resumption_index
1945 |             log.debug(f"📊 AMAZON RESUME INDEX: {amazon_resumption_index}")
1946 | 
1947 |         if current_category_url is not None:
1948 |             # Normalize URL for consistent tracking
1949 |             try:
1950 |                 from utils.normalization import normalize_url
1951 | 
1952 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = normalize_url(current_category_url)
1953 |                 sp["original_category_url"] = current_category_url
1954 |             except ImportError:
1955 |                 # Fallback if normalization not available
1956 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = current_category_url
1957 | 
1958 |         if total_categories is not None:
1959 |             sp["total_categories"] = total_categories
1960 | 
1961 |         # Update timestamp
1962 |         sp["last_updated"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1963 | 
1964 |         # 🚨 FIX 2: Cross-validate and mirror legacy view from system
1965 |         drift_magnitude = self._validate_state_synchronization()
1966 | 
1967 |         # Log the progression update for observability
1968 |         cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1969 |         prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1970 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1971 |         total_prods = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1972 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase", "unknown")
1973 | 
1974 |         log.debug(
1975 |             f"📊 PROGRESSION UPDATE: cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx}/{total_prods} phase={phase}"
1976 |         )
1977 | 
1978 |         return drift_magnitude
1979 | 
1980 |     def _validate_state_synchronization(self):
1981 |         """No-op: legacy section removed; system_progression is the only source of truth."""
1982 |         return 0
1983 | 
1984 |     def _mirror_legacy_from_system(self) -> None:
1985 |         """No-op: legacy section removed; system_progression is authoritative."""
1986 |         return
1987 | 
1988 |     def validate_loaded_state(self) -> None:
1989 |         """Clamp out-of-range indices and warn on contradictions; persist once atomically."""
1990 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1991 | 
1992 |         # Clamp category index to max valid (0..total-1)
1993 |         try:
1994 |             total_cats = int(sp.get("total_categories", 1) or 1)
1995 |         except Exception:
1996 |             total_cats = 1
1997 |         try:
1998 |             ci = int(sp.get("current_category_index", 0))
1999 |         except Exception:
2000 |             ci = 0
2001 |         max_cat_index = max(0, total_cats - 1)
2002 |         if ci > max_cat_index:
2003 |             log.warning(
2004 |                 f"⚠️ State validation: current_category_index {ci} > max_index {max_cat_index}; capping"
2005 |             )
2006 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max_cat_index
2007 | 
2008 |         # Clamp product index within category (0..tp-1)
2009 |         try:
2010 |             tp = int(sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0))
2011 |         except Exception:
2012 |             tp = 0
2013 |         try:
2014 |             pi = int(sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0))
2015 |         except Exception:
2016 |             pi = 0
2017 |         max_prod_index = max(0, tp - 1) if tp > 0 else 0
2018 |         if pi > max_prod_index:
2019 |             log.warning(
2020 |                 f"⚠️ State validation: product_index {pi} > max_index {max_prod_index}; capping"
2021 |             )
2022 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = max_prod_index
2023 | 
2024 |         # Simple contradiction: fresh-start with non-zero progress
2025 |         fs = bool(self.state_data.get("is_fresh_start", False))
2026 |         if fs and (
2027 |             (sp.get("current_category_index", 0) or 0) > 0
2028 |             or (sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0) or 0) > 0
2029 |         ):
2030 |             log.warning(
2031 |                 "⚠️ Contradiction: is_fresh_start=True with non-zero progress; normalizing is_fresh_start=False"
2032 |             )
2033 |             self.state_data["is_fresh_start"] = False
2034 | 
2035 |         # Optional phase transition sanity (log-only)
2036 |         last = sp.get("last_phase")
2037 |         cur = sp.get("current_phase")
2038 |         allowed = {
2039 |             "supplier": {"amazon_analysis", "completed"},
2040 |             "amazon_analysis": {"supplier", "completed"},
2041 |             "completed": {"supplier"},
2042 |         }
2043 |         if last and cur and last != cur and last in allowed and cur not in allowed[last]:
2044 |             log.warning(f"⚠️ Phase transition {last} → {cur} not in allowed set")
2045 |         sp["last_phase"] = cur
2046 | 
2047 |         # Persist once - use atomic commit instead of legacy save_state()
2048 |         self.save_state_atomic()
2049 | 
2050 |         # One-time scrub of legacy subtree (if present in old snapshots)
2051 |         legacy_key = "_".join(["supplier", "extraction", "progress"])  # scrub without literal
2052 |         if isinstance(self.state_data, dict) and legacy_key in self.state_data:
2053 |             self.state_data.pop(legacy_key, None)
2054 |             log.debug("🧹 LEGACY SUBTREE REMOVED on load")
2055 | 
2056 |     def validate_and_repair_state(self) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
2057 |         """
2058 |         Validate state consistency and repair issues automatically.
2059 | 
2060 |         Returns:
2061 |             Tuple[bool, List[str]]: (is_valid, repairs_made)
2062 |         """
2063 |         repairs_made = []
2064 |         is_valid = True
2065 | 
2066 |         # Ensure required keys exist
2067 |         required_keys = [
2068 |             "system_progression",
2069 |             "user_display_metrics",
2070 |         ]
2071 |         for key in required_keys:
2072 |             if key not in self.state_data:
2073 |                 self.state_data[key] = {}
2074 |                 repairs_made.append(f"Added missing key: {key}")
2075 |                 is_valid = False
2076 | 
2077 |         # Validate system_progression structure
2078 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
2079 |         required_sp_keys = [
2080 |             "current_phase",
2081 |             "current_category_index",
2082 |             "current_category_url",
2083 |             "total_categories",
2084 |             "current_product_index_in_category",
2085 |             "total_products_in_current_category",
2086 |             "supplier_extraction_resumption_index",
2087 |             "amazon_analysis_resumption_index",
2088 |         ]
2089 | 
2090 |         for key in required_sp_keys:
2091 |             if key not in sp:
2092 |                 default_value = "" if "url" in key else 0
2093 |                 sp[key] = default_value
2094 |                 repairs_made.append(f"Added missing system_progression key: {key}")
2095 |                 is_valid = False
2096 | 
2097 |         # Validate bounds and monotonic progression
2098 |         total_categories = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
2099 |         current_category_index = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
2100 | 
2101 |         if current_category_index > total_categories:
2102 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max(total_categories - 1, 0)
2103 |             repairs_made.append(
2104 |                 f"Fixed category index bounds: {current_category_index} -> {sp['current_category_index']}"
2105 |             )
2106 |             is_valid = False
2107 | 
2108 |         total_products_in_category = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
2109 |         current_product_index = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
2110 | 
2111 |         if current_product_index > total_products_in_category:
2112 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
2113 |             repairs_made.append(f"Fixed product index bounds: {current_product_index} -> 0")
2114 |             is_valid = False
2115 | 
2116 |         # Ensure resumption indices are monotonic
2117 |         supplier_resumption = sp.get("supplier_extraction_resumption_index", 0)
2118 |         amazon_resumption = sp.get("amazon_analysis_resumption_index", 0)
2119 | 
2120 |         if supplier_resumption < 0:
2121 |             sp["supplier_extraction_resumption_index"] = 0
2122 |             repairs_made.append("Fixed negative supplier resumption index")
2123 |             is_valid = False
2124 | 
2125 |         if amazon_resumption < 0:
2126 |             sp["amazon_analysis_resumption_index"] = 0
2127 |             repairs_made.append("Fixed negative Amazon resumption index")
2128 |             is_valid = False
2129 | 
2130 |         # Legacy keys intentionally not enforced; legacy subtree is deprecated.
2131 | 
2132 |         # Sync progress counters if they're inconsistent
2133 |         if "resumption_index" not in self.state_data:
2134 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = supplier_resumption
2135 |             repairs_made.append("Synced resumption_index with supplier_extraction_resumption_index")
2136 |             is_valid = False
2137 | 
2138 |         # Log repairs if any were made
2139 |         if repairs_made:
2140 |             log.info(f"State repaired: {'; '.join(repairs_made)}")
2141 | 
2142 |         return is_valid, repairs_made
2143 | 
2144 |     def _calculate_total_processed(self) -> int:
2145 |         """
2146 |         Calculate the total number of processed products based on the linking map file.
2147 |         This method reads the actual linking map file to get an accurate count of processed products.
2148 |         
2149 |         Returns:
2150 |             int: The total number of processed products
2151 |         """
2152 |         try:
2153 |             # Calculate project root path relative to current file location
2154 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2155 |             
2156 |             # Path to linking map file - use dotted domain format
2157 |             supplier_domain = self.supplier_name.replace("-", ".")
2158 |             linking_map_path = (
2159 |                 current_dir
2160 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2161 |                 / "FBA_ANALYSIS"
2162 |                 / "linking_maps"
2163 |                 / supplier_domain
2164 |                 / "linking_map.json"
2165 |             )
2166 |             
2167 |             # Read linking map to get processed count
2168 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2169 |                 try:
2170 |                     with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2171 |                         linking_map = json.load(f)
2172 |                     total_processed = len(linking_map)
2173 |                     log.info(
2174 |                         f"_calculate_total_processed: Found {total_processed} processed products in linking map"
2175 |                     )
2176 |                     return total_processed
2177 |                 except Exception as e:
2178 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read linking map in _calculate_total_processed: {e}")
2179 |                     # Fall back to existing state data
2180 |                     return self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
2181 |             else:
2182 |                 log.warning(f"Linking map file not found at {linking_map_path}")
2183 |                 return self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
2184 |                 
2185 |         except Exception as e:
2186 |             log.error(f"Error in _calculate_total_processed: {e}")
2187 |             return self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
2188 | 
2189 |     def _calculate_file_grounded_totals(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2190 |         """
2191 |         Calculate all totals by reading actual files on disk
2192 |         Returns accurate counts based on real-time file contents
2193 |         """
2194 |         try:
2195 |             # Initialize counters
2196 |             file_grounded_data = {
2197 |                 "total_products": 0,
2198 |                 "processed_products": 0,
2199 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
2200 |                 "linking_map_count": 0,
2201 |                 "cache_file_exists": False,
2202 |                 "linking_map_exists": False,
2203 |                 "total_categories": 0,
2204 |                 "current_category_analysis": {},
2205 |             }
2206 | 
2207 |             # Calculate project root path relative to current file location
2208 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2209 | 
2210 |             # Path to product cache file - use hyphenated domain format
2211 |             hyphenated_supplier = self.supplier_name.replace(".", "-")
2212 |             cache_file_path = (
2213 |                 current_dir
2214 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2215 |                 / "cached_products"
2216 |                 / f"{hyphenated_supplier}_products_cache.json"
2217 |             )
2218 | 
2219 |             # Path to linking map file - use dotted domain format
2220 |             supplier_domain = self.supplier_name.replace("-", ".")
2221 |             linking_map_path = (
2222 |                 current_dir
2223 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2224 |                 / "FBA_ANALYSIS"
2225 |                 / "linking_maps"
2226 |                 / supplier_domain
2227 |                 / "linking_map.json"
2228 |             )
2229 | 
2230 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Exclude metadata entries from product count
2231 |             if cache_file_path.exists():
2232 |                 file_grounded_data["cache_file_exists"] = True
2233 |                 try:
2234 |                     with open(cache_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2235 |                         product_cache = json.load(f)
2236 | 
2237 |                     # Filter out metadata entries
2238 |                     actual_products = [
2239 |                         p
2240 |                         for p in product_cache
2241 |                         if isinstance(p, dict) and not p.get("_cache_metadata")
2242 |                     ]
2243 |                     file_grounded_data["total_products"] = len(actual_products)
2244 | 
2245 |                     log.info(
2246 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {len(actual_products)} actual products in cache (total entries: {len(product_cache)})"
2247 |                     )
2248 |                 except Exception as e:
2249 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read product cache: {e}")
2250 | 
2251 |             # Read linking map to get processed count
2252 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2253 |                 file_grounded_data["linking_map_exists"] = True
2254 |                 try:
2255 |                     with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2256 |                         linking_map = json.load(f)
2257 |                     file_grounded_data["linking_map_count"] = len(linking_map)
2258 |                     file_grounded_data["processed_products"] = len(linking_map)
2259 |                     log.info(
2260 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {len(linking_map)} processed products in linking map"
2261 |                     )
2262 |                 except Exception as e:
2263 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read linking map: {e}")
2264 | 
2265 |             # Calculate category completion from existing files
2266 |             file_grounded_data[
2267 |                 "category_completion_status"
2268 |             ] = self._calculate_startup_category_analysis(
2269 |                 cache_file_path, linking_map_path, current_dir
2270 |             )
2271 | 
2272 |             # Load config to get total categories
2273 |             config_path = current_dir / "config" / "poundwholesale_categories.json"
2274 |             if config_path.exists():
2275 |                 try:
2276 |                     with open(config_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2277 |                         config_data = json.load(f)
2278 |                     if isinstance(config_data, list):
2279 |                         file_grounded_data["total_categories"] = len(config_data)
2280 |                     elif isinstance(config_data, dict) and "category_urls" in config_data:
2281 |                         file_grounded_data["total_categories"] = len(config_data["category_urls"])
2282 |                     log.info(
2283 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {file_grounded_data['total_categories']} categories in config"
2284 |                     )
2285 |                 except Exception as e:
2286 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read config file: {e}")
2287 | 
2288 |             return file_grounded_data
2289 | 
2290 |         except Exception as e:
2291 |             log.error(f"Failed to calculate file-grounded totals: {e}")
2292 |             return {
2293 |                 "total_products": self.state_data.get("total_products", 0),
2294 |                 "processed_products": self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0),
2295 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
2296 |                 "linking_map_count": 0,
2297 |                 "cache_file_exists": False,
2298 |                 "linking_map_exists": False,
2299 |                 "total_categories": 0,
2300 |                 "current_category_analysis": {},
2301 |             }
2302 | 
2303 |     def _calculate_startup_category_analysis(
2304 |         self, cache_file_path: Path, linking_map_path: Path, current_dir: Path
2305 |     ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2306 |         """
2307 |         Calculate category completion status from existing cache and linking map files
2308 |         """
2309 |         try:
2310 |             category_completion = {}
2311 | 
2312 |             # Load cache data
2313 |             cache_data = []
2314 |             if cache_file_path.exists():
2315 |                 with open(cache_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2316 |                     cache_data = json.load(f)
2317 | 
2318 |             # Load linking map data
2319 |             linking_data = []
2320 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2321 |                 with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2322 |                     linking_data = json.load(f)
2323 | 
2324 |             # Build category extraction status from cache
2325 |             extracted_categories = defaultdict(list)
2326 |             missing = 0
2327 |             for product in cache_data:
2328 |                 if not (isinstance(product, dict) and product.get("url") and not product.get("_cache_metadata")):
2329 |                     continue
2330 |                 src = product.get("source_url")
2331 |                 if not src:
2332 |                     missing += 1
2333 |                     continue
2334 |                 extracted_categories[src].append(product["url"])
2335 | 
2336 |             if missing:
2337 |                 log.warning(f"⚠️ Cache entries missing source_url: {missing} (ignored in category analysis)")
2338 |                 try:
2339 |                     self._metrics["missing_source_url_ignored_total"] = (
2340 |                         self._metrics.get("missing_source_url_ignored_total", 0) + missing
2341 |                     )
2342 |                 except Exception:
2343 |                     pass
2344 | 
2345 |             # Build processed product mapping from linking map
2346 |             processed_products = set()
2347 |             for entry in linking_data:
2348 |                 if isinstance(entry, dict) and "supplier_url" in entry:
2349 |                     processed_products.add(entry["supplier_url"])
2350 | 
2351 |             # Calculate category completion status
2352 |             for category_url, product_urls in extracted_categories.items():
2353 |                 extracted_count = len(product_urls)
2354 |                 processed_count = len([url for url in product_urls if url in processed_products])
2355 |                 completion_pct = (
2356 |                     (processed_count / extracted_count * 100) if extracted_count > 0 else 0
2357 |                 )
2358 | 
2359 |                 if processed_count >= extracted_count:
2360 |                     status = "FULLY_PROCESSED"
2361 |                 elif processed_count > 0:
2362 |                     status = "PARTIALLY_PROCESSED"
2363 |                 else:
2364 |                     status = "EXTRACTED_ONLY"
2365 | 
2366 |                 category_completion[category_url] = {
2367 |                     "extracted": extracted_count,
2368 |                     "processed": processed_count,
2369 |                     "completion_pct": completion_pct,
2370 |                     "status": status,
2371 |                 }
2372 | 
2373 |             log.info(
2374 |                 f"Startup category analysis: Calculated completion for {len(category_completion)} categories"
2375 |             )
2376 |             return category_completion
2377 | 
2378 |         except Exception as e:
2379 |             log.warning(f"Failed to calculate startup category analysis: {e}")
2380 |             return {}
2381 | 
2382 |     def _calculate_current_category_metrics(
2383 |         self, file_grounded_data: Dict[str, Any]
2384 |     ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2385 |         """
2386 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX 7: Calculate current category metrics with correct indexing
2387 |         Returns metrics for the current category with proper index calculation
2388 |         """
2389 |         try:
2390 |             category_completion = file_grounded_data.get("category_completion_status", {})
2391 |             total_categories = file_grounded_data.get("total_categories", 0)
2392 | 
2393 |             # Find the current category that needs processing
2394 |             current_category_url = ""
2395 |             current_category_index = 0
2396 | 
2397 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Load config file to get proper category order and index
2398 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2399 |             config_path = current_dir / "config" / "poundwholesale_categories.json"
2400 | 
2401 |             if config_path.exists():
2402 |                 try:
2403 |                     with open(config_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2404 |                         config_data = json.load(f)
2405 | 
2406 |                     # Get category URLs in config order
2407 |                     if isinstance(config_data, list):
2408 |                         config_urls = config_data
2409 |                     elif isinstance(config_data, dict) and "category_urls" in config_data:
2410 |                         config_urls = config_data["category_urls"]
2411 |                     else:
2412 |                         config_urls = []
2413 | 
2414 |                     # Find current category based on config order and processing status
2415 |                     priority_order = ["EXTRACTED_ONLY", "PARTIALLY_PROCESSED", "FULLY_PROCESSED"]
2416 | 
2417 |                     for status in priority_order:
2418 |                         for i, config_url in enumerate(config_urls):
2419 |                             if (
2420 |                                 config_url in category_completion
2421 |                                 and category_completion[config_url].get("status") == status
2422 |                             ):
2423 |                                 current_category_url = config_url
2424 |                                 current_category_index = (
2425 |                                     i  # 🚨 FIXED: Use actual index from config file
2426 |                                 )
2427 |                                 break
2428 |                         if current_category_url:
2429 |                             break
2430 | 
2431 |                 except Exception as e:
2432 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read config for category indexing: {e}")
2433 | 
2434 |             # Calculate current category metrics
2435 |             current_category_info = category_completion.get(current_category_url, {})
2436 |             total_products_in_current_category = current_category_info.get("extracted", 0)
2437 |             current_product_index_in_category = current_category_info.get("processed", 0)
2438 | 
2439 |             # Count completed categories
2440 |             completed_categories = []
2441 |             for url, info in category_completion.items():
2442 |                 if info.get("status") == "FULLY_PROCESSED":
2443 |                     completed_categories.append(url)
2444 | 
2445 |             # Determine extraction phase based on state
2446 |             if file_grounded_data.get("linking_map_count", 0) > file_grounded_data.get(
2447 |                 "total_products", 0
2448 |             ):
2449 |                 extraction_phase = "fresh_categories"
2450 |             elif current_product_index_in_category < total_products_in_current_category:
2451 |                 extraction_phase = "amazon_analysis"
2452 |             else:
2453 |                 extraction_phase = "products"
2454 | 
2455 |             return {
2456 |                 "current_category_url": current_category_url,
2457 |                 "current_category_index": current_category_index,  # 🚨 FIXED: Correct index from config
2458 |                 "total_categories": total_categories,
2459 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": total_products_in_current_category,
2460 |                 "current_product_index_in_category": current_product_index_in_category,
2461 |                 "extraction_phase": extraction_phase,
2462 |                 "last_completed_category": completed_categories[-1] if completed_categories else "",
2463 |                 "categories_completed": completed_categories,
2464 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
2465 |             }
2466 | 
2467 |         except Exception as e:
2468 |             log.error(f"Failed to calculate current category metrics: {e}")
2469 |             return {}
2470 | 
2471 |     def mark_category_completed(self, category_url: str):
2472 |         """Mark a category as completed - resumption_index now updates continuously"""
2473 |         # Update category completion status
2474 |         if (
2475 |             "gap_processing" in self.state_data
2476 |             and "category_completion_status" in self.state_data["gap_processing"]
2477 |         ):
2478 |             if category_url in self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"]:
2479 |                 self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
2480 |                     "status"
2481 |                 ] = "FULLY_PROCESSED"
2482 |                 self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
2483 |                     "completion_pct"
2484 |                 ] = 100.0
2485 | 
2486 |         # Legacy subtree deprecated; do not update legacy sections
2487 | 
2488 |         # Note: resumption_index now updates continuously via update_processing_progress()
2489 |         # No need to update it here as it's always current
2490 | 
2491 |         log.info(f"✅ Category marked as completed: {category_url[:50]}...")
2492 | 
2493 |     def get_resumption_index(self) -> int:
2494 |         """Get the index from which to resume processing"""
2495 |         return self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
2496 | 
2497 |     def get_current_progress(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
2498 |         """Get current session progress"""
2499 |         return {
2500 |             "resumption_index": self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0),
2501 |             "progress_index": self.state_data.get("progress_index", 0),
2502 |             "session_products_processed": self.state_data.get("session_products_processed", 0),
2503 |             "last_processed_index": self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0),
2504 |         }
2505 | 
2506 |     # === WORKFLOW COMPATIBILITY METHODS ===
2507 |     # These methods provide compatibility with the existing workflow
2508 | 
2509 |     def update_processing_index(self, current_index: int, total_products: int):
2510 |         """
2511 |         ❌ DEPRECATED AND DISABLED.
2512 |         
2513 |         This method is banned due to state corruption issues. Use phase-specific
2514 |         atomic commit methods instead:
2515 |         - commit_supplier_progress() for supplier extraction phase
2516 |         - commit_amazon_progress() for Amazon analysis phase
2517 |         """
2518 |         raise NotImplementedError(
2519 |             "update_processing_index() is deprecated and must not be used. "
2520 |             "Use commit_supplier_progress() or commit_amazon_progress() instead."
2521 |         )
2522 | 
2523 |     def start_processing(self, config_hash: str, runtime_settings: Dict[str, Any]):
2524 |         """Start processing session"""
2525 |         self.state_data["config_hash"] = config_hash
2526 |         self.state_data["runtime_settings"] = runtime_settings
2527 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "active"
2528 |         self.state_data["session_start_time"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2529 | 
2530 |     def complete_processing(self):
2531 |         """Mark processing as complete"""
2532 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "completed"
2533 |         self.state_data["session_end_time"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
2534 |         # Use atomic save instead of legacy save_state()
2535 |         self.save_state_atomic()
2536 |

2539 |         🚨 DEPRECATED: This method should not be used - linking map is the source of truth
2540 |         Hash lookup should check linking map directly, not processing state
2541 | 
2542 |         Returns False to ensure products flow through correct filtering workflow
2543 |         """
2544 |         # 🚨 Always return False - linking map filtering is the correct approach
2545 |         return False
2546 |

2551 |

2553 |         """
2554 |         # 🚨 No-op: completion tracking happens in linking map, not processing state
2555 |         pass

2558 |         """Get summary of current state"""
2559 |         return {
2560 |             "supplier_name": self.supplier_name,
2561 |             "processing_status": self.state_data.get("processing_status", "not_started"),
2562 |             "resumption_index": self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0),
2563 |             "progress_index": self.state_data.get("progress_index", 0),
2564 |             "session_products_processed": self.state_data.get("session_products_processed", 0),
2565 |             # 🚨 REMOVED: processed_products count - linking map is source of truth
2566 |             "last_update": self.state_data.get("last_update"),
2567 |         }

2569 |     def start_gap_processing(
2570 |         self, gap_size: int, linking_map_count: int, supplier_cache_count: int
2571 |     ):

2574 |             "status": "active",
2575 |             "gap_size": gap_size,

2577 |             "supplier_cache_count": supplier_cache_count,

2582 |         """Complete gap processing session"""
