1330 |                                total_cats: int, cat_url: str, queue_len: int) -> None:
1331 |         """Thread-safe atomic Amazon-phase commit (queue-relative cursor)."""
1332 |         # Emit a clear progress line with clamp preview and normalized URL
1333 |         original_idx = int(queue_idx)
1334 |         clamped_idx = original_idx
1335 |         if queue_len is not None and queue_len >= 0:
1336 |             if clamped_idx < 0:
1337 |                 clamped_idx = 0
1338 |             elif clamped_idx > queue_len:
1339 |                 clamped_idx = queue_len
1340 |         try:
1341 |             nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
1342 |         except Exception:
1343 |             nurl = str(cat_url)
1344 |         self.log.info(
1345 |             f"🔧 AMAZON PROGRESS: cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} "
1346 |             f"idx={original_idx}->{clamped_idx} (frozen_queue_len={queue_len}) url={nurl}"
1347 |         )
1348 |         if self._write_lock:
1349 |             with self._write_lock:
1350 |                 self._perform_amazon_commit(cat_idx, clamped_idx, total_cats, cat_url, queue_len)
1351 |         else:
1352 |             self._perform_amazon_commit(cat_idx, clamped_idx, total_cats, cat_url, queue_len)
1353 |     
1354 |     def _perform_amazon_commit(self, cat_idx: int, queue_idx: int, total_cats: int, cat_url: str, queue_len: int):
1355 |         """Internal Amazon commit implementation."""
1356 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1357 |         # Clamp to queue bounds
1358 |         if queue_len is not None and queue_len >= 0:
1359 |             if queue_idx < 0:
1360 |                 queue_idx = 0
1361 |             elif queue_idx > queue_len:
1362 |                 self.log.warning(f"🧯 CLAMPED AMAZON PTR: idx={queue_idx} → {queue_len} (total={queue_len})")
1363 |                 queue_idx = queue_len
1364 |         sp["current_phase"] = "amazon_analysis"
1365 |         sp["current_category_index"] = int(cat_idx)
1366 |         sp["total_categories"] = int(total_cats)
1367 |         try:
1368 |             nurl = normalize_url(cat_url)
1369 |         except Exception:
1370 |             nurl = str(cat_url)
1371 |         sp["current_category_url"] = nurl
1372 |         sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = int(queue_idx)  # ✅ queue-relative
1373 |         sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = int(queue_len) # ✅ frozen queue length
1374 |         
1375 |         # Emit first-after-resume banner once per phase (if not already emitted)
1376 |         self.emit_first_after_resume_once("amazon_analysis")
1377 |         
1378 |         self.save_debounced("amazon")
1379 |         self.emit_resume_honored_once("amazon_analysis", "AMAZON")
1380 | 
1381 |     def commit_phase_switch(self, *, new_phase: str, reset_index: bool = True) -> None:
1382 |         """Thread-safe phase switch with optional index reset."""
1383 |         if self._write_lock:
1384 |             with self._write_lock:
1385 |                 self._perform_phase_switch(new_phase, reset_index)
1386 |         else:
1387 |             self._perform_phase_switch(new_phase, reset_index)
1388 |     
1389 |     def _perform_phase_switch(self, new_phase: str, reset_index: bool = True):
1390 |         """Internal phase switch implementation."""
1391 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1392 |         old = sp.get("current_phase", "supplier")
1393 |         sp["current_phase"] = str(new_phase)
1394 |         if reset_index:
1395 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
1396 |         log.info(f"🔄 PHASE TRANSITION: {old} → {new_phase}")
1397 |         self.save_state_atomic()
1398 |    
1399 |    def commit_amazon_progress(self, product_url: str, increment: int = 1) -> None:
1400 |        """Commit progress during Amazon analysis phase with atomic save"""
1401 |        # Update system progression for Amazon analysis phase
1402 |        sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1403 |        sp["current_phase"] = "amazon_analysis"
1404 |        sp["amazon_analysis_resumption_index"] = (
1405 |            sp.get("amazon_analysis_resumption_index", 0) + increment
1406 |        )
1407 |        
1408 |        # Update user display metrics
1409 |        ud = self.state_data.setdefault("user_display_metrics", {})
1410 |        ud["session_products_processed"] = ud.get("session_products_processed", 0) + increment
1411 |        
1412 |        # Perform atomic save
1413 |        self.save_state_atomic()
1414 |        
1415 |        # Log resume proof after commit
1416 |        self.log_resume_proof_after_commit("amazon_analysis")
1417 |         self.log_resume_proof_after_commit("PHASE_SWITCH")
1418 | 
1419 |     def log_resume_proof_after_commit(self, commit_type: str):
1420 |         """Log resume proof after atomic commit for audit trail."""
1421 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1422 |         cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1423 |         prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1424 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1425 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase", "unknown")
1426 |         
1427 |         # Check if this is the first commit after resume (detect resume behavior)
1428 |         resumption_ptr = sp.get("resumption_ptr", {})
1429 |         resume_cat = resumption_ptr.get("cat_idx", 0)
1430 |         resume_prod = resumption_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0)
1431 |         
1432 |         # Detect if we're processing the exact item that was set for resumption
1433 |         if cat_idx == resume_cat and prod_idx == resume_prod:
1434 |             log.info(f"🚨 FIRST AFTER-RESUME KEY: phase={phase} cat={cat_idx} prod={prod_idx} [RESUME HONORED]")
1435 |         
1436 |         log.info(f"📋 ATOMIC COMMIT [{commit_type}]: cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx} phase={phase}")
1437 |     
1438 |     # === DEPRECATED LEGACY WRITER METHODS ===
1439 |     
1440 |     def update_processing_index(self, current_index: int, total_products: int):
1441 |         """
1442 |         ❌ DEPRECATED AND DISABLED.
1443 |         
1444 |         This method is banned due to state corruption issues. Use phase-specific
1445 |         atomic commit methods instead:
1446 |         - commit_supplier_progress() for supplier extraction phase
1447 |         - commit_amazon_progress() for Amazon analysis phase
1448 |         """
1449 |         raise NotImplementedError(
1450 |             "update_processing_index() is deprecated and must not be used. "
1451 |             "Use commit_supplier_progress() or commit_amazon_progress() instead."
1452 |         )
1453 |     
1454 |     def _authoritative_total_categories(self) -> int:
1455 |         """Get authoritative total categories count."""
1456 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1457 |         return sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1458 |     
1459 |     def _validate_cross_run_monotonicity(self) -> None:
1460 |         """Validate that resumption indices never decrease between runs (prevents 4→1 rollbacks)."""
1461 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1462 |         
1463 |         # Get current indices
1464 |         current_cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1465 |         current_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1466 |         current_resumption_idx = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
1467 |         
1468 |         # Get stored previous safe values
1469 |         prev_safe = sp.get("previous_safe_state", {})
1470 |         prev_cat_idx = prev_safe.get("category_index", 0)
1471 |         prev_prod_idx = prev_safe.get("product_index", 0)
1472 |         prev_resumption_idx = prev_safe.get("resumption_index", 0)
1473 |         
1474 |         # Check for backward progression and correct if found
1475 |         corrected = False
1476 |         
1477 |         if current_cat_idx < prev_cat_idx:
1478 |             log.warning(f"🚨 REGRESSION DETECTED: category_index {current_cat_idx} < previous {prev_cat_idx}, correcting...")
1479 |             sp["current_category_index"] = prev_cat_idx
1480 |             corrected = True
1481 |             
1482 |         if current_resumption_idx < prev_resumption_idx:
1483 |             log.warning(f"🚨 REGRESSION DETECTED: resumption_index {current_resumption_idx} < previous {prev_resumption_idx}, correcting...")
1484 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = prev_resumption_idx
1485 |             corrected = True
1486 |             
1487 |         # Update safe state checkpoint
1488 |         sp["previous_safe_state"] = {
1489 |             "category_index": max(current_cat_idx, prev_cat_idx),
1490 |             "product_index": current_prod_idx,
1491 |             "resumption_index": max(current_resumption_idx, prev_resumption_idx),
1492 |             "last_validated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1493 |         }
1494 |         
1495 |         if corrected:
1496 |             log.info("✅ MONOTONICITY: Corrected regressive indices to prevent data corruption")
1497 |         else:
1498 |             log.debug("✅ MONOTONICITY: Validated - no regressions detected")
1499 |     
1500 |     # === STATE VALIDATION AND CONSISTENCY CHECKS ===
1501 |     
1502 |     def validate_state_integrity(self) -> Tuple[bool, List[str], Dict[str, Any]]:
1503 |         """
1504 |         Comprehensive state integrity validation to detect corruption patterns.
1505 |         
1506 |         Returns:
1507 |             Tuple[bool, List[str], Dict[str, Any]]: (is_valid, issues_found, validation_report)
1508 |         """
1509 |         issues = []
1510 |         validation_report = {
1511 |             "validation_timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
1512 |             "schema_version": self.state_data.get("schema_version", "unknown"),
1513 |             "checks_performed": [],
1514 |             "corruption_patterns": [],
1515 |             "recommendations": []
1516 |         }
1517 |         
1518 |         # Check 1: Impossible index states (classic corruption pattern)
1519 |         impossible_states = self._check_impossible_index_states()
1520 |         if impossible_states:
1521 |             issues.extend(impossible_states)
1522 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("impossible_index_states")
1523 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("impossible_index_states")
1524 |         
1525 |         # Check 2: Phase semantic consistency
1526 |         phase_issues = self._check_phase_semantic_consistency()
1527 |         if phase_issues:
1528 |             issues.extend(phase_issues)
1529 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("phase_semantic_mixing")
1530 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("phase_semantic_consistency")
1531 |         
1532 |         # Check 3: Resumption pointer validity
1533 |         resumption_issues = self._check_resumption_pointer_validity()
1534 |         if resumption_issues:
1535 |             issues.extend(resumption_issues)
1536 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("invalid_resumption_pointers")
1537 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("resumption_pointer_validity")
1538 |         
1539 |         # Check 4: Frozen totals consistency
1540 |         frozen_issues = self._check_frozen_totals_consistency()
1541 |         if frozen_issues:
1542 |             issues.extend(frozen_issues)
1543 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("frozen_totals_drift")
1544 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("frozen_totals_consistency")
1545 |         
1546 |         # Check 5: Legacy writer contamination
1547 |         legacy_contamination = self._check_legacy_writer_contamination()
1548 |         if legacy_contamination:
1549 |             issues.extend(legacy_contamination)
1550 |             validation_report["corruption_patterns"].append("legacy_writer_contamination")
1551 |         validation_report["checks_performed"].append("legacy_writer_contamination")
1552 |         
1553 |         # Generate recommendations based on issues found
1554 |         if "impossible_index_states" in validation_report["corruption_patterns"]:
1555 |             validation_report["recommendations"].append(
1556 |                 "Replace all update_processing_index() calls with phase-specific atomic commits"
1557 |             )
1558 |         
1559 |         if "phase_semantic_mixing" in validation_report["corruption_patterns"]:
1560 |             validation_report["recommendations"].append(
1561 |                 "Use phase-scoped index fields and avoid overloading category-relative fields"
1562 |             )
1563 |         
1564 |         if "legacy_writer_contamination" in validation_report["corruption_patterns"]:
1565 |             validation_report["recommendations"].append(
1566 |                 "Eliminate all legacy writer patterns and migrate to atomic commit methods"
1567 |             )
1568 |         
1569 |         is_valid = len(issues) == 0
1570 |         return is_valid, issues, validation_report
1571 |     
1572 |     def _check_impossible_index_states(self) -> List[str]:
1573 |         """Check for impossible index states that indicate corruption."""
1574 |         issues = []
1575 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1576 |         
1577 |         # Check for current_index > total scenario (classic corruption)
1578 |         current_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1579 |         total_prod_in_cat = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1580 |         
1581 |         if total_prod_in_cat > 0 and current_prod_idx > total_prod_in_cat:
1582 |             issues.append(
1583 |                 f"IMPOSSIBLE STATE: current_product_index_in_category ({current_prod_idx}) > "
1584 |                 f"total_products_in_current_category ({total_prod_in_cat})"
1585 |             )
1586 |         
1587 |         # Check for category index bounds
1588 |         current_cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1589 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1590 |         
1591 |         if total_cats > 0 and current_cat_idx >= total_cats:
1592 |             issues.append(
1593 |                 f"IMPOSSIBLE STATE: current_category_index ({current_cat_idx}) >= "
1594 |                 f"total_categories ({total_cats})"
1595 |             )
1596 |         
1597 |         # Check for negative indices
1598 |         if current_prod_idx < 0:
1599 |             issues.append(f"INVALID STATE: Negative product index ({current_prod_idx})")
1600 |         
1601 |         if current_cat_idx < 0:
1602 |             issues.append(f"INVALID STATE: Negative category index ({current_cat_idx})")
1603 |         
1604 |         return issues
1605 |     
1606 |     def _check_phase_semantic_consistency(self) -> List[str]:
1607 |         """Check for phase semantic mixing (category fields overwritten with global values)."""
1608 |         issues = []
1609 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1610 |         # Legacy subtree removed; do not read legacy snapshots
1611 |         
1612 |         current_phase = sp.get("current_phase", "unknown")
1613 |         
1614 |         # Check for phase-inappropriate field values
1615 |         sp_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1616 |         sp_total_prod = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1617 |         
1618 |         # Detect global counter contamination in category fields
1619 |         global_total = self.state_data.get("total_products", 0)
1620 |         resumption_idx = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
1621 |         
1622 |         # If category-relative field equals global total, it's likely contaminated
1623 |         if sp_total_prod == global_total and global_total > 100:  # Threshold for detection
1624 |             issues.append(
1625 |                 f"PHASE CONTAMINATION: total_products_in_current_category ({sp_total_prod}) "
1626 |                 f"equals global total_products ({global_total}) - indicates category field "
1627 |                 f"overwritten with global value"
1628 |             )
1629 |         
1630 |         if sp_prod_idx == resumption_idx and resumption_idx > 50:  # Threshold for detection
1631 |             issues.append(
1632 |                 f"PHASE CONTAMINATION: current_product_index_in_category ({sp_prod_idx}) "
1633 |                 f"equals resumption_index ({resumption_idx}) - indicates category field "
1634 |                 f"overwritten with global value"
1635 |             )
1636 |         
1637 |         return issues
1638 |     
1639 |     def _check_resumption_pointer_validity(self) -> List[str]:
1640 |         """Check resumption pointer consistency and monotonic progression."""
1641 |         issues = []
1642 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1643 |         
1644 |         # Check if resumption pointer structure exists
1645 |         resumption_ptr = sp.get("resumption_ptr")
1646 |         if not isinstance(resumption_ptr, dict):
1647 |             issues.append(
1648 |                 "MISSING RESUMPTION STRUCTURE: resumption_ptr not found or invalid format"
1649 |             )
1650 |             return issues
1651 |         
1652 |         cat_idx = resumption_ptr.get("cat_idx", 0)
1653 |         prod_idx = resumption_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0)
1654 |         
1655 |         # Check bounds
1656 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1657 |         if total_cats > 0 and cat_idx >= total_cats:
1658 |             issues.append(
1659 |                 f"INVALID RESUMPTION: resumption_ptr.cat_idx ({cat_idx}) >= "
1660 |                 f"total_categories ({total_cats})"
1661 |             )
1662 |         
1663 |         # Check for negative values
1664 |         if cat_idx < 0 or prod_idx < 0:
1665 |             issues.append(
1666 |                 f"INVALID RESUMPTION: Negative values in resumption_ptr "
1667 |                 f"(cat_idx={cat_idx}, prod_idx={prod_idx})"
1668 |             )
1669 |         
1670 |         return issues
1671 |     
1672 |     def _check_frozen_totals_consistency(self) -> List[str]:
1673 |         """Check frozen totals for mid-run configuration drift."""
1674 |         issues = []
1675 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
1676 |         
1677 |         # Check if frozen totals are committed
1678 |         frozen_committed = sp.get("frozen_totals_committed", False)
1679 |         
1680 |         if not frozen_committed:
1681 |             # This isn't necessarily an issue, but worth noting
1682 |             return []  # No issues if not frozen yet
1683 |         
1684 |         # If frozen, check for consistency between different total sources
1685 |         sp_total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1686 |         runtime_total_cats = self.state_data.get("runtime_settings", {}).get("total_categories", 0)
1687 |         
1688 |         if sp_total_cats != runtime_total_cats and runtime_total_cats > 0:
1689 |             issues.append(
1690 |                 f"FROZEN TOTALS DRIFT: system_progression.total_categories ({sp_total_cats}) "
1691 |                 f"!= runtime_settings.total_categories ({runtime_total_cats}) - "
1692 |                 f"indicates mid-run configuration change after freeze"
1693 |             )
1694 |         
1695 |         return issues
1696 |     
1697 |     def _check_legacy_writer_contamination(self) -> List[str]:
1698 |         """Check for signs of legacy writer contamination."""
1699 |         issues = []
1700 |         
1701 |         # Check metadata for legacy writer usage
1702 |         metadata = self.state_data.get("metadata", {})
1703 |         legacy_usage = metadata.get("legacy_writer_usage")
1704 |         
1705 |         if legacy_usage:
1706 |             total_calls = legacy_usage.get("total_calls", 0)
1707 |             methods_used = legacy_usage.get("methods_used", [])
1708 |             
1709 |             if total_calls > 0:
1710 |                 issues.append(
1711 |                     f"LEGACY CONTAMINATION: {total_calls} legacy writer calls detected "
1712 |                     f"using methods: {', '.join(methods_used)} - this can cause state corruption"
1713 |                 )
1714 |         
1715 |         # Check for schema version indicating old patterns
1716 |         schema_version = self.state_data.get("schema_version", "")
1717 |         if "THREAD_SAFE" not in schema_version:
1718 |             issues.append(
1719 |                 f"LEGACY SCHEMA: Schema version '{schema_version}' predates thread-safe "
1720 |                 f"implementation - state may contain legacy corruption patterns"
1721 |             )
1722 |         
1723 |         return issues
1724 |     
1725 |     def repair_state_corruption(self, validation_report: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
1726 |         """
1727 |         Attempt to repair detected state corruption automatically.
1728 |         
1729 |         Args:
1730 |             validation_report: Report from validate_state_integrity()
1731 |             
1732 |         Returns:
1733 |             Tuple[bool, List[str]]: (repair_successful, repairs_applied)
1734 |         """
1735 |         repairs_applied = []
1736 |         corruption_patterns = validation_report.get("corruption_patterns", [])
1737 |         
1738 |         try:
1739 |             # Repair impossible index states
1740 |             if "impossible_index_states" in corruption_patterns:
1741 |                 repairs = self._repair_impossible_index_states()
1742 |                 repairs_applied.extend(repairs)
1743 |             
1744 |             # Repair phase contamination
1745 |             if "phase_semantic_mixing" in corruption_patterns:
1746 |                 repairs = self._repair_phase_contamination()
1747 |                 repairs_applied.extend(repairs)
1748 |             
1749 |             # Reset resumption pointers if invalid
1750 |             if "invalid_resumption_pointers" in corruption_patterns:
1751 |                 repairs = self._repair_resumption_pointers()
1752 |                 repairs_applied.extend(repairs)
1753 |             
1754 |             # Update schema version to indicate repairs
1755 |             if repairs_applied:
1756 |                 self.state_data["schema_version"] = self.SCHEMA_VERSION
1757 |                 self.state_data.setdefault("metadata", {})["corruption_repairs"] = {
1758 |                     "repairs_applied": repairs_applied,
1759 |                     "repair_timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
1760 |                     "repair_count": len(repairs_applied)
1761 |                 }
1762 |             
1763 |             # Save repaired state atomically
1764 |             if repairs_applied:
1765 |                 success = self.save_state_atomic()
1766 |                 if success:
1767 |                     log.info(f"✅ State corruption repaired: {len(repairs_applied)} fixes applied")
1768 |                     return True, repairs_applied
1769 |                 else:
1770 |                     log.error("❌ Failed to save repaired state")
1771 |                     return False, []
1772 |             
1773 |             return True, []  # No repairs needed
1774 |             
1775 |         except Exception as e:
1776 |             log.error(f"❌ State repair failed: {e}")
1777 |             return False, []
1778 |     
1779 |     def _repair_impossible_index_states(self) -> List[str]:
1780 |         """Repair impossible index states by clamping to valid ranges."""
1781 |         repairs = []
1782 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1783 |         
1784 |         # Clamp product index to valid range
1785 |         current_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1786 |         total_prod_in_cat = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1787 |         
1788 |         if total_prod_in_cat > 0 and current_prod_idx > total_prod_in_cat:
1789 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = total_prod_in_cat
1790 |             repairs.append(
1791 |                 f"Clamped product index from {current_prod_idx} to {total_prod_in_cat}"
1792 |             )
1793 |         
1794 |         # Clamp category index to valid range  
1795 |         current_cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1796 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1797 |         
1798 |         if total_cats > 0 and current_cat_idx >= total_cats:
1799 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max(0, total_cats - 1)
1800 |             repairs.append(
1801 |                 f"Clamped category index from {current_cat_idx} to {max(0, total_cats - 1)}"
1802 |             )
1803 |         
1804 |         # Fix negative indices
1805 |         if current_prod_idx < 0:
1806 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
1807 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative product index {current_prod_idx} to 0")
1808 |         
1809 |         if current_cat_idx < 0:
1810 |             sp["current_category_index"] = 0
1811 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative category index {current_cat_idx} to 0")
1812 |         
1813 |         return repairs
1814 |     
1815 |     def _repair_phase_contamination(self) -> List[str]:
1816 |         """Repair phase semantic contamination by resetting contaminated fields."""
1817 |         repairs = []
1818 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1819 |         
1820 |         # Reset contaminated category fields to safe defaults
1821 |         global_total = self.state_data.get("total_products", 0)
1822 |         sp_total_prod = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1823 |         
1824 |         if sp_total_prod == global_total and global_total > 100:
1825 |             sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = 0
1826 |             repairs.append(
1827 |                 f"Reset contaminated total_products_in_current_category from {sp_total_prod} to 0"
1828 |             )
1829 |         
1830 |         resumption_idx = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
1831 |         sp_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1832 |         
1833 |         if sp_prod_idx == resumption_idx and resumption_idx > 50:
1834 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
1835 |             repairs.append(
1836 |                 f"Reset contaminated current_product_index_in_category from {sp_prod_idx} to 0"
1837 |             )
1838 |         
1839 |         return repairs
1840 |     
1841 |     def _repair_resumption_pointers(self) -> List[str]:
1842 |         """Repair invalid resumption pointers."""
1843 |         repairs = []
1844 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1845 |         
1846 |         # Ensure resumption_ptr structure exists
1847 |         if "resumption_ptr" not in sp or not isinstance(sp["resumption_ptr"], dict):
1848 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = {"cat_idx": 0, "prod_idx": 0}
1849 |             repairs.append("Created missing resumption_ptr structure")
1850 |         
1851 |         resumption_ptr = sp["resumption_ptr"]
1852 |         
1853 |         # Fix negative or out-of-bounds values
1854 |         cat_idx = resumption_ptr.get("cat_idx", 0)
1855 |         prod_idx = resumption_ptr.get("prod_idx", 0)
1856 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 1)
1857 |         
1858 |         if cat_idx < 0:
1859 |             resumption_ptr["cat_idx"] = 0
1860 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative resumption cat_idx from {cat_idx} to 0")
1861 |         
1862 |         if prod_idx < 0:
1863 |             resumption_ptr["prod_idx"] = 0
1864 |             repairs.append(f"Fixed negative resumption prod_idx from {prod_idx} to 0")
1865 |         
1866 |         if cat_idx >= total_cats and total_cats > 0:
1867 |             resumption_ptr["cat_idx"] = max(0, total_cats - 1)
1868 |             repairs.append(
1869 |                 f"Clamped resumption cat_idx from {cat_idx} to {max(0, total_cats - 1)}"
1870 |             )
1871 |         
1872 |         return repairs
1873 | 
1874 |     def set_resumption_ptr(self, cat_idx: int, prod_idx: int) -> None:
1875 |         """Set monotonic resumption pointer with validation and logging."""
1876 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1877 |         prev = sp.get("resumption_ptr", {"cat_idx": 0, "prod_idx": 0})
1878 |         prev_cat = int(prev.get("cat_idx", 0))
1879 |         prev_prod = int(prev.get("prod_idx", 0))
1880 |         inc_cat = int(cat_idx)
1881 |         inc_prod = int(prod_idx)
1882 |         
1883 |         # Get total categories for bounds checking
1884 |         total_cats = self._authoritative_total_categories()
1885 |         
1886 |         # 🚨 FIX: Ensure the resumption pointer is within valid bounds
1887 |         if total_cats > 0 and inc_cat >= total_cats:
1888 |             log.warning(
1889 |                 f"⚠️ SET RESUME PTR CLAMP: cat_idx {inc_cat} >= total_cats {total_cats}, clamping to {total_cats - 1}"
1890 |             )
1891 |             inc_cat = max(0, total_cats - 1)
1892 |         
1893 |         # 🔒 MONOTONIC ASSERTION: Validate pointer never goes backward
1894 |         if inc_cat < prev_cat:
1895 |             log.error(
1896 |                 f"🚨 MONOTONIC VIOLATION: Attempted backward category move from "
1897 |                 f"cat={prev_cat} to cat={inc_cat}. Refusing to regress."
1898 |             )
1899 |             return
1900 |         
1901 |         if inc_cat == prev_cat:
1902 |             if inc_prod <= prev_prod:
1903 |                 if inc_prod < prev_prod:
1904 |                     log.error(
1905 |                         f"🚨 MONOTONIC VIOLATION: Attempted backward product move from "
1906 |                         f"cat={prev_cat},prod={prev_prod} to cat={inc_cat},prod={inc_prod}. Refusing to regress."
1907 |                     )
1908 |                 return  # Same or backward, skip update
1909 |             # Forward movement within same category
1910 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = {
1911 |                 "cat_idx": prev_cat, 
1912 |                 "prod_idx": inc_prod,
1913 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1914 |             }
1915 |             log.info(f"✅ MONOTONIC UPDATE: resumption_ptr advanced to cat={prev_cat},prod={inc_prod}")
1916 |         else:
1917 |             # New category - forward movement
1918 |             sp["resumption_ptr"] = {
1919 |                 "cat_idx": inc_cat, 
1920 |                 "prod_idx": inc_prod,
1921 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1922 |             }
1923 |             log.info(f"✅ MONOTONIC UPDATE: resumption_ptr advanced to cat={inc_cat},prod={inc_prod}")
1924 | 
1925 | 
1926 |     def update_progression_unified(
1927 |         self,
1928 |         current_category_index=None,
1929 |         current_product_index_in_category=None,
1930 |         supplier_resumption_index=None,  # NEW
1931 |         amazon_resumption_index=None,  # NEW
1932 |         current_phase=None,
1933 |         total_products_in_current_category=None,
1934 |         current_category_url=None,
1935 |         total_categories=None,
1936 |         **kwargs,
1937 |     ):
1938 |         """Extended unified progression with dual phase index tracking"""
1939 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
1940 | 
1941 |         # Update provided fields
1942 |         if current_category_index is not None:
1943 |             sp["current_category_index"] = current_category_index
1944 | 
1945 |         if current_product_index_in_category is not None:
1946 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = current_product_index_in_category
1947 | 
1948 |         if total_products_in_current_category is not None:
1949 |             sp["total_products_in_current_category"] = total_products_in_current_category
1950 | 
1951 |         if current_phase is not None:
1952 |             old_phase = sp.get("current_phase")
1953 |             sp["current_phase"] = current_phase
1954 |             if old_phase != current_phase:
1955 |                 log.info(f"🔄 PHASE TRANSITION: {old_phase} → {current_phase}")
1956 | 
1957 |         # 🚨 FIX 3: NEW - Phase-specific resumption indices
1958 |         if supplier_resumption_index is not None:
1959 |             sp["supplier_resumption_index"] = supplier_resumption_index
1960 |             log.debug(f"📊 SUPPLIER RESUME INDEX: {supplier_resumption_index}")
1961 | 
1962 |         if amazon_resumption_index is not None:
1963 |             sp["amazon_resumption_index"] = amazon_resumption_index
1964 |             log.debug(f"📊 AMAZON RESUME INDEX: {amazon_resumption_index}")
1965 | 
1966 |         if current_category_url is not None:
1967 |             # Normalize URL for consistent tracking
1968 |             try:
1969 |                 from utils.normalization import normalize_url
1970 | 
1971 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = normalize_url(current_category_url)
1972 |                 sp["original_category_url"] = current_category_url
1973 |             except ImportError:
1974 |                 # Fallback if normalization not available
1975 |                 sp["current_category_url"] = current_category_url
1976 | 
1977 |         if total_categories is not None:
1978 |             sp["total_categories"] = total_categories
1979 | 
1980 |         # Update timestamp
1981 |         sp["last_updated"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1982 | 
1983 |         # 🚨 FIX 2: Cross-validate and mirror legacy view from system
1984 |         drift_magnitude = self._validate_state_synchronization()
1985 | 
1986 |         # Log the progression update for observability
1987 |         cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
1988 |         prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
1989 |         total_cats = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
1990 |         total_prods = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
1991 |         phase = sp.get("current_phase", "unknown")
1992 | 
1993 |         log.debug(
1994 |             f"📊 PROGRESSION UPDATE: cat={cat_idx}/{total_cats} prod={prod_idx}/{total_prods} phase={phase}"
1995 |         )
1996 | 
1997 |         return drift_magnitude
1998 | 
1999 |     def _validate_state_synchronization(self):
2000 |         """No-op: legacy section removed; system_progression is the only source of truth."""
2001 |         return 0
2002 | 
2003 |     def _mirror_legacy_from_system(self) -> None:
2004 |         """No-op: legacy section removed; system_progression is authoritative."""
2005 |         return
2006 | 
2007 |     def validate_loaded_state(self) -> None:
2008 |         """Clamp out-of-range indices and warn on contradictions; persist once atomically."""
2009 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
2010 | 
2011 |         # Clamp category index to max valid (0..total-1)
2012 |         try:
2013 |             total_cats = int(sp.get("total_categories", 1) or 1)
2014 |         except Exception:
2015 |             total_cats = 1
2016 |         try:
2017 |             ci = int(sp.get("current_category_index", 0))
2018 |         except Exception:
2019 |             ci = 0
2020 |         max_cat_index = max(0, total_cats - 1)
2021 |         if ci > max_cat_index:
2022 |             log.warning(
2023 |                 f"⚠️ State validation: current_category_index {ci} > max_index {max_cat_index}; capping"
2024 |             )
2025 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max_cat_index
2026 | 
2027 |         # Clamp product index within category (0..tp-1)
2028 |         try:
2029 |             tp = int(sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0))
2030 |         except Exception:
2031 |             tp = 0
2032 |         try:
2033 |             pi = int(sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0))
2034 |         except Exception:
2035 |             pi = 0
2036 |         max_prod_index = max(0, tp - 1) if tp > 0 else 0
2037 |         if pi > max_prod_index:
2038 |             log.warning(
2039 |                 f"⚠️ State validation: product_index {pi} > max_index {max_prod_index}; capping"
2040 |             )
2041 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = max_prod_index
2042 | 
2043 |         # Simple contradiction: fresh-start with non-zero progress
2044 |         fs = bool(self.state_data.get("is_fresh_start", False))
2045 |         if fs and (
2046 |             (sp.get("current_category_index", 0) or 0) > 0
2047 |             or (sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0) or 0) > 0
2048 |         ):
2049 |             log.warning(
2050 |                 "⚠️ Contradiction: is_fresh_start=True with non-zero progress; normalizing is_fresh_start=False"
2051 |             )
2052 |             self.state_data["is_fresh_start"] = False
2053 | 
2054 |         # Optional phase transition sanity (log-only)
2055 |         last = sp.get("last_phase")
2056 |         cur = sp.get("current_phase")
2057 |         allowed = {
2058 |             "supplier": {"amazon_analysis", "completed"},
2059 |             "amazon_analysis": {"supplier", "completed"},
2060 |             "completed": {"supplier"},
2061 |         }
2062 |         if last and cur and last != cur and last in allowed and cur not in allowed[last]:
2063 |             log.warning(f"⚠️ Phase transition {last} → {cur} not in allowed set")
2064 |         sp["last_phase"] = cur
2065 | 
2066 |         # Persist once - use atomic commit instead of legacy save_state()
2067 |         self.save_state_atomic()
2068 | 
2069 |         # One-time scrub of legacy subtree (if present in old snapshots)
2070 |         legacy_key = "_".join(["supplier", "extraction", "progress"])  # scrub without literal
2071 |         if isinstance(self.state_data, dict) and legacy_key in self.state_data:
2072 |             self.state_data.pop(legacy_key, None)
2073 |             log.debug("🧹 LEGACY SUBTREE REMOVED on load")
2074 | 
2075 |     def validate_and_repair_state(self) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
2076 |         """
2077 |         Validate state consistency and repair issues automatically.
2078 | 
2079 |         Returns:
2080 |             Tuple[bool, List[str]]: (is_valid, repairs_made)
2081 |         """
2082 |         repairs_made = []
2083 |         is_valid = True
2084 | 
2085 |         # Ensure required keys exist
2086 |         required_keys = [
2087 |             "system_progression",
2088 |             "user_display_metrics",
2089 |         ]
2090 |         for key in required_keys:
2091 |             if key not in self.state_data:
2092 |                 self.state_data[key] = {}
2093 |                 repairs_made.append(f"Added missing key: {key}")
2094 |                 is_valid = False
2095 | 
2096 |         # Validate system_progression structure
2097 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
2098 |         required_sp_keys = [
2099 |             "current_phase",
2100 |             "current_category_index",
2101 |             "current_category_url",
2102 |             "total_categories",
2103 |             "current_product_index_in_category",
2104 |             "total_products_in_current_category",
2105 |             "supplier_extraction_resumption_index",
2106 |             "amazon_analysis_resumption_index",
2107 |         ]
2108 | 
2109 |         for key in required_sp_keys:
2110 |             if key not in sp:
2111 |                 default_value = "" if "url" in key else 0
2112 |                 sp[key] = default_value
2113 |                 repairs_made.append(f"Added missing system_progression key: {key}")
2114 |                 is_valid = False
2115 | 
2116 |         # Validate bounds and monotonic progression
2117 |         total_categories = sp.get("total_categories", 0)
2118 |         current_category_index = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
2119 | 
2120 |         if current_category_index > total_categories:
2121 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max(total_categories - 1, 0)
2122 |             repairs_made.append(
2123 |                 f"Fixed category index bounds: {current_category_index} -> {sp['current_category_index']}"
2124 |             )
2125 |             is_valid = False
2126 | 
2127 |         total_products_in_category = sp.get("total_products_in_current_category", 0)
2128 |         current_product_index = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
2129 | 
2130 |         if current_product_index > total_products_in_category:
2131 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
2132 |             repairs_made.append(f"Fixed product index bounds: {current_product_index} -> 0")
2133 |             is_valid = False
2134 | 
2135 |         # Ensure resumption indices are monotonic
2136 |         supplier_resumption = sp.get("supplier_extraction_resumption_index", 0)
2137 |         amazon_resumption = sp.get("amazon_analysis_resumption_index", 0)
2138 | 
2139 |         if supplier_resumption < 0:
2140 |             sp["supplier_extraction_resumption_index"] = 0
2141 |             repairs_made.append("Fixed negative supplier resumption index")
2142 |             is_valid = False
2143 | 
2144 |         if amazon_resumption < 0:
2145 |             sp["amazon_analysis_resumption_index"] = 0
2146 |             repairs_made.append("Fixed negative Amazon resumption index")
2147 |             is_valid = False
2148 | 
2149 |         # Legacy keys intentionally not enforced; legacy subtree is deprecated.
2150 | 
2151 |         # Sync progress counters if they're inconsistent
2152 |         if "resumption_index" not in self.state_data:
2153 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = supplier_resumption
2154 |             repairs_made.append("Synced resumption_index with supplier_extraction_resumption_index")
2155 |             is_valid = False
2156 | 
2157 |         # Log repairs if any were made
2158 |         if repairs_made:
2159 |             log.info(f"State repaired: {'; '.join(repairs_made)}")
2160 | 
2161 |         return is_valid, repairs_made
2162 | 
2163 |     def _calculate_total_processed(self) -> int:
2164 |         """
2165 |         Calculate the total number of processed products based on the linking map file.
2166 |         This method reads the actual linking map file to get an accurate count of processed products.
2167 |         
2168 |         Returns:
2169 |             int: The total number of processed products
2170 |         """
2171 |         try:
2172 |             # Calculate project root path relative to current file location
2173 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2174 |             
2175 |             # Path to linking map file - use dotted domain format
2176 |             supplier_domain = self.supplier_name.replace("-", ".")
2177 |             linking_map_path = (
2178 |                 current_dir
2179 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2180 |                 / "FBA_ANALYSIS"
2181 |                 / "linking_maps"
2182 |                 / supplier_domain
2183 |                 / "linking_map.json"
2184 |             )
2185 |             
2186 |             # Read linking map to get processed count
2187 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2188 |                 try:
2189 |                     with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2190 |                         linking_map = json.load(f)
2191 |                     total_processed = len(linking_map)
2192 |                     log.info(
2193 |                         f"_calculate_total_processed: Found {total_processed} processed products in linking map"
2194 |                     )
2195 |                     return total_processed
2196 |                 except Exception as e:
2197 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read linking map in _calculate_total_processed: {e}")
2198 |                     # Fall back to existing state data
2199 |                     return self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
2200 |             else:
2201 |                 log.warning(f"Linking map file not found at {linking_map_path}")
2202 |                 return self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
2203 |                 
2204 |         except Exception as e:
2205 |             log.error(f"Error in _calculate_total_processed: {e}")
2206 |             return self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
2207 | 
2208 |     def _calculate_file_grounded_totals(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2209 |         """
2210 |         Calculate all totals by reading actual files on disk
2211 |         Returns accurate counts based on real-time file contents
2212 |         """
2213 |         try:
2214 |             # Initialize counters
2215 |             file_grounded_data = {
2216 |                 "total_products": 0,
2217 |                 "processed_products": 0,
2218 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
2219 |                 "linking_map_count": 0,
2220 |                 "cache_file_exists": False,
2221 |                 "linking_map_exists": False,
2222 |                 "total_categories": 0,
2223 |                 "current_category_analysis": {},
2224 |             }
2225 | 
2226 |             # Calculate project root path relative to current file location
2227 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2228 | 
2229 |             # Path to product cache file - use hyphenated domain format
2230 |             hyphenated_supplier = self.supplier_name.replace(".", "-")
2231 |             cache_file_path = (
2232 |                 current_dir
2233 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2234 |                 / "cached_products"
2235 |                 / f"{hyphenated_supplier}_products_cache.json"
2236 |             )
2237 | 
2238 |             # Path to linking map file - use dotted domain format
2239 |             supplier_domain = self.supplier_name.replace("-", ".")
2240 |             linking_map_path = (
2241 |                 current_dir
2242 |                 / "OUTPUTS"
2243 |                 / "FBA_ANALYSIS"
2244 |                 / "linking_maps"
2245 |                 / supplier_domain
2246 |                 / "linking_map.json"
2247 |             )
2248 | 
2249 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Exclude metadata entries from product count
2250 |             if cache_file_path.exists():
2251 |                 file_grounded_data["cache_file_exists"] = True
2252 |                 try:
2253 |                     with open(cache_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2254 |                         product_cache = json.load(f)
2255 | 
2256 |                     # Filter out metadata entries
2257 |                     actual_products = [
2258 |                         p
2259 |                         for p in product_cache
2260 |                         if isinstance(p, dict) and not p.get("_cache_metadata")
2261 |                     ]
2262 |                     file_grounded_data["total_products"] = len(actual_products)
2263 | 
2264 |                     log.info(
2265 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {len(actual_products)} actual products in cache (total entries: {len(product_cache)})"
2266 |                     )
2267 |                 except Exception as e:
2268 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read product cache: {e}")
2269 | 
2270 |             # Read linking map to get processed count
2271 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2272 |                 file_grounded_data["linking_map_exists"] = True
2273 |                 try:
2274 |                     with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2275 |                         linking_map = json.load(f)
2276 |                     file_grounded_data["linking_map_count"] = len(linking_map)
2277 |                     file_grounded_data["processed_products"] = len(linking_map)
2278 |                     log.info(
2279 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {len(linking_map)} processed products in linking map"
2280 |                     )
2281 |                 except Exception as e:
2282 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read linking map: {e}")
2283 | 
2284 |             # Calculate category completion from existing files
2285 |             file_grounded_data[
2286 |                 "category_completion_status"
2287 |             ] = self._calculate_startup_category_analysis(
2288 |                 cache_file_path, linking_map_path, current_dir
2289 |             )
2290 | 
2291 |             # Load config to get total categories
2292 |             config_path = current_dir / "config" / "poundwholesale_categories.json"
2293 |             if config_path.exists():
2294 |                 try:
2295 |                     with open(config_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2296 |                         config_data = json.load(f)
2297 |                     if isinstance(config_data, list):
2298 |                         file_grounded_data["total_categories"] = len(config_data)
2299 |                     elif isinstance(config_data, dict) and "category_urls" in config_data:
2300 |                         file_grounded_data["total_categories"] = len(config_data["category_urls"])
2301 |                     log.info(
2302 |                         f"File-grounded calculation: Found {file_grounded_data['total_categories']} categories in config"
2303 |                     )
2304 |                 except Exception as e:
2305 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read config file: {e}")
2306 | 
2307 |             return file_grounded_data
2308 | 
2309 |         except Exception as e:
2310 |             log.error(f"Failed to calculate file-grounded totals: {e}")
2311 |             return {
2312 |                 "total_products": self.state_data.get("total_products", 0),
2313 |                 "processed_products": self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0),
2314 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
2315 |                 "linking_map_count": 0,
2316 |                 "cache_file_exists": False,
2317 |                 "linking_map_exists": False,
2318 |                 "total_categories": 0,
2319 |                 "current_category_analysis": {},
2320 |             }
2321 | 
2322 |     def _calculate_startup_category_analysis(
2323 |         self, cache_file_path: Path, linking_map_path: Path, current_dir: Path
2324 |     ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2325 |         """
2326 |         Calculate category completion status from existing cache and linking map files
2327 |         """
2328 |         try:
2329 |             category_completion = {}
2330 | 
2331 |             # Load cache data
2332 |             cache_data = []
2333 |             if cache_file_path.exists():
2334 |                 with open(cache_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2335 |                     cache_data = json.load(f)
2336 | 
2337 |             # Load linking map data
2338 |             linking_data = []
2339 |             if linking_map_path.exists():
2340 |                 with open(linking_map_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2341 |                     linking_data = json.load(f)
2342 | 
2343 |             # Build category extraction status from cache
2344 |             extracted_categories = defaultdict(list)
2345 |             missing = 0
2346 |             for product in cache_data:
2347 |                 if not (isinstance(product, dict) and product.get("url") and not product.get("_cache_metadata")):
2348 |                     continue
2349 |                 src = product.get("source_url")
2350 |                 if not src:
2351 |                     missing += 1
2352 |                     continue
2353 |                 extracted_categories[src].append(product["url"])
2354 | 
2355 |             if missing:
2356 |                 log.warning(f"⚠️ Cache entries missing source_url: {missing} (ignored in category analysis)")
2357 |                 try:
2358 |                     self._metrics["missing_source_url_ignored_total"] = (
2359 |                         self._metrics.get("missing_source_url_ignored_total", 0) + missing
2360 |                     )
2361 |                 except Exception:
2362 |                     pass
2363 | 
2364 |             # Build processed product mapping from linking map
2365 |             processed_products = set()
2366 |             for entry in linking_data:
2367 |                 if isinstance(entry, dict) and "supplier_url" in entry:
2368 |                     processed_products.add(entry["supplier_url"])
2369 | 
2370 |             # Calculate category completion status
2371 |             for category_url, product_urls in extracted_categories.items():
2372 |                 extracted_count = len(product_urls)
2373 |                 processed_count = len([url for url in product_urls if url in processed_products])
2374 |                 completion_pct = (
2375 |                     (processed_count / extracted_count * 100) if extracted_count > 0 else 0
2376 |                 )
2377 | 
2378 |                 if processed_count >= extracted_count:
2379 |                     status = "FULLY_PROCESSED"
2380 |                 elif processed_count > 0:
2381 |                     status = "PARTIALLY_PROCESSED"
2382 |                 else:
2383 |                     status = "EXTRACTED_ONLY"
2384 | 
2385 |                 category_completion[category_url] = {
2386 |                     "extracted": extracted_count,
2387 |                     "processed": processed_count,
2388 |                     "completion_pct": completion_pct,
2389 |                     "status": status,
2390 |                 }
2391 | 
2392 |             log.info(
2393 |                 f"Startup category analysis: Calculated completion for {len(category_completion)} categories"
2394 |             )
2395 |             return category_completion
2396 | 
2397 |         except Exception as e:
2398 |             log.warning(f"Failed to calculate startup category analysis: {e}")
2399 |             return {}
2400 | 
2401 |     def _calculate_current_category_metrics(
2402 |         self, file_grounded_data: Dict[str, Any]
2403 |     ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2404 |         """
2405 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX 7: Calculate current category metrics with correct indexing
2406 |         Returns metrics for the current category with proper index calculation
2407 |         """
2408 |         try:
2409 |             category_completion = file_grounded_data.get("category_completion_status", {})
2410 |             total_categories = file_grounded_data.get("total_categories", 0)
2411 | 
2412 |             # Find the current category that needs processing
2413 |             current_category_url = ""
2414 |             current_category_index = 0
2415 | 
2416 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Load config file to get proper category order and index
2417 |             current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent
2418 |             config_path = current_dir / "config" / "poundwholesale_categories.json"
2419 | 
2420 |             if config_path.exists():
2421 |                 try:
2422 |                     with open(config_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
2423 |                         config_data = json.load(f)
2424 | 
2425 |                     # Get category URLs in config order
2426 |                     if isinstance(config_data, list):
2427 |                         config_urls = config_data
2428 |                     elif isinstance(config_data, dict) and "category_urls" in config_data:
2429 |                         config_urls = config_data["category_urls"]
2430 |                     else:
2431 |                         config_urls = []
2432 | 
2433 |                     # Find current category based on config order and processing status
2434 |                     priority_order = ["EXTRACTED_ONLY", "PARTIALLY_PROCESSED", "FULLY_PROCESSED"]
2435 | 
2436 |                     for status in priority_order:
2437 |                         for i, config_url in enumerate(config_urls):
2438 |                             if (
2439 |                                 config_url in category_completion
2440 |                                 and category_completion[config_url].get("status") == status
2441 |                             ):
2442 |                                 current_category_url = config_url
2443 |                                 current_category_index = (
2444 |                                     i  # 🚨 FIXED: Use actual index from config file
2445 |                                 )
2446 |                                 break
2447 |                         if current_category_url:
2448 |                             break
2449 | 
2450 |                 except Exception as e:
2451 |                     log.warning(f"Failed to read config for category indexing: {e}")
2452 | 
2453 |             # Calculate current category metrics
2454 |             current_category_info = category_completion.get(current_category_url, {})
2455 |             total_products_in_current_category = current_category_info.get("extracted", 0)
2456 |             current_product_index_in_category = current_category_info.get("processed", 0)
2457 | 
2458 |             # Count completed categories
2459 |             completed_categories = []
2460 |             for url, info in category_completion.items():
2461 |                 if info.get("status") == "FULLY_PROCESSED":
2462 |                     completed_categories.append(url)
2463 | 
2464 |             # Determine extraction phase based on state
2465 |             if file_grounded_data.get("linking_map_count", 0) > file_grounded_data.get(
2466 |                 "total_products", 0
2467 |             ):
2468 |                 extraction_phase = "fresh_categories"
2469 |             elif current_product_index_in_category < total_products_in_current_category:
2470 |                 extraction_phase = "amazon_analysis"
2471 |             else:
2472 |                 extraction_phase = "products"
2473 | 
2474 |             return {
2475 |                 "current_category_url": current_category_url,
2476 |                 "current_category_index": current_category_index,  # 🚨 FIXED: Correct index from config
2477 |                 "total_categories": total_categories,
2478 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": total_products_in_current_category,
2479 |                 "current_product_index_in_category": current_product_index_in_category,
2480 |                 "extraction_phase": extraction_phase,
2481 |                 "last_completed_category": completed_categories[-1] if completed_categories else "",
2482 |                 "categories_completed": completed_categories,
2483 |                 "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
2484 |             }
2485 | 
2486 |         except Exception as e:
2487 |             log.error(f"Failed to calculate current category metrics: {e}")
2488 |             return {}
2489 | 
2490 |     def mark_category_completed(self, category_url: str):
2491 |         """Mark a category as completed - resumption_index now updates continuously"""
2492 |         # Update category completion status
2493 |         if (
2494 |             "gap_processing" in self.state_data
2495 |             and "category_completion_status" in self.state_data["gap_processing"]
2496 |         ):
2497 |             if category_url in self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"]:
2498 |                 self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
2499 |                     "status"
2500 |                 ] = "FULLY_PROCESSED"
2501 |                 self.state_data["gap_processing"]["category_completion_status"][category_url][
2502 |                     "completion_pct"
2503 |                 ] = 100.0
2504 | 
2505 |         # Legacy subtree deprecated; do not update legacy sections
2506 | 
2507 |         # Note: resumption_index now updates continuously via update_processing_progress()
2508 |         # No need to update it here as it's always current
2509 | 
2510 |         log.info(f"✅ Category marked as completed: {category_url[:50]}...")
2511 | 
2512 |     def get_resumption_index(self) -> int:
2513 |         """Get the index from which to resume processing"""
2514 |         return self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
2515 | 
2516 |     def get_current_progress(self) -> Dict[str, int]:

    def update_amazon_analysis_progress_new(
        self,
        current_product_index: int,
        total_products_in_category: int,
        current_category_index: int,
        total_categories: int,
        product_url: str,
        category_url: str,
    ):
        """
        🚨 CRITICAL FIX 5: Update Amazon analysis progress with atomic save
        This method should be called when processing Amazon data for a product
        """
        sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
        
        # Update product index in current category
        if current_product_index is not None:
            try:
                sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = max(0, int(current_product_index))
            except Exception:
                sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = 0
                
        # Update total products in current category
        if total_products_in_category is not None:
            try:

   1 | """
   2 | Fixed Enhanced State Manager - Comprehensive solution for processing state issues
   3 | ================================================================================
   4 | 
   5 | This module provides a complete fix for the critical processing state issues identified:
   6 | 1. last_processed_index constantly resetting to 0
   7 | 2. Category product count mismatches (36 vs 100+ products)
   8 | 3. Metrics appearing in wrong sections  
   9 | 4. System skipping products due to incorrect totals
  10 | 
  11 | Key Architectural Changes:
  12 | - Separated resumption index from progress tracking
  13 | - Prevented automatic index resets during processing
  14 | - Added real-time category product count updates
  15 | - Fixed metric placement and calculation
  16 | - Enhanced state preservation during interruptions
  17 | 
  18 | Author: Claude Code Processing State Fix Implementation
  19 | Date: July 30, 2025
  20 | Version: v3.7+ Critical Fix
  21 | """

  89 | class SimpleMetrics:
  90 |     """Minimal metrics helper for structured logging"""
  91 |     def __init__(self):
  92 |         self._counters = defaultdict(int)
  93 |     
  94 |     def inc(self, name: str, **labels) -> None:
  95 |         self._counters[name] += 1
  96 |         label_str = " ".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in labels.items())
  97 |         log.info(f"METRIC name={name} value={self._counters[name]} {label_str}".strip())

 100 | class FixedEnhancedStateManager:
 101 |     """
 102 |     Fixed Enhanced State Manager - Solves critical processing state issues with thread safety
 103 | 
 104 |     Key Fixes:
 105 |     1. Separates resumption index from progress tracking
 106 |     2. Only performs reverse gap detection on startup, not every save
 107 |     3. Updates category totals with real-time scraping discoveries
 108 |     4. Fixes metric placement in processing state
 109 |     5. Preserves interruption state correctly
 110 |     6. Thread-safe atomic operations with file locking
 111 |     7. Phase-specific atomic commit methods
 112 |     """
 113 | 
 114 |     SCHEMA_VERSION = "1.2_THREAD_SAFE"
 115 |     _ALLOW_OVERWRITE_ENV = "ALLOW_DENOMINATOR_OVERWRITE"
 116 | 
 117 |     def __init__(self, supplier_name: str, base_path: Optional[str] = None, lock_timeout: float = 5.0):
 118 |         # Ensure instance logger for methods that use self.log
 119 |         self.log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 120 |         self.supplier_name = supplier_name
 121 |         self.lock_timeout = lock_timeout
 122 | 
 123 |         # Use relative base path if not provided
 124 |         if base_path is None:
 125 |             self.base_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent  # Go up to project root
 126 |         else:
 127 |             self.base_path = Path(base_path)
 128 | 
 129 |         self.state_file_path = get_processing_state_path(supplier_name)
 130 |         self.state_data = self._initialize_state()
 131 |         self._startup_completed = False  # Track if startup analysis is done
 132 |         self._last_save_time = 0.0
 133 |         self._drift_mirrored_once = False  # Mirror legacy from system at most once per run
 134 |         
 135 |         # Thread safety components (re-entrant to avoid self-deadlock on nested saves)
 136 |         self._write_lock = threading.RLock() if threading else None
 137 |         self._atomic_writer = ThreadSafeStateWriter(self.state_file_path) if ThreadSafeStateWriter else None
 138 |         
 139 |         # Legacy writer tracking for deprecation
 140 |         self._legacy_writer_calls = 0
 141 |         self._deprecated_methods_used = []
 142 |         
 143 |         # Metrics for observability
 144 |         self.metrics = SimpleMetrics()
 145 |         
 146 |         # one-time phase latches
 147 |         self._phase_first_after_resume_emitted: dict = {}
 148 |         self._phase_resume_honored_emitted: dict = {}
 149 |         
 150 |         # Single-writer proof 
 151 |         self._writer_session_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())
 152 |         
 153 |         # Load system config for toggles (best-effort)
 154 |         self.system_config: Dict[str, Any] = {}
 155 |         try:
 156 |             from config.system_config_loader import SystemConfigLoader
 157 | 
 158 |             self.system_config = SystemConfigLoader().get_system_config()
 159 |         except Exception:
 160 |             self.system_config = {}
 161 | 
 162 |     def enter_runtime_phase(self) -> None:
 163 |         """
 164 |         One-way latch indicating that startup initialization (freezing totals,
 165 |         manifest, config hash, etc.) has completed. After this point, NO save
 166 |         path may call file-grounded recomputation.
 167 |         """
 168 |         sp = self.state_data.setdefault("system_progression", {})
 169 |         self._validate_and_clamp_resume_ptr(sp)
 170 |         self._startup_completed = True
 171 |         log.debug("STATE: entered runtime phase")
 172 | 
 173 |     def _initialize_state(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 174 |         """Initialize state structure with all required fields and FIXED architecture"""
 175 |         return {
 176 |             "schema_version": self.SCHEMA_VERSION,
 177 |             "created_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 178 |             "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 179 |             "supplier_name": self.supplier_name,
 180 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX 1: Separate resumption from progress tracking
 181 |             "last_processed_index": 0,  # Legacy field for backward compatibility
 182 |             "resumption_index": 0,  # Where to resume after interruption
 183 |             "progress_index": 0,  # Current progress in active session
 184 |             "session_products_processed": 0,  # Products processed in current session
 185 |             "total_products": 0,
 186 |             "processing_status": "initialized",
 187 |             "is_fresh_start": True,  # 🚨 FIX 1: Add missing fresh start field (corrected by _detect_actual_fresh_start)
 188 |             "category_performance": {},
 189 |             "error_log": [],
 190 |             "successful_products": 0,
 191 |             "profitable_products": 0,
 192 |             "total_profit_found": 0.0,
 193 |             "processing_statistics": {
 194 |                 "start_time": None,
 195 |                 "end_time": None,
 196 |                 "total_runtime_seconds": 0,
 197 |                 "average_time_per_product": 0,
 198 |                 "products_per_hour": 0,
 199 |             },
 200 |             "metadata": {
 201 |                 "version": "3.8+_THREAD_SAFE",
 202 |                 "config_hash": "",
 203 |                 "runtime_settings": {},
 204 |                 "system_info": {},
 205 |                 "fix_applied": "legacy_writer_migration_20250906",
 206 |                 "thread_safety": "enabled",
 207 |                 "atomic_operations": "enabled",
 208 |             },
 209 |             # 🚨 REMOVED: processed_products section - hash search uses linking map + cached products only
 210 |             # Legacy section removed: single source of truth is system_progression only.
 211 |             # Keep empty dict for read-compat with old snapshots.
 212 |             # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX 3: Enhanced gap processing with better tracking
 213 |             "gap_processing": {
 214 |                 "phase": "not_started",
 215 |                 "gap_products_total": 0,
 216 |                 "gap_products_processed": 0,
 217 |                 "gap_start_time": None,
 218 |                 "gap_end_time": None,
 219 |                 "gap_profitable_found": 0,
 220 |                 "gap_last_processed_url": "",
 221 |                 "category_completion_status": {},
 222 |                 "reverse_gap_detected": False,  # 🚨 NEW: Track reverse gap state
 223 |                 "startup_analysis_completed": False,  # 🚨 NEW: Track startup completion
 224 |             },
 225 |             # ✅ NEW: Separated progression metrics for precise resumption
 226 |             "system_progression": {
 227 |                 "current_phase": "supplier",
 228 |                 "current_category_index": 0,
 229 |                 "current_category_url": "",
 230 |                 "total_categories": 0,
 231 |                 "current_product_index_in_category": 0,
 232 |                 "total_products_in_current_category": 0,
 233 |                 "supplier_extraction_resumption_index": 0,
 234 |                 "amazon_analysis_resumption_index": 0,
 235 |             },
 236 |             # ✅ NEW: User-facing metrics (not used for resumption logic)
 237 |             "user_display_metrics": {
 238 |                 "total_products": 0,
 239 |                 "successful_products": 0,
 240 |                 "progress_count": 0,
 241 |                 "session_products_processed": 0,
 242 |             },
 243 |         }
 244 | 
 245 |     def _detect_actual_fresh_start(self):
 246 |         """Enhanced fresh start detection with processed product validation"""
 247 | 
 248 |         # Original flag-based logic
 249 |         flag_fresh_start = self.state_data.get("is_fresh_start", True)
 250 | 
 251 |         # NEW: Validate against actual processed data
 252 |         successful_products = self.state_data.get("successful_products", 0)
 253 |         global_counters = self.state_data.get("global_counters", {})
 254 |         total_processed = global_counters.get("total_products_processed", 0)
 255 |         system_progression = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 256 |         current_category = system_progression.get("current_category_index")
 257 | 
 258 |         # True fresh start criteria: No processed products AND no category progress
 259 |         actual_fresh_start = (
 260 |             successful_products == 0 and total_processed == 0 and current_category is None
 261 |         )
 262 | 
 263 |         # Detect and log contradictions
 264 |         if flag_fresh_start != actual_fresh_start:
 265 |             log.warning(
 266 |                 f"🚨 FRESH START CONTRADICTION DETECTED: "
 267 |                 f"flag={flag_fresh_start} actual={actual_fresh_start} "
 268 |                 f"products={successful_products} processed={total_processed} "
 269 |                 f"category={current_category}"
 270 |             )
 271 | 
 272 |             # Use actual state rather than flag
 273 |             self.state_data["is_fresh_start"] = actual_fresh_start
 274 | 
 275 |         return actual_fresh_start
 276 | 
 277 |     def load_state(self) -> bool:
 278 |         """
 279 |         Load existing state from file, with backward compatibility
 280 |         Returns True if state was loaded, False if starting fresh
 281 |         """
 282 |         if not self.state_file_path.exists():
 283 |             log.info(f"No existing state file found for {self.supplier_name}, starting fresh")
 284 |             return False
 285 | 
 286 |         try:
 287 |             with open(self.state_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 288 |                 loaded_data = json.load(f)
 289 | 
 290 |             # Handle backward compatibility
 291 |             if isinstance(loaded_data, dict) and "schema_version" not in loaded_data:
 292 |                 log.info("Converting legacy state format to fixed enhanced format")
 293 |                 self._migrate_legacy_state(loaded_data)
 294 |             else:
 295 |                 # Merge loaded data with initialized structure
 296 |                 self._merge_state_data(loaded_data)
 297 | 
 298 |             log.info(
 299 |                 f"Loaded state for {self.supplier_name} - resumption from index {self.state_data['resumption_index']}"
 300 |             )
 301 |             # 🧹 MIGRATION SCRUB: remove legacy subtree if present
 302 |             if isinstance(self.state_data, dict) and "supplier_extraction_progress" in self.state_data:
 303 |                 try:
 304 |                     del self.state_data["supplier_extraction_progress"]
 305 |                     log.info("🧹 Removed legacy 'supplier_extraction_progress' from state on load")
 306 |                     self.save_state_atomic()
 307 |                 except Exception as e:
 308 |                     log.warning(f"⚠️ Could not remove legacy subtree: {e}")
 309 |             return True
 310 | 
 311 |         except Exception as e:
 312 |             log.warning(f"Failed to load state file: {e}, starting fresh")
 313 |             return False
 314 | 
 315 |     def _migrate_legacy_state(self, legacy_data: Dict[str, Any]):
 316 |         """Migrate legacy state format to fixed enhanced format"""
 317 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Properly migrate legacy index to resumption_index
 318 |         legacy_index = legacy_data.get("last_processed_index", 0)
 319 |         self.state_data["last_processed_index"] = legacy_index
 320 |         self.state_data["resumption_index"] = legacy_index
 321 |         self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0  # Always start fresh progress
 322 |         self.state_data["processing_status"] = "migrated_from_legacy"
 323 | 
 324 |         # Migrate other legacy fields if they exist
 325 |         # Legacy subtree removed; no legacy-specific migration beyond indices
 326 |         log.info(f"Successfully migrated legacy state format - resumption index: {legacy_index}")
 327 | 
 328 |     def _merge_state_data(self, loaded_data: Dict[str, Any]):
 329 |         """Merge loaded data with initialized structure"""
 330 | 
 331 |         def deep_merge(base: Dict, overlay: Dict) -> Dict:
 332 |             result = base.copy()
 333 |             for key, value in overlay.items():
 334 |                 if key in result and isinstance(result[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
 335 |                     result[key] = deep_merge(result[key], value)
 336 |                 else:
 337 |                     result[key] = value
 338 |             return result
 339 | 
 340 |         self.state_data = deep_merge(self.state_data, loaded_data)
 341 |         self.state_data["last_updated"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
 342 | 
 343 |         # 🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Ensure new fields exist even in merged data
 344 |         if "resumption_index" not in self.state_data:
 345 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = self.state_data.get("last_processed_index", 0)
 346 |         if "progress_index" not in self.state_data:
 347 |             self.state_data["progress_index"] = 0
 348 |         if "session_products_processed" not in self.state_data:
 349 |             self.state_data["session_products_processed"] = 0
 350 |         
 351 |         # 🚨 CROSS-RUN MONOTONICITY GUARD: Validate resumption pointer never decreases between runs
 352 |         self._validate_cross_run_monotonicity()
 353 | 
 354 |     def _validate_cross_run_monotonicity(self) -> None:
 355 |         """Validate that resumption indices never decrease between runs (prevents 4→1 rollbacks)."""
 356 |         sp = self.state_data.get("system_progression", {})
 357 |         
 358 |         # Get current indices
 359 |         current_cat_idx = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
 360 |         current_prod_idx = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
 361 |         current_resumption = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 362 |         
 363 |         # Get stored previous run values (persistent across sessions)
 364 |         last_known_cat = self.state_data.get("_last_known_category_index", 0)
 365 |         last_known_prod = self.state_data.get("_last_known_product_index", 0)
 366 |         last_known_resumption = self.state_data.get("_last_known_resumption_index", 0)
 367 |         
 368 |         # Check for regression
 369 |         regression_detected = False
 370 |         
 371 |         if current_cat_idx < last_known_cat:
 372 |             log.error(f"🚨 CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Category index {current_cat_idx} < previous {last_known_cat}")
 373 |             regression_detected = True
 374 |             
 375 |         if current_cat_idx == last_known_cat and current_prod_idx < last_known_prod:
 376 |             log.error(f"🚨 CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Product index {current_prod_idx} < previous {last_known_prod}")
 377 |             regression_detected = True
 378 |             
 379 |         if current_resumption < last_known_resumption:
 380 |             log.error(f"🚨 CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Resumption index {current_resumption} < previous {last_known_resumption}")
 381 |             regression_detected = True
 382 |             
 383 |         if regression_detected:
 384 |             log.warning("🔧 CORRECTING CROSS-RUN REGRESSION: Restoring previous indices")
 385 |             # Restore to safe previous values
 386 |             sp["current_category_index"] = max(current_cat_idx, last_known_cat)
 387 |             sp["current_product_index_in_category"] = max(current_prod_idx, last_known_prod)
 388 |             self.state_data["resumption_index"] = max(current_resumption, last_known_resumption)
 389 |             
 390 |         # Update stored values for next run
 391 |         self.state_data["_last_known_category_index"] = sp.get("current_category_index", 0)
 392 |         self.state_data["_last_known_product_index"] = sp.get("current_product_index_in_category", 0)
 393 |         self.state_data["_last_known_resumption_index"] = self.state_data.get("resumption_index", 0)
 394 | 
 395 |     def perform_startup_analysis(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 396 |         """
 397 |         🚨 CRITICAL FIX: Perform reverse gap detection and category analysis ONLY on startup
 398 |         This method should be called ONCE at the beginning of a session, not on every save
 399 |         """
 400 |         if self._startup_completed:
 401 |             log.info("Startup analysis already completed for this session")
 402 |             return self.state_data.get("gap_processing", {}).get("category_completion_status", {})
 403 |
